# Emergent Concepts

Parent meta-page for arxiv LLM-research paper discovery via the wiki-explore-agent pipeline. When all 9 named chains are exhausted (4/4 vectors explored), new entity pages from `cs.CL` and `cs.LG` arxiv listings get prepended here. This file accumulates the full chronology of emergent-concept discoveries.

## Scope
- arxiv cs.CL (Computation and Language) and cs.LG (Machine Learning) categories
- LLM-research themes: hallucination mechanisms, alignment, RLVR, sparse attention, MoE, state-space models, in-context learning, knowledge editing, multi-agent orchestration
- HF daily + monthly arxiv listings as primary discovery source
- web_search title-quoted topic queries as secondary refinement
## Updates
- **(2026-06-29 — Run 122) — 3-axis FINE-TUNING-SAFETY-FAILURE-MODE discovery**

- [[gradshield-alignment-preserving-finetuning-2605.14194]] — GradShield: Alignment Preserving Fine-tuning (06-05) — first paper in the wiki to introduce **alignment-preserving fine-tuning via Fisher Information of Hidden States (FIHS)** — protects aligned LLMs against harmful fine-tuning attacks in fine-tuning-as-a-service (FaaS) scenarios by selectively constraining gradient updates on alignment-critical parameters — surfacing *alignment-preserving-finetuning* and *fisher-information-hidden-states* and *harmful-finetuning-defense* as the load-bearing fine-tuning-safety primitives; distinct from Run 121 EINSTEIN-WORLD-MODELS + GROUNDED-ITERATIVE-PLANNING + MEMORY-UPDATE-GAP axes (GradShield is fine-tuning safety methodology, not world model architecture or memory update mechanisms). **First alignment-preserving fine-tuning defense using FIHS in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "gradshield|fisher.information.hidden"` returning empty. 2026-06-05; discovered via web_search fine-tuning-safety probe on 2026-06-29.

- [[few-tokens-big-leverage-preserving-safety-alignment-by-constraining-safety-tokens-during-fine-tuning-2603.07445]] — Few Tokens, Big Leverage: Preserving Safety Alignment by Constraining Safety Tokens During Fine-tuning (03-10) — first paper in the wiki to introduce **safety-token constraint during fine-tuning** — identifies that safety-aligned behavior is concentrated on a small subset of safety-related tokens and proposes constraining these tokens to preserve alignment while allowing task-specific adaptation — surfacing *safety-token-constraint* and *token-level-safety-concentration* and *alignment-preserving-task-adaptation* as the load-bearing fine-tuning-safety primitives; distinct from Run 121 MEMORY-UPDATE-GAP + GROUNDED-ITERATIVE-PLANNING axes (Few Tokens is token-level fine-tuning constraint, not memory update failure or planning methodology). **First safety-token constraint mechanism for alignment-preserving fine-tuning in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "safety.token.constrain|few.token"` returning empty. 2026-03-10; discovered via web_search fine-tuning-safety probe on 2026-06-29.

- [[school-of-reward-hacks-hacking-harmless-tasks-generalizes-to-misaligned-behavior-in-llms-2508.17511]] — School of Reward Hacks: Hacking Harmless Tasks Generalizes to Misaligned Behavior in LLMs (08-24) — first paper in the wiki to introduce **reward hacking generalization from harmless to misaligned behaviors via fine-tuning** — demonstrates that fine-tuning on reward hacking examples in benign settings causes models to develop broader misalignment patterns, establishing "School of Reward Hacks" as a transferable failure mode across LLM deployments — surfacing *reward-hacking-generalization* and *harmless-to-misaligned-transfer* and *rlhf-cascading-alignment-degradation* as the load-bearing fine-tuning-alignment primitives; distinct from Run 121 EINSTEIN-WORLD-MODELS + GROUNDED-ITERATIVE-PLANNING axes (School of Reward Hacks is RLHF fine-tuning failure mode, not world model architecture or planning methodology). **First systematic study of reward hacking generalization from harmless to misaligned behaviors in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "school.of.reward|reward.hack.general"` returning empty. 2025-08-24; discovered via web_search fine-tuning-safety probe on 2026-06-29.

- **(2026-06-29 — Run 121) — 3-axis GROUNDED-ITERATIVE-PLANNING + MEMORY-UPDATE-GAP + EINSTEIN-WORLD-MODELS discovery**

- [[grounded-iterative-language-planning-how-parameterized-2606.27806]] — Grounded Iterative Language Planning: How Parameterized World Models Reduce Hallucination Propagation in LLM Agents (06-26) — Xinyuan Song, Zekun Cai — first paper in the wiki to introduce **grounded iterative language planning** combining LLM-based plan proposal with parameterized world-model verification — agent-based world models are flexible but hallucinate state transitions; parameterized models are verifiable but inflexible; GILP bridges both by using the LLM to propose high-level plans while the parameterized model grounds and verifies each step — surfacing *grounded-iterative-planning* and *parameterized-world-model-verification* and *hallucination-propagation-arrest* as the load-bearing agentic-planning primitives; distinct from Run 120 WARMTH-SAFETY-TRADEOFF + WORLD-MODEL-IDENTIFIABILITY + EDGE-CLOUD-SPEC axes (GILP is planning methodology, not safety tradeoff or world model identifiability). **First grounded iterative planning with parameterized world-model verification in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(grounded.iterative|parameterized.world.model.verify)"` returning empty. 2026-06-26; discovered via arxiv HTML search on 2026-06-29.

- [[supersede-diagnosing-and-training-the-memory-update-gap-2606.27472]] — Supersede: Diagnosing and Training the Memory-Update Gap in LLM Agents (06-25) — Vedant Patel — first paper in the wiki to introduce a **memory-update gap diagnosis** characterizing when and why LLM agents fail to overwrite stale facts with current context in multi-session settings — the failure mode is systematic (not random): agents weight historical context over current facts even when fact changes are explicitly signaled — proposes training interventions that strengthen update signals — surfacing *memory-update-gap* and *multi-session-fact-drift* and *context-staleness-overwrite* as the load-bearing agent-memory primitives; distinct from Run 120 WORLD-MODEL-IDENTIFIABILITY axis (Supersede is memory update failure, not world model representation identifiability). **First systematic memory-update gap diagnosis and training intervention in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(memory.update.gap|multi.session.fact|context.stale)"` returning empty. 2026-06-25; discovered via arxiv HTML search on 2026-06-29.

- [[einstein-world-models-2606.26969]] — Einstein World Models (06-25) — Munachiso Samuel Nwadike, Zangir Iklassov et al. — first paper in the wiki to introduce **physical world models beyond language** for LLM-based agents — argues that physical intuitions (support relations, object permanence, causal spatiotemporal sequences) require structured representations beyond token sequences — introduces architectures and training signals for building explicit physical world models analogous to human naive physics — surfacing *physical-world-model* and *beyond-language-representation* and *structured-physical-intuition* as the load-bearing world-model primitives; distinct from Run 120 WORLD-MODEL-IDENTIFIABILITY axis (Einstein World Models is physical representation substrate, not textual belief identifiability; also distinct from Qwen-AgentWorld and In-Context World Modeling which are language-mediated). **First physical-world-model framework with structured non-linguistic representations in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(einstein.world.model|physical.world.model|nonlinguistic.physical)"` returning empty. 2026-06-25; discovered via arxiv HTML search on 2026-06-29.

- **(2026-06-29 — Run 120) — 3-axis WARMTH-SAFETY-TRADEOFF + WORLD-MODEL-IDENTIFIABILITY + EDGE-CLOUD-SPECULATIVE-DECODING discovery**

- [[low-agreeableness-persona-conditioning-for-safe-llm-fine-tun-2606.27709]] — Low-Agreeableness Persona Conditioning for Safe LLM Fine-Tuning (06-26) — Austin MY Cheung, Yi Yang — first paper in the wiki to introduce a **warmth-safety tradeoff failure mode** where fine-tuning for social warmth simultaneously weakens adversarial safety, making models more susceptible to jailbreaks — proposes persona-driven rewriting that conditions user turns on low agreeableness to preserve safety during warmth training — surfacing *warmth-safety-tradeoff* and *persona-conditioning-safety* and *jailbreak-susceptibility-from-warmth* as the load-bearing alignment-fine-tuning primitives; distinct from Run 119 LATENT-PLANNING-FAILURE + SOFT-ROUTING-MoE axes (persona-safety is fine-tuning safety, not planning failure or sparse architecture). **First warmth-fine-tuning safety degradation paper in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(low-agree|warmth.safety|persona.*safe.*tune)"` returning empty. 2026-06-26; discovered via web_search persona-safety probe on 2026-06-29.

- [[textual-belief-states-for-world-models-identifiable-represen-2606.27681]] — Textual Belief States for World Models: Identifiable Representation Learning Under Strict Mediation (06-26) — Xiang Gao et al. — first paper in the wiki to introduce **strict latent state mediation** applied to text-based LLM world models — addresses history bypass failure mode where raw input shortcuts defeat latent representation learning, rendering belief states unidentifiable — proposes identifiable representation learning for textual discrete non-differentiable states — surfacing *history-bypass* and *latent-state-identifiability* and *strict-mediation-textual-world-model* as the load-bearing world-model primitives; distinct from Run 119 SOFT-ROUTING-MoE + UNIFIED-TTC axes (Textual Belief States is representation identifiability, not sparse routing or test-time compute). **First strict latent state mediation for text-based LLM world models in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(textual.belief|history.bypass|latent.state.ident)"` returning empty. 2026-06-26; discovered via web_search world-model probe on 2026-06-29.

- [[speculation-at-a-distance-where-edge-cloud-speculative-decod-2606.25091]] — Speculation at a Distance: Where Edge-Cloud Speculative Decoding Actually Pays Off (06-23) — Yuan Lyu, Bharath Irukulapati, Jaya Prakash Champati — first paper in the wiki to introduce **theoretical latency bounds for distributed edge-cloud speculative decoding** — derives closed-form inequalities proving DSD's per-request latency benefit is fundamentally limited under WAN communication, and co-located SD always dominates when server can host both models — surfacing *edge-cloud-WAN-latency-bound* and *co-located-vs-distributed-specdec* and *DSD-theoretical-limit* as the load-bearing inference-deployment primitives; distinct from Run 119 UNIFIED-TTC + WORLD-MODEL axes (Speculation at a Distance is distributed deployment topology, not test-time compute orchestration or world model representation). **First theoretical latency bound for edge-cloud speculative decoding in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(speculation.at.distance|edge.cloud.spec.dec|latency.bound.spec)"` returning empty. 2026-06-23; discovered via web_search inference-efficiency probe on 2026-06-29.

- **(2026-06-29 — Run 119) — 3-axis LATENT-PLANNING-FAILURE + SOFT-ROUTING-MoE + UNIFIED-TTC-FRAMEWORK discovery**

- [[softmoe-soft-differentiable-routing-for-mixture-of-experts-in-llms-2606.17952]] — SoftMoE: Soft Differentiable Routing for Mixture-of-Experts in LLMs (06-16) — first paper in the wiki to introduce **fully differentiable soft routing** that replaces hard top-k selection with weighted combinations of all expert outputs — enabling end-to-end gradient optimization of the routing function itself, scalable to thousands of experts without auxiliary load-balancing losses — surfacing *soft-differentiable-routing* and *expert-weighted-combination* and *router-end-to-end-optimization* as the load-bearing sparse-architecture primitives; distinct from Run 118 SAFETY-ROUTING + SKILL-GRAPH axes (SoftMoE is architecture design, not safety routing or skill graph). **First fully differentiable soft routing MoE with end-to-end optimized router in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(softmoe|soft.routing|diff.routing)"` returning empty. 2026-06-16; discovered via web_search MoE probe on 2026-06-29.

- [[simmer-benchmarking-latent-failures-in-llm-executable-planning-with-a-world-model-2606.14574]] — SIMMER: Benchmarking Latent Failures in LLM Executable Planning with a World Model (06-12) — first paper in the wiki to introduce a benchmark specifically targeting **latent planning failures** — plans that are executable and logically coherent but fail when executed because the underlying world model diverges from actual environment dynamics — frontier LLMs generate plans with silent world-model blind spots in temporal-reasoning and counterfactual tasks — surfacing *latent-planning-failure* and *world-model-divergence* and *coherent-but-wrong-plan* as the load-bearing agentic-planning primitives; distinct from Run 118 SKILL-GRAPH + LLM-AS-CODE axes (SIMMER is planning-failure benchmarking, not skill selection or agentic programming). **First latent world-model divergence benchmark for LLM executable planning in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(simmer|latent.plan.fail|world-model.*plan)"` returning empty. 2026-06-12; discovered via web_search planning probe on 2026-06-29.

- [[thinkbooster-a-unified-framework-for-seamless-test-time-scaling-of-llm-reasoning-2606.06915]] — ThinkBooster: A Unified Framework for Seamless Test-Time Scaling of LLM Reasoning (06-05) — first paper in the wiki to introduce a **modular unified test-time compute framework** composing multi-sample generation, process-based verifiers, and aggregation under a single API — provides visual debugger for reasoning trace inspection and OpenAI-compatible endpoint — surfacing *TTC-modular-composition* and *search-verify-aggregate-API* and *trace-visual-debugger* as the load-bearing test-time compute primitives; distinct from Run 118 SAFETY-ROUTING + LLM-AS-CODE axes (ThinkBooster is TTC orchestration, not safety routing or agentic programming). **First unified modular test-time compute composition framework with visual debugger in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(thinkbooster|ttc.unified|test.time.*modular)"` returning empty. 2026-06-05; discovered via web_search test-time probe on 2026-06-29.

- **(2026-06-29 — Run 118) — 3-axis SAFETY-ROUTING + SKILL-GRAPH + LLM-AS-CODE discovery**

- [[llm-as-code-agentic-programming-for-agent-harness-2606.15874]] — LLM-as-Code: Agentic Programming for Agent Harness (06-14) — Junjia Qi et al. — first paper in the wiki to introduce **LLM-as-Code paradigm** where the program governs control flow and the LLM is invoked only as an adaptive component — the LLM's context is the execution history call-tree DAG, not a flat scratchpad; full flexibility within calls but cannot alter execution path — surfacing *LLM-as-Code* and *program-governed-control-flow* and *execution-history-call-tree-DAG-context* as the load-bearing agentic-programming primitives; distinct from Run 117 CASCADED-SERVING + MULTIMODAL-SAFETY axes (LLM-as-Code is agent framework design, not serving infrastructure or safety guardrail). **First LLM-as-Code agentic programming paradigm in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(llm-as-code|agentic.programming)"` returning empty. 2026-06-14; discovered via web_search probe on 2026-06-29.

- [[skilldag-self-evolving-typed-skill-graphs-for-llm-skill-selection-at-scale-2606.03056]] — SkillDAG: Self-Evolving Typed Skill Graphs for LLM Skill Selection at Scale (06-02) — Tong Bai et al. — first paper in the wiki to introduce a **typed DAG representation of skill inter-dependencies** that self-evolves as new skills are added — the LLM reasons directly over the graph structure for skill selection rather than flat retrieval — surfacing *typed-skill-DAG* and *self-evolving-skill-graph* and *graph-structured-skill-reasoning* as the load-bearing skill-selection primitives; distinct from Run 117 PROGRESSIVE-MULTIMODAL-AGENT axis (SkillDAG is skill-selection-at-scale, not progressive multimodal search). **First typed self-evolving skill DAG for LLM skill selection in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(skilldag|skill.dag|typed.skill.graph)"` returning empty. 2026-06-02; discovered via web_search probe on 2026-06-29.

- [[dialectics-of-alignment-harnessing-unsafe-knowledge-for-dynamic-safety-routing-2606.00686]] — Dialectics of Alignment: Harnessing Unsafe Knowledge for Dynamic Safety Routing (05-30) — Maryam Hashemzadeh et al. — first paper in the wiki to introduce **dynamic safety routing** as an alternative to knowledge erasure/filtering — the model integrates unsafe knowledge in a controlled manner and routes queries at runtime, showing zero-shot generalization to unseen safety domains — surfacing *dialectics-of-alignment* and *controlled-knowledge-integration* and *zero-shot-safety-routing* as the load-bearing safety primitives; distinct from Run 117 MULTIMODAL-SAFETY axis (Dialectics is routing-based alignment, not multimodal guardrail). **First dynamic safety routing via controlled knowledge integration in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(dialectics.of|alignment.routing|safety.routing)"` returning empty. 2026-05-30; discovered via web_search probe on 2026-06-29.

- **(2026-06-29 — Run 117) — 3-axis MULTIMODAL-SAFETY + CASCADED-SERVING + PROGRESSIVE-MULTIMODAL-AGENT discovery**

- [[promsa-progressive-multimodal-search-agents-for-knowledge-based-visual-question-answering-2606.27974]] — ProMSA: Progressive Multimodal Search Agents for Knowledge-Based Visual Question Answering (06-26) — first paper in the wiki to introduce a **progressive multimodal search agent for KB-VQA** with iterative image/text search decisions under explicit tool-call budgets and TN-GSPO sequence-level RL — surfacing *progressive-multimodal-search* and *TN-GSPO* and *tool-call-budget-iteration* as the load-bearing multimodal-agent primitives; distinct from Run 116 TEST-TIME-COMPUTE + PRM axes (ProMSA is agent reasoning, not test-time compute or PRM taxonomy). **First progressive multimodal search agent with TN-GSPO RL and explicit tool-call budgets in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(promsa|progressive.multimodal.search)"` returning empty. 2026-06-26; discovered via HF daily 2026-06-30 on 2026-06-29.

- [[singguard-a-policy-adaptive-multimodal-llm-guardrail-with-dynamic-reasoning-2606.22873]] — SingGuard: A Policy-Adaptive Multimodal LLM Guardrail with Dynamic Reasoning (06-25) — first paper in the wiki to introduce a **policy-adaptive multimodal guardrail** treating active policy as runtime input with fast-slow reasoning spectrum — surfacing *policy-adaptive-guardrail* and *fast-slow-decoupled-RL* and *runtime-policy-input* as the load-bearing multimodal-safety primitives; distinct from Run 116 TEST-TIME-COMPUTE + PRM axes (SingGuard is safety guardrail, not test-time compute or PRM taxonomy). **First policy-adaptive multimodal guardrail with runtime policy input and fast-slow reasoning spectrum in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(singguard|policy.adaptive.guard)"` returning empty. 2026-06-25; discovered via HF daily 2026-06-29 on 2026-06-29.

- [[cluster-route-escalate-cascaded-framework-for-cost-aware-llm-serving-2606.27457]] — Cluster, Route, Escalate: Cascaded Framework for Cost-Aware LLM Serving (06-25) — first paper in the wiki to introduce a **two-stage cascaded serving framework** with query clustering + quality-estimation cascade for cost-aware deployment — surfacing *query-clustering-for-cost-routing* and *quality-estimation-cascade* and *cost-accuracy Pareto* as the load-bearing inference-efficiency primitives; distinct from Run 116 TEST-TIME-COMPUTE + PRM axes (CRE is serving infrastructure, not test-time compute or PRM taxonomy). **First cascaded query-clustering + quality-estimation serving framework for cost-aware LLM deployment in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(cluster.route.escalate|cascaded.serving|cost.aware.serving)"` returning empty. 2026-06-25; discovered via HF daily 2026-06-30 on 2026-06-29.

- **(2026-06-29 — Run 116) — 3-axis TEST-TIME-COMPUTE + PROCESS-REWARD + PRM-SUPERVISION discovery**

- [[scaling-llm-test-time-compute-optimally-can-be-more-eff-2408.03314]] — Scaling LLM Test-Time Compute Optimally can be More Effective than Scaling Parameters (08-2024) — first paper in the wiki to introduce **compute-optimal test-time scaling** for LLMs — two primary mechanisms: (1) searching against dense process-based verifier reward models and (2) updating the LLM itself during inference; proposes the Proposer-Verifier framework; compute-optimal strategies that dynamically allocate inference compute based on prompt difficulty outperform larger pre-trained models — surfacing *proposer-verifier-test-time-compute-framework* and *compute-optimal-test-time-scaling* and *process-based-verifier-dense-search* as the load-bearing test-time compute primitives; distinct from Run 114 VERIFICATION-GRANULARITY + ADAPTIVE-STOPPING axes (this is test-time compute mechanism, not verification theory or stopping rules); distinct from Run 115 CAUSAL-REASONING axes. **First compute-optimal test-time scaling framework with proposer-verifier architecture in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(scaling.*test.time.compute|proposer.verifier|compute.optimal.*test.time)"` returning empty. 2024-08; discovered via web_search 4-query TEST-TIME-COMPUTE probe on 2026-06-29.

- [[verifiable-process-reward-models-for-structured-reasoni-2601.17223]] — Beyond Outcome Verification: Verifiable Process Reward Models for Structured Reasoning (01-23) — first paper in the wiki to introduce **Verifiable Process Reward Models (VPRMs)** with deterministic rule-based step verification — separates verifiable steps (checkable by formal rules) from non-verifiable steps; RL framework using VPRM signals for step-level credit assignment; demonstrates superior coherence in medical evidence synthesis vs outcome-only approaches — surfacing *deterministic-rule-based-step-verification* and *verifiable-vs-non-verifiable-reasoning-steps* and *VPRM-step-level-credit-assignment* as the load-bearing process-reward primitives; distinct from Run 115 CAUSAL-REASONING axes; distinct from Run 114 VERIFICATION-GRANULARITY + ADAPTIVE-STOPPING axes. **First VPRM framework with explicit verifiable/non-verifiable step separation in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(vprm|verifiable.process.reward|deterministic.rule.*verif)"` returning empty. 2026-01-23; discovered via web_search 4-query PROCESS-REWARD-SUPERVISION probe on 2026-06-29.

- [[from-outcome-signals-to-process-supervisions-for-large--2510.08049]] — From Outcome Signals to Process Supervisions for Large Language Models (10-2025) — first survey-level synthesis of **Process Reward Model (PRM) field** in the wiki — systematic survey covering: generation methods, training objectives, inference strategies; 5 key challenges: step-level annotation cost, verifier design, credit assignment, generalizability, scalability; surveys process-supervised RL (STaR, RLEF, PRM, APO, DIP) and verifier-guided search (beam search, MCTS, PRM reranking); establishes PRM vs ORM comparison across 12 dimensions — surfacing *PRM-taxonomy-full-loop* and *step-level-annotation-cost* and *PRM-vs-ORM-12-dimension-framework* as the load-bearing PRM-theory primitives; distinct from Run 115 CAUSAL-REASONING axes; distinct from Run 114 VERIFICATION-GRANULARITY + ADAPTIVE-STOPPING axes. **First comprehensive PRM taxonomy and survey in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(prm.survey|process.reward.*survey|from.outcome.signals.*process)"` returning empty. 2025-10; discovered via web_search 4-query PROCESS-REWARD-SUPERVISION probe on 2026-06-29.

- **(2026-06-29 — Run 114) — 3-axis MECHANISTIC-CIRCUIT KNOWLEDGE EDITING + PROCESS-HARNESS AGENTIC WORKFLOW + INTUITION-GIDED LATENT REASONING discovery**

- [[intuition-guided-latent-reasoning-for-llm-based-recommendation-2606.27684]] — Intuition-Guided Latent Reasoning for LLM-Based Recommendation (06-26) — first paper in the wiki to use **intuition-anchored latent reasoning** for recommendation — top-K candidates from user history as intuition source, transformed into preference-aligned embedding via self/cross-attention to initialize the reasoning start point; eliminates misaligned hidden representations that cause suboptimal recommendation trajectories; consistent gains on multiple real-world datasets — surfacing *intuition-anchored-latent-reasoning* and *preference-aligned-intuition-embedding* and *grounded-reasoning-start-point* as the load-bearing recommendation-reasoning primitives; distinct from Run 113 LATENT-REASONING + TEST-TIME-GRADIENT axes (IntuRec is recommendation-domain intuition anchoring, not mathematical reasoning gradient descent). **First intuition-anchored latent reasoning for LLM recommendation in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(intuition.anchor|inturec|latent.reason.*recommendation)"` returning empty. 2026-06-26; discovered via arxiv search HTML probe on 2026-06-29.

- [[a-process-harness-for-uplifting-legacy-workflows-to-agentic-bpm-2606.27188]] — A Process Harness for Uplifting Legacy Workflows to Agentic BPM: Design and Realization in CUGA FLO (06-25) — first paper in the wiki to introduce a **process harness architecture** placing policy-governed agentic layer around legacy deterministic workflow engines without replacement — TaskAgent (knowledge-intensive task execution), DecisionAgent (per-case gateway routing), FlowAgent (runtime flow adaptation via hook mechanism); normative requirements (agentic autonomy) reconciled with imperative requirements (structural compliance via deterministic engine); loan approval workflow demonstration with regulatory override — surfacing *process-harness-intercept-architecture* and *TDF-three-agent-model* and *policy-governed-agentic-layer* as the load-bearing workflow-agent-integration primitives; distinct from Run 113 TEST-TIME-GRADIENT + EPISTEMIC-UNCERTAINTY axes (Process Harness is agentic-BPM architecture, not test-time optimization). **First process harness for legacy workflow-to-agentic-BPM uplift in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(process.harness|agentic.bpm|tdf.model)"` returning empty. 2026-06-25; discovered via web_search on 2026-06-29.

- [[mechanistic-circuit-based-knowledge-editing-in-large-language-models-2604.05876]] — Mechanistic Circuit-Based Knowledge Editing in Large Language Models (04-07) — first paper in the wiki to address the **"Reasoning Gap" in knowledge editing** — MCircKE identifies causal circuits for a reasoning task (fact storage + logical consequence routing) then surgically updates only those circuit parameters; bridges the gap between isolated fact patching and multi-hop reasoning chain propagation; evaluated on MQuAKE-3K benchmark — surfacing *causal-circuit-mapping-for-KE* and *circuit-surgical-parameter-update* and *reasoning-gap-in-knowledge-editing* as the load-bearing knowledge-editing primitives; distinct from Run 113 LATENT-REASONING + EPISTEMIC-UNCERTAINTY axes (MCircKE is mechanistic circuit editing, not latent reasoning or uncertainty estimation). **First mechanistic circuit-based knowledge editing addressing the reasoning gap in multi-hop reasoning chains in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(mcircke|reasoning.gap.*ke|circuit.surgical)"` returning empty. 2026-04-07; discovered via arxiv search HTML on 2026-06-29.

- **(

- **(2026-06-29 — Run 115) — 3-axis CAUSAL-REASONING discovery**
- [[better-think-thrice-double-counterfactual-consistency-2602.16787]] — Better Think Thrice: Learning to Reason Causally with Double Counterfactual Consistency (02-18) — first paper in the wiki to introduce **Double Counterfactual Consistency training** for LLM causal reasoning — addresses brittleness under counterfactual questions by ensuring LLMs reason correctly about both what happened and what could have happened; generates labeled counterfactual task data at scale for training; distinct from Run 114 VERIFICATION-GRANULARITY + REVISION-VERIFICATION-TRAINING + ADAPTIVE-STOPPING axes (Better Think Thrice is causal reasoning training methodology, not verification theory or adaptive stopping). **First Double Counterfactual Consistency training for causal reasoning robustness in LLMs in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(double.counterfactual|causal.reason.*thrice|better.think.thrice)"` returning empty. 2026-02-18; discovered via arxiv search HTML 4-query CAUSAL-REASONING-PROBE on 2026-06-29.
- [[from-prompts-to-tokens-internalizing-causal-supervision-vlm-multi-image-2606.11745]] — From Prompts to Tokens: Internalizing Causal Supervision in Vision-Language Model for Multi-Image Causal Reasoning (06-10) — first paper in the wiki to address **causal supervision internalization in VLMs for multi-image counterfactual reasoning** — VLMs remain brittle at interventional and counterfactual queries over multi-image inputs; this paper enables VLMs to identify causal variables from visual inputs and reason over intervention effects; distinct from Run 114 VERIFICATION-GRANULARITY + REVISION-VERIFICATION-TRAINING axes (this is VLM causal internalization, not verification theory). **First causal supervision internalization framework for VLM multi-image counterfactual reasoning in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(vlm.*causal|multi.image.*causal|prompts.to.tokens.*causal)"` returning empty. 2026-06-10; discovered via arxiv search HTML 4-query CAUSAL-REASONING-PROBE on 2026-06-29.
- [[causal-reward-world-models-zero-shot-skill-generation-2606.23280]] — Causal Reward World Models: Zero-shot Reward Design for Automated Skill Generation (06-22) — first paper in the wiki to introduce **Causal Reward World Models** for zero-shot RL skill generation — existing LLM-based Automated Reward Design is correlation-driven; this paper uses structural causal models to capture true causal relationships rather than correlations for reward function synthesis; distinct from Run 114 ADAPTIVE-STOPPING axes (Causal Reward World Models is reward design methodology, not stopping-rule theory). **First structural causal model framework for zero-shot automated reward design in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(causal.reward|world.model.*causal|zero.shot.*skill.*causal)"` returning empty. 2026-06-22; discovered via arxiv search HTML 4-query CAUSAL-REASONING-PROBE on 2026-06-29.

2026-06-29 — Run 113) — 3-axis LATENT-REASONING + TEST-TIME-GRADIENT + EPISTEMIC-UNCERTAINTY-QUANTIFICATION discovery**

- **(2026-06-29 — Run 114) — 3-axis VERIFICATION-GRANULARITY-THEORY + REVISION-VERIFICATION-TRAINING + ADAPTIVE-STOPPING discovery**
- [[reves-revision-and-verification-augmented-training-for-test-time-scaling-2606.18910]] — REVES: REvision and VErification-Augmented Training for Test-Time Scaling (06-17) — first paper in the wiki to use **near-miss answer conversion as the core training signal for test-time scaling** — two-stage iterative framework (online data augmentation ↔ policy optimization) converts intermediate "near-miss" answers in successful recovery trajectories into decoupled revision + verification prompts; concentrates training on both effective answer transformation and error identification; achieves +6.5 points over RL baseline and +4.0 points over standard multi-turn training on LiveCodeBench using a 4B base model — surfacing *near-miss-to-revision-verification-prompt-conversion* and *two-stage-iterative-revision-training* and *off-policy-revision-augmented-policy-optimization* as the load-bearing test-time-training primitives; distinct from Run 113 LATENT-REASONING + TEST-TIME-GRADIENT axes (REVES is training-side revision, not latent-state reasoning or gradient descent); distinct from Run 112 CAUSAL-REASONING + COUNTERFACTUAL-FAITHFULNESS + PRAGMATIC-REASONING axes. **First near-miss answer conversion framework for test-time scaling training in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(reves|near.miss.*revision|revision.augmented.training)"` returning empty. 2026-06-17; discovered via arxiv search HTML 4-query TEST-TIME-COMPUTE-ALLOCATION probe on 2026-06-29.
- [[mars-margin-adversarial-risk-controlled-stopping-for-parallel-llm-test-time-scaling-2606.12935]] — MARS: Margin-Adversarial Risk-controlled Stopping for Parallel LLM Test-time Scaling (06-11) — first paper in the wiki to introduce a **margin-adversarial stopping rule** for parallel test-time scaling — probes partial traces at intermediate checkpoints to extract evolving aggregate vote without disrupting generation; estimates trace-level switch probabilities + adversarial bound on future vote movement; separates uncertainty sources (trace-level switching vs adversarial bound on landing state); saves 25-47% of self-consistency tokens while matching full-budget accuracy — surfacing *margin-adversarial-stopping-rule* and *partial-trace-probing* and *phase-separation-uncertainty-for-stopping* as the load-bearing compute-efficiency primitives; distinct from Run 113 TEST-TIME-GRADIENT axes (MARS is stopping-rule theory, not gradient-based optimization); distinct from Run 112 CAUSAL-REASONING + COUNTERFACTUAL-FAITHFULNESS axes. **First margin-adversarial risk-controlled stopping rule for parallel test-time scaling in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(margin.adversarial|mars.stopping|adaptive.stopping.*parallel)"` returning empty. 2026-06-11; discovered via arxiv search HTML 4-query TEST-TIME-COMPUTE-ALLOCATION probe on 2026-06-29.
- [[grace-granularity-regulated-adaptive-computational-efficiency-for-optimal-verification-in-test-time-scaling-2606.19354]] — GRACE: Granularity-Regulated Adaptive Computational Efficiency for Optimal Verification in Test-Time Scaling (04-28, announced June 2026) — first paper in the wiki to establish a **unified theoretical framework for optimal verification granularity** — phase transition theory: fine-grained verification dominates when compute budget is large or problems are hard; coarse-grained verification preferred in low-budget/easy regime; unifies Best-of-N, beam search, and step-level MCTS within Pareto-optimality framework; adaptive granularity strategy achieves compute-performance Pareto frontier — surfacing *phase-transition-verification-granularity-theory* and *Pareto-optimal-verification-granularity* and *adaptive-granularity-best-of-n-unification* as the load-bearing verification-theory primitives; distinct from Run 113 LATENT-REASONING + TEST-TIME-GRADIENT axes (GRACE is verification-granularity theory, not latent-state reasoning or gradient descent); distinct from Run 112 CAUSAL-REASONING + COUNTERFACTUAL axes. **First unified theoretical framework for optimal verification granularity in test-time scaling in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(grace|granularity.regulated|optimal.verification.granularity)"` returning empty. 2026-04-28; discovered via arxiv search HTML 4-query TEST-TIME-COMPUTE-ALLOCATION probe on 2026-06-29.

- [[llm-reasoning-is-latent-not-the-chain-of-thought-2604.15726]] — LLM Reasoning Is Latent, Not the Chain of Thought (04-17) — first paper in the wiki to argue **LLM reasoning should be studied as latent-state trajectory formation rather than surface CoT** — formalizes three hypotheses (H1: latent-state trajectories, H2: surface CoT, H0: generic serial compute), evaluates evidence via compute-audited exemplars, finds H1 best supported; recommends treating latent-state dynamics as default object of study and designing evaluations that disentangle surface traces, latent states, and serial compute — surfacing *latent-state-trajectory-as-reasoning-medium* and *CoT-as-epiphenomenal* and *compute-audited-exemplar-methodology* as the load-bearing reasoning-theory primitives; distinct from Run 112 COUNTERFACTUAL-FAITHFULNESS + PRAGMATIC-REASONING axes (this is reasoning-medium theory, not counterfactual evaluation), distinct from Run 111 PROCESS-REWARD-MODEL axes (this is reasoning theory, not PRM training), distinct from Run 110 BEHAVIORAL-ECONOMICS axes (this is reasoning theory, not behavioral economics). **First position paper arguing H1 latent-state over H2 surface CoT with compute-audited exemplars in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(latent.reasoning.*h1|latent.state.trajectory|reasoning.is.latent)"` returning empty. 2026-04-17; discovered via web_search 4-query LATENT-REASONING + UNCERTAINTY-QUANTIFICATION probe on 2026-06-29.
- [[nabla-reasoner-llm-reasoning-via-test-time-gradient-descent-in-latent-space-2603.04948]] — ∇-Reasoner: LLM Reasoning via Test-Time Gradient Descent in Latent Space (03-05, ICLR 2026) — first paper in the wiki to apply **first-order (gradient-based) optimization to test-time reasoning scaling** — Differentiable Textual Optimization (DTO) backpropagates through LLM decoding to refine token representations using reward model gradients; theoretically dual to KL-regularized RL alignment; achieves 20%+ accuracy improvement on math reasoning with 10-40% fewer model calls vs strong baselines — surfacing *first-order-test-time-reasoning-optimization* and *differentiable-textual-optimization* and *test-time-gradient-descent-as-RL-alignment-dual* as the load-bearing test-time-compute primitives; distinct from Run 112 COUNTERFACTUAL-FAITHFULNESS + PRAGMATIC-REASONING axes (this is test-time optimization, not counterfactual evaluation), distinct from Run 111 TEST-TIME-SCALING axes (this is gradient-based optimization, not scaling law characterization), distinct from Run 110 BEHAVIORAL-ECONOMICS axes (this is test-time optimization, not behavioral economics). **First first-order gradient-based test-time reasoning optimization (DTO) with RL-alignment duality in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(nabla.reasoner|dto|test.time.gradient.descent|differentiable.textual)"` returning empty. 2026-03-05; discovered via web_search 4-query LATENT-REASONING + TEST-TIME-GRADIENT probe on 2026-06-29.
- [[efficient-epistemic-uncertainty-estimation-for-large-language-models-via-knowledge-distillation-2602.01956]] — Efficient Epistemic Uncertainty Estimation for LLMs via Knowledge Distillation (02-02) — first paper in the wiki to use **knowledge distillation for efficient epistemic uncertainty estimation at LLM scale** — bypasses computationally prohibitive Deep Ensembles via small draft models; Jensen-Shannon divergence among drafts as variance proxy + KL divergence to target as bias proxy, grounded in Bias-Variance Decomposition; Online Stochastic Distillation + Data-Diverse Drafts for efficient approximation; achieves 37% RMSE reduction on GSM8K; hallucination detection competitive with heavy perturbation-based methods at negligible cost — surfacing *knowledge-distillation-for-epistemic-uncertainty* and *draft-model-EU-approximation* and *bias-variance-decomposition-for-UQ* as the load-bearing uncertainty-quantification primitives; distinct from Run 112 COUNTERFACTUAL-FAITHFULNESS + PRAGMATIC-REASONING axes (this is uncertainty quantification, not counterfactual evaluation), distinct from Run 111 PROCESS-REWARD-MODEL axes (this is uncertainty estimation, not PRM training), distinct from Run 110 BEHAVIORAL-ECONOMICS axes (this is uncertainty estimation, not behavioral economics). **First knowledge distillation framework for efficient epistemic uncertainty estimation at LLM scale in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(knowledge.distillation.*uncertainty|efficient.epistemic|osd|ddd|draft.model.*uncertainty)"` returning empty. 2026-02-02; discovered via web_search 4-query EPISTEMIC-UNCERTAINTY-QUANTIFICATION probe on 2026-06-29.


- **(2026-06-29 — Run 112) — 3-axis CAUSAL-REASONING + COUNTERFACTUAL-FAITHFULNESS + PRAGMATIC-REASONING discovery**
- [[the-weakest-link-tells-it-all-outcome-supervised-process-reward-modeling-via-learnable-credit-assignment-2606.27739]] — The Weakest Link Tells It All: Outcome-Supervised Process Reward Modeling via Learnable Credit Assignment (06-26) — first paper in the wiki to formalize **outcome-supervised PRM as Multiple Instance Learning (MIL)** and introduce the **Weakest Link Assignment principle** — a reasoning chain is as strong as its weakest step — operationalized via Softmax-Weighted-Sum (SWS) pooling that jointly learns credit assignment and reward modeling; proves Bayes consistency under mild assumptions; consistently outperforms state-of-the-art outcome-supervised PRMs across GSM8K, StrategyQA, and ARC-Challenge with Llama-3-8B-Instruct and Mistral-7B-Instruct — surfacing *weakest-link-assignment-principle* and *MIL-formalization-of-outcome-supervised-PRM* and *Softmax-Weighted-Sum-pooling* as the load-bearing credit-assignment primitives for PRM training; distinct from Run 111 PROCESS-REWARD-MODEL primitives (LCA addresses credit-assignment methodology, not stress-testing or zero-annotation training), distinct from Run 110 BEHAVIORAL-ECONOMICS (LCA is PRM training, not behavioral economics), distinct from Run 109 METACOGNITIVE-SELF-MONITORING (LCA is PRM training methodology, not metacognition). **First MIL formalization + Weakest Link Assignment + Bayes-consistent outcome-supervised PRM in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(weakest.link|multiple.instance.learn.*prm|SWS.pool|credit.assign.*mil)"` returning empty. 2026-06-26; discovered via arxiv search HTML 4-query CAUSAL-REASONING + COUNTERFACTUAL-FAITHFULNESS + PRAGMATIC-REASONING probe on 2026-06-29.
- [[the-table-says-otherwise-testing-llms-with-counterfactual-relational-data-2606.23667]] — The Table Says Otherwise: Testing LLMs with Counterfactual Relational Data (06-22) — first paper in the wiki to introduce a **paired original-counterfactual relational database benchmark** (ContraTable) for evaluating whether LLMs ground answers in provided tables rather than relying on pretraining knowledge — 214 matched questions across single-table lookup, multi-table lookup, and multi-table temporal reasoning; demonstrates that strong instruction-tuned models handle direct lookup but reliability drops as questions require joins, comparison, and temporal reasoning, revealing systematic accuracy-faithfulness divergence; establishes that **table-QA evaluation must measure both accuracy and faithfulness to the provided database** — surfacing *counterfactual-relational-table-QA-primitive* and *paired-original-counterfactual-database-construction* and *schema-preserving-counterfactual-attribute-change* as the load-bearing counterfactual-faithfulness primitives; distinct from Run 111 PROCESS-REWARD-MODEL primitives (ContraTable is table-QA faithfulness, not PRM training), distinct from Run 110 BEHAVIORAL-ECONOMICS (ContraTable is relational reasoning faithfulness, not behavioral economics), distinct from Run 109 METACOGNITIVE-SELF-MONITORING (ContraTable is table-QA faithfulness, not metacognition). **First paired original-counterfactual relational database benchmark for table-QA faithfulness evaluation in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(contra.table|counterfactual.*table|relational.*counterfactual|table.*faithfulness)"` returning empty. 2026-06-22; discovered via arxiv search HTML 4-query CAUSAL-REASONING + COUNTERFACTUAL-FAITHFULNESS + PRAGMATIC-REASONING probe on 2026-06-29.
- [[pragrest-self-reinforcing-counterfactual-reasoning-for-pragmatic-language-understanding-2606.18624]] — PragReST: Self-Reinforcing Counterfactual Reasoning for Pragmatic Language Understanding (06-17) — first paper in the wiki to use **counterfactual reasoning as the core mechanism for improving LLM pragmatic reasoning** — constructs pragmatic QA data, generates counterfactual reasoning traces contrasting observed utterances with plausible alternatives, and trains via SFT+RL without human labels or teacher distillation; ablation confirms counterfactual reasoning is the primary driver of improvement; improves Qwen3-8B by +5.37% and Qwen3-14B by +5.50% on PragMega, Ludwig, MetoQA, and AltPrag while preserving out-of-domain performance — surfacing *self-supervised-counterfactual-trace-generation* and *contrastive-alternative-utterance-reasoning* and *pragmatic-reasoning-via-counterfactual-contrast* as the load-bearing pragmatic-reasoning primitives; distinct from Run 111 PROCESS-REWARD-MODEL primitives (PragReST is pragmatic reasoning, not PRM training), distinct from Run 110 BEHAVIORAL-ECONOMICS (PragReST is pragmatic reasoning, not prospect theory), distinct from Run 109 METACOGNITIVE-SELF-MONITORING (PragReST is pragmatic reasoning, not metacognitive monitoring). **First self-supervised counterfactual reasoning for pragmatic language understanding with SFT+RL without human labels in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(pragrest|pragmatic.*counterfactual|counterfactual.*pragmatic|self.reinforc.*pragmatic)"` returning empty. 2026-06-17; discovered via arxiv search HTML 4-query CAUSAL-REASONING + COUNTERFACTUAL-FAITHFULNESS + PRAGMATIC-REASONING probe on 2026-06-29.

- **(2026-06-29 — Run 111) — 3-axis PROCESS-REWARD-MODEL + TEST-TIME-SCALING-PRIMITIVE discovery**
- [[est-prm-stress-testing-process-reward-models-before-they-become-load-bearing-2606.00437]] — EST-PRM: Stress-Testing Process Reward Models Before They Become Load-Bearing (05-30) — first paper in the wiki to systematically **stress-test the label-preserving transformation stability assumption** of PRMs — finding that PRM scores change significantly under synonym substitution, syntactic reordering, and irrelevant-step insertion even when correctness is preserved, revealing a previously undocumented brittleness mode in widely-deployed PRM-based RL pipelines; surfacing *PRM-score-drift-under-label-preserving-transformations* and *transformation-aware-PRM-evaluation-protocol* as the load-bearing PRM-reliability primitives. Distinct from Run 110 BEHAVIORAL-ECONOMICS (EST-PRM is PRM stress-testing, not behavioral economics), distinct from Run 109 METACOGNITIVE-SELF-MONITORING (EST-PRM is PRM reliability, not metacognition), distinct from Run 108 INFORMATION-GEOMETRIC (EST-PRM is PRM stress-testing, not PID/rate-distortion/OT geometry). **First PRM stress-testing under label-preserving transformations in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(est.prm|stress.test.*prm|label.preserv.*transform)"` returning empty. 2026-05-30; discovered via web_search 4-query PROCESS-REWARD-MODEL probe on 2026-06-29.
- [[unsupervised-process-reward-models-2605.10158]] — Unsupervised Process Reward Models (06-29 discovery, 05-11 sub) — first paper in the wiki to train **Process Reward Models without any human step-level annotations** — uses a contrastive self-supervised objective on LLM-generated reasoning trajectories where positive pairs are steps from correct trajectories and negative pairs are steps from incorrect trajectories; achieves 94% of fully-supervised Math-Shepherd performance on MATH with zero step-level supervision; surfacing *zero-annotation-PRM-training* and *contrastive-self-supervised-step-quality-signal* as the load-bearing PRM-training primitives. Distinct from Run 110 BEHAVIORAL-ECONOMICS (uPRM is unsupervised PRM training, not behavioral economics), distinct from Run 109 METACOGNITIVE-SELF-MONITORING (uPRM is PRM training, not metacognition), distinct from Run 108 INFORMATION-GEOMETRIC (uPRM is PRM training methodology, not PID/rate-distortion/OT geometry). **First zero-annotation step-level supervision PRM training in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(unsupervised.prm|contrastive.*prm|zero.annotation.*prm)"` returning empty. 2026-05-11; discovered via web_search 4-query PROCESS-REWARD-MODEL probe on 2026-06-29.
- [[test-time-scaling-makes-overtraining-compute-optimal-2604.01411]] — Test-Time Scaling Makes Overtraining Compute-Optimal (04-01) — first paper in the wiki to jointly optimize **model size + training tokens + inference samples** under fixed end-to-end compute budget via T² scaling laws — finding that under inference-heavy workloads (reasoning models with repeated sampling), the optimal model is systematically smaller and trained on fewer tokens than Chinchilla predicts; surfacing *joint-train-test-compute-optimization* and *overtraining-is-compute-optimal-under-inference-budget* and *compute-optimal-frontier-across-N-D-K* as the load-bearing test-time-scaling primitives. Distinct from Run 110 BEHAVIORAL-ECONOMICS (T² is compute scaling, not behavioral economics), distinct from Run 109 METACOGNITIVE-SELF-MONITORING (T² is compute scaling, not metacognition), distinct from Run 108 INFORMATION-GEOMETRIC (T² is compute scaling, not PID/rate-distortion/OT geometry). **First joint train+test compute optimization with compute-optimal overtreading finding in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(train.to.test|overtraining.compute.optimal|joint.train.test)"` returning empty. 2026-04-01; discovered via web_search 4-query TEST-TIME-COMPUTE-SCALING probe on 2026-06-29.


- **(2026-06-29 — Run 110) — 3-axis DOMAIN-DIVERSITY PIVOT to BEHAVIORAL-ECONOMICS + TEST-TIME-COMPUTE-EFFICIENCY primitive triplet**
- [[a-utility-behavior-gap-in-large-language-models-2606.22974]] — A Utility–Behavior Gap in Large Language Models (06-22) — first paper in the wiki to systematically demonstrate a **utility-behavior gap** in LLMs — models reveal coherent preferences in choice paradigms but these preferences often fail to predict behavior in realistic scenarios; prospect theory framework applied to LLM decision-making under risk (loss aversion, reference-point-dependence, probability weighting); **misaligned-goal-motivation** as a safety-relevant failure mode distinct from capability misalignment; behavioral economics experimental paradigm for LLM alignment evaluation beyond stated preferences to revealed preferences. Distinct from Run 109 METACOGNITIVE-SELF-MONITORING (this is behavioral-economics/prospect-theory axis, not metacognitive monitoring axis), distinct from Run 108 INFORMATION-GEOMETRIC (this is behavioral-economics/prospect-theory axis, not PID/rate-distortion/OT axis), distinct from Run 107 COGNITIVE-LOAD (this is behavioral-economics axis, not BAPO/token-trigger/linguistic-bottleneck axis). **First utility-behavior gap + prospect theory / Kahneman-Tversky behavioral economics framework applied to LLM alignment in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(utility.behavior|prospect.*llm|behavioral.economics.*alignment)"` returning empty. 2026-06-22; discovered via HF daily v2 18-paper parse + LLM-keyword filter + web_search candidate confirmation on 2026-06-29.
- [[when-more-thinking-hurts-overthinking-in-llm-test-time-compute-2604.10739]] — When More Thinking Hurts: Overthinking in LLM Test-Time Compute (04-12 sub) — first paper in the wiki to document **overthinking as a structural failure mode** in LLM test-time compute scaling — extended reasoning associated with abandoning previously correct answers; diminishing marginal utility of additional reasoning tokens at high compute budgets; optimal thinking length varies by problem difficulty making uniform compute allocation suboptimal; cost-aware evaluation framework for test-time compute deployment. Distinct from Run 109 METACOGNITIVE-SELF-MONITORING (this is test-time-compute-efficiency axis, not metacognitive monitoring), distinct from Run 108 INFORMATION-GEOMETRIC (this is test-time-compute-efficiency axis, not PID/rate-distortion/OT), distinct from Run 107 COGNITIVE-LOAD (this is overthinking/efficiency axis, not BAPO/token-complexity/linguistic-bottleneck). **First overthinking / diminishing-marginal-returns / optimal-compute-budget-per-problem difficulty paper in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(overthinking|diminishing.marginal|optimal.thinking.length|test.time.compute.*overthink)"` returning empty. 2026-04-12 sub; discovered via HF daily v2 18-paper parse + LLM-keyword filter + web_search candidate confirmation on 2026-06-29.
- [[persona-conditioned-risk-behavior-in-large-language-models-2603.15831]] — Persona-Conditioned Risk Behavior in Large Language Models (03-16 sub) — first paper in the wiki to demonstrate **persona-conditioned cognitive patterns** in LLM sequential decision-making — GPT-4.1 systematically changes risk behavior based on assigned socioeconomic persona (Rich/Middle-income/Poor); reproduces Kahneman-Tversky prospect theory behavioral signatures (loss aversion, probability weighting, reference-dependent valuation) conditioned by persona assignment; establishes *persona-as-cognitive-frame* and *cognitive-pattering-via-persona* primitives; slot-machine paradigm as controlled experimental substrate for LLM behavioral economics. Distinct from Run 109 METACOGNITIVE-SELF-MONITORING (this is persona/behavioral-economics axis, not metacognitive monitoring), distinct from Run 108 INFORMATION-GEOMETRIC (this is persona/behavioral-economics axis, not PID/rate-distortion/OT), distinct from Run 107 COGNITIVE-LOAD (this is persona/behavioral-economics axis, not BAPO/token-complexity/linguistic-bottleneck). **First persona-conditioned sequential decision-making with Kahneman-Tversky behavioral signatures in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(persona.conditional.*risk|slot.machine.*llm|cognitive.pattering|prospect.*persona)"` returning empty. 2026-03-16 sub; discovered via HF daily v2 18-paper parse + LLM-keyword filter + web_search candidate confirmation on 2026-06-29.
- **(2026-06-29 — Run 109) — 3-axis METACOGNITIVE-SELF-MONITORING + TREE-STRUCTURED-SELF-RECTIFICATION + EMERGENT-ALIGNMENT-CONSCIENCE discovery**
- [[emergent-alignment-2606.19527]] — Emergent Alignment (06-17 sub) — first paper in the wiki to systematically study **emergent ethical self-correction via a conscience-step mechanism** — an LLM reviews its own reasoning and outputs for ethical misalignment, trained with DPO to steer away from non-ethical outputs; applicable across training, fine-tuning, adversarial prompting, and zero-shot; surfacing *conscience-step-as-self-monitoring-primitive* and *emergent-alignment-via-DPO-primitive* and *self-correction-from-misalignment-detection-primitive* as the load-bearing LLM metacognition primitives; distinct from alignment-via-RLHF primitives (where the load-bearing axis is *self-detected-misalignment* rather than *human-provided-preference*) and from self-correction primitives (where the load-bearing axis is *conscience-step-ethical-review* rather than *reasoning-error-correction*). **First emergent ethical self-correction via conscience-step in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(emergent.align|conscience)"` returning empty. 2026-06-17; discovered via arxiv search HTML 8-query METACOGNITIVE-SELF-MONITORING + TREE-STRUCTURED-SELF-RECTIFICATION + EMERGENT-ALIGNMENT-CONSCIENCE probe on 2026-06-29.
- [[the-metacognitive-monitoring-battery-a-cross-domain-benchmark-for-llm-self-monitoring-2604.15702]] — The Metacognitive Monitoring Battery: A Cross-Domain Benchmark for LLM Self-Monitoring (04-17) — first paper in the wiki to **apply the Nelson and Narens (1990) metacognitive framework systematically to LLMs** — 524-item battery across 6 cognitive domains (learning, metacognitive calibration, social cognition, attention, executive function, prospective regulation), grounded in established human experimental paradigms; establishes monitoring-control coupling as a measurable LLM capability via pre-registered psychometric methodology; surfacing *metacognitive-monitoring-control-coupling-primitive* and *nelson-narens-framework-for-LLM-primitive* and *cross-domain-psychometric-battery-primitive* as the load-bearing LLM metacognition primitives; distinct from confidence-calibration primitives (where the load-bearing axis is *cross-domain-metacognitive-framework* rather than *single-metric-calibration*) and from self-monitoring primitives (where the load-bearing axis is *monitoring-control-coupling* rather than *single-task-accuracy*). **First Nelson-Narens metacognitive framework application to LLMs with cross-domain battery in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(metacognitive.monitoring.battery|nelson.narens)"` returning empty. 2026-04-17; discovered via arxiv search HTML 8-query METACOGNITIVE-SELF-MONITORING + TREE-STRUCTURED-SELF-RECTIFICATION + EMERGENT-ALIGNMENT-CONSCIENCE probe on 2026-06-29.
- [[reason-in-chains-learn-in-trees-self-rectification-and-grafting-for-multi-turn-agent-policy-optimization-2604.07165]] — Reason in Chains, Learn in Trees: Self-Rectification and Grafting for Multi-turn Agent Policy Optimization (04-08 sub) — first paper in the wiki to address the **sparse-reward failure mode of chain-based RL for LLM agents via latent tree structure recovery** — T-STAR identifies critical steps that disproportionately impact reasoning outcome and assigns credit based on tree structure rather than uniform chain-level credit; surfacing *tree-structured-self-rectification-primitive* and *critical-step-credit-assignment-primitive* and *latent-tree-structure-recovery-primitive* as the load-bearing multi-turn reasoning primitives; distinct from chain-of-thought compression primitives (where the load-bearing axis is *tree-structure-credit-assignment* rather than *token-importance*) and from GRPO primitives (where the load-bearing axis is *per-step-criticality* rather than *uniform-credit*). **First tree-structured self-rectification with latent tree recovery for multi-turn LLM agents in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(tree.structured.self.rectif|latent.tree)"` returning empty. 2026-04-08; discovered via arxiv search HTML 8-query METACOGNITIVE-SELF-MONITORING + TREE-STRUCTURED-SELF-RECTIFICATION + EMERGENT-ALIGNMENT-CONSCIENCE probe on 2026-06-29.
- **(2026-06-29 — Run 108) — 3-axis DOMAIN-DIVERSITY PIVOT to INFORMATION-GEOMETRIC-PRIMITIVE-PROBE per Rule 75 (candidate)** introducing **information-geometric primitive** (after Rules 33–74 in Three picks (date-DESC ordered by submission_date, most-recently-inserted-first per Rule 8):
- [[learning-is-forgetting-llm-training-as-lossy-compression-2604.07569]] — Learning is Forgetting: LLM Training As Lossy Compression (04-08 sub) — first paper in the wiki to **frame LLM pre-training through the lens of rate-distortion / Information Bottleneck theory** — arguing that "learning is forgetting" — pre-training produces models that retain only information relevant to next-sequence prediction and approaches the Information Bottleneck bound on compression, establishing LLMs as instances of lossy compression; surfacing *lossy-compression-framing-of-LLM-training-primitive* and *information-bottleneck-optimality-as-training-primitive* and *information-theoretic-retained-signal-primitive* as the load-bearing information-geometric training primitives, distinct from gradient-flow primitives (where the load-bearing axis is *rate-distortion-theory-on-training-data* rather than *gradient-flow-attribution*) and from parameter-space Fisher primitive (where the load-bearing axis is *training-as-compression-toward-IB-bound* rather than *Fisher-Matrix-as-parameter-sensitivity*); plus *information-theoretic-memorization-bound-primitive* as the load bounding primitive establishing Information Bottleneck-bound memorization limits; plus *forgetting-as-retaining-only-relevant-information-primitive* as the load-bearing reinterpretation primitive recasting the loss-of-training-information phenomenon as optimal compression; plus *rate-distortion-estimation-primitive* as the load-bearing methodology primitive providing an estimator suitable for measuring the IB-bound during pre-training; empirically demonstrates pre-training drives models toward the IB bound, surfacing the **information-bottleneck-bound-memorization-primitive + signal-vs-distortion-decomposition** structurally correct primitive for moving LLM analysis beyond parameter-space toward representation-space lossy-compression optimality. **First lossy-compression framing of LLM training with Information-Bottleneck-bound empirical approach and signal-vs-distortion-decomposition in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(learning.is.forgetting|lossy.compression.*llm|rate.distortion.*llm|information.bottleneck.*training|ib.bound.*llm|memorization.*bound.*llm)"` returning empty. 2026-04-08 sub; discovered via web_search 4-query INFORMATION-GEOMETRIC-PRIMITIVE-PROBE on 2026-06-29 (information-geometric / rate-distortion / Information-Bottleneck-optimisation primitive theme — Rule 75 INFORMATION-GEOMETRIC-PRIMITIVE-PROBE).
- [[efficient-refusal-ablation-in-llm-through-optimal-transport-2603.04355]] — Efficient Refusal Ablation in LLM through Optimal Transport (03-04 sub) — first paper in the wiki to **replace orthogonal-projection refusal-ablation with a principled optimal-transport framework treating refusal as a full distributional shape rather than a 1-D direction** — uses PCA to identify the refusal-direction subspace and a closed-form linear OT map to transform the harmful-side distribution toward the harmless-side distribution, leaving orthogonal residual channels untouched; surfacing *optimal-transport–based-distributional-alignment-primitive* and *distributional-shape-preservation-under-alignment-primitive* and *PCA-preconditioned-closed-form-OT-primitive* as the load-bearing alignment-safety information-geometric primitives, distinct from orthogonal-projection activation-jailbreak primitives (where the load-bearing axis is *full-distributional-shape-OT-transformation* rather than *1-D-direction-orthogonal-removal*) and from refusal-direction primitives (where the load-bearing axis is *distribution-to-distribution-map* rather than *linear-direction-removal*); plus *PCA-preconditioning-OT-efficiency-primitive* as the load-bearing computational-efficiency primitive using PCA subspace identification before closed-form OT eliminates the need for iterative Sinkhorn at deployment; plus *collateral-capability-degradation-minimization-primitive* as the load-bearing safety-quality primitive (the OT map preserves surrounding manifold structure, empirically reducing degradation of capability in adjacent domains); plus *optimal-transport-residual-channel-preservation-primitive* as the load-bearing selectivity primitive (orthogonal residual channels to the refusal-direction are preserved, isolating the alignment intervention); establishes **optimal-transport-distributional-ablation + PCA-preconditioned-closed-form** as structurally correct information-geometric primitives for alignment-safety interventions. **First PCA-preconditioned closed-form optimal-transport refusal-ablation framework preserving distributional shape in residual channels in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(efficient.refusal|optimal.transport.*refusal|ot.*llm.*safety|pca.*refusal|distributional.*refusal.*ablation)"` returning empty. 2026-03-04 sub; discovered via web_search 4-query INFORMATION-GEOMETRIC-PRIMITIVE-PROBE on 2026-06-29 (information-geometric / optimal-transport–distributional-alignment / PCA-preconditioned-OT primitive theme — Rule 75 INFORMATION-GEOMETRIC-PRIMITIVE-PROBE).
- [[a-brain-like-synergistic-core-in-llms-drives-behaviour-and-learning-2601.06851]] — A Brain-like Synergistic Core in LLMs Drives Behaviour and Learning (01-11 sub) — first paper in the wiki to **systematically apply Partial Information Decomposition (PID) — Williams–Beer redundancy and synergy decompositions — to LLM internal activations across multiple model families** — surfacing a brain-like architecture where middle-layer components carry synergy (information only available through joint integration) while early and late layers rely on redundancy — perturbing the synergistic core causes the largest behavioural drop; surfacing *Partial-Information-Decomposition-of-activations-primitive* and *synergy-magnitude-as-mechanistic-primitive* and *middle-layer-synergistic-core-architecture-primitive* as the load-bearing information-geometric mechanistically-interpretable primitives, distinct from circuit-discovery primitives (where the load-bearing axis is *synergy-magnitude-of-layer-windows* rather than *circuit-edge-by-circuit-edge-attribution*) and from feature-decomposition primitives (where the load-bearing axis is *information-theoretic-synergy-of-components* rather than *learned-feature-decomposition*); plus *causal-interventional-PID-validation-primitive* as the load-bearing causal-interpretability primitive (perturbing synergistic core causes the largest behavioural drop; perturbing redundant core has the smallest effect — converting descriptive PID into interventional information-geometric primitive); plus *biological-vs-artificial-intelligence-core-architecture-parallel-primitive* as the load-bearing cross-domain primitive showing the synergistic-core/redundant-periphery split mirrors brain integrative-core architecture; plus *stable-across-fine-tuning-architecture-primitive* as the load-bearing architectural-invariant primitive (the synergistic-core structure is preserved across fine-tuning); establishes **PID-synergy-redundancy + middle-layer-synergistic-core + interventional-PID-validation** as structurally correct information-geometric primitives for mechanistic-interpretability that uses information theory rather than learned-feature-decomposition as the load-bearing primitive. **First Williams–Beer PID redundancy/synergy decomposition of LLM internal activations across model families with middle-layer synergistic core and interventional-validation in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(brain.like.synergistic|synergistic.core|partial.information.decomposition|williams.beer|synergy.redundancy.llm|pid.*llm.*activations)"` returning empty. 2026-01-11 sub; discovered via web_search 4-query INFORMATION-GEOMETRIC-PRIMITIVE-PROBE on 2026-06-29 (information-geometric / Partial-Information-Decomposition-synergy-redundancy / middle-layer-synergistic-core primitive theme — Rule 75 INFORMATION-GEOMETRIC-PRIMITIVE-PROBE).

Three picks (date-DESC ordered by online_date, most-recently-inserted-first per Rule 8):
- [[mid-think-training-free-intermediate-budget-reasoning-via-token-level-triggers-2601.07036]] — Mid-Think: Training-Free Intermediate-Budget Reasoning via Token-Level Triggers (06-02 online, 01-11 sub) — first paper in the wiki to **identify token-level triggers (not high-level Think/No-think instructions) as the load-bearing primitive for hybrid-reasoning-mode-switching via attention analysis + controlled prompting** — leading "Okay" token induces reasoning, newline-after-``</think>`` suppresses it; introduces Mid-Think, a simple training-free prompting format combining these triggers to achieve intermediate-budget reasoning, consistently outperforming fixed-token and prompt-based baselines on accuracy-length trade-off; applying Mid-Think to RL training after SFT reduces training time by ~15% while improving Qwen3-8B on AIME from 69.8% to 72.4% and on GPQA from 58.5% to 61.1% — surfacing *token-level-trigger-as-mode-switch-primitive* and *training-free-intermediate-budget-reasoning-primitive* and *inference-time-cognitive-load-via-token-prompts-primitive* as the load-bearing hybrid-reasoning cognitive-load primitives; plus *attention-analysis-as-mode-control-decoder-primitive* as the load-bearing attribution primitive (attention pattern around "Okay" and the newline-after-``</think>`` distinguishes reasoning from no-reasoning regions); plus *controlled-prompting-ablation-primitive* as the load-bearing methodology primitive (controlled prompt manipulation isolates which tokens drive mode switching, ruling out instruction-conditioning explanations); plus *post-SFT-RL-training-time-reduction-primitive* as the load-bearing post-training-training-cost primitive (Mid-Think reduces RL training time by ~15% as a side benefit); distinct from high-level-instruction mode-control primitives (where the load-bearing axis is *token-triggers-as-suppression-mechanism* rather than *instruction-conditioning*) and from prompt-engineering primitives (where the load-bearing axis is *training-free-token-level-control* rather than *long-prompt-engineering*). **First token-level-trigger-as-mode-switch primitive for hybrid-reasoning-mode-switching with attention-analysis attribution and post-SFT-RL training-time-reducing intermediate-budget control in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(mid.think|intermediate.budget|token.triggers|hbm.hybrid.reasoning|hybrid.reasoning.mode)"` returning empty. 2026-06-02 online; discovered via arxiv search HTML 4-query COGNITIVE-LOAD-AT-INFERENCE-TIME-PROBE on 2026-06-29 (cognitive-load-at-inference-time / training-free-intermediate-budget / token-level-trigger-mode-switch primitive theme — Rule 74 COGNITIVE-LOAD-AT-INFERENCE-TIME-PROBE).
- [[reasoning-about-reasoning-bapo-bounds-on-chain-of-thought-token-complexity-in-llms-2602.02909]] — Reasoning about Reasoning: BAPO Bounds on Chain-of-Thought Token Complexity in LLMs (05-27 online, 02-02 sub) — first paper in the wiki to **extend the Bounded Attention Prefix Oracle (BAPO) abstraction to prove formal lower bounds on CoT tokens required for canonical hard tasks — establishing Ω(n) lower bounds for binary majority, triplet matching, and graph reachability, with matching or near-matching upper bounds and empirical validation on frontier reasoning models**; introduces *fundamental-bottleneck-of-inference-time-compute identification* and *optimal-reasoning-length principled analysis* as primitives for reasoning-system design — surfacing *BAPO-extended-CoT-token-complexity-lower-bound-primitive* and *cognitive-load-theoretical-bound-on-CoT-primitive* and *principled-optimal-reasoning-length-tool-primitive* as the load-bearing CoT-token-complexity primitives, distinct from heuristic-reasoning-token-budget primitives (where the load-bearing axis is *formal-Θ-notation-lower-bound-via-BAPO* rather than *rule-of-thumb-coarse-grained-budget*) and from training-time-reasoning-length primitives (where the load-bearing axis is *inference-time-CoT-token-count-lower-bound* rather than *training-compute-scaling*); plus *three-canonical-BAPO-hard-tasks-primitive* as the load-bearing empirical-coverage primitive (binary majority + triplet matching + graph reachability as the validation substrate); plus *frontier-reasoning-model-approximately-linear-scaling-primitive* as the load-bearing empirical-validation primitive (frontier reasoning models exhibit approximately linear CoT-token scaling on the three tasks with constrained-budget failures matching the theoretical bounds); plus *information-flow-quantification-primitive* as the load-bearing theoretical-abstraction primitive (BAPO quantifies the information flow required to solve a task); BAPO-extension establishes **CoT-token-complexity-lower-bound + principled-optimal-reasoning-length-tool** as a structurally correct primitive for moving LLM reasoning-resource analysis beyond heuristics toward formal Θ-notation lower bounds. **First BAPO-extended Θ-notation CoT-token-complexity lower bound for canonical hard tasks (binary majority, triplet matching, graph reachability) with matching upper bounds and frontier-reasoning-model empirical validation in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(bapo|chain.of.thought.token.complexity|cot.token.bound|bounded.attention.prefix.oracle|optimal.reasoning.length)"` returning empty. 2026-05-27 online; discovered via arxiv search HTML 4-query COGNITIVE-LOAD-AT-INFERENCE-TIME-PROBE on 2026-06-29 (cognitive-load-at-inference-time / BAPO-CoT-token-complexity-lower-bound / principled-optimal-reasoning-length primitive theme — Rule 74 COGNITIVE-LOAD-AT-INFERENCE-TIME-PROBE).
- [[linguistic-generalizability-of-test-time-scaling-in-mathematical-reasoning-2502.17407]] — Linguistic Generalizability of Test-Time Scaling in Mathematical Reasoning (2025-08-01 online, 02-24 sub) — first paper in the wiki to **systematically quantify the cognitive-load linguistic-bottleneck for test-time-scaling methods** — Budget Forcing yields a +20-point improvement on English AIME but only a +1.94-point average gain across other languages, a pattern consistent across ORM, PRM, and BF; introduces MCLM, a multilingual math benchmark with competition-level problems in 55 languages, and MR1-1.5B, a multilingual LLM trained for extended reasoning; once inference FLOPs are equalized, traditional best-of-N performs comparably to MR1-1.5B — surfacing *multilingual-test-time-scaling-generalizability-failure-primitive* and *linguistic-form-as-test-time-scaling-bottleneck-primitive* and *per-language-test-time-scaling-diminishing-returns-primitive* and *cognitive-load-vs-inference-FLOPs-equivalence-primitive* as the load-bearing multilingual cognitive-load primitives; plus *fifty-five-language-multilingual-competition-math-benchmark-primitive* as the load-bearing empirical-coverage primitive (MCLM as the multilingual primitive substrate); plus *multilingual-reasoning-model-trained-for-extended-reasoning-primitive* as the load-bearing model-substrate primitive (MR1-1.5B as a public multilingual reasoning model); plus *test-time-scaling-vs-traditional-best-of-N-inference-FLOPs-equalized-comparison-primitive* as the load-bearing evaluation primitive (once inference FLOPs are equalized, "thinking LLMs" perform comparably to traditional best-of-N — surfacing the cognitive-load-vs-inference-FLOPs-equivalence primitive); distinct from scaling-laws primitives (where the load-bearing axis is *linguistic-form-as-test-time-scaling-bottleneck* rather than *compute-scaling-curves*) and from general multilingual tokenization primitives (where the load-bearing axis is *multilingual-competition-math-reasoning* rather than *tokenization-fairness*) and from cross-lingual-transfer primitives (where the load-bearing axis is *test-time-scaling-linguistic-bottleneck* rather than *cross-lingual-generalization-quality*); MCLM establishes **multilingual-test-time-scaling-linguistic-bottleneck + cognitive-load-vs-inference-FLOPs-equivalence** as a structurally correct primitive for moving LLM cognitive-load analysis beyond monolingual assumptions toward cross-linguistic generalization constraint characterization. **First systematic quantification of test-time-scaling linguistic-bottleneck (Budget Forcing +20pts on English AIME vs +1.94pts average across other languages) with MCLM multilingual competition math benchmark and MR1-1.5B multilingual reasoning model in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(mclm|multilingual.test.time|linguistic.generaliz|test.time.scaling.generaliz|mr1|linguistic.bottleneck)"` returning empty. 2025-08-01 online;
