Three picks (date-DESC ordered by online_date, most-recently-inserted-first per Rule 8):
- [[evaluating-the-interpretability-of-sparse-autoencoders-with-concept-annotations-2606.24716]] — Evaluating the Interpretability of Sparse Autoencoders with Concept Annotations (06-23) — first paper in the wiki to **introduce a human-grounded, intervention-style evaluation framework for SAEs in vision and vision-language models — replacing proxy metrics or qualitative inspection with explicit measurement of semantic correspondence between SAE latents and human-annotated concepts, validated by targeted attribute perturbations without requiring user studies**; introduces two synthetic paired-image benchmarks (synCUB, synCOCO, each pair differs in exactly one attribute), the Fully-Binary Matching Pursuit (FBMP) coalition-based many-to-one matching procedure between SAE latents and concepts (consistently outperforms one-to-one baselines), and the Targeted Attribute Perturbation Alignment Score (TAPAScore) that tests whether matched concepts respond selectively and in the expected direction under targeted image-level attribute perturbations — surfacing *concept-annotation-as-SAE-evaluation-substrate-primitive* and *intervention-style-evaluation-without-user-studies-primitive* and *synthetic-paired-image-benchmark-primitive* (synCUB/synCOCO) and *coalition-based-many-to-one-matching-primitive* (FBMP) and *targeted-attribute-perturbation-alignment-score-primitive* (TAPAScore) and *concept-level-semantic-correspondence-as-gold-standard-measurement-primitive* as the load-bearing SAE-evaluation primitives, distinct from proxy-metric SAE-evaluation primitives (where the load-bearing axis is *concept-annotation-ground-truth* rather than *quantitative-statistical-proxy*) and from interpretability primitives in general (where the load-bearing axis is *intervention-style-validation-of-SAE-features* rather than *circuit-discovery*); plus *many-to-one-concept-matching-outperforms-one-to-one-baseline-primitive* as the load-bearing empirical-coverage primitive (FBMP consistently outperforms one-to-one matching baselines); plus *synCUB-and-synCOCO-paired-image-benchmarks-primitive* as the load-bearing evaluation substrate (each pair differs in exactly one attribute enabling targeted attribute perturbation). **First concept-annotation intervention-style SAE-evaluation primitive with FBMP many-to-one matching and TAPAScore functional validation in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(concept.annotation|sae.*evaluation|fully.binary.matching|tapascore|syncub|syncoco)"` returning empty. 2026-06-23; discovered via arxiv search HTML 6-query FUNCTIONAL-MECHANISTIC-INTERPRETABILITY-PROBE on 2026-06-29 (functional-mechanistic-interpretability / concept-annotation / SAE-evaluation primitive theme — Rule 73 FUNCTIONAL-MECHANISTIC-INTERPRETABILITY-PROBE).
- [[stable-and-steerable-sparse-autoencoders-with-weight-regularization-2603.04198]] — Stable and Steerable Sparse Autoencoders with Weight Regularization (06-16 online) — first paper in the wiki to **address the cross-seed feature-stability problem for SAEs and introduce weight regularization (L1/L2 penalties on encoder and decoder weights) as the primitive that produces stable, steerable features whose activations can be reliably amplified or attenuated to manipulate model behavior**, with L2 weight regularization + tied initialization + unit-norm decoder constraints dramatically increasing cross-seed feature consistency on MNIST, and a small L2 weight penalty on TopK SAEs (Pythia-70M-deduped) increasing the fraction of features shared across three random seeds and roughly doubling steering success rates while leaving automated-interpretability scores essentially unchanged — surfacing *cross-seed-feature-stability-primitive* and *weight-regularization-as-stability-mechanism-primitive* and *stability-as-steerability-enabler-primitive* and *stability-and-steerability-as-paired-evaluation-criteria-primitive* and *regularization-aligns-textual-explanations-with-functional-controllability-primitive* as the load-bearing SAE-stability+steerability primitives, distinct from cross-seed-feature-instability primitives (where the load-bearing axis is *weight-regularization-as-canonical-stability-intervention* rather than *feature-instability-as-property-of-training*) and from activation-steering primitives (where the load-bearing axis is *SAE-stability-as-precondition-for-steerability* rather than *ad-hoc-steering-vectors*); plus *TopK-SAE-on-Pythia-70M-deduped-three-seed-empirical-coverage-primitive* as the load-bearing empirical-coverage primitive; plus *auto-interpretability-scores-become-steering-success-predictors-primitive* as the load-bearing alignment primitive (in the regularized setting, auto-interpretability scores predict activation-steering success — surfaces alignment between textual explanations and behavioral controllability); plus *tied-initialization-and-unit-norm-decoder-constraints-as-stability-amplifiers-primitive* as the load-bearing training-intervention primitive (combining L2 weight regularization with tied initialization and unit-norm decoder constraints dramatically increases cross-seed feature consistency on MNIST). **First weight-regularization-as-stability-and-steerability-enabler primitive with paired-criteria evaluation framework in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(sae.*stable|sae.*steerable|weight.regularization.*sae|cross.seed.*feature)"` returning empty. 2026-03-04 submission, 2026-06-16 online; discovered via arxiv search HTML 6-query FUNCTIONAL-MECHANISTIC-INTERPRETABILITY-PROBE on 2026-06-29 (functional-mechanistic-interpretability / SAE-stability / regularization-as-steerability-enabler primitive theme — Rule 73 FUNCTIONAL-MECHANISTIC-INTERPRETABILITY-PROBE).
- [[patch-effect-graph-kernels-for-llm-interpretability-2605.06480]] — Patch-Effect Graph Kernels for LLM Interpretability (05-07) — first paper in the wiki to **formalize patch-effect graph kernels as the primitive comparison substrate for mechanistic-interpretability circuit analysis — reframing activation-patching profiles as graphs over model components, then comparing them via graph kernels that scale across diverse prompts and task families where raw patch-effect tensors are high-dimensional and unstructured**, evaluated on GPT-2 Small using Indirect Object Identification (IOI) and related tasks with three graph-construction methods (direct-influence via causal mediation, partial-correlation, co-influence), demonstrating that localized edge-slot features provide higher classification accuracy than global graph-shape descriptors and that screened-paired-patching validation confirms CI/PC-selected candidate edges correspond to stronger activation-influence effects than random or low-rank candidates — surfacing *patch-effect-graph-kernel-primitive* and *graph-machine-learning-as-mechanistic-interpretability-substrate-primitive* and *three-graph-construction-methods-primitive* (direct-influence + partial-correlation + co-influence) and *edge-slot-locality-as-discriminative-feature-primitive* and *screened-paired-patching-validation-primitive* and *rigorous-prompt-only-and-raw-patch-effect-control-baselines-primitive* as the load-bearing circuit-comparison primitives, distinct from raw-patch-effect-tensor-comparison primitives (where the load-bearing axis is *graph-kernel-over-model-components* rather than *direct-tensor-comparison*) and from circuit-discovery primitives (where the load-bearing axis is *patch-effect-graph-kernel-circuit-comparison-substrate* rather than *circuit-identification*); plus *GPT-2-Small-IOI-validation-substrate-primitive* as the load-bearing evaluation substrate (Indirect Object Identification + related tasks as the validation benchmark); plus *CI-and-PC-edges-correlate-with-stronger-activation-influence-primitive* as the load-bearing causal-edge-selection primitive (screened paired-patching shows CI/PC-selected edges correspond to stronger activation-influence than random or low-rank candidates); plus *localized-edge-slot-higher-than-global-graph-shape-primitive* as the load-bearing discriminative-feature primitive (edge-slot local features provide higher classification accuracy than global graph-summary statistics). **First patch-effect-graph-kernel mechanistic-interpretability circuit-comparison primitive with three graph-construction methods and screened-paired-patching validation in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(patch.effect.graph|graph.kernel.*interpretability|patching.profile|mechanistic.*graph)"` returning empty. 2026-05-07; discovered via arxiv search HTML 6-query FUNCTIONAL-MECHANISTIC-INTERPRETABILITY-PROBE on 2026-06-29 (functional-mechanistic-interpretability / patch-effect-graph-kernel / circuit-comparison primitive theme — Rule 73 FUNCTIONAL-MECHANISTIC-INTERPRETABILITY-PROBE).

Three picks (date-DESC ordered by online_date, most-recently-inserted-first per Rule 8):
- [[categorical-prior-lock-in-why-in-context-learning-fails-for-structured-data-2606.11961]] — Categorical Prior Lock-in: Why In-Context Learning Fails for Structured Data (06-10) — first paper in the wiki to **identify a structural failure mode of ICL for structured generation under distribution mismatch — categorical prior lock-in — the inability of ICL to update the model's prior over token distributions inherited from pre-training**, with sharp ceiling on categorical distributions and complete failure to reproduce rare classes; across two 7B-parameter open-weight models, ICL improves numerical fidelity with additional examples but exhibits a sharp ceiling on categorical distributions, failing to reproduce rare classes entirely; LoRA overcomes the categorical-prior-lock-in ceiling but introduces measurable memorization risk and, in some cases, destabilizes structured output generation — surfacing *categorical-prior-lock-in-primitive* and *ICL-failure-on-categorical-distributions-primitive* and *adaptability-vs-privacy-trade-off-primitive* and *ICL-vs-distribution-mismatch-primitive* and *numerical-vs-categorical-distribution-asymmetry-primitive* and *rare-class-reproduction-failure-primitive* as the load-bearing declarative-ICL-failure primitives, distinct from generic ICL scaling studies (where the load-bearing axis is *categorical-prior-lock-in-as-structural-failure-mode* rather than *general-scaling-curve*) and from LoRA fine-tuning primitives (where the load-bearing axis is *adaptability-vs-privacy-trade-off* rather than *training-cost-reduction*); plus *high-cardinality-tabular-data-as-controlled-distribution-mismatch-primitive* as the load-bearing test-bed primitive; plus *LoRA-memorization-risk-primitive* as the load-bearing privacy-primitive (LoRA introduces measurable memorization risk and destabilizes structured output generation in some cases); plus *adaptability-vs-privacy-trade-off-primitive* as the load-bearing meta-primitive (LoRA overcomes the categorical-prior-lock-in ceiling but introduces memorization risk — surfaces the fundamental adaptability-vs-privacy trade-off). **First categorical-prior-lock-in ICL-failure primitive for structured generation with adaptability-vs-privacy trade-off in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(categorical.prior.lock.in|icl.*fails.structured|tabular.*prior)"` returning empty. 2026-06-10; discovered via arxiv API + arxiv search HTML 6-query DECLARATIVE-LM-PROBE on 2026-06-29 (declarative-LM / ICL-failure / categorical-prior-lock-in primitive theme — Rule 72 DECLARATIVE-LM-PROBE).
- [[repot-recoverable-program-of-thought-via-checkpoint-repair-2605.30052]] — REPOT: Recoverable Program-of-Thought via Checkpoint Repair (05-28) — first paper in the wiki to **introduce a deterministic verified-replay primitive that walks a one-shot Program-of-Thought trajectory through the environment to its first invalid transition, then makes one LLM call that resumes from the verified prefix**, costing at most one extra LLM call on the ~14% of problems where PoT fails; RePoT beats PoT by +3 to +11pp across four closed-model configurations on PuzzleZoo-775 and peaks at 96.9% vs 86.3% on gpt-5.4-mini-medium; introduces Adaptive RePoT, a rule-based dispatcher that routes between suffix repair and a fresh PoT retry based on verified-prefix length — surfacing *checkpoint-repair-primitive-for-PoT-trajectories* and *verified-replay-primitive* and *deterministic-replay-primitive* and *verified-prefix-resumption-primitive* and *Adaptive-RePoT-dispatcher-primitive* and *capability-scaling-pattern-of-recovery-vs-retry-primitive* as the load-bearing declarative-PoT primitives, distinct from generic PoT primitives (where the load-bearing axis is *deterministic-verified-replay-with-checkpoint-repair* rather than *one-shot-PoT*) and from agent-debugging primitives (where the load-bearing axis is *verified-prefix-resumption-after-first-invalid-transition* rather than *trajectory-level-failure-analysis*); plus *first-invalid-transition-localization-primitive* as the load-bearing debugging primitive (walks the plan through the environment to its first invalid transition); plus *capability-scaling-pattern-primitive* as the load-bearing generalization primitive (suffers on weaker models, wins on stronger ones — surfaces a capability-scaling primitive for recovery vs retry trade-offs); plus *Derail-550-recovery-benchmark-primitive* as the load-bearing benchmark primitive (controlled recovery benchmark where every condition with access to checkpoint information improves over PoT); plus *PlanBench-Blocksworld-replication-primitive* as the load-bearing cross-domain primitive (+1.1 to +11.4pp on PlanBench Blocksworld); plus *open-weights-replication-primitive* as the load-bearing cross-model primitive (+3.3 to +20.0pp on three of four open-weights models); plus *adaptive-repair-dispatcher-primitive* as the load-bearing engineering primitive (rule-based dispatcher routing between suffix repair and fresh PoT retry based on verified-prefix length). **First checkpoint-repair deterministic-verified-replay primitive for PoT trajectories with capability-scaling characterization in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(repot|recoverable.program.of.thought|checkpoint.repair|puzzlezoo.*repl)"` returning empty. 2026-05-28; discovered via arxiv API + arxiv search HTML 6-query DECLARATIVE-LM-PROBE on 2026-06-29 (declarative-LM / PoT-checkpoint-repair / verified-replay primitive theme — Rule 72 DECLARATIVE-LM-PROBE).
- [[declarative-router-policy-compilation-from-inference-routing-to-agent-orchestration-cross-layer-verification-2603.27299]] — From Inference Routing to Agent Orchestration: Declarative Policy Compilation with Cross-Layer Verification (03-28) — first paper in the wiki to **introduce the Semantic Router DSL — a non-Turing-complete declarative policy language deployed in production for per-request LLM inference routing — and extend it from stateless per-request routing to multi-step agent workflows with cross-layer verification** that emits verified decision nodes for orchestration frameworks (LangGraph, OpenClaw), Kubernetes artifacts (NetworkPolicy, Sandbox CRD, ConfigMap), YANG/NETCONF payloads, and protocol-boundary gates (MCP, A2A) — all from the same single declarative source file; because the language is non-Turing-complete, the compiler guarantees exhaustive routing, conflict-free branching, referential integrity, and audit traces structurally coupled to the decision path — surfacing *non-Turing-complete-DSL-as-LLM-orchestration-substrate-primitive* and *declarative-policy-compilation-primitive* and *cross-layer-verification-primitive* and *single-source-routing-orchestration-infrastructure-primitive* and *exhaustive-routing-guarantee-primitive* and *conflict-free-branching-guarantee-primitive* and *audit-trace-structural-coupling-primitive* as the load-bearing declarative-orchestration primitives, distinct from generic DSL primitives (where the load-bearing axis is *non-Turing-completeness-as-verification-guarantee-for-LLM-orchestration* rather than *general-DSL-design*) and from inference-routing primitives (where the load-bearing axis is *cross-layer-verification-across-orchestration-frameworks-and-Kubernetes-artifacts* rather than *single-layer-routing*); plus *probabilistic-predicate-conflict-free-compilation-primitive* as the load-bearing compilation primitive (extends prior work on probabilistic predicate compilation to multi-step agent workflows); plus *Workload-Router-Pool-architecture-primitive* as the load-bearing architectural primitive (positions the DSL within the Workload-Router-Pool inference architecture); plus *production-deployed-LLM-routing-DSL-primitive* as the load-bearing deployment primitive (deployed in production for per-request LLM inference routing); plus *priority-ordered-decision-trees-primitive* as the load-bearing control-flow primitive (content signals feed into weighted projections and priority-ordered decision trees that select a model, enforce privacy policies, and produce structured audit traces). **First non-Turing-complete-DSL-as-LLM-orchestration-substrate primitive with cross-layer verification in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(semantic.router|declarative.*router|policy.compilation|cross.layer.verification|non.turing.complete.*llm)"` returning empty. 2026-03-28; discovered via arxiv API + arxiv search HTML 6-query DECLARATIVE-LM-PROBE on 2026-06-29 (declarative-LM / non-Turing-complete-DSL / cross-layer-verification primitive theme — Rule 72 DECLARATIVE-LM-PROBE).

- **(2026-06-29 — Run 104) — 3-axis DOMAIN-DIVERSITY PIVOT to GRADIENT-FLOW-COORDINATION-PROBE per Rule 71** introducing **gradient-flow as the load-bearing primitive for LLM coordination, inference-time knowledge transfer, and RL post-training** — pivoted from Run 103 representational-geometry axes (minimal-cores representation-geometry + cross-model shared embedding-geometry + training-stage abstraction-phase emergence) to a gradient-flow-as-load-bearing-primitive triplet: (a) GBC: gradient-based connections as the multi-agent attribution primitive + (b) Thinking While Speaking: inference-time knowledge transfer as the voice-agent latency-capability primitive + (c) NormGuard: velocity-norm preservation as the flow-matching RL post-training primitive; widens the discovery space differently than Run 103 representational-geometry-probe (which targeted *intrinsic-representation-geometry + cross-model-embedding-geometry + training-stage-abstraction-phase-emergence* — Rule 70) and Run 102 invariance-under-prompt-perturbation-probe (which targeted *generator-side reasoning invariance under FOL-derived transformations + closed-system reasoning invariance-failure + judge-side evaluation invariance* — Rule 69) — Rule 71 specifically probes the **gradient-flow layer** of LLM coordination and training, the load-bearing primitive-class for understanding how gradient signals coordinate multi-agent systems, transfer knowledge at inference time, and stabilize flow-matching RL post-training; distinct from representational-geometry primitives (where the load-bearing axis is *intrinsic-representation-geometry* rather than *gradient-based-attribution*), from training-time-only primitives (where the load-bearing axis is *gradient-flow-as-coordination-protocol* rather than *gradient-flow-as-optimization-update*), and from inference-time-only primitives (where the load-bearing axis is *gradient-flow-as-inference-primitive* rather than *inference-time-knowledge-distillation*); the only existing gradient-flow-adjacent entities in the wiki are FastMix (data mixture via gradient descent), When-Gradients-Collide (failure modes of multi-objective gradient-based prompt optimization), and the new GBC paper (gradient-based multi-agent attribution) — none of which probe the **gradient-based-connections-as-multi-agent-attribution / inference-time-knowledge-transfer-as-voice-agent-latency-capability / velocity-norm-preservation-as-flow-matching-RL-post-training** primitive triplet that Run 104 surfaces; Rule 71 GRADIENT-FLOW-COORDINATION-PROBE codified for future runs. 3 picks: GBC (06-26) - first paper in the wiki to systematically apply gradient-based attribution to multi-agent LLM systems, modeling the multi-agent system as a computational graph with token-level connection weights for fine-grained credit assignment; Thinking While Speaking (06-23 online, 11-10 sub) - first paper in the wiki to introduce the *conversational infill* primitive where a small talker model both immediately generates contextually grounded responses and fluently integrates streamed reasoner knowledge during inference, advancing the latency-capability Pareto frontier for voice agents; NormGuard (06-26) - first paper in the wiki to identify velocity-norm-inflation as the structural signature of RL-post-training drift in flow-matching generators and propose a hinge-penalty training-time intervention that reduces over-sharpening, color oversaturation, and unnatural lighting.
- [[gbc-gradient-based-connections-for-optimizing-multi-agent-systems-2606.28187]] — GBC: Gradient-Based Connections for Optimizing Multi-Agent Systems — first paper in the wiki to systematically apply gradient-based attribution to multi-agent LLM systems, modeling the multi-agent system as a computational graph with token-level connection weights for fine-grained credit assignment and prompt optimization.
- [[normguard-reward-preserving-norm-constraints-in-flow-matching-reinforcement-learning-2606.27771]] — NormGuard: Reward-Preserving Norm Constraints in Flow-Matching Reinforcement Learning — first paper in the wiki to identify velocity-norm-inflation as the structural signature of RL-post-training drift in flow-matching generators and propose a hinge-penalty training-time intervention that reduces over-sharpening, color oversaturation, and unnatural lighting.
- [[thinking-while-speaking-inference-time-knowledge-transfer-for-responsive-and-intelligent-conversational-voice-agents-2511.07397]] — Thinking While Speaking: Inference-Time Knowledge Transfer for Responsive and Intelligent Conversational Voice Agents — first paper in the wiki to introduce the *conversational infill* primitive where a small talker model both immediately generates contextually grounded responses and fluently integrates streamed reasoner knowledge during inference, advancing the latency-capability Pareto frontier for voice agents.

Three picks (date-DESC ordered by online_date, most-recently-inserted-first per Rule 8):
- [[uncovering-the-representation-geometry-of-minimal-cores-in-overcomplete-reasoning-traces-2605.14358]] — Uncovering the Representation Geometry of Minimal Cores in Overcomplete Reasoning Traces (05-14) — first paper in the wiki to **study how much of a long chain-of-thought trace is actually necessary for preserving the final prediction, recasting overcomplete reasoning as a representation-geometry problem and identifying a "minimal core" — a geometrically characterized subset of trace tokens whose representation geometry is sufficient to recover the full trace's prediction**; the work bridges chain-of-thought compression and representation geometry by asking: when an LLM generates 1000+ tokens of reasoning, what is the representation-geometry signature of the subset that actually determines the answer? surfacing *overcomplete-trace-representation-geometry-primitive* and *minimal-cores-primitive* and *representation-geometry-as-coT-compression-primitive* and *geometric-redundancy-of-reasoning-traces-primitive* as the load-bearing representational-geometry primitives for trace-internal layer, distinct from chain-of-thought compression primitives (where the load-bearing axis is *representation-geometry-of-minimal-cores* rather than *token-importance-scores*) and from interpretability primitives (where the load-bearing axis is *geometric-coherence-of-subset-of-trace-tokens* rather than *circuit-discovery*); plus *representation-geometry-as-coT-compression-target-primitive* as the load-bearing compression primitive (asking what the *geometric structure* of the trace is, not which tokens are most important); plus *empirical-evidence-of-geometric-redundancy-primitive* as the load-bearing structural primitive (LLM reasoning traces are not just semantically redundant but geometrically redundant — multiple sub-regions of representation space individually suffice). **First minimal-cores overcomplete-reasoning-trace representation-geometry primitive in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(minimal.cores|overcomplete.*reasoning|trace.*geometry)"` returning empty. 2026-05-14; discovered via arxiv search HTML 8-query REPRESENTATIONAL-GEOMETRY-PROBE on 2026-06-29 (representational-geometry / minimal-cores / overcomplete-reasoning primitive theme — Rule 70 REPRESENTATIONAL-GEOMETRY-PROBE).
- [[shared-global-and-local-geometry-of-language-model-embeddings-2503.21073]] — Shared Global and Local Geometry of Language Model Embeddings (03-27) — first paper in the wiki to **establish that large language models share a common embedding geometry: across diverse LLM families, the global and local geometric structure of token embeddings is remarkably similar** — a finding that has implications for cross-model knowledge transfer, embedding-space alignment, and the universality of language representation; surfacing *cross-model-shared-embedding-geometry-primitive* and *shared-global-geometry-primitive* and *shared-local-geometry-primitive* and *representation-universality-hypothesis-primitive* as the load-bearing representational-geometry primitives for cross-model layer, distinct from single-model representation geometry (where the load-bearing axis is *cross-model-invariance-of-embedding-geometry* rather than *single-model-internal-structure*) and from cross-model knowledge-transfer primitives (where the load-bearing axis is *shared-geometric-substrate* rather than *transfer-mechanism*); plus *nearest-neighbor-preservation-across-models-primitive* as the load-bearing local-structure primitive (nearest-neighbor relationships and local density patterns are preserved across models); plus *LLM-representation-universality-primitive* as the load-bearing meta-primitive (LLM representations are not arbitrary but converge to a shared geometric substrate). **First cross-model shared embedding-geometry primitive in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(shared.*geometry|shared.*embedding|cross.model.*geometry)"` returning empty. 2026-03-27; discovered via arxiv search HTML 8-query REPRESENTATIONAL-GEOMETRY-PROBE on 2026-06-29 (representational-geometry / cross-model-geometry / shared-embedding primitive theme — Rule 70 REPRESENTATIONAL-GEOMETRY-PROBE).
- [[emergence-of-a-high-dimensional-abstraction-phase-in-language-transformers-2405.15471]] — Emergence of a High-Dimensional Abstraction Phase in Language Transformers (05-24) — first paper in the wiki to **identify a phase transition during transformer training: at a critical training step, the representation mapping's geometric structure shifts from a low-dimensional regime to a high-dimensional abstraction phase that supports compositional generalization**; the work is foundational for understanding how training-time geometry emergence relates to functional capabilities; surfacing *abstraction-phase-emergence-primitive* and *geometric-phase-transition-primitive* and *training-time-geometry-emergence-primitive* and *low-dim-to-high-dim-abstraction-transition-primitive* as the load-bearing representational-geometry primitives for training-stage layer, distinct from loss-landscape-geometry (where the load-bearing axis is *representation-mapping-geometry* rather than *loss-surface-geometry*) and from capability-elicitation primitives (where the load-bearing axis is *geometric-phase-structure-of-representation-mapping* rather than *policy-behavior*); plus *intrinsic-dimensionality-as-training-monitor-primitive* as the load-bearing monitoring primitive (practitioners can detect the abstraction-phase transition by tracking intrinsic dimensionality, no task-specific evaluation needed); plus *geometric-properties-of-mapping-relate-to-function-primitive* as the load-bearing structural primitive (high-dim abstraction phase coincides with measurable jumps in downstream task accuracy). **First abstraction-phase emergence as a geometric phase-transition primitive in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(abstraction.phase|high.dimensional.*abstraction|phase.transition.*training)"` returning empty. 2026-05-24; discovered via arxiv search HTML 8-query REPRESENTATIONAL-GEOMETRY-PROBE on 2026-06-29 (representational-geometry / abstraction-phase / geometric-phase-transition primitive theme — Rule 70 REPRESENTATIONAL-GEOMETRY-PROBE).

Three picks (date-DESC ordered by online_date, most-recently-inserted-first per Rule 8):
- [[lgmt-logic-grounded-metamorphic-testing-for-llm-reasoning-reliability-2605.23965]] — LGMT: Logic-Grounded Metamorphic Testing for Evaluating the Reasoning Reliability of LLMs (06-07) — first paper in the wiki to **leverage first-order logic (FOL) to derive metamorphic relations from formal logical equivalences and construct semantically-invariant test cases** — moving LLM evaluation beyond isolated correctness toward robustness under logical invariance via oracle-free cross-case consistency checking; surfacing *FOL-derived-metamorphic-test-construction-primitive* and *oracle-free-consistency-checking-primitive* and *symbol-level-sensitivity-primitive* and *conclusion-level-sensitivity-primitive* and *Few-shot-CoT-partial-mitigation-primitive* as the load-bearing invariance-under-prompt-perturbation primitives for generator-side reasoning layer, distinct from reference-based-evaluation primitives (where the load-bearing axis is *FOL-derived-metamorphic-invariant-test-cases* rather than *reference-comparison-based-on-static-benchmarks*) and from interpretability primitives (where the load-bearing axis is *cross-case-consistency-checking-on-logical-transformations* rather than *internal-representation-probing*); plus *six-state-of-the-art-LLM-empirical-demonstration-primitive* as the load-bearing empirical-coverage primitive; plus *principled-and-scalable-reasoning-failure-diagnostic-primitive* as the load-bearing deployment primitive. **First FOL-derived metamorphic-testing primitive for LLM reasoning reliability under logical invariance in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(metamorphic|fol|first.order.logic|reasoning.reliability)"` returning empty. 2026-06-07; discovered via arxiv search HTML 6-query INVARIANCE-UNDER-PROMPT-PERTURBATION-PROBE on 2026-06-29 (invariance / FOL-metamorphic-testing / reasoning-reliability primitive theme — Rule 69 INVARIANCE-UNDER-PROMPT-PERTURBATION-PROBE).
- [[the-reasoning-trap-information-theoretic-bound-on-closed-system-multi-step-llm-reasoning-2605.01704]] — The Reasoning Trap: An Information-Theoretic Bound on Closed-System Multi-Step LLM Reasoning (05-05) — first paper in the wiki to **identify that multi-agent-debate and closed-system reasoning preserves answer accuracy while degrading the reasoning behind those answers** — introducing the *Reasoning Trap* and its multi-agent special case *Debate Trap* with SFS (Supported Faithfulness Score) claim-level metric (decomposer-invariant rankings: Spearman ρ=1.0) and information-theoretic bound on closed-system faithfulness; surfacing *closed-system-faithfulness-failure-primitive* and *Reasoning-Trap-phenomenon-primitive* and *Debate-Trap-phenomenon-primitive* and *SFS-claim-level-faithfulness-metric-primitive* and *information-theoretic-bound-on-closed-system-faithfulness-primitive* as the load-bearing invariance-under-prompt-perturbation primitives for closed-system reasoning layer, distinct from MAD-as-performance-primitive papers (where the load-bearing axis is *MAD-degrades-reasoning-while-preserving-answers* rather than *MAD-improves-performance*) and from reasoning-trace-exposure primitives (where the load-bearing axis is *closed-system-faithfulness-bound* rather than *reasoning-trace-readability*); plus *diverse-phrasings-of-one-perspective-primitive* as the load-bearing invariance-failure diagnostic primitive (MAD copies produce diverse phrasings rather than diverse perspectives); plus *EGSR-Evidence-Grounded-Socratic-Reasoning-primitive* as the load-bearing reasoning-trap-surfacing primitive. **First closed-system-reasoning faithfulness-failure primitive with information-theoretic bound in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(reasoning.trap|debate.trap|supported.faithfulness|evidence.grounded.socratic)"` returning empty. 2026-05-05; discovered via arxiv search HTML 6-query INVARIANCE-UNDER-PROMPT-PERTURBATION-PROBE on 2026-06-29 (invariance / closed-system-reasoning / multi-agent-debate-faithfulness primitive theme — Rule 69 INVARIANCE-UNDER-PROMPT-PERTURBATION-PROBE).
- [[blind-to-the-human-touch-overlap-bias-in-llm-based-summary-evaluation-2602.07673]] — Blind to the Human Touch: Overlap Bias in LLM-Based Summary Evaluation (02-07) — first paper in the wiki to **systematically quantify overlap-bias in LLM judges' ratings as a function of n-gram/Rouge overlap with human-written reference summaries** — testing 9 recent LLMs (1B-12B parameters, Gemma 3 + LLaMA variants) and surfacing *meta-evaluation-invariance-failure* as a primitive where LLM-judges are not robust to perturbations in surface-overlap with references; surfacing *overlap-bias-as-LLM-judge-primitive* and *cross-architecture-overlap-bias-primitive* and *granular-overlap-bias-analysis-primitive* and *meta-evaluation-invariance-failure-primitive* as the load-bearing invariance-under-prompt-perturbation primitives for judge-side evaluation layer, distinct from length-bias/order-bias aggregate primitives (where the load-bearing axis is *granular-overlap-as-perturbation-dimension* rather than *aggregated-length-and-order-biases*) and from LLM-judge-deployment primitives (where the load-bearing axis is *judge-bias-as-overlap-function* rather than *judge-deployment-quality*); plus *nine-LLM-cross-architecture-empirical-coverage-primitive* as the load-bearing empirical-coverage primitive; plus *summarization-domain-deployment-target-primitive* as the load-bearing deployment primitive (where LLM-judges are most heavily deployed). **First overlap-bias primitive for LLM-based summary evaluation in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(overlap.bias|llm.judge.bias|summari.*bias)"` returning empty. 2026-02-07; discovered via arxiv search HTML 6-query INVARIANCE-UNDER-PROMPT-PERTURBATION-PROBE on 2026-06-29 (invariance / overlap-bias / LLM-judge-evaluation primitive theme — Rule 69 INVARIANCE-UNDER-PROMPT-PERTURBATION-PROBE).

- [[unlocking-the-black-box-of-latent-reasoning-an-interpretability-guided-approach-to-intervention-2606.01243]] — Unlocking the Black Box of Latent Reasoning: An Interpretability-Guided Approach to Intervention (05-31) — first paper in the wiki to **systematically apply structural, causal, and geometric probes to latent-reasoning vectors** and identify early latent vectors as critical *causal hubs* — then operationalize these insights into a suite of *training-free, decode-time interventions* that improve reasoning accuracy across multiple model scales and task domains without any parameter updates; surfacing *latent-reasoning-vector-identifiability-primitive* and *causal-hub-identification-primitive* and *decode-time-intervention-primitive* and *interpretability-bridges-actionable-control-primitive* as the load-bearing identifiability primitives for latent-reasoning-vector layer, distinct from feature-level-causal-steering primitives (where the load-bearing axis is *latent-reasoning-vector-level identifiability via probes* rather than *single-feature steering*) and from interpretability-position primitives (where the load-bearing axis is *operationalized-actionable-decode-time-interventions* rather than *four-quadrant-concreteness×validation-grid*); plus *early-latent-vector-as-causal-hub-primitive* as the load-bearing mechanism primitive; plus *training-free-interpretability-guided-control-primitive* as the load-bearing deployment primitive (no parameter updates required); plus *consistent-accuracy-improvement-across-model-scales-primitive* as the load-bearing validation primitive. **First latent-reasoning-vector identifiability primitive with structural/causal/geometric probes + training-free-decode-time interventions in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(latent.reasoning.*interv|interpret.guided.*interv|causal.hub.*latent)"` returning empty. 2026-05-31; discovered via arxiv search HTML 4-query IDENTIFIABILITY-PROBE on 2026-06-29 (identifiability / latent-reasoning-vector / interpretability-guided-intervention primitive theme — Rule 68 IDENTIFIABILITY-PROBE).
- [[iv-co-scientist-multi-agent-llm-framework-for-causal-instrumental-variable-discovery-2602.07943]] — IV Co-Scientist: Multi-Agent LLM Framework for Causal Instrumental Variable Discovery (04-06) — first paper in the wiki to **treat LLM agents as a multi-agent proposer-critic-refiner architecture for instrumental-variable discovery from observational data in the presence of unobserved confounding**, evaluated via a two-stage framework (recovery of established IVs + rejection of discredited ones) plus a statistical consistency test for the no-ground-truth regime; surfacing *unobserved-confounder-causal-effect-identifiability-primitive* and *multi-agent-IV-discovery-primitive* and *no-ground-truth-consistency-test-primitive* as the load-bearing identifiability primitives for unobserved-confounder layer, distinct from causal-discovery primitives (where the load-bearing axis is *instrument-discovery-as-multi-agent-task* rather than *autonomous-causal-structure-discovery*) and from causal-reasoner primitives (where the load-bearing axis is *unobserved-confounder-isolation* rather than *universal-causal-reasoning*); plus *two-stage-IV-evaluation-framework-primitive* as the load-bearing evaluation primitive (recovery + rejection); plus *proposer-critic-refiner-multi-agent-primitive* as the load-bearing architecture primitive. **First IV-discovery-via-multi-agent-LLM-framework with two-stage evaluation and no-ground-truth consistency test in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(instrumental.variable.*llm|iv.*discovery|iv.co.scientist)"` returning empty. 2026-04-06; discovered via arxiv search HTML 4-query IDENTIFIABILITY-PROBE on 2026-06-29 (identifiability / instrumental-variable / unobserved-confounder primitive theme — Rule 68 IDENTIFIABILITY-PROBE).
- [[liberty-a-causal-framework-for-benchmarking-concept-based-explanations-of-llms-with-structural-counterfactuals-2601.10700]] — LIBERTy: A Causal Framework for Benchmarking Concept-Based Explanations of LLMs with Structural Counterfactuals (01-18) — first paper in the wiki to **ground concept-based-explanation benchmarking in explicitly defined Structural Causal Models (SCMs)** and generate structural counterfactual pairs by propagating interventions on a concept through the SCM until an LLM produces the counterfactual text — replacing costly human-written counterfactuals; introduces three structurally-diverse datasets (disease detection, CV screening, workplace-violence prediction) and a new *order-faithfulness* metric; surfacing *SCM-grounded-counterfactual-identifiability-primitive* and *concept-explanation-faithfulness-primitive* and *intervention-propagation-through-SCM-primitive* and *order-faithfulness-metric-primitive* as the load-bearing identifiability primitives for SCM-grounded-counterfactual layer, distinct from human-written-counterfactual-benchmark primitives (where the load-bearing axis is *SCM-grounded structural counterfactuals* rather than *human-annotated-counterfactuals-as-imperfect-proxy*) and from causal-methods-survey primitives (where the load-bearing axis is *operationalized-counterfactual-substrate* rather than *taxonomic-survey*); plus *high-stakes-decision-domain-coverage-primitive* as the load-bearing deployment primitive (disease + CV + workplace-violence); plus *five-LLM-comparative-evaluation-with-substantial-headroom-primitive* as the load-bearing evaluation primitive. **First SCM-grounded counterfactual benchmark for LLM concept-based explanations with order-faithfulness metric in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(liberty|concept.based.explanation.*causal|order.faithful|structural.counterfactual.*benchmark)"` returning empty. 2026-01-18; discovered via arxiv search HTML 4-query IDENTIFIABILITY-PROBE on 2026-06-29 (identifiability / SCM-grounded-counterfactual / concept-explanation-faithfulness primitive theme — Rule 68 IDENTIFIABILITY-PROBE).

Three picks (date-DESC ordered by online_date):
- [[an-empirical-study-of-many-shot-in-context-learning-for-machine-translation-of-low-resource-languages-2604.02596]] — An Empirical Study of Many-Shot In-Context Learning for Machine Translation of Low-Resource Languages (06-23) — first paper in the wiki to systematically characterize **many-shot ICL scaling behavior for low-resource machine translation** — exposing the dual budget of context-length cost and per-example informativeness that governs ICL deployment in low-resource language communities where compute budgets cannot absorb context-length expansion; surfacing *many-shot-ICL-scaling-law-primitive* and *low-resource-ICL-sample-efficiency-frontier-primitive* and *context-length-budget-vs-example-quality-tradeoff-primitive* as the load-bearing sample-efficiency-limit primitives for low-resource LLM deployment, distinct from generic ICL scaling studies (where the load-bearing axis is *low-resource-language-ICL-deployment-economics* rather than *general-scaling-curve*) and from coreset-selection primitives (where the load-bearing axis is *many-shot-ICL-context-length-cost* rather than *data-pruning-at-training*); plus *example-selection-under-context-length-constraints-primitive* as the load-bearing engineering primitive for low-resource deployment; the paper establishes **ICL context-length budget as a sample-efficiency primitive** for low-resource deployment — distinct from the high-resource regime where context-length cost is amortized across larger user populations. **First many-shot ICL sample-efficiency frontier study for low-resource machine translation in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(many.shot|low.resource.*icl|icl.*sample.efficiency)"` returning empty. 2026-06-23; discovered via arxiv search HTML 4-query SAMPLE-EFFICIENCY-LIMIT-PROBE on 2026-06-29 (sample-efficiency-limit / many-shot-ICL / low-resource-deployment primitive theme — Rule 67 SAMPLE-EFFICIENCY-LIMIT-PROBE).
- [[sample-efficient-demonstration-selection-for-in-context-learning-2506.08607]] — Sample Efficient Demonstration Selection for In-Context Learning (06-10-2025) — first paper in the wiki to formulate **exemplar selection as a top-m best-arms identification problem from multi-armed bandit theory** — directly bridging bandit theory and LLM ICL, with the key challenge being the exponentially large number of candidate demonstration arms; addresses via CASE (Challenger Arm Sampling for Exemplar selection), a sample-efficient bandit algorithm that identifies the m-best demonstrations under context-length budget constraints; surfacing *best-arm-identification-formulation-for-exemplar-selection-primitive* and *sample-efficient-bandit-algorithm-for-ICL-primitive* and *exponentially-large-arm-budget-primitive* as the load-bearing sample-efficiency-limit primitives for ICL exemplar selection, distinct from generic few-shot-pool sampling primitives (where the load-bearing axis is *top-m best-arm identification under exponentially-large-arm-budgets* rather than *uniform-random-or-diversity-sampling*) and from bandit-curriculum-learning primitives (where the load-bearing axis is *exemplar-selection-as-pure-bandit-problem* rather than *curriculum-as-bandit-policy*); plus *challenger-arm-sampling-primitive* as the load-bearing algorithmic primitive for sample-efficient identification under large action spaces; plus *context-length-budget-as-bandit-cost-primitive* as the load-bearing budget primitive; the paper establishes **exemplar selection as a multi-armed bandit problem** as the structurally correct primitive for sample-efficient ICL under context-length constraints, moving ICL beyond ad-hoc selection toward principled sample-efficient demonstration discovery. **First best-arm-identification formulation for exemplar selection in ICL with sample-efficient bandit algorithm (CASE) in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(sample.efficient.*demonstration|case.*challenger|exemplar.*selection.*bandit|best.arm.*identification.*icl)"` returning empty. 2025-06-10; discovered via arxiv search HTML 4-query SAMPLE-EFFICIENCY-LIMIT-PROBE on 2026-06-29 (sample-efficiency-limit / best-arm-identification / bandit-ICL primitive theme — Rule 67 SAMPLE-EFFICIENCY-LIMIT-PROBE).
- [[data-efficient-fine-tuning-for-llm-based-recommendation-2401.17197]] — Data-efficient Fine-tuning for LLM-based Recommendation (06-04-2024) — first paper in the wiki to propose **data pruning for efficient LLM-based recommendation** — identifying representative samples tailored for LLM few-shot fine-tuning as a coreset-selection problem; addresses the practical cost barrier of fine-tuning LLMs on rapidly expanding recommendation data, where full-data fine-tuning cost is the load-bearing deployment barrier; surfacing *data-pruning-as-LLM-deployment-enabler-primitive* and *coreset-selection-for-LLM-fine-tuning-primitive* and *sample-efficiency-as-domain-adaptation-enabler-primitive* as the load-bearing sample-efficiency-limit primitives for vertical-domain LLM deployment, distinct from generic data-pruning primitives (where the load-bearing axis is *data-pruning-for-LLM-recommendation-fine-tuning* rather than *data-pruning-for-vision-classifier-training*) and from generic coreset-selection primitives (where the load-bearing axis is *sample-efficiency-as-deployment-enabler-for-LLM* rather than *training-cost-reduction-for-classifier*); plus *few-shot-fine-tuning-as-deployment-barrier-primitive* as the load-bearing deployment primitive; plus *representative-sample-selection-for-LLM-primitive* as the load-bearing selection primitive; the paper establishes **coreset-selection for LLM domain-adaptation** as a structurally distinct sample-efficiency primitive operating on the FT layer rather than the ICL layer, complementing Run 100's exemplar-selection primitive (ICL layer) and many-shot ICL primitive (deployment layer) — completing the **3-layer sample-efficiency primitive-class triplet** (FT + ICL + deployment). **First data-pruning-for-LLM-fine-tuning primitive with coreset-selection as deployment-enabler for vertical-domain LLM in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(data.efficient.*fine.tun|coreset.*llm|data.prun.*llm|sample.efficient.*recommendation)"` returning empty. 2024-06-04; discovered via arxiv search HTML 4-query SAMPLE-EFFICIENCY-LIMIT-PROBE on 2026-06-29 (sample-efficiency-limit / coreset-selection / data-pruning-primitive theme — Rule 67 SAMPLE-EFFICIENCY-LIMIT-PROBE).
- **(2026-06-29 - Run 99) - 3-axis DOMAIN-DIVERSITY PIVOT to NULL-MODEL-COMPARISON-PROBE per Rule 66** introducing **null-model-comparison primitive** (after Rules 33-65 in sequence - cycle rotation -> sub-cluster -> new-axis -> combined-axis -> PROBE-AXIS -> deep-sub-cluster -> deep-sub-cluster-other-primitive -> axis-inversion -> temporal-anchor -> axis-orthogonality -> negative-result-probe -> adversarial-axis-probe -> meta-probe -> cross-discipline-probe -> application-domain-probe -> failure-mode-catalog -> emerging-pretraining-objective-probe -> multi-agent-consensus-probe -> reward-model-geometry-probe -> neuro-symbolic-grounding-probe -> representation-engineering-probe -> uncertainty-quantification-probe -> causal-inference-probe -> graph-structured-reasoning-probe -> self-improvement-loop-probe -> test-time-compute-scaling-probe -> watermarking-model-fingerprinting-probe -> inference-efficiency-probe -> agent-memory-architecture-probe -> multi-agent-coordination-collaboration-probe -> model-combination-fusion-primitive-probe -> collaborative-learning-probe -> cross-agent-teaching-probe -> environmental-evolution-probe -> tokenizer-level-primitive-probe -> axiomatic-formalization-probe -> emergent-capabilities-probe -> negative-result-as-primitive-probe have all been deployed in sequence), Run 99 introduces NULL-MODEL-COMPARISON-PROBE - pick axes that explicitly probe *null-model-comparison primitives for LLM* (minimal-pair-anchored controlled-comparison, falsifiable-behavioral-arc, strict-evaluation-negative-result, reasoning-mode null-result, constraint-inference-failure-localization, representational-decoding-causal-control-dissociation, prompt-only-directional-mismatch, latent-readout-with-control-failure, behavioral-economics cross-discipline controlled comparison, probability-of-necessity PN/PS/PNS framework, abstract-machine natural-language interface view, likelihood-of-causation suite, necessity-vs-sufficiency decomposition, reasoning-emergence formal criterion, math-example-illustrations, reasoning-mode-not-significant-after-capability-control); widens the discovery space differently than Run 98's negative-result-as-primitive-probe (which targeted *pre-registered controlled negative results where the null finding is itself the architectural primitive* - Rule 65) and Run 97's emergent-capabilities-probe (which targeted the *emergent-capability-mechanism layer* - Rule 64) and Run 96's axiomatic-formalization-probe (which targeted the *formal-foundation layer* of LLM reasoning - Rule 63) and Run 95's tokenizer-level-primitive-probe (which targeted *tokenizer design/construction/fairness* - Rule 62) - Rule 66 specifically probes the **null-model-comparison layer** of LLM evaluation, the load-bearing primitive-class for moving LLM evaluation beyond benchmark scores toward necessity-vs-sufficiency-decomposition and controlled-comparison null-model baselines (random/RNN/shuffled baselines, ablation controls, synthetic-controls comparisons); distinct from positive-method primitives (where the load-bearing axis is *minimal-pair-anchored-controlled-comparison* rather than *positive-accuracy-on-benchmark*), from negative-result primitives (Rule 65, where the load-bearing axis is *reasoning-mode-not-significant-after-capability-control* rather than *pre-registered null finding*), and from mechanistic-interpretability primitives (where the load-bearing axis is *decoding-vs-control-dissociation* rather than *circuit-discovery*); the only existing necessity-sufficiency / minimal-pair / probability-of-causation entities in the wiki are cross-discipline/medical/finance application primitives (Tone-Check, HeLa-Mem, GlobalDentBench, ZenBrain, MA-ProofBench) - - [[the-model-says-walk-how-surface-heuristics-override-implicit-constraints-in-llm-reasoning-2603.29025]] - The Model Says Walk: How Surface Heuristics Override Implicit Constraints in LLM Reasoning (06-09) - first paper in the wiki to **introduce a controlled minimal-pair-anchored benchmark (HOB) for surface-heuristic-vs-implicit-constraint conflicts in LLM reasoning** with a falsifiable behavioral arc (diagnose-measure-bridge-treat) that establishes context-independent sigmoid heuristics where the distance cue has 8.7-38x more influence than the goal and attribution better matches keyword association than compositional inference; surfacing *minimal-pair-anchored-controlled-comparison-primitive* and *falsifiable-behavioral-arc-primitive* and *constraint-inference-failure-localization-primitive* and *strict-evaluation-negative-result-primitive* and *reasoning-mode-null-result-primitive* as the load-bearing null-model-comparison primitives, distinct from positive-reasoning-method primitives (where the load-bearing axis is *falsifiable-behavioral-decomposition-of-surface-heuristics* rather than *capability-curves*); plus *sigmoid-heuristic-context-independent-primitive* as the load-bearing behavioral-characterization primitive (distance cue vs goal with explicit 8.7-38x influence ratio); plus *minimal-pair-explicitness-gradient-primitive* as the load-bearing benchmark-construction primitive (500 instances across 4 heuristic families x 5 constraint families with minimal pairs and explicitness gradients); plus *strict-10/10-evaluation-primitive* as the load-bearing evaluation primitive (no model exceeds 75% on 10/10 evaluation, presence constraints hardest at 44%); plus *thinking-mode-ablation-recovery-primitive* as the load-bearing mechanism primitive (Gemini 3.1 Pro drops from 74.6% thinking-on to 58.4% thinking-off, recovers to 71.2% with explicit goal decomposition); plus *reasoning-mode-not-significant-after-capability-control-primitive* as the load-bearing null result (residual reasoning-mode effect 1.8 pp, not significant after controlling for capability rank); plus *constraint-enumeration-as-active-ingredient-primitive* as the load-bearing recipe primitive (goal-decomposition 5.0 pp vs generic-CoT 3.1 pp, isolating constraint enumeration as the active ingredient); plus *conservative-bias-on-constraint-removal-primitive* as the load-bearing bias primitive (12 of 14 models perform worse when constraint removed, by up to 39 pp); plus *parametric-probe-generalization-primitive* as the load-bearing generalization primitive (sigmoid pattern generalizes to cost, efficiency, semantic-similarity heuristics via parametric probes); HOB establishes **minimal-pair-anchored controlled-comparison + falsifiable behavioral arc + strict-evaluation null result + reasoning-mode null result** as the structurally correct primitives for surface-heuristic-override vulnerability characterization in LLM reasoning. **First minimal-pair-anchored controlled-comparison heuristic-override benchmark with falsifiable behavioral decomposition in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "minimal-pair"`.
- [[representation-without-control-testing-the-realization-effect-in-language-models-2605.25151]] - Representation Without Control: Testing the Realization Effect in Language Models (05-24) - first paper in the wiki to **establish a representational-decoding-causal-control dissociation** for LLM behavioral simulation - using the realization effect from behavioral economics (paper vs realized gains/losses) as a controlled comparison - and showing that **behavioral sensitivity, linear readout of internal representations, and causal control via activation steering are three distinct properties that do not automatically co-occur**; surfacing *three-level-dissociation-primitive* and *representational-decoding-causal-control-gap-primitive* and *cross-discipline-behavioral-economics-null-model-comparison-primitive* as the load-bearing null-model-comparison primitives for LLM behavioral simulation, distinct from positive-mechanistic-interpretability primitives (where the load-bearing axis is *decoding-vs-control-dissociation* rather than *mechanism-discovery*), and from surface-pattern-matching primitives (where the load-bearing axis is *null-result-on-causal-steering* rather than *prompt-only-reproduction*); plus *prompt-only-directional-mismatch-primitive* as the load-bearing surface-pattern null result (directional pattern does not reproduce human realization-effect predictions even when systematic condition sensitivity is present); plus *latent-readout-positive-result-primitive* as the load-bearing decoding primitive (Gemma layer 18 contains a linearly decodable realization-status signal that generalizes to held-out prompts); plus *causal-steering-null-result-primitive* as the load-bearing control primitive (steering along the realization-status direction does not reliably shift downstream risk choices, with null result holding across positive scales and in a negative sign-symmetry run); plus *decoding-insufficient-for-behavioral-reliance-primitive* as the load-bearing meta-primitive (successful latent readout is insufficient evidence that a model behaviorally relies on a representation during downstream decision-making); plus *cross-discipline-controlled-comparison-primitive* as the load-bearing domain-translation primitive (applying a well-characterized behavioral-economics finding as a controlled comparison test for LLM behavioral simulation); the paper formalizes the gap between *decoding* (linear readout succeeds) and *control* (steering fails) as a primitive distinction in mechanistic interpretability, going beyond the standard "is the feature present" question to ask "does the model behaviorally rely on the feature". **First representational-decoding-causal-control three-level dissociation test with explicit null result on causal steering in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(realization.effect|behavioral.simulation)"`.
- [[does-reasoning-emerge-examining-the-probabilities-of-causation-in-large-language-models-2408.08210]] - Does Reasoning Emerge? Examining the Probabilities of Causation in Large Language Models (08-15-2024) - first paper in the wiki to **introduce a formal application of the Probability of Necessity (PN) and Probability of Sufficiency (PS) - two foundational primitives from causal inference - to characterize when LLMs are capable of reasoning**, framing LLMs as abstract machines that process information through a natural language interface and deriving the conditions under which suitable approximations of PN/PS/PNS (Probability of Necessity and Sufficiency) can be computed; surfacing *probability-of-necessity-primitive* and *probability-of-sufficiency-primitive* and *probability-of-necessity-and-sufficiency-primitive* and *necessity-vs-sufficiency-decomposition-primitive* and *abstract-machine-natural-language-interface-primitive* as the load-bearing null-model-comparison primitives for reasoning-emergence assessment, distinct from positive-reasoning-benchmark primitives (where the load-bearing axis is *PN/PS/PNS-causal-necessity-sufficiency-decomposition* rather than *accuracy-on-task-X*); plus *likelihood-of-causation-suite-primitive* as the load-bearing unified-measure primitive (PNS as a single scalar combining PN and PS for measuring causation strength); plus *math-example-illustrations-primitive* as the load-bearing reproducibility primitive (worked math examples showing how to operationalize PN/PS/PNS computations on LLM outputs); plus *reasoning-emergence-formal-criterion-primitive* as the load-bearing meta-primitive (anchoring the reasoning-emergence debate in causal-inference primitives rather than benchmark scores); the paper marks the first step toward a deeper understanding of *when* LLMs are capable of reasoning, providing a theoretical foundation for controlled-comparison studies that separate necessity and sufficiency questions (would the conclusion fail without this step? is the step enough on its own to produce the conclusion?). **First probability-of-causation framework applied to LLM reasoning emergence in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(probability.of.causation|necessity.sufficiency|reasoning.emerge)`.


- **(2026-06-29 — Run 98) — 3-axis DOMAIN-DIVERSITY PIVOT to NEGATIVE-RESULT-AS-PRIMITIVE-PROBE per Rule 65** introducing **negative-result-as-architectural-primitive** (after Rules 33-64 in sequence — cycle rotation → sub-cluster → new-axis → combined-axis → PROBE-AXIS → deep-sub-cluster → deep-sub-cluster-other-primitive → axis-inversion → temporal-anchor → axis-orthogonality → negative-result-probe → adversarial-axis-probe → meta-probe → cross-discipline-probe → application-domain-probe → failure-mode-catalog → emerging-pretraining-objective-probe → multi-agent-consensus-probe → reward-model-geometry-probe → neuro-symbolic-grounding-probe → representation-engineering-probe → uncertainty-quantification-probe → causal-inference-probe → graph-structured-reasoning-probe → self-improvement-loop-probe → test-time-compute-scaling-probe → watermarking-model-fingerprinting-probe → inference-efficiency-probe → agent-memory-architecture-probe → multi-agent-coordination-collaboration-probe → model-combination-fusion-primitive-probe → collaborative-learning-probe → cross-agent-teaching-probe → environmental-evolution-probe → tokenizer-level-primitive-probe → axiomatic-formalization-probe → emergent-capabilities-probe have all been deployed in sequence), Run 98 introduces NEGATIVE-RESULT-AS-PRIMITIVE-PROBE — pick axes that explicitly probe *negative-result-as-architectural-primitive surfaces for LLM* (corpus-statistics-as-rule-fate-determinant, asymmetric-recovery-control of destroyed rules, displacement-as-forgetting-mechanism, low-rank-behavior-subspace-interaction, asymmetric-control-points across behaviors, intervention-side-effect-as-architectural-property, agent-judgment-collapse with capability-asymmetric-deference, tool-pinning-as-bypass with information-limited-selective-invocation-remedies); widens the discovery space differently than Run 97's emergent-capabilities-probe (which targeted the **capability-elicitation-mechanism** axis — Rule 64) and Run 96's axiomatic-formalization-probe (which targeted the **formal-foundation** axis — Rule 63) — Rule 65 specifically probes the **negative-space account** of LLM mechanics, the load-bearing primitive-class for understanding what training/eval/post-training systems **cannot do** (cannot restore destroyed rules, cannot decouple targeted interventions from side-effects, cannot make stronger agents exercise more judgment), establishing **design-constraint-by-strong-negative-result** as a primitive class distinct from positive-method primitives; distinct from adversarial-axis-probe (which targeted adversarial-attack-vs-defense surfaces — Rule 39) and failure-mode-catalog-probe (which targeted systematic-failure-classification surfaces — Rule 43) — Rule 65 specifically probes *pre-registered controlled negative results* where the null finding is itself the architectural primitive (not a failure-mode classification or an adversarial finding); the only existing negative-result entities in the wiki are pitfall-recognition papers (BFI-Replication-Failure, Signgle-Preference-Identity-Assignment-Failure) and benchmark-saturation discoveries (Multi-Agent-Advantage-Falsification) — none of which probe the **pre-registered-corpus-statistics-as-fate-decider / pre-registered-low-rank-subspace-as-control-points / pre-registered-agent-tool-deference-as-capability-amplifier** primitive triplet that Run 98 surfaces; pivoting per Rule 27 from Run 97 numerical-precision-as-Slingshot-trigger + commutator-defect-as-grokking-precursor + strategic-exploration-suppression-as-RL-capability-hiding (emergent-capabilities primitive-class triplet) to corpus-statistics-as-rule-fate-determinant + low-rank-subspace-as-intervention-control-points + agent-tool-deference-as-capability-amplifier (negative-result-as-primitive-class triplet — fully orthogonal to Run 97 emergent-capabilities axes, orthogonal to Run 96 axiomatic-formalization axes, orthogonal to Run 95 tokenizer-level axes, orthogonal to Run 94 environmental-evolution axes, orthogonal to Run 93 cross-agent-teaching axes, orthogonal to Run 92 collaborative-learning axes, orthogonal to Run 91 model-combination-fusion axes, orthogonal to Run 90 multi-agent-coordination-collaboration axes, orthogonal to Run 89 agent-memory-architecture axes, orthogonal to Run 88 inference-efficiency axes, orthogonal to Run 87 watermarking-model-fingerprinting axes, orthogonal to Run 86 test-time-compute-scaling axes, orthogonal to Run 85 self-improvement-loop axes, orthogonal to Run 84 graph-structured-reasoning axes, orthogonal to Run 83 causal-inference axes, orthogonal to Run 82 uncertainty-quantification axes, orthogonal to Run 81 representation-engineering axes, orthogonal to Run 80 neuro-symbolic-grounding axes, orthogonal to Run 79 reward-model-geometry axes, orthogonal to Run 78 multi-agent-consensus axes, orthogonal to Run 77 emerging-pretraining-objective axes, orthogonal to Run 76 failure-mode-catalog axes, orthogonal to Run 75 application-domain axes, orthogonal to Run 74 cross-discipline axes, orthogonal to Run 73 representation-engineering axes, orthogonal to Run 72 adversarial-axis axes). HF daily v2 returned 13 unique papers across 2026-06-29/30/07-01 sparse daily content all CV/3D/multimodal/agent-eval heavy (PhysisForcing, Translation-as-Bridging-Action, Qwen-Image-2.0-RL, SingGuard, ProMSA, SimFoundry, Formalizing-Latent-Thoughts, Ko-WideSearch, Learning-to-Fold, NormGuard, Paper-Assistant, Boundary-Aware-EEG, Object-Centric-Residual-RL), 0 negative-result-as-architectural-primitive primitives; web_search 4-query NEGATIVE-RESULT-AS-PRIMITIVE-PROBE escape hatch fired (44th consecutive pitfall-83 — longest in wiki history by 31 runs) via 4 negative-result axes: (a) pre-registered controlled study null result on intervention/scaffolding evaluation + (b) cross-model-activation-transfer causal-injection negative-result + (c) multi-agent-judgment-deference-as-capability-amplifier + (d) corpus-statistics-as-rule-fate-decider with asymmetric-recovery-control — Rule 65 NEGATIVE-RESULT-AS-PRIMITIVE-PROBE WORKED, fresh-LLM return at typical base rate with full orthogonality to Run 97 emergent-capabilities picks. Three picks (date-DESC ordered by online_date with same-date insertion-order tiebreaker):
- [[natural-ungrokking-asymmetric-control-of-which-rules-survive-pretraining-2606.26050]] — Natural Ungrokking: Asymmetric Control of Which Rules Survive Pretraining (06-24) — first paper in the wiki to **establish that learned rules can be silently and permanently destroyed during ordinary pretraining with no trace in the loss curve** — and that destruction is predictable from a single corpus statistic (rule-support frequency), with **asymmetric control** of the rule's fate (easy to kill with counter-evidence, impossible to restore by injecting support back even at 450× the natural-sustaining level); surfacing *corpus-statistics-as-rule-fate-determinant-primitive* and *asymmetric-recovery-control-primitive* and *displacement-as-forgetting-mechanism-primitive* as the load-bearing negative-result primitives for pretraining dynamics, distinct from positive-method training interventions (where the load-bearing axis is *corpus-decides-which-rules-to-keep-as-silent-decider* rather than *gradient-update-controls-which-rules-survive*), and from intrinsic-optimization-dynamics accounts (where the load-bearing axis is *predictable-from-support-frequency-across-2-corpora-x-3-budgets-x-3-seeds* rather than *fitted-loss-landscape-features*); plus *natural-ungrokking-within-run-reversal-primitive* as the load-bearing rule-collapse primitive (cue "Sue cried because" → resolves to "she" at step 925 with 0.94 generalization, then collapses to ~0 by step 3,500); plus *cross-Pythia-checkpoint-replication-primitive* as the load-bearing cross-corpus primitive confirming collapse depth orders with model scale as predicted; plus *100-training-step-margin-crossing-primitive* as the load-bearing mechanism primitive (competing surface pattern's log-prob margin crosses zero within 100 steps of behavioral collapse); plus *monotone-counter-evidence-dose-response-primitive* as the load-bearing intervention primitive (flipping support to counter-evidence kills the rule with monotone dose-response across two unrelated rules); plus *pre-registration-of-all-thresholds-primitive* as the load-bearing methodology primitive (every confirmatory threshold and prediction was pre-registered before the data it governed was read); the load-bearing claim is the **negative result** — injecting support back (even at 450× natural-sustaining level) buys no recovery — establishing **rule-revival-as-impossible-archaeological-recovery** as a primitive class in the wiki. **First within-run-rule-silent-collapse pre-registered study with corpus-statistics-as-rule-fate-determinant and asymmetric-recovery-control in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(natural.ungrokking|asymmetric.control.*rules|corpus.statistics.*fate|displacement.as.forgetting|rule.survive.*pretraining)"` returning empty. 2026-06-24; discovered via web_search 4-query NEGATIVE-RESULT-AS-PRIMITIVE-PROBE on 2026-06-29 (negative-result / corpus-statistics-as-fate-decider / asymmetric-recovery-of-destroyed-rules primitive theme — Rule 65 NEGATIVE-RESULT-AS-PRIMITIVE-PROBE).
- [[when-the-tool-decides-llm-agents-defer-blindly-to-graph-neural-network-tools-and-stronger-backbones-defer-more-2606.14476]] — When the Tool Decides: LLM Agents Defer Blindly to Graph Neural Network Tools, and Stronger Backbones Defer More (06-12) — first paper in the wiki to **systematically demonstrate that LLM agents with callable GNN tools do not exercise judgment but instead defer blindly to the tool's output — and that stronger backbones defer more, not less**, establishing **agent-judgment-collapse-as-capability-amplifier-primitive** with pre-registered experimental design; surfacing *agent-judgment-collapse-primitive* and *capability-deference-not-reasoning-primitive* and *tool-pinning-as-bypass-primitive* as the load-bearing negative-result primitives for agent-tool evaluation, distinct from positive-tool-use-reward-modeling (where the load-bearing axis is *97.6-99.2%-agreement-with-raw-GNN-output-across-5-seeds* rather than *tool-use-improvement-curves*), and from agent-orchestration-failure-mode primitives (Multi-Agent-Advantage-Falsification, where the load-bearing axis is *stronger-models-defer-more-from-1.5B-to-7B-0.60-to-0.98* rather than *agents-underperform-monolithic-baselines*); plus *agent-cost-of-deference-doesnt-shrink-with-capability-primitive* as the load-bearing oracle-gap primitive (per-node oracle over available actions beats the parrot by 0.09-0.18 at 3B, 0.12-0.22 at 7B, **roughly doubling at high homophily** — where the agent's alternatives improve while the parrot remains pinned); plus *alternative-tool-substitution-failure-primitive* as the load-bearing tool-choice-not-rationality primitive (a simple neighbor-label tool overtakes the GNN at high homophily — 0.81 vs 0.71 — yet the agent still defers to the less-accurate GNN); plus *information-limited-selective-invocation-bound-primitive* as the load-bearing remedy-bounded-by-information primitive (held-out estimates bound the best achievable gate over standard test-time features to **at most one-third of the oracle headroom**, framing reliable selective invocation as information-limited rather than router-design-limited); plus *ogbn-arxiv-plus-WikiCS-replication-primitive* as the load-bearing cross-benchmark primitive confirming the deference finding on replicated text-attributed graphs; plus *Qwen2.5-0.5B-to-7B-backbone-sweep-primitive* as the load-bearing scale-doesnt-rescue primitive ruling out weak-model-artifact explanations; the load-bearing claim is the **negative result** — that *evaluations of agent+tool systems cannot assume the agent adds judgment on top of the tool, and selective invocation must be designed in rather than expected to emerge from scale* — establishing **agent-judgment-emergence-not-from-scale** as a primitive class in the wiki with pre-registered experimental design. **First systematic agent-tool-deference-as-capability-amplifier controlled study in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(when.the.tool.decides|gnn.parrot|agent.deference|capability.deference|tool.pinning|information.limited.gate)"` returning empty. 2026-06-12; discovered via web_search 4-query NEGATIVE-RESULT-AS-PRIMITIVE-PROBE on 2026-06-29 (negative-result / agent-judgment-collapse / capability-deference-as-amplifier primitive theme — Rule 65 NEGATIVE-RESULT-AS-PRIMITIVE-PROBE).
- [[a-low-rank-subspace-analysis-of-llm-interventions-2606.14388]] — A Low-Rank Subspace Analysis of LLM Interventions (06-12) — first paper in the wiki to **systematically diagnose why interventions that aim to modify one LLM behavior (e.g. refusal) unintentionally alter other behaviors (e.g. sycophancy)** — modeling behaviors as low-rank subspaces in activation space and showing that interventions propagate asymmetrically across behaviors, with **upstream control points** whose interventions broadly affect others; surfacing *low-rank-behavior-subspace-primitive* and *asymmetric-control-points-primitive* and *intervention-side-effect-as-architectural-property-primitive* as the load-bearing negative-result primitives for safety-intervention engineering, distinct from positive steering-method primitives (where the load-bearing axis is *geometry-quantified-predictability-of-side-effects-via-subspace-overlap* rather than *steering-effectiveness-curves*), and from linear-instability-of-refusal primitives (where the load-bearing axis is *intervention-on-one-behavior-alters-another-asymmetrically* rather than *refusal-direction-collapses-under-fine-tune*); plus *cross-instruction-tuned-7B-to-70B-replication-primitive* as the load-bearing cross-scale primitive (findings hold across 7B-70B models); plus *three-behavior-cross-setting-replication-primitive* as the load-bearing cross-behavior primitive (refusal + jailbreak + sycophancy all show asymmetric propagation); plus *two-geometry-quantities-as-predictors-primitive* as the load-bearing predictive-primitive (subspace overlap = average squared cosine of principal angles; behavior-subspace-to-decision-subspace angle as second predictor); plus *upstream-control-point-identification-primitive* as the load-bearing intervention-design primitive (some behaviors act as upstream control points whose interventions propagate broadly while others remain isolated); the load-bearing claim is the **negative result** — that *behaviors are difficult to modify independently, as interventions can propagate through shared representations and asymmetric interactions* — establishing **safety-intervention-decoupling-as-architecturally-bounded** as a primitive class in the wiki with geometry-quantified predictability. **First systematic low-rank-subspace-asymmetric-control-points intervention-side-effect diagnostic framework in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(low.rank.subspace.*intervention|asymmetric.control.points|intervention.side.effect.*subspace|upstream.control.point|behavior.subspace.angle)"` returning empty. 2026-06-12;
