Three picks (date-DESC ordered by online_date):

- [[human-inspired-memory-architecture-for-llm-agents-2605.08538]] — Human-Inspired Memory Architecture for LLM Agents (05-08) — first paper in the wiki to introduce a *biologically-grounded six-mechanism memory architecture for LLM agents* that imports six cognitive mechanisms from human memory theory — (1) sleep-phase consolidation, (2) interference-based forgetting, (3) engram maturation, (4) reconsolidation upon retrieval, (5) entity knowledge graphs, (6) hybrid multi-cue retrieval — paired with a synthetic-only calibration methodology that derives every pipeline threshold without benchmark data exposure, eliminating a structural source of evaluation leakage present in earlier memory systems; evaluated on a real VSCode issue-tracking workload (13K issues, 120K events) where dedup-based consolidation achieves 97.2% retention precision with 58% store reduction (+21.8 pp over baseline), and on the LongMemEval first streaming M-tier evaluation (475 sessions, ~540K unique turns) where at 200K context budget the pipeline matches raw-retrieval accuracy (70.1% vs 71.2%, overlapping 95% CI) while exposing a tunable accuracy/store-size operating curve and yielding +13.3 pp in preference recall at S-tier scale. **First biologically-grounded six-cognitive-mechanism memory architecture with synthetic-only calibration eliminating evaluation leakage in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(human.inspired|biological.*cognitive.*memory|6.mechanism|sleep.phase.consolidation|engram.maturation)"` returning empty. 2026-05-08; discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-29 (agent-memory-architecture / biologically-grounded cognitive-mechanism architecture / synthetic-calibration-no-eval-leakage primitive theme — Rule 56 AGENT-MEMORY-ARCHITECTURE-PROBE).

- [[memory-for-autonomous-llm-agents-mechanisms-evaluation-emerging-frontiers-2603.07670]] — Memory for Autonomous LLM Agents: Mechanisms, Evaluation, and Emerging Frontiers (03-08) — first comprehensive 2022-early-2026 survey of memory for LLM agents in the wiki; introduces a three-dimensional taxonomy (temporal scope × representational substrate × control policy), examines five mechanism families in depth (context-resident compression, retrieval-augmented stores, reflective self-improvement, hierarchical virtual context, policy-learned management), traces the evaluation-front-end shift from static recall benchmarks to multi-session agentic tests that interleave memory with decision-making, surfaces four recent benchmarks with stubborn gaps, surveys applications where memory is the differentiating primitive (personal assistants, coding agents, open-world games, scientific reasoning, multi-agent teamwork), and closes with five falsifiable open challenges (continual consolidation, causally grounded retrieval, trustworthy reflection, learned forgetting, multimodal embodied memory). **First comprehensive 2022-early-2026 agent-memory survey with three-dimensional taxonomy and five-mechanism-family framework in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(memory.*autonomous.*llm|memory.*survey.*2022|write.manage.read|three.dimensional.*memory.*taxonomy|five.mechanism.*family)"` returning empty. 2026-03-08; discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-29 (agent-memory-architecture / comprehensive-survey / three-dimensional-taxonomy primitive theme — Rule 56 AGENT-MEMORY-ARCHITECTURE-PROBE).

- [[continuum-memory-architectures-for-long-horizon-llm-agents-2601.09913]] — Continuum Memory Architectures for Long-Horizon LLM Agents (01-14) — first paper in the wiki to formalize the *Continuum Memory Architecture (CMA)* as a class of systems maintaining and updating internal state across interactions through five architectural requirements — persistent storage, selective retention, associative routing, temporal chaining, and consolidation into higher-order abstractions — while surfacing the *RAG-vs-CMA structural diagnostic* demonstrating CMA is a necessary architectural primitive for tasks requiring *accumulating, mutating, and disambiguating* memory rather than mere retrieval; empirically probes four capabilities (knowledge updates, temporal association, associative recall, contextual disambiguation) that RAG structurally cannot provide, with consistent CMA advantages on each; enumerates honest open challenges (latency, drift, interpretability) to give downstream work a falsifiable target. **First Continuum Memory Architecture (CMA) primitive-formalization with RAG-vs-CMA structural diagnostic and four-capability empirical probe in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(continuum.memory|cma.*architectures|long.horizon.*memory.*primitive|rag.vs.cma|persistent.storage.*agent)"` returning empty. 2026-01-14; discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-29 (agent-memory-architecture / continuum-memory-architecture / formal-architectural-primitive primitive theme — Rule 56 AGENT-MEMORY-ARCHITECTURE-PROBE).
- **(2026-06-29 — Run 88) — 3-axis DOMAIN-DIVERSITY PIVOT to INFERENCE-EFFICIENCY-PROBE per Rule 55** introducing **inference-side efficiency primitive** (after Rule 54 watermarking-model-fingerprinting-probe + all preceding Rules 36d→53 in sequence, Run 88 introduces an INFERENCE-EFFICIENCY-PROBE — pick axes that explicitly probe *inference-side efficiency primitives for LLM* (information-theoretic KV-cache eviction, draft-augmented sparse verification, compression-aware adaptive speculation length); distinct from Rule 53 test-time-compute-scaling-probe which targeted compute-allocation/routing/aggregation at inference time (Sequential/parallel/MCTS/compute-optimal) — Rule 55 targets the *implementation-layer efficiency primitives* that govern how inference memory bandwidth, attention compute, and acceptance-rate evidence are conserved); widens the discovery space by deliberately surfacing papers from *inference-efficiency surfaces* — primitives that reduce the *engineering cost* of inference (KV-cache bytes, verification-time per draft token, fixed-gamma-decode inefficiency under compression); pivoting per Rule 27 from Run 87 signature-filtering-statistical-watermark-detection-hardening + hook-action-trajectory-copyright-94.3-detection-AUC + targeted-unlearning-LoRA-fingerprint-100%-ownership-verification (watermarking-model-fingerprinting primitive-class triplet) to information-theoretic-KV-cache-eviction-under-long-reasoning + draft-augmented-sparse-verification-primitives + compression-aware-adaptive-gamma-selection-primitives (inference-side-efficiency primitive-class triplet, fully orthogonal to Run 87 watermarking-model-fingerprinting axes, orthogonal to Run 86 test-time-compute-scaling axes, orthogonal to Run 85 self-improvement-loop axes, orthogonal to Run 84 graph-structured-reasoning axes, orthogonal to Run 83 causal-inference axes, orthogonal to Run 82 uncertainty-quantification axes, orthogonal to Run 81 representation-engineering axes, orthogonal to Run 80 neuro-symbolic-grounding axes, orthogonal to Run 79 reward-model-geometry axes, orthogonal to Run 78 multi-agent-consensus axes, orthogonal to Run 77 post-NTP-pretraining-objective axes, orthogonal to Run 76 failure-mode-catalog axes, orthogonal to Run 75 vertical-application axes, orthogonal to Run 74 cross-discipline axes). web_search 4-query escape hatch fired (34th consecutive pitfall-83 — HF daily v2 returned 10+ papers on 2026-06-29/28 with 100% CV/3D/Robotics heavy and ZERO inference-efficiency-centric primitives (PhysisForcing, SingGuard multimodal LLM guardrail, Translation-as-Bridging-Action, ProMSA VQA, Qwen-Image-2.0-RL, Ko-WideSearch, Formalizing-Latent-Thoughts, NormGuard, Automating-Scientific-Review, SimFoundry); HF v3 href-regex pool 100% non-inference-efficiency-centric; web_search surfaced 3+ fresh LLM candidates via the INFERENCE-EFFICIENCY-PROBE query template bracketing 4 inference-efficiency axes: (a) KV-cache compression for long-reasoning traces + (b) speculative-decoding verification-as-bottleneck + (c) compression-aware adaptive speculation-length budgeting + (d) inference-engine attention optimization for serving systems — Rule 55 inference-efficiency-probe WORKED, fresh-LLM return at typical base rate with full orthogonality to Run 87 watermarking-model-fingerprinting picks). Three picks (date-DESC ordered by online_date):

- [[information-aware-kv-cache-compression-for-long-reasoning-2606.26875]] — Information-Aware KV Cache Compression for Long Reasoning (06-28) — first paper in the wiki to introduce an **information-theoretic KV cache compression framework** that periodically compresses the KV cache for generated tokens during *long-decoding* reasoning — selecting eviction candidates by an information-content score that respects both per-token semantic contribution and cumulative decay in the chain-of-thought trace; surfaces *information-theoretic-eviction-primitive* as the structurally correct retention primitive for long-reasoning compression, distinct from sliding-window primitives where the load-bearing axis is *information-content-driven-eviction-via-decoding-fidelity-loss* rather than *recency-window*, and from attention-magnitude primitives where the load-bearing axis is *information-content-downstream-decoding-fidelity-loss* rather than *static-attention-score*. The framework compresses at decoding time (not only prefill), keeping long-reasoning traces bounded in memory while preserving reasoning accuracy within a narrow margin of the uncompressed baseline across long prefilling and long decoding benchmarks. **First information-theoretic KV-cache compression framework with periodic long-decoding compression for reasoning traces in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "information.aware.*kv|kv.*information.aware|long.*reasoning.*kv.compression|long.decoder.*kv.cache"` returning empty. 2026-06-28; discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-29 (inference-efficiency / KV-cache-eviction / long-reasoning primitive theme — Rule 55 INFERENCE-EFFICIENCY-PROBE).

- [[dustin-draft-augmented-sparse-verification-for-efficient-long-context-generation-with-speculative-decoding-2606.24957]] — Dustin: Draft-Augmented Sparse Verification for Efficient Long-Context Generation with Speculative Decoding (06-26) — first paper in the wiki to introduce **Dustin** — a *sparse verification framework for long-context speculative decoding* that integrates lookahead signals from the draft model with historical attention from the target model to identify critical draft tokens with high fidelity, addressing the fact that verification accounts for up to 87.5% of decoding latency at 32k input with batch size 16 and limits end-to-end speculative-decoding speedup; surfaces *draft-augmented-sparse-verification-primitive* as the structurally correct verification-budget primitive for long-context speculative decoding, distinct from full-tree-verification primitives (JetSpec/EAGLE3) where the load-bearing axis is *verification-criticality-detection-via-draft-plus-history-fusion* rather than *parallel-tree-exhaustive-verification*, and from fixed-gamma primitives (SpecKV, vanilla speculative decoding) where the load-bearing axis is *sparse-verify-only-critical-draft-tokens* rather than *gamma-tuning-with-full-verify*. The framework achieves substantial end-to-end speedups on long-context workloads while keeping acceptance rate close to vanilla. **First draft-augmented sparse verification framework cutting verification-as-87.5%-of-latency-bottleneck in long-context speculative decoding in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(dustin|sparse.verification|draft.augmented|long.context.*speculative)"` returning empty. 2026-06-26; discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-29 (inference-efficiency / speculative-decoding / verification-as-bottleneck primitive theme — Rule 55 INFERENCE-EFFICIENCY-PROBE).

- [[speckv-adaptive-speculative-decoding-with-compression-aware-gamma-selection-2605.02888]] — SpecKV: Adaptive Speculative Decoding with Compression-Aware Gamma Selection (05-05) — first paper in the wiki to introduce **SpecKV** — an *adaptive speculative-decoding framework* that tunes the speculation length (gamma) on the fly using draft-model confidence signals, achieving strong performance under compressed KV-cache settings where fixed-gamma speculative decoding plateaus; surfaces *compression-aware-adaptive-gamma-selection-primitive* as the structurally correct speculation-budget primitive when KV-cache compression shrinks the model's effective receptive field, distinct from fixed-gamma primitives (vanilla speculative decoding) where the load-bearing axis is *adaptive-gamma-policy-with-draft-evidence-under-compression* rather than *fixed-budget*, and from SpecDec-style tree-drafting primitives where the load-bearing axis is *compression-aware-budget-allocator* rather than *tree-expansion-policy*. SpecKV empirically beats fixed-gamma baselines across KV-cache compression settings, keeping acceptance high while freeing decoder bandwidth with negligible accuracy degradation. **First compression-aware adaptive gamma-selection speculative-decoding framework in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(speckv|adaptive.gamma|compression.aware.*gamma|adaptive.speculative)"` returning empty. 2026-05-05; discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-29 (inference-efficiency / adaptive-speculation-budget / KV-cache-compression-coupling primitive theme — Rule 55 INFERENCE-EFFICIENCY-PROBE).

Three picks (date-DESC ordered by online_date):

- [[signature-filtering-a-lightweight-enhancement-for-watermark-detection-2606.18430]] — Signature Filtering: A Lightweight Enhancement for Watermark Detection (06-24) — first paper in the wiki to introduce *signature filtering* — a detection-time module that learns a small set of "signature" tokens whose presence makes watermark tests unreliable, then removes those tokens before detection — yielding a *plug-in robustness layer* for existing statistical LLM watermarks (Kgw, Sweet, Unigram, Exp) without modifying watermark embedding or text generation; by solving a mixed-integer linear program on a small training set with constraints that maximize the true-positive rate under finite-sample and asymptotic bounds (color-blind, color-adaptive, distributionally correlated attacker models), the work exposes *detection-time pre-filtering as a structurally correct robustness primitive* for statistical LLM watermarks when texts are repetitive, watermarks are weak, or attacks dilute/drop/substitute tokens; surfacing *signature-filtering-as-detection-time-hardening-primitive* as the load-bearing primitive for watermark robustness, distinct from redesign-the-watermark approaches because the load-bearing axis is *pre-detection-signature-token-removal-from-input* rather than *redesigned-embedding-scheme*, and from retrain-the-detector approaches because the load-bearing axis is *small-set-of-attack-signature-tokens-learned-via-MILP* rather than *end-to-end-detector-retraining-on-adversarial-data* — plus *finite-sample-and-asymptotic-bounds-under-three-attacker-models-primitive* as the load-bearing *threat-model-formalization primitive* giving guarantees for color-blind (no knowledge of embedding key), color-adaptive (knowledge of embedding key, not signature set), and distributionally correlated (knowledge of both) adversaries, plus *stress-test-robustness-under-25-50%-token-perturbation-primitive* as the load-bearing *engineering primitive* showing 2-gram filters for Kgw-style watermarks preserve detection gains under scrambled-sentence + dilution + deletion + substitution stress tests, often matching or outperforming the advanced WinMax watermark. Across four well-known watermark families (Kgw, Sweet, Unigram, Exp), four benchmark corpora (C4, MBPP, HumanEval, Code-Search-Net), and six LLMs (OPT-1.3b, OPT-6.7b, Llama2-13b, Llama3.1-8b, Qwen2.5-14b, Phi-3-medium-14b), 2- and 3-gram signatures raise weak-signal and low-entropy detection rates from 8-31% to 78-99% while keeping false positives controllable. **First detection-time signature-filtering hardening layer for statistical LLM watermarks with MILP-learned signature sets and finite-sample guarantees in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(signature.*filter|watermark.*hardening|signature.*filtering.*watermark|signature.*robust)"` returning empty. 2026-06-24; discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-29.

- [[acthook-watermarking-llm-agent-trajectories-2602.18700]] — ActHook: Watermarking LLM Agent Trajectories (05-04) — first paper in the wiki to introduce *ActHook* — a watermarking method tailored for LLM agent trajectory datasets, inspired by hook mechanisms in software engineering, that embeds hook actions activated by a secret input key without altering the original task outcome; because LLM agents operate sequentially, hook actions can be inserted at decision points without disrupting task flow; when the activation key is present, an LLM agent trained on watermarked trajectories produces these hook actions at a significantly higher rate, enabling reliable black-box detection; on Qwen-2.5-Coder-7B, ActHook achieves an average detection AUC of 94.3 across mathematical reasoning, web searching, and software engineering agents with negligible performance degradation — surfacing *hook-action-as-copyright-protection-primitive for high-value agent trajectory datasets* as the load-bearing primitive when trajectory data is the product (not the model), distinct from text-output watermarks because the load-bearing axis is *decision-point-trigger-during-agent-execution* rather than *token-distribution-shift-at-output*, and from model-weight watermarks because the load-bearing axis is *trajectory-dataset-copyright-without-altering-task-outcome* rather than *weight-embedded-trigger-response* — plus *software-engineering-hook-mechanism-imported-as-agent-IP-primitive* as the load-bearing *domain-translation-primitive* importing the hook mechanism (a piece of code activated by a secret input key) from programming-language design into LLM-agent copyright protection, plus *sequential-decision-point-insertion-with-task-outcome-invariance-primitive* as the load-bearing *trajectory-invariance primitive* showing that hook actions can be inserted at sequential decision points without disrupting the original task outcome, enabling non-degrading trajectory watermarks. **First hook-action watermarking framework for LLM agent trajectory datasets with 94.3 detection AUC and zero task-outcome alteration in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(acthook|hook.*action.*watermark|agent.*trajectory.*watermark|hook.*mechanism.*llm)"` returning empty. 2026-05-04; discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-29.

- [[forgetmark-stealthy-fingerprint-embedding-via-targeted-unlearning-2601.08189]] — ForgetMark: Stealthy Fingerprint Embedding via Targeted Unlearning (01-20) — first paper in the wiki to introduce a *stealthy LLM fingerprinting framework* that encodes model-instance provenance via targeted unlearning rather than backdoor triggers — building a compact, human-readable key-value set with an assistant model and predictive-entropy ranking, then training lightweight LoRA adapters to *suppress* the original values on their keys while preserving general capabilities; by relying on probabilistic forgetting traces rather than fixed trigger-response patterns, ForgetMark avoids high-perplexity triggers, reduces heuristic-detector exposure, and lowers false triggers, achieving 100% ownership verification on fingerprinted models across diverse architectures while surpassing backdoor baselines in stealthiness and robustness to model merging and moderate incremental fine-tuning; surfacing *targeted-unlearning-as-stealthy-fingerprint-primitive* as the load-bearing provenance primitive when backdoor-style triggers are too easy to filter, distinct from trigger-response-backdoor-fingerprints because the load-bearing axis is *probabilistic-forgetting-traces-via-LoRA-suppression* rather than *fixed-trigger-response-pattern*, and from output-watermarks because the load-bearing axis is *model-instance-provenance* rather than *text-output-attribution* — plus *predictive-entropy-ranked-key-value-set-construction-primitive* as the load-bearing *fingerprint-set-construction primitive* building a compact, human-readable key-value set via assistant-model predictive-entropy ranking, surfacing *interpretability-preserving fingerprint construction* as a load-bearing design property, plus *likelihood-plus-semantic-evidence-aggregation-for-black-gray-box-ownership-verification-primitive* as the load-bearing *verification primitive* aggregating likelihood evidence (model is less likely to generate the value under the key) and semantic evidence (semantic similarity between generated and original value degrades on key prompts) into a fingerprint success rate, robust under black-box and gray-box access. **First targeted-unlearning LoRA fingerprint framework for LLM model-instance provenance with 100% ownership verification and robustness to model merging in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(forgetmark|forgetting.*fingerprint|targeted.unlearning.*fingerprint|stealthy.*fingerprint)"` returning empty. 2026-01-20; discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-29.

widens the discovery space differently than axis-inversion (which inverts one prior run's axes), axis-orthogonality (which picks axes orthogonal to prior runs), negative-result-probe (which deliberately probes known-difficult axes), adversarial-axis-probe (which surfaces papers from adversarial elicitation surfaces), meta-probe (which surfaces research-about-research papers), cross-discipline-probe (which bridges LLM-research with adjacent disciplines), application-domain-probe (which surfaces papers from vertical-industry-application surfaces), failure-mode-catalog (which systematically catalogs LLM failure modes), emerging-pretraining-objective-probe (which surfaces novel post-NTP pretraining objectives), multi-agent-consensus-probe (which surfaces papers from multi-agent consensus mechanisms), reward-model-geometry-probe (which probes reward-model structural and bounding properties), neuro-symbolic-grounding-probe (which bridges LLM representations with classical symbol-grounding frameworks), representation-engineering-probe (which directly intervenes on residual-stream activations), uncertainty-quantification-probe (which quantifies, decomposes, and routes LLM uncertainty), causal-inference-probe (which discovers, intervenes upon, and counterfactually-evaluates LLM causal structure), graph-structured-reasoning-probe (which organizes retrieval and reasoning over structured graph representations), and self-improvement-loop-probe (which surfaces papers from closed-loop iterative refinement of model-derived supervision) by deliberately surfacing papers from *test-time-compute-scaling surfaces* — primitives that govern how LLM inference allocates, routes, and aggregates additional computation at deployment to improve reasoning quality, decision accuracy, and budget efficiency; pivoting per Rule 27 from Run 85 agent-side-in-distribution-RL-critic-for-self-evolving-agents + closed-loop-SE-generalization-gap-theoretical-analysis-on-Knights-and-Knaves + VLM-dual-role-questioner-solver-with-quality-supervisor-and-skill-aware-dynamic-balancing (self-improvement-loop primitive-class triplet — agent-side-RL-critic + closed-loop-SE-theoretical-analysis + VLM-dual-role-closed-loop primitives) to Lagrangian-constrained-policy-optimization-for-test-time-compute-allocation + active-inference-grounded-difficulty-aware-routing-with-free-energy-fusion + propagation-based-delegation-aggregator-with-letter-entropy-and-reasoning-geometry (test-time-compute-scaling primitive-class triplet — constrained-policy-optimization-budget-allocation + active-inference-routing-with-free-energy-fusion + propagation-based-multi-sample-aggregation primitives, fully orthogonal to Run 85's self-improvement-loop axes, orthogonal to Run 84's graph-structured-reasoning axes, orthogonal to Run 83's causal-inference axes, orthogonal to Run 82's uncertainty-quantification axes, orthogonal to Run 81's representation-engineering axes, orthogonal to Run 80's neuro-symbolic-grounding axes, orthogonal to Run 79's reward-model-geometry axes, orthogonal to Run 78's multi-agent-consensus axes, orthogonal to Run 77's post-NTP-pretraining-objective axes, orthogonal to Run 76's failure-mode-catalog axes, orthogonal to Run 75's vertical-application axes, orthogonal to Run 74's cross-discipline axes). web_search 4-query escape hatch fired (32nd consecutive pitfall-83 — HF daily v2 returned 9+25=34 papers across 2026-06-28/29 dates with 20 fresh after 5-store dedup but ZERO pure test-time-compute-scaling primitives (all CV/3D/agents/eval); HF v3 href-regex pool 100% non-LLM-centric; web_search surfaced 17 fresh LLM candidates via the TEST-TIME-COMPUTE-SCALING-PROBE query template bracketing 4 test-time-compute-scaling axes: (a) test-time compute scaling laws + sequential inference-time reasoning + (b) parallel test-time compute via Best-of-N / verifier search + (c) MCTS / tree-of-thoughts / graph-of-thoughts inference-time search + (d) compute-optimal inference scaling / test-time-compute-efficiency — Rule 53 test-time-compute-scaling-probe WORKED, fresh-LLM return at typical base rate ~17 with full orthogonality to Run 85 self-improvement-loop picks). Three picks (date-DESC ordered by online_date):

- [[when-does-delegation-beat-majority-a-delegation-based-aggregator-for-multi-sample-llm-inference-2606.08098]] — When Does Delegation Beat Majority? A Delegation-Based Aggregator for Multi-Sample LLM Inference (06-11) — first paper in the wiki to show that **Propagational Proxy Voting (PPV)** — a *delegation-based unsupervised aggregator* — beats majority voting on MMLU-Pro by +1.5 pp overall and +2.24 pp on the non-trivial subset (paired McNemar p ≈ 1.0e-14, n = 8,099); Sakai, Yasushi; Song, Allen; Larson, Kent diagnose the structural failure of *majority voting* to exploit two signals every multi-sample inference carries — *within-group letter entropy* and *between-group reasoning geometry* — and propose PPV, a delegation-based aggregator that exposes two per-voter levers (`When` = how much weight a voter keeps on its own pick; `Whom` = how it splits the remainder across peers); PPV partitions 128 sampled generations into 16 groups per question, computes each group's letter-level semantic entropy and reasoning-embedding centroid, and feeds both into a stochastic delegation matrix whose stationary distribution selects the consensus answer — surfacing *propagation-based-multi-sample-aggregation-primitive* as the structurally correct primitive for unsupervised multi-sample aggregation when within-group semantic-uncertainty and between-group geometric-coherence are both binding signals, distinct from majority-voting because the load-bearing axis is *per-voter-When-Whom-delegation-rules-with-letter-entropy-and-reasoning-geometry* rather than *collapsed-majority-on-letter-frequency*, and from confidence-weighted-ensembling because the load-bearing axis is *stochastic-delegation-matrix-stationary-distribution* rather than *heuristic-confidence-weighting*; plus *overturns-clear-majority-when-majority-is-geometrically-incoherent-primitive* as the load-bearing *weak-majority-detection primitive* surfacing a worked example where PPV overturns a 10-6 majority for the wrong letter because the 10-voter majority cluster is geometrically incoherent (mean within-cluster cosine -0.02) while the 6-voter minority is tight (+0.26), so propagated delegation mass concentrates on the minority's answer — even though entropy alone would keep the majority ahead, plus *no-within-question-ensemble-of-confidence-modes-closes-oracle-gap-primitive* as the load-bearing *negative-result-design-space-constraint* reporting that no within-question ensemble of confidence modes closes the oracle gap, constraining the design space for unsupervised LLM aggregation. No gold labels and no auxiliary training required. **First propagation-based delegation aggregator for multi-sample LLM inference beating majority voting via letter-entropy + reasoning-geometry in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(propagational.proxy|delegation.*aggregator|majority.*delegation|ppv.*voting|delegation.*majority)"` returning empty. 2026-06-11; discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-29.

- [[adaptive-test-time-compute-allocation-for-reasoning-llms-via-constrained-policy-optimization-2604.14853]] — Adaptive Test-Time Compute Allocation for Reasoning LLMs via Constrained Policy Optimization (04-16) — first paper in the wiki to formalize *per-instance test-time compute allocation as a constrained optimization problem* (maximize expected accuracy subject to an average compute budget) and solve it with a **Solve-then-Learn** pipeline; Zhai, Zhiyuan; Li, Bingcong; Xiao, Bingnan; Li, Ming; Wang, Xin diagnose the structural limitation of *uniform compute schedules* (costly, hard to attribute, can trigger overthinking with diminishing returns) and propose (i) a *solve stage* using Lagrangian relaxation that decomposes the global constraint into per-instance sub-problems admitting closed-form oracle actions that optimally price accuracy against cost, (ii) a *learn stage* that trains a lightweight classifier to predict oracle actions from cheap input features, amortizing the allocation rule for real-time deployment; the induced cost is monotone in the dual variable, enabling exact budget targeting via binary search — surfacing *constrained-policy-optimization-test-time-compute-allocation-primitive* as the structurally correct primitive for compute-optimal inference under explicit budgets, distinct from uniform-sampling-allocation because the load-bearing axis is *Lagrangian-relaxation-per-instance-closed-form-oracle-actions* rather than *homogeneous-budget-distribution*, and from heuristic-difficulty-routing because the load-bearing axis is *dual-variable-monotone-cost-exact-budget-targeting-via-binary-search* rather than *learned-classifier-routing*; plus *task-level-regret-bound-by-imitation-error-times-worst-case-per-instance-gap-primitive* as the load-bearing *theoretical-guarantee primitive* yielding a clean reduction from constrained inference to supervised classification, and *Solve-then-Learn-amortization-primitive* as the load-bearing *engineering-deployment primitive* achieving 12.8% relative accuracy improvement on MATH under matched budget, 91%+ imitation accuracy to the Lagrangian oracle, and consistent gains across DeepSeek-V3, GPT-4o-mini, and Qwen2.5-7B. **First constrained-policy-optimization test-time-compute allocation with Solve-then-Learn amortization and Lagrangian-relaxation-exact-budget-targeting in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(solve.then.learn|constrained.policy.*test.time|lagrangian.*allocation|test.time.compute.*allocation)"` returning empty. 2026-04-16; discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-29.

- [[odar-principled-adaptive-routing-for-llm-reasoning-via-active-inference-2602.23681]] — ODAR: Principled Adaptive Routing for LLM Reasoning via Active Inference (02-27) — first paper in the wiki to introduce **ODAR-Expert**, an adaptive routing framework grounded in *amortized active inference* that replaces uniform brute-force test-time compute (fixed Best-of-N, self-consistency) with difficulty-aware routing between a heuristic Fast Agent and a deliberative Slow Agent; Ma, Siyuan; Gao, Bo; Jia, Xiaojun; Qin, Simeng; Li, Tianlin; Ma, Ke; Jia, Xiaoshuang; Ren, Wenqi; Liu, Yang diagnose the structural failure of *uniform brute-force sampling* (costly, hard to attribute, can trigger overthinking with diminishing returns) and propose (i) a *difficulty estimator* grounded in amortized active inference that routes easy queries to the Fast Agent and hard queries to the Slow Agent, (ii) a *free-energy-principled fusion mechanism* that selects answers by minimizing a variational free energy objective, balancing log-likelihood with epistemic uncertainty (varentropy) as a principled alternative to ad hoc voting over heterogeneous candidates; surfacing *active-inference-grounded-difficulty-aware-routing-with-free-energy-fusion-primitive* as the structurally correct primitive for test-time-compute allocation when difficulty-estimation must be principled rather than heuristic, distinct from constrained-policy-optimization-allocation because the load-bearing axis is *Bayesian-active-inference-difficulty-estimator* rather than *Lagrangian-relaxation-closed-form-oracle*, and from learned-classifier-difficulty-routing because the load-bearing axis is *variational-free-energy-minimization-with-epistemic-uncertainty-varentropy* rather than *imitation-of-oracle-actions*; plus *risk-sensitive-variational-free-energy-fusion-primitive* as the load-bearing *answer-aggregation primitive* replacing ad hoc voting with minimization of variational free energy that explicitly balances log-likelihood and epistemic uncertainty, plus *compute-accuracy-frontier-improvement-primitive* as the load-bearing *engineering primitive* achieving 98.2% on MATH and 54.8% on Humanity's Last Exam while reducing computational cost by 82% on a fully open-source Llama 4 + DeepSeek stack, validated across 23 benchmarks. **First active-inference-grounded difficulty-aware routing framework with free-energy-principled fusion replacing uniform brute-force test-time compute in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(odar|active.inference.*routing|free.energy.*fusion|active.inference.*llm|amortized.active.inference)"` returning empty. 2026-02-27; discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-29.
- [[q-evolve-self-evolving-llm-agents-with-in-distribution-optimization-2606.07367]] — Q-Evolve: Self-Evolving LLM Agents with In-Distribution Optimization: introduces a self-evolving framework for LLM agents that unifies automatic process-reward labeling and policy learning inside a single in-distribution reinforcement learning loop, eliminating the distribution shift that destabilizes prior self-evolving-agent pipelines; Zhang, Fang, Chen learn an in-distribution critic from a hybrid off-policy dataset (expert demos + agent trajectories) using weighted Implicit Q-Learning to stabilize Bellman backups under sparse-reward long-horizon agent settings, then derive step-wise dense process rewards from the critic via advantage estimation and apply behavior-proximal policy optimization on the same trajectories used for labeling; 2026-06-05 (cs.CL); discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-29 (self-improvement-loop / agent-self-evolution / in-distribution-RL-critic / process-reward-labeling theme — Rule 52 SELF-IMPROVEMENT-LOOP-PROBE). Complements the actor-critic-online-data-mixing and generative-verifier-RL entries on this page by surfacing the *agent-side* counterpart: where AC-ODM isolates the actor-critic split for LLM pretraining and MaxProof scales single-step proof generation with a generative verifier, Q-Evolve extends both into the long-horizon agent loop where sparse-reward credit assignment is the load-bearing primitive.

- [[on-the-generalization-gap-in-self-evolving-language-model-reasoning-2606.01075]] — On the Generalization Gap in Self-Evolving Language Model Reasoning: asks the principled question under a strict closed-loop self-evolution setup — with only an unlabeled prompt set and a base model, no oracle, no human feedback, no external reward — how close can internally generated supervision come to oracle-supervised training; Qi, Baby, Baby analyze four representative SE strategies (single-round verification, multi-turn revision with feedback, iterative training, curriculum learning) in a unified offline framework on Knights and Knaves logical reasoning, finding that all four improve over the base but plateau at different points and leave a non-trivial gap to oracle supervision, with multi-turn critic-revision + large models (Gemma 12B) nearly matching oracle on Knights and Knaves but transferring only modestly to real-world reasoning benchmarks; 2026-06-02 (cs.CL); discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-29 (self-improvement-loop / closed-loop-SE / generalization-gap / offline-reasoning theme — Rule 52 SELF-IMPROVEMENT-LOOP-PROBE). Complements the LLM-reasoning-failures survey and the periodic-table-of-LLM-reasoning survey on this page by providing the *theoretical-empirical boundary condition* on closed-loop self-evolution: where reasoning failures catalog what self-evolution cannot recover from, this paper characterizes the plateau and generalization gap of the closed-loop SE primitive itself.

- [[rise-reliable-improvement-in-self-evolving-vision-language-models-2605.20914]] — RISE: Reliable Improvement in Self-Evolving Vision-Language Models: diagnoses three coupled failure modes in prior VLM self-evolving pipelines — coarse-grained role alternation, progressive question-quality degradation, and mode collapse toward a narrow question distribution — and proposes a reliable self-evolving framework with fine-grained role alternation, a learned quality supervisor for pseudo-label reliability, and skill-aware dynamic balancing for mode-collapse mitigation; Xu, Miao, Zhang show that unlabeled-image self-evolution can match the gains of human-supervised VLM post-training when the closed-loop primitives keep question generation and solver adaptation tightly coupled; 2026-05-26 (cs.CV); discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-29 (self-improvement-loop / VLM-dual-role / closed-loop-questioner-solver / mode-collapse-mitigation theme — Rule 52 SELF-IMPROVEMENT-LOOP-PROBE). Complements the density-ridge-selective-prediction and pathology-VLM-structured-reasoning entries on this page by providing the *multimodal self-evolution primitive* — where the density-ridge entry surfaces out-of-distribution VLM outputs and pathology-VLM structures reasoning for a single vertical, RISE prevents the closed-loop from producing those failures in the first place via fine-grained role alternation + skill-aware question balancing.
- **(2026-06-29 — Run 84) — 3-axis DOMAIN-DIVERSITY PIVOT to GRAPH-STRUCTURED-REASONING-PROBE per Rule 51** introducing **graph-structured-reasoning primitive** (after Rule 36d axis-inversion + Rule 36e temporal-anchor + Rule 37 axis-orthogonality + Rule 38 negative-result-probe + Rule 39 adversarial-axis-probe + Rule 40 meta-probe + Rule 41 cross-discipline-probe + Rule 42 application-domain-probe + Rule 43 failure-mode-catalog + Rule 44 emerging-pretraining-objective-probe + Rule 45 multi-agent-consensus-probe + Rule 46 reward-model-geometry-probe + Rule 47 neuro-symbolic-grounding-probe + Rule 48 representation-engineering-probe + Rule 49 uncertainty-quantification-probe + Rule 50 causal-inference-probe have all been deployed in sequence, Run 84 introduces a GRAPH-STRUCTURED-REASONING-PROBE — pick axes that explicitly probe *graph-structured-reasoning primitives for LLM* (knowledge-graph reasoning, KG-grounded embedding-space retrieval, hierarchical KG construction for RAG, KG-grounded VLM training, multi-path logical reasoning via neuro-symbolic generation, declarative reasoning via answer-set-programming, scene-graph/temporal-graph/heterogeneous-graph integration); widens the discovery space differently than axis-inversion (which inverts one prior run's axes), axis-orthogonality (which picks axes orthogonal to prior runs), negative-result-probe (which deliberately probes known-difficult axes), adversarial-axis-probe (which surfaces papers from adversarial elicitation surfaces), meta-probe (which surfaces research-about-research papers), cross-discipline-probe (which bridges LLM-research with adjacent disciplines), application-domain-probe (which surfaces papers from vertical-industry-application surfaces), failure-mode-catalog (which systematically catalogs LLM failure modes), emerging-pretraining-objective-probe (which surfaces novel post-NTP pretraining objectives), multi-agent-consensus-probe (which surfaces papers from multi-agent consensus mechanisms), reward-model-geometry-probe (which probes reward-model structural and bounding properties), neuro-symbolic-grounding-probe (which bridges LLM representations with classical symbol-grounding frameworks), representation-engineering-probe (which directly intervenes on residual-stream activations), uncertainty-quantification-probe (which quantifies, decomposes, and routes LLM uncertainty), and causal-inference-probe (which discovers, intervenes upon, and counterfactually-evaluates LLM causal structure) by deliberately surfacing papers from *graph-structured-reasoning surfaces* — primitives that organize, retrieve, and reason over structured graph representations (knowledge graphs, scene graphs, hierarchical graphs, embedding-space KG reasoning) to ground LLM inference in symbolic structure; pivoting per Rule 27 from Run 83 SCM-on-CoT-units-as-local-causal-attribution + universal-causal-reasoner-data-generation-across-Pearl-Ladder + meta-causal-framework-for-LLM-development (causal-inference primitive-class triplet — representation-level-causal-attribution + training-data-causal-reasoner + meta-causal-evaluation-framework primitives) to KG-grounded-embedding-space-reasoning-rule-rerank + graph-native-AI-systems-tutorial-synergies + KG-grounded-VLM-Entity-Reward-mechanism (graph-structured-reasoning primitive-class triplet — KGQA-embedding-rule-application + survey-level-integration-framework + KG-grounded-RL-trained-VLM primitives, fully orthogonal to Run 83's causal-inference axes, orthogonal to Run 82's uncertainty-quantification axes, orthogonal to Run 81's representation-engineering axes, orthogonal to Run 80's neuro-symbolic-grounding axes, orthogonal to Run 79's reward-model-geometry axes, orthogonal to Run 78's multi-agent-consensus axes, orthogonal to Run 77's post-NTP-pretraining-objective axes, orthogonal to Run 76's failure-mode-catalog axes, orthogonal to Run 75's vertical-application axes). web_search 4-query escape hatch fired (30th consecutive pitfall-83 — HF daily v2 data-props returned 25+9=34 papers across 2026-06-28/29 with only 15 fresh LLM-keyword-matched candidates, most borderline or non-LLM; web_search surfaced 15 fresh LLM candidates via the GRAPH-STRUCTURED-REASONING-PROBE query template bracketing 4 graph-structured-reasoning axes: (a) knowledge-graph LLM reasoning retrieval-augmented GNN integration + (b) graph-of-thoughts tree-of-thoughts structured graph reasoning + (c) scene-graph temporal-graph heterogeneous-graph LLM + (d) knowledge-graph reasoning pathology hallucination factual — Rule 51 graph-structured-reasoning-probe WORKED, fresh-LLM return at typical base rate ~15 with full orthogonality to Run 83 causal-inference picks). Three picks (date-DESC ordered by online_date):

- [[llms-graphs-toward-graph-native-synergistic-ai-systems-2606.11560]] — LLMs+Graphs: Toward Graph-Native, Synergistic AI Systems (06-10) — first tutorial-style survey in the wiki to synthesize the algorithms, systems, and design principles driving the convergence of LLMs, graph data management, graph mining, graph ML, and agentic computation into next-generation *graph-native* AI systems; the authors identify three complementary synergies — (i) LLMs augmented with graph computation for retrieval and reasoning, (ii) bidirectional integration where LLMs support KG construction/curation and KGs enforce semantic constraints and factual consistency, (iii) AI agents strengthened by graph algorithms for planning, decision making, and multi-step reasoning — and the new graph-ML capabilities that LLMs unlock via natural-language interfaces and hybrid LLM-graph-neural-network pipelines; surfacing *three-synergies-LLM-graph-integration-framework-primitive* as the structurally correct primitive for graph-structured-reasoning research, distinct from LLM-only-reasoning because the load-bearing axis is *bidirectional-LLM-KG-co-construction-and-factuality-enforcement* rather than *single-direction-LLM-over-text* — and *graph-data-management-via-NL-interface-primitive* as the load-bearing *graph-ML primitive* enabling LLMs to introduce qualitatively new capabilities in graph data management and graph machine learning, plus *unified-perspective-LLMs-graphs-graph-ML-agents-primitive* as the load-bearing *integration-framework primitive* offering data-science and data-mining researchers a unified perspective on integrating LLMs, graph data management, graph mining, graph ML, and agentic computation into graph-native AI systems. **First tutorial-style survey synthesizing LLM+graph convergence into three-synergies graph-native AI systems framework in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(llms.*graphs.*synergistic|graph.native.*ai|llms.*graphs.*tutorial|llm.*kg.*synergies)"` returning empty. 2026-06-10; discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-29.

- [[multi-hop-reasoning-and-retrieval-in-embedding-space-embrag-2603.13266]] — Multi-hop Reasoning and Retrieval in Embedding Space (EMBRAG) (02-25) — first embedding-space reasoning framework in the wiki that addresses two structural failures of LLM-based KGQA — KG-understanding-induced query misinterpretation and KG-incompleteness-induced retrieval failures — by generating *multiple logical rules grounded in a knowledge graph, applying them in the embedding space guided by the KG, and reranking the resulting trajectories*; the authors propose EMBRAG, a *three-stage pipeline* (rule generation → embedding-space rule application → reranking) that bypasses the brittleness of pure-symbolic KG traversal over noisy/incomplete KGs by keeping reasoning inside the embedding space but conditioning the rules on KG-grounded logical forms; surfacing *KG-grounded-embedding-space-rule-application-primitive* as the structurally correct primitive for robust KGQA when KG-understanding and KG-incompleteness are both binding constraints, distinct from pure-symbolic-KG-traversal because the load-bearing axis is *KG-grounded-rule-conditioned-embedding-reasoning* rather than *ungrounded-symbolic-walk* — and *multi-rule-embedding-reranking-primitive* as the load-bearing *disambiguation primitive* generating multiple candidate logical rules to handle query-interpretation ambiguity, then applying each in embedding space to handle KG incompleteness via semantic neighborhood, plus *rule-trajectory-reranker-primitive* as the load-bearing *answer-selection primitive* decoupling symbolic-rule generation from embedding-space reasoning and answer selection, making the system robust to low-quality rules. State-of-the-art on two benchmark KGQA datasets. **First KG-grounded multi-rule embedding-space reasoning framework with reranker for robust KGQA under query-ambiguity and KG-incompleteness in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(embrag|multi.hop.*embedding|kgqa.*embedding|embedding.*rule.*reasoning)"` returning empty. 2026-02-25; discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-29.

- [[pathreasoner-r1-instilling-structured-reasoning-into-pathology-vision-language-models-2601.21617]] — PathReasoner-R1: Instilling Structured Reasoning into Pathology Vision-Language Models (01-29) — first large-scale whole-slide-image reasoning dataset (PathReasoner) and a KG-grounded RL-trained VLM (PathReasoner-R1) in the wiki that achieves *verifiable evidence-linked reasoning* for computational pathology; the authors build the 20K+ PathReasoner dataset via a *rigorous knowledge-guided generation pipeline* leveraging medical knowledge graphs to align structured pathological findings and clinical reasoning with diagnoses, then propose PathReasoner-R1 which synergizes *trajectory-masked supervised fine-tuning* with *reasoning-oriented reinforcement learning* and an *Entity Reward mechanism strictly aligned with knowledge graphs* that effectively guides the model to optimize for logical consistency rather than mere outcome matching, enhancing robustness; surfacing *KG-grounded-Entity-Reward-mechanism-primitive* as the structurally correct primitive for verifiable clinical VLM training, distinct from outcome-matching-reward because the load-bearing axis is *logical-consistency-via-KG-aligned-entity-coverage* rather than *answer-correctness-via-string-match* — and *trajectory-masked-SFT-plus-reasoning-RL-primitive* as the load-bearing *training-paradigm primitive* combining trajectory-level supervised fine-tuning with reasoning-oriented reinforcement learning to instill structured CoT capabilities, plus *knowledge-guided-dataset-generation-primitive* as the load-bearing *data-quality primitive* generating 20K+ high-quality instructional samples by aligning structured pathological findings with clinical reasoning via KG grounding rather than unverified distillation. State-of-the-art on PathReasoner + public benchmarks across various image scales. **First KG-grounded Entity-Reward RL-trained pathology VLM with verifiable evidence-linked reasoning in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(pathreasoner|pathology.*structured.reasoning|kg.grounded.reward.*vlm|entity.reward.*pathology)"` returning empty. 2026-01-29; discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-29.
- **(2026-06-29 — Run 83) — 3-axis DOMAIN-DIVERSITY PIVOT to CAUSAL-INFERENCE-PROBE per Rule 50** introducing **causal-inference primitive** (after Rule 36d axis-inversion + Rule 36e temporal-anchor + Rule 37 axis-orthogonality + Rule 38 negative-result-probe + Rule 39 adversarial-axis-probe + Rule 40 meta-probe + Rule 41 cross-discipline-probe + Rule 42 application-domain-probe + Rule 43 failure-mode-catalog + Rule 44 emerging-pretraining-objective-probe + Rule 45 multi-agent-consensus-probe + Rule 46 reward-model-geometry-probe + Rule 47 neuro-symbolic-grounding-probe + Rule 48 representation-engineering-probe + Rule 49 uncertainty-quantification-probe have all been deployed in sequence, Run 83 introduces a CAUSAL-INFERENCE-PROBE — pick axes that explicitly probe *causal-inference primitives for LLM* (structural causal models on CoT units, universal causal-reasoner data generation across Pearl's Causal Ladder, meta-causal-framework for LLM development and evaluation); widens the discovery space differently than axis-inversion (which inverts one prior run's axes), axis-orthogonality (which picks axes orthogonal to prior runs), negative-result-probe (which deliberately probes known-difficult axes), adversarial-axis-probe (which surfaces papers from adversarial elicitation surfaces), meta-probe (which surfaces research-about-research papers), cross-discipline-probe (which bridges LLM-research with adjacent disciplines), application-domain-probe (which surfaces papers from vertical-industry-application surfaces), failure-mode-catalog (which systematically catalogs LLM failure modes), emerging-pretraining-objective-probe (which surfaces novel post-NTP pretraining objectives), multi-agent-consensus-probe (which surfaces papers from multi-agent consensus mechanisms), reward-model-geometry-probe (which probes reward-model structural and bounding properties), neuro-symbolic-grounding-probe (which bridges LLM representations with classical symbol-grounding frameworks), representation-engineering-probe (which directly intervenes on residual-stream activations), and uncertainty-quantification-probe (which quantifies, decomposes, and routes LLM uncertainty) by deliberately surfacing papers from *causal-inference surfaces* — primitives that discover, intervene upon, and counterfactually-evaluate LLM causal structure, both at the representation level (local CoT-unit causal attribution) and at the development/evaluation pipeline level (causal methods as the principled framework replacing purely-predictive approaches); pivoting per Rule 27 from Run 82 three-component-semantic-uncertainty-decomposition + kinematic-feature-density-ridge-selective-prediction + intra-layer-cross-layer-agreement-UQ (uncertainty-quantification primitive-class triplet — semantic-component-attribution + manifold-density-ridge-recovery + representation-grounded-cross-layer-agreement primitives) to local-CoT-unit-causal-attribution-via-SCM + universal-causal-reasoner-data-generation + meta-causal-framework-for-LLM-development (causal-inference primitive-class triplet — representation-level-causal-attribution + training-data-causal-reasoner + meta-causal-evaluation-framework primitives, fully orthogonal to Run 82's uncertainty-quantification axes, orthogonal to Run 81's representation-engineering axes, orthogonal to Run 80's neuro-symbolic-grounding axes, orthogonal to Run 79's reward-model-geometry axes, orthogonal to Run 78's multi-agent-consensus axes, orthogonal to Run 77's post-NTP-pretraining-objective axes). web_search 4-query escape hatch fired (29th consecutive pitfall-83 — HF daily v2 data-props returned 0 on /papers/date/2026-06-29 and /papers/date/2026-06-28, v3 href-regex returned 34 papers across both dates with 17 LLM-keyword-matched fresh candidates but most non-LLM; the 4-query CAUSAL-INFERENCE-PROBE web_search template bracketed 4 causal-inference axes: (a) causal inference LLM counterfactual reasoning SCM + (b) structural causal model LLM treatment effect intervention estimation + (c) causal mediation analysis LLM chain-of-thought causal attribution + (d) causal methods for LLM development evaluation pipeline — Rule 50 causal-inference-probe WORKED, fresh-LLM return at typical base rate ~14 candidates with full orthogonality to Run 82 uncertainty-quantification picks). Three picks (date-DESC ordered by online_date):

- [[local-causal-attribution-of-chain-of-thought-reasoning-2606.21821]] — Local Causal Attribution of Chain-of-Thought Reasoning (06-20) — first paper in the wiki to introduce **AttriCoT**, a *black-box local causal-attribution algorithm* for LLM chain-of-thought reasoning that constructs a *structural causal model (SCM) on the individual "units" of a single CoT trace*, relates each unit to the log probability of generating subsequent output units, and estimates importance parameters in O(U) forward passes; Wei, Dennis; Belkhiter, Yannis; Miehling, Erik; Marinescu, Radu diagnose the structural limitation of *global feature-attribution methods* and *probing-based interpretability* when applied to a specific CoT trace, and propose a *local* approach toward understanding the causal structure of a language model's thought process by analyzing the causal relationships among individual units of a *given, specific* CoT trace; the SCM-on-units formulation yields importance parameters that quantify how each unit causally contributes to subsequent generation, evaluated via perturbation curves across 5 datasets and 4 reasoning models where AttriCoT produces attributions *more faithful to the model's behavior* than alternative methods and reveals notable differences in thought structure between models and domains; surfacing *SCM-on-CoT-units-as-local-causal-attribution-primitive* as the structurally correct primitive for trace-level causal interpretability research, distinct from global-feature-attribution (which aggregates across inputs) because the load-bearing axis is *per-trace-SCM-with-unit-as-causal-node* rather than *aggregate-feature-importance* — and *O(U)-forward-pass-complexity-primitive* as the load-bearing *computational-tractability primitive* making per-trace attribution linear in the number of CoT units, plus *black-box-access-only-primitive* as the load-bearing *deployment-flexibility primitive* requiring only log-probability access with no internal activations or gradients, enabling attribution on closed-weight models. **First local causal-attribution framework via structural causal models on individual chain-of-thought units with O(U) forward passes and black-box access in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(attricot|local.causal.attribution|scm.*cot|structural.causal.*chain|causal.attribution.*thought)"` returning empty. 2026-06-20; discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-29.

- [[causal-methods-for-llm-development-and-evaluation-2605.25998]] — Causal Methods for LLM Development and Evaluation (05-25) — first paper in the wiki to make the *position-paper case* that many central questions in LLM development and evaluation are inherently causal — "What is the effect of adding a data domain during pretraining? How do annotator preferences change when LLMs generate text in a different style? Should a prompt be routed to a larger or smaller model given inference cost constraints?" — and argue that purely predictive approaches are fragile under three structural conditions of LLM pipelines: *logged-data confounding*, *biased learned judges*, and *non-stationary deployment environments*; Frauen, Dennis; Brockschmidt, Marie; Hess, Konstantin; Ma, Haorui; Ma, Yuchen map opportunities for causal methods across the entire LLM development pipeline (pretraining, alignment, routing, agentic workflows, evaluation) and argue that causal identification and estimation methods should replace or augment purely predictive methods in these settings; surfacing *meta-causal-framework-for-LLM-pipeline-design-primitive* as the structurally correct primitive for principled LLM development research, distinct from predictive-A/B-evaluation because the load-bearing axis is *interventional-causal-identification-under-confounding-and-distribution-shift* rather than *correlational-outcome-comparison* — and *three-structural-fragility-conditions-as-causal-intervention-motivation-primitive* as the load-bearing *diagnostic-primitive* arguing that *confounded-logged-data*, *biased-learned-judges*, and *non-stationary-deployment* are the three classes of conditions where predictive methods are fragile and causal methods provide principled remedies, plus *causal-opportunity-mapping-across-pretraining-alignment-routing-agents-eval-primitive* as the load-bearing *pipeline-coverage-primitive* offering a unified framework that identifies where causal methods can replace purely-predictive methods across the entire LLM development stack. **First systematic position paper arguing that LLM development/evaluation questions are inherently causal and mapping causal-method opportunities across pretraining/alignment/routing/agentic/evaluation in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(causal.methods.*llm|causal.llm.development|causal.llm.evaluation|interventional.llm|causal.framework.*llm)"` returning empty. 2026-05-25; discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-29.

- [[towards-a-universal-causal-reasoner-unico-2605.24873]] — Towards a Universal Causal Reasoner (UniCo) (05-24) — first paper in the wiki to introduce a *data-generation framework that addresses all 18 causal query types across Pearl's Causal Ladder* (association, intervention, counterfactual) and translates natively-symbolic causal examples into code and natural language forms to simulate real-world use cases where causal terms are not explicitly specified; Dai, Qirun; Liu, Xiao; Zhang, Jiawei; Zhang, Dylan; Peng, Hao propose UniCo which filters reasoning shortcuts by grounding answers with exact causal inference, then supervised-finetune Qwen3-4B, Qwen3-8B and Olmo-3-7B-Instruct on 66.6K UniCo-generated instances achieving 22.9% improvements across all 18 in-distribution query types, 8.1% over state-of-the-art causal data-generation frameworks on 7 out-of-distribution causal benchmarks, and 20.2% faithfulness improvement on real-world medical / legal / tabular reasoning; surfacing *universal-causal-reasoner-data-generation-across-Pearl-ladder-primitive* as the structurally correct primitive for training generalizable causal reasoners, distinct from per-aspect-causal-benchmarks (which only evaluate specific causal capabilities) because the load-bearing axis is *full-Pearl-ladder-coverage-with-symbolic-to-natural-translation-as-realism-bridge* rather than *single-aspect-evaluation* — and *reasoning-shortcut-filtering-via-exact-causal-inference-grounding-primitive* as the load-bearing *data-quality-primitive* rejecting paths that produce correct outputs via non-causal heuristics by requiring exact causal-inference grounding for every generated instance, plus *cross-stage-faithfulness-transfer-primitive* as the load-bearing *transfer-primitive* showing that causality-centered training not only strengthens causal reasoning but equips LLMs with a "causal mindset" in general reasoning tasks — surfacing a deep generalization property: causal SFT improves non-causal reasoning faithfulness. **First data-generation framework covering all 18 causal query types across Pearl's Causal Ladder with reasoning-shortcut filtering for training universal causal reasoners in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(unico|universal.causal|pearl.ladder.*training|causal.reasoner.*data)"` returning empty. 2026-05-24; discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-29.

- **(2026-06-29 — Run 82) — 3-axis DOMAIN-DIVERSITY PIVOT to UNCERTAINTY-QUANTIFICATION-PROBE per Rule 49** introducing **uncertainty quantification primitive** (after Rule 36d axis-inversion + Rule 36e temporal-anchor + Rule 37 axis-orthogonality + Rule 38 negative-result-probe + Rule 39 adversarial-axis-probe + Rule 40 meta-probe + Rule 41 cross-discipline-probe + Rule 42 application-domain-probe + Rule 43 failure-mode-catalog + Rule 44 emerging-pretraining-objective-probe + Rule 45 multi-agent-consensus-probe + Rule 46 reward-model-geometry-probe + Rule 47 neuro-symbolic-grounding-probe + Rule 48 representation-engineering-probe have all been deployed in sequence, Run 82 introduces an UNCERTAINTY-QUANTIFICATION-PROBE — pick axes that probe *explicit uncertainty-quantification primitives for LLM* (semantic decomposition, selective prediction with density-ridge geometry, cross-layer information-score UQ, conformal prediction, evidential deep learning, epistemic-aleatoric separation, calibration); widens the discovery space differently than axis-inversion (which inverts one prior run's axes), axis-orthogonality (which picks axes orthogonal to prior runs), negative-result-probe (which deliberately probes known-difficult axes), adversarial-axis-probe (which surfaces papers from adversarial elicitation surfaces), meta-probe (which surfaces research-about-research papers), cross-discipline-probe (which bridges LLM-research with adjacent disciplines), application-domain-probe (which surfaces papers from vertical-industry-application surfaces), failure-mode-catalog (which systematically catalogs LLM failure modes), emerging-pretraining-objective-probe (which surfaces novel post-NTP pretraining objectives), multi-agent-consensus-probe (which surfaces papers from multi-agent consensus mechanisms), reward-model-geometry-probe (which probes reward-model structural and bounding properties), neuro-symbolic-grounding-probe (which bridges LLM representations with classical symbol-grounding frameworks), and representation-engineering-probe (which directly intervenes on residual-stream activations) by deliberately surfacing papers from *uncertainty-quantification surfaces* — primitives that quantify, decompose, and route LLM uncertainty to support hallucination detection, selective prediction, calibration, and trust evaluation; pivoting per Rule 27 from Run 81 superposition-aware-random-projection-steering + cross-layer-consistency-evolutionary-steering-refinement + white-box-black-box-surjectivity-separation-theorem (representation-engineering primitive-class triplet — projection-space-discriminative-recovery + cross-layer-geometry-consistency + intervention-class-separation primitives) to three-component-semantic-uncertainty-decomposition + kinematic-feature-density-ridge-selective-prediction + intra-layer-cross-layer-agreement-UQ (uncertainty-quantification primitive-class triplet — semantic-component-attribution + manifold-density-ridge-recovery + representation-grounded-cross-layer-agreement primitives, fully orthogonal to Run 81's representation-engineering axes, orthogonal to Run 80's neuro-symbolic-grounding axes, orthogonal to Run 79's reward-model-geometry axes, orthogonal to Run 78's multi-agent-consensus axes, orthogonal to Run 77's post-NTP-pretraining-objective axes, orthogonal to Run 76's failure-mode-catalog axes, orthogonal to Run 75's vertical-application axes, orthogonal to Run 74's cross-discipline axes). web_search 4-query escape hatch fired (28th consecutive pitfall-83 — HF daily v2 data-props returned 0 on /papers/date/2026-06-29 and /papers/date/2026-06-28, v3 href-regex returned 0 matches on both pages; HF monthly already in prior runs; web_search surfaced 15 fresh LLM candidates via the UNCERTAINTY-QUANTIFICATION-PROBE query template bracketing 4 UQ axes: (a) calibration + selective prediction via conformal/decision-theoretic scoring + (b) intra-layer cross-layer information-score UQ + (c) semantic-entropy epistemic-aleatoric decomposition + (d) evidential deep learning + conformal-RAG — Rule 49 uncertainty-quantification-probe WORKED, fresh-LLM return at typical base rate ~15 with full orthogonality to Run 81 representation-engineering picks). Three picks (date-DESC ordered by online_date):

- [[density-ridge-selective-prediction-for-llm-and-vlm-hallucination-detection-via-kinematic-feature-density-of-hidden-state-trajectories-2606.10198]] — Density Ridge Selective Prediction for LLM and VLM Hallucination Detection via Kinematic Feature Density of Hidden State Trajectories (06-10) — first paper in the wiki to introduce a *density-ridge recovery of the LLM response manifold* via a six-dimensional kinematic feature map of hidden-state generation trajectories; Shamsi, Nina I. addresses the structural failure of both unsupervised Semantic-Entropy detectors (label-free but plateau in quality) and supervised probes (in-distribution-strong but degrade sharply when calibration labels are scarce) by recovering the response manifold of an LLM as the *density ridge* of a kernel density estimate built on the kinematic-feature map of hidden-state trajectories — surfacing *response-manifold-as-density-ridge-primitive* as the structurally correct primitive for selective prediction when both label-scarcity (supervised-probe failure) and semantic-clustering-plateau (Semantic-Entropy failure) are present, distinct from supervised-probe-selective-prediction because the load-bearing axis is *unsupervised-yet-rigorous-ridge-estimation* rather than *label-driven-probing*, and from semantic-entropy-clustering because the load-bearing axis is *manifold-geometry-via-density-ridge* rather than *token-semantic-clustering*; plus *six-dimensional-kinematic-feature-map-primitive* as the load-bearing *feature-engineering primitive* capturing the geometric structure of generation trajectories in a compact 6D space, and *kinematic-feature-density-ridge-as-modality-agnostic-primitive* as the load-bearing *cross-modal primitive* enabling unified hallucination detection in both LLMs and VLMs via the same ridge-estimation framework. **First density-ridge-based selective prediction framework using kinematic features of hidden-state trajectories for hallucination detection in LLMs and VLMs in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(density.ridge|kinematic.feature|ridge.selective|ridge.hallucination)"` returning empty. 2026-06-10; discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-29.

- [[the-anatomy-of-uncertainty-in-llms-a-three-component-semantic-framework-for-input-ambiguity-knowledge-gaps-and-decoding-randomness-2603.24967]] — The Anatomy of Uncertainty in LLMs: A Three-Component Semantic Framework for Input Ambiguity, Knowledge Gaps, and Decoding Randomness (03-26) — first paper in the wiki to introduce a *three-component semantic decomposition of LLM uncertainty* that disambiguates causally-distinct sources of unreliable generation; Taparia, Aditya; Senanayake, Ransalu; Thopalli, Kowshik; Narayanaswamy, Vivek diagnose the structural failure of single-score uncertainty methods and the classical aleatoric-epistemic dichotomy to offer actionable insights — and propose decomposing LLM uncertainty into (i) input ambiguity (irreducible, from genuinely-ambiguous inputs), (ii) knowledge gaps (reducible via data, from missing/stale training), (iii) decoding randomness (reducible via inference-time control, from sampling-induced variance) — surfacing *three-component-semantic-uncertainty-decomposition-primitive* as the structurally correct primitive for actionable UQ research, distinct from classical-aleatoric-epistemic-dichotomy because the load-bearing axis is *causally-distinct-semantic-components* rather than *reducibility-classes*, and from single-score-uncertainty because the load-bearing axis is *per-axis-measurement-via-targeted-interventions* rather than *collapsed-scalar-aggregation*; plus *targeted-intervention-methodology-primitive* as the load-bearing *measurement primitive* where each component is measured via a distinct intervention (input perturbation / retrieval-augmentation / sampling-temperature), and *three-axis-remediation-primitive* as the load-bearing *remediation primitive* enabling principled per-axis fix (improve inputs / expand training data / tune decoding) instead of blind-model-retraining. **First three-component semantic-decomposition framework for LLM uncertainty disambiguating input ambiguity, knowledge gaps, and decoding randomness in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(anatomy.uncertainty|three.component.*uncertainty|input.ambiguity.*uncertainty|decoding.randomness.*uncertainty)"` returning empty. 2026-03-26; discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-29.

- [[between-the-layers-lies-the-truth-uncertainty-estimation-via-intra-layer-local-information-scores-2603.22299]] — Between the Layers Lies the Truth: Uncertainty Estimation via Intra-Layer Local Information Scores (03-17) — first paper in the wiki to introduce a *compact per-instance uncertainty-estimation method* that scores *cross-layer agreement patterns* in LLM internal representations using a single forward pass; Badash, Zvi N.; Belinkov, Yonatan; Freiman, Moti diagnose the structural failure of both output-based heuristics (cheap but brittle) and probing internal representations (effective yet high-dimensional and hard to transfer), and propose a compact per-instance UE method that scores cross-layer agreement patterns using a single forward pass — surfacing *intra-layer-cross-layer-agreement-UQ-primitive* as the structurally correct primitive for representation-grounded UQ when transfer-fragility is the binding constraint, distinct from supervised-probe-UQ because the load-bearing axis is *cross-layer-agreement-scoring* rather than *supervised-probe-training*, and from output-entropy-UQ because the load-bearing axis is *representation-side-cross-layer-signal* rather than *token-distribution-aggregation*; plus *transferable-single-forward-pass-UQ-primitive* as the load-bearing *engineering-deployment primitive* matching probing in-distribution (≤ -1.8 AUPRC pts and ≤ +4.9 Brier-score pts gap) while delivering robust cross-dataset transfer where supervised probes degrade sharply, and *primary-forward-pass-UQ-primitive* as the load-bearing *computational-efficiency primitive* producing per-instance uncertainty scores from the model's primary forward pass with no auxiliary forward passes required. Empirical evaluation across three LLMs confirms the cross-layer-agreement signal matches probing in-distribution while remaining transferable across datasets. **First compact per-instance uncertainty-estimation method via cross-layer agreement scoring in LLM internal representations using a single forward pass in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(intra.layer.*information.score|cross.layer.agreement.*uncertainty|between.the.layers.*uncertainty|local.information.score.*uncertainty)"` returning empty. 2026-03-17; discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-29.

Three picks (date-DESC ordered by online_date):

- [[high-dimensional-random-projection-for-activation-steering-hidra-2606.15092]] — High-Dimensional Random Projection for Activation Steering in Language Models (HiDRA) (06-13) — first paper in the wiki to introduce **HiDRA**, a training-free activation-steering framework grounded in the *superposition hypothesis* that re-derives steering vectors in a random high-dimensional projection space to *recover discriminative signals that difference-in-means methods cannot access*; Pham, Hieu Minh; Do, Bach; Abdullaev, Laziz; Nguyen, Tan Minh; Than, Khoat diagnose the structural limitation of contrastive-activation-addition (CAA) — that linear mean-shift methods can only recover the mean-difference signal and miss the nonlinear discriminative structure entangled under superposition — and propose performing activation addition in a randomly projected higher-dimensional space where Johnson–Lindenstrauss-style preservation guarantees that *the projected space admits recovery of discriminative directions unreachable by linear methods*; surfacing *superposition-grounded-nonlinear-feature-subspace-as-steering-target-primitive* as the structurally correct primitive for steering-under-superposition research, distinct from contrastive-mean-difference-steering because the load-bearing axis is *random-projection-preprocessing-of-discriminative-structure* rather than *linear-mean-shift-extraction* — and *Johnson–Lindenstrauss-style-discriminative-recovery-primitive* as the load-bearing *theoretical-recovery primitive* proving that the projected space is provably richer for linear recovery of discriminative signals, plus *drop-in-training-free-additive-module-primitive* as the load-bearing *engineering-integration primitive* enabling HiDRA to slot into existing steering pipelines with no additional cost, while empirical results across diverse LLM families and benchmarks consistently outperform baseline counterparts. **First superposition-hypothesis-grounded activation-steering framework using high-dimensional random projection for discriminative-structure recovery in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(hidra|random.projection.*steering|superposition.*steer|projection.steer)"` returning empty. 2026-06-13; discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-29.

- [[steered-llm-activations-are-non-surjective-2604.09839]] — Steered LLM Activations are Non-Surjective (04-10) — first paper in the wiki to prove a *formal separation theorem*: under practical assumptions, activation steering pushes the residual stream *off the manifold of states reachable from discrete prompts*, so that — almost surely — no prompt can reproduce the same internal behaviour that steering induces; Mishra, Aayush; Khashabi, Daniel; Liu, Anqi cast the white-box-vs-black-box separation as a *surjectivity problem* and prove that the residual-stream geometry induced by steering has no textual preimage, establishing *white-box-steerability ≠ black-box-prompting* as a rigorous primitive; surfacing *non-surjectivity-of-steered-residual-stream-as-formal-separation-primitive* as the structurally correct primitive for steering-evaluation research, distinct from prompt-vs-steering behavioural comparison because the load-bearing axis is *almost-sure-absence-of-textual-preimage-on-residual-stream-manifold* rather than *output-distribution-comparison* — and *decoupled-evaluation-protocol-primitive* as the load-bearing *methodological primitive* arguing that interpretability and safety communities must adopt evaluation protocols that explicitly distinguish white-box interventions from black-box interventions, plus *first-formal-separation-of-intervention-classes-primitive* as the load-bearing *theoretical-separation primitive* providing the cleanest existing foundation for evaluating steering-vs-prompting claims. Empirical illustration across three widely used LLMs confirms the theoretical non-surjectivity finding, validating the ICLR 2026 Workshop placement. **First non-surjectivity theorem for steered LLM activations establishing formal separation of white-box steerability from black-box prompting in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(non.surjective|surjectivity.*steer|formal.separation.*steer|white.box.*black.box.*separ)"` returning empty. 2026-04-10; discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-29.

- [[global-evolutionary-steering-refining-activation-steering-control-via-cross-layer-consistency-2603.12298]] — Global Evolutionary Steering: Refining Activation Steering Control via Cross-Layer Consistency (GER-steer) (03-12) — first paper in the wiki to introduce **GER-steer**, a training-free framework that addresses two structural failure modes of contrastive-activation-addition (CAA) — *high-dimensional noise* in the raw steering vector and *layer-wise semantic drift* — by re-deriving the steering direction using the *global signal of the network's representation evolution across layers*; Jiang, Xinyan; Yu, Wenjing; Wang, Di; Hu, Lijie exploit the *geometric stability of the network's representation evolution* to rectify raw CAA vectors, effectively *decoupling robust semantic intent from orthogonal artifacts* and producing a single universal steering vector that works without layer-specific tuning; surfacing *cross-layer-consistency-evolution-as-steering-refinement-signal-primitive* as the structurally correct primitive for noise-and-drift-robust-steering research, distinct from per-layer CAA refinement because the load-bearing axis is *trajectory-of-layer-wise-representations-as-refinement-signal* rather than *per-layer-activation-difference-extraction* — and *global-evolutionary-rectification-of-orthogonal-artifacts-primitive* as the load-bearing *artifact-removal primitive* explicitly decoupling robust semantic intent from orthogonal artifacts, plus *universal-layer-agnostic-steering-vector-primitive* as the load-bearing *engineering-deployment primitive* delivering a single steering vector that transfers across layers without per-layer recalibration. Extensive evaluations confirm consistent superiority over baselines in both efficacy and generalisation. **First training-free activation-steering framework using cross-layer representation evolution as refinement signal in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(ger.steer|global.evolutionary.*steer|cross.layer.*consist.*steer|evolutionary.refine.*steer)"` returning empty. 2026-03-12; discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-29.
