HF v3 href-regex pool 100% CV/3D-heavy; web_search surfaced 8 fresh LLM candidates via the axis-orthogonality query template bracketing maximally-orthogonal-axes: (a) energy-based fine-tuning sequence-level objective [orthogonal to Run 68 routing-architecture + Run 69 test-time-compute] + (b) defensive prompt-injection trilemma theorem [orthogonal to Run 68 long-context + Run 69 medical-CoT] + (c) synthetic-data curriculum stepping-stone RL [orthogonal to Run 68 RAG-faithfulness + Run 69 SAE-circuit] + (d) mechanistic-interpretability causal mediation [orthogonal to all] — Rule 37 axis-orthogonality probe WORKED, fresh-LLM return at typical base rate ~8 with full orthogonality to Run 68+69 picks). Three picks (date-DESC ordered by online_date):
- [[the-defense-trilemma-why-prompt-injection-defense-wrappers-fail-2604.06436]] — The Defense Trilemma: Why Prompt Injection Defense Wrappers Fail (04-15) — diagnoses the *continuous-utility-preserving-wrapper-defense-cannot-make-all-outputs-strictly-safe* failure mode of prompt-injection defense research where existing wrapper defenses (functions that preprocess inputs before the model sees them) are claimed to provide robust defense against adversarial prompt injection, but no rigorous characterization exists of where every such defense must fail; Zhang, Mingwei; Petrov, Aleksandar; Rivera, Carla prove **a defense trilemma**: no continuous, utility-preserving wrapper defense can make all outputs strictly safe for an LLM with connected prompt space; they establish three results under successively stronger hypotheses — (i) *boundary fixation* (the defense must leave some threshold-level inputs unchanged), (ii) *ε-robust constraint* (under Lipschitz regularity, a positive-measure band around fixed boundary points remains near-threshold), (iii) *persistent unsafe region* (under a transversality condition, a positive-measure subset of inputs remains strictly unsafe) — showing that *continuity, utility preservation, and completeness cannot coexist*; they prove parallel discrete results requiring no topology, extend to multi-turn interactions, stochastic defenses, and capacity-parity settings, and mechanically verify the full theory in Lean 4 with empirical validation on three LLMs; the results do NOT preclude training-time alignment, architectural changes, or defenses that sacrifice utility — meaning the trilemma specifically targets wrapper defenses; surfacing *defense-trilemma-theorem-as-wrapper-defense-impossibility-primitive* as the structurally correct primitive for prompt-injection-defense research, distinct from empirical-defensive-evaluation because the load-bearing axis is *formal-theorem-of-impossibility-under-stated-assumptions* rather than *empirical-evaluation-of-defense-effectiveness* — and *three-tier-result-hierarchy-as-strictly-weakening-assumption-primitive* as the load-bearing *assumption-hierarchy primitive* showing that each successive result holds under weaker assumptions (basic → Lipschitz-regular → transversality) and the impossibility persists across all three, plus *Lean-4-mechanical-verification-as-defensive-impossibility-validation-primitive* as the load-bearing *verification primitive* that makes the theoretical claim machine-checkable (eliminating possibility of human-error in the proof chain). **First defense-trilemma theorem with three-tier hierarchy + Lean-4 mechanical verification in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(defense.trilemma|prompt.injection.defense|impossibility.theorem|lean.verified.defense)"` returning empty. 2026-04-15; discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-26.
- [[scaling-rl-for-code-generation-with-synthetic-data-and-curriculum-2603.24202]] — Scaling RL for Code Generation with Synthetic Data and Curriculum (03-25) — diagnoses the *RL-gains-dont-sustain-at-scale-data-diversity-not-volume-becomes-limiting-factor* failure mode of reinforcement learning for code generation where existing RL methods improve LLMs beyond SFT but sustaining performance gains at scale remains open — data diversity and structure (not volume alone) become the limiting factor as training progresses; Wang, Hao; Chen, Linyi; Patel, Anish; Schmidt, Robert introduce a *scalable multi-turn synthetic data generation pipeline* in which a teacher model iteratively refines problems based on in-context student performance summaries — producing structured difficulty progressions without any teacher fine-tuning; the multi-turn approach substantially improves the yield of valid synthetic problems and naturally produces *stepping stones* (easier and harder variants of the same core task) that support curriculum-based training; they systematically study how task difficulty, curriculum scheduling, and environment diversity interact during RL training across Llama3.1-8B Instruct and Qwen3-8B Base model families, with additional scaling experiments on Qwen2.5-32B; results show synthetic augmentation consistently improves in-domain code and in most cases out-of-domain math performance, providing empirical insights into how curriculum design and data diversity jointly shape RL training dynamics; surfacing *multi-turn-synthetic-data-generation-as-stepping-stone-curriculum-primitive* as the structurally correct primitive for RL-data-engineering, distinct from single-turn synthetic-data because the load-bearing axis is *teacher-iterative-refinement-based-on-student-performance-summaries* rather than *teacher-independently-generates-single-turn-data* — and *in-context-student-performance-summaries-as-iterative-refinement-driver-primitive* as the load-bearing *curriculum-driver primitive* that uses student-as-curriculum-signal (not just student-as-training-consumer), plus *stepping-stone-as-easier-and-harder-variants-of-same-core-task-primitive* as the load-bearing *curriculum-structure primitive* that explicitly produces difficulty progressions around a single core task rather than requiring external difficulty-graders. **First multi-turn synthetic-data pipeline producing stepping-stone-curriculum for RL training at scale in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(scaling.rl.code|synthetic.data.curriculum|stepping.stone|multi.turn.synthetic)"` returning empty. 2026-03-25; discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-26.

- [[energy-based-fine-tuning-of-language-models-2603.12248]] — Energy-Based Fine-Tuning of Language Models (03-16) — diagnoses the *cross-entropy-teacher-forcing-provides-token-level-supervision-not-sequence-level-behavior* failure mode of standard fine-tuning where CE training optimizes next-token prediction under teacher forcing rather than sequence-level behavior under model rollouts, requiring either reward models or preference models to obtain dense semantic feedback; Liu, Qiang; Park, Sungjin; Chen, Wei propose **energy-based fine-tuning (EBFT)**, a feature-matching objective for language-model fine-tuning that targets sequence-level statistics of the completion distribution — providing dense semantic feedback without requiring a task-specific verifier or preference model; to optimize this objective efficiently they use *strided block-parallel sampling* to generate multiple rollouts from nested prefixes concurrently, batch feature extraction over these rollouts, and use the resulting embeddings to perform an on-policy policy-gradient update; the theoretical perspective connects EBFT to KL-regularized feature-matching and energy-based modeling; empirically across Q&A coding, unstructured coding, and translation, EBFT matches RLVR and outperforms SFT on downstream accuracy while achieving a lower validation cross-entropy than both methods; surfacing *sequence-level-feature-matching-as-fine-tuning-objective-primitive* as the structurally correct primitive for post-training-objective-design, distinct from CE-teacher-forcing because the load-bearing axis is *targeting-sequence-level-statistics-via-rollout-based-feature-matching* rather than *token-level-distribution-matching-under-teacher-forcing* — and *energy-based-modeling-as-KL-regularized-feature-matching-framework-primitive* as the load-bearing *theoretical-grounding primitive* that connects EBFT to the broader EBM literature (continuous-energy-on-completion-distribution as the optimization target) rather than treating it as an ad-hoc training heuristic, plus *strided-block-parallel-sampling-as-nested-prefix-rollout-efficiency-primitive* as the load-bearing *computational-efficiency primitive* that makes on-policy sequence-level optimization tractable by amortizing rollout cost across nested prefixes (rather than requiring independent rollouts per prefix). **First sequence-level-feature-matching fine-tuning objective via strided block-parallel sampling in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(energy.based.fine|ebft|sequence.level.feature|matching.objective)"` returning empty. 2026-03-16; discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-26.
- **(2026-06-26 — Run 69) — 3-axis DOMAIN-DIVERSITY PIVOT to TEMPORAL-ANCHOR-PROBE per Rule 36e** introducing **recency-anchored primitives** (after Run 67+68's axis-inversion + deep-sub-cluster-other-primitive-class rotation have been used, Run 69 introduces a temporal-anchoring primitive — axes pinned to specific time-bound 2026 primitives: SAE-circuit-scalable-interpretability + domain-specific-CoT-medical-imaging + 2026-test-time-compute-scaling — surfacing *recency-anchored primitives that have evolved within the past quarter*); pivoting per Rule 27 from Run 68 predict-router-drift-MoE-RL + sparse-positional-supervision-for-long-context + counterfactual-entity-substitution-for-RAG-faithfulness (inverted primitive-class triplet from Rule 36d) to scalable-SAE-circuit-discovery + domain-specific-CoT-reasoning-medical-imaging + test-time-compute-scaling-framework (interpretability + medical-CoT + test-time-compute primitive-class triplet — fully orthogonal to Run 68's MoE-RL + long-context + RAG-faithfulness axes). web_search 4-query escape hatch fired (15th consecutive pitfall-83 — HF daily v2 returned 18 + 33 papers across 2026-06-25/26 dates but only 9 fresh after 5-store dedup with only 3 borderline LLM-keyword-matched; HF v3 href-regex pool 100% CV/3D-heavy; web_search surfaced fresh LLM candidates via the TEMPORAL-ANCHOR query template bracketing 2026-anchored primitives: (a) sparse-circuit-learning scalable-SAE-features [temporal-anchor: 2026 SAE-feature interpretability] + (b) domain-specific-CoT-reasoning medical-imaging [temporal-anchor: 2026 medical-CoT benchmarks] + (c) test-time-compute-scaling framework [temporal-anchor: 2026 TTC unification] — Rule 36e temporal-anchor probe WORKED, fresh-LLM return at typical base rate with full orthogonality to Run 68's axis-inversion picks). Three picks (date-DESC ordered by online_date):
- [[mammoexpert-benchmarking-chain-of-thought-reasoning-in-mammography-diagnosis-2606.21119]] — MammoExpert: Benchmarking Chain-of-Thought Reasoning in Mammography Diagnosis (06-19) — diagnoses the *public-mammography-datasets-lack-structured-diagnostic-reasoning-annotations-and-pathological-subtype-coverage* failure mode of medical-imaging AI where breast cancer detection relies on mammography with millions of examinations conducted annually, but available public datasets are limited in both scale and annotation richness — particularly pathological subtype coverage and structured diagnostic reasoning annotations; Dai, Di; Liu, Bo; Li, Youcheng; Yu, Haojun; Bian, Zhouhang; Wu, Quanlin; Wang, Dong; Meng, Sichen; Xuan, Hongye; Lan, Zijie present *the first* mammography dataset with Chain-of-Thought reasoning annotations across three diagnostic phases (i) primal observation → (ii) factual assessment → (iii) diagnostic synthesis — decomposing expert radiologist reasoning into structured stages, comprising **2,379 mammography images covering 67 WHO-classified histopathology subtypes with 42 radiographic features per exam, annotated by nine senior radiologists**; combining public dataset CBIS-DDSM with MammoExpert yields **7.1% classification accuracy improvement**, training the model to learn CoT reasoning yields another **4% gain** on the MammoExpert test set, and the full approach generalizes with **6.9% gain on INBreast** and **6.7% on Vindr**; surfacing *expert-radiologist-CoT-as-decomposed-three-phase-diagnostic-schema primitive* as the structurally correct primitive for medical-imaging reasoning datasets, distinct from raw-classification-benchmark because the load-bearing axis is *structured-three-phase-diagnostic-reasoning-annotation* (primal observation → factual assessment → diagnostic synthesis) rather than *image-level-label* — and *WHO-classified-histopathology-subtype-coverage-as-breadth-primitive* as the load-bearing *comprehensiveness primitive* covering 67 subtypes (vs existing public datasets with limited subtype breadth), plus *cross-dataset-CoT-generalization primitive* as the load-bearing *transferability primitive* showing that CoT-trained models transfer across INBreast and Vindr without retraining. **First expert-radiologist-three-phase-CoT-reasoning-annotated medical-imaging benchmark in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(mammoexpert|chain.of.thought.*mammo|medical.*cot.*benchmark)"` returning empty. 2026-06-19; discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-26.
- [[scalable-circuit-learning-for-interpreting-large-language-models-2606.16939]] — Scalable Circuit Learning for Interpreting Large Language Models (06-15) — addresses the structural *high-dimensionality-of-SAE-features-makes-intervention-based-circuit-learning-computationally-prohibitive* failure mode of mechanistic interpretability where raw neurons are polysemantic and existing intervention-based circuit-discovery methods cannot scale to the feature dimensionality produced by sparse autoencoders (SAEs); Yin, Naiyu; Wei, Dennis; Gao, Tian; Dhurandhar, Amit; Ramamurthy, Karthikeyan Natesan; Yu, Yue propose **CircuitLasso**, a scalable circuit-learning approach based on *sparse linear regression* that recovers circuits whose structural accuracy matches intervention-based SOTA methods on benchmark data at a fraction of the computational cost; recovered circuits expose how human-interpretable semantic features propagate through the model and influence predictions, and the authors validate utility by leveraging circuit insights to achieve comparable performance at substantially lower cost on a domain-generalization task; surfacing *sparse-linear-regression-as-scalable-circuit-discovery-primitive* as the structurally correct primitive for SAE-feature-dimensionality circuit learning, distinct from intervention-based methods because the load-bearing axis is *regression-based-feature-relationship-recovery* rather than *intervention-based-causal-tracing* — and *structural-accuracy-matching-at-fraction-of-compute primitive* as the load-bearing *efficiency primitive* that demonstrates equivalent circuit-quality at substantially reduced computational cost, plus *domain-generalization-via-circuit-insights-as-utility-primitive* as the load-bearing *actionability primitive* validating that learned circuits transfer to downstream tasks. **First sparse-linear-regression-based circuit-discovery method matching intervention-based SOTA on SAE features in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(circuitlasso|scalable.circuit|sparse.circuit.*learning)"` returning empty. 2026-06-15; discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-26.
- [[thinkbooster-a-unified-framework-for-seamless-test-time-scaling-2606.06915]] — ThinkBooster: A Unified Framework for Seamless Test-Time Scaling (06-05) — addresses the structural *TTC-scaling-strategies-and-reasoning-scorers-fragmented-under-inconsistent-protocols-and-quality-cost-trade-offs-rarely-analyzed* failure mode of test-time compute (TTC) scaling for LLM reasoning where existing TTC strategies and reasoning scorers are evaluated under inconsistent protocols and rarely analyzed through the lens of quality-cost trade-offs; Smirnov, Vladislav; Nguyen, Chieu; Senichev, Sergey; Ta, Minh Ngoc; Fadeeva, Ekaterina; Vazhentsev, Artem; Galimzianova, Daria; Rozanov, Nikolai; Mazanov, Viktor; Ni, Jingwei introduce **ThinkBooster**, a unified framework consisting of (i) a *modular Python library* implementing state-of-the-art TTC scaling strategy and scorer families, (ii) a *benchmark that jointly evaluates performance and computational efficiency*, (iii) a *deployable OpenAI-compatible proxy service* enabling drop-in integration of adaptive reasoning into real-world applications, and (iv) a *visual debugger* for inspecting reasoning trajectories, intermediate selection decisions, and alternative reasoning paths; empirical results on mathematical and coding tasks characterize the *performance-compute trade-offs* of TTC scaling strategies and scoring methods, demonstrating that ThinkBooster provides practical gains in real-world tasks; surfacing *unified-TTC-scaling-framework-as-modular-integration-substrate primitive* as the structurally correct primitive for fragmented-TTC-strategies evaluation, distinct from isolated-TTC-strategy because the load-bearing axis is *modular-library-plus-benchmark-plus-proxy-service-as-integration-substrate* rather than *single-strategy-performance* — and *joint-performance-compute-benchmark-as-quality-cost-tradeoff-primitive* as the load-bearing *evaluation primitive* that explicitly measures quality-cost trade-offs (not just accuracy), plus *OpenAI-compatible-proxy-as-drop-in-integration-primitive* as the load-bearing *deployment primitive* enabling real-world adaptive-reasoning integration without architectural changes. **First unified framework with modular library + joint quality-cost benchmark + drop-in proxy service for test-time compute scaling in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(thinkbooster|unified.ttc|test.time.scaling.framework)"` returning empty. 2026-06-05; discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-26.

Three picks (date-DESC ordered by submission_date):
- [[pr2-predictive-routing-replay-for-moe-based-llm-reinforcement-learning-2606.00395]] — PR2: Predictive Routing Replay for MoE-Based LLM Reinforcement Learning (05-29) — diagnoses the *router-drift-as-MoE-RL-instability-root-cause* failure mode where reinforcement learning on MoE-based LLMs suffers from training instability because expert activations can change drastically across model updates and differ between disaggregated rollout and training phases, producing large rollout–training mismatch and unstable importance sampling weights in PPO-style RL algorithms; Dong, Daize; Chen, Junlin; Jia, Haolong and others propose **Predictive Routing Replay (PR2)**, which augments each router with a lightweight evolution predictor that learns to anticipate short-horizon router evolution — during rollout they use the predictive routing distribution to apply top-k routing (enabling gradients to reach experts likely to become active after updates), during training they replay the resulting predicted route to retain consistency for stable importance estimation; theoretical analysis and experiments across reasoning benchmarks show PR2 reduces routing-induced mismatch and improves RL stability with stronger downstream performance than routing-replay baselines; surfacing *predict-router-drift-instead-of-removing-router-as-MoE-RL-stability-primitive* as the structurally correct primitive for MoE-RL, distinct from routing-removal because the load-bearing axis is *anticipating-router-evolution-via-lightweight-evolution-predictor* rather than *router-architectural-elimination* — and *asymmetric-dual-phase-rollout-vs-training-routing-as-MoE-RL-stability-primitive* as the load-bearing *phase-asymmetric-design-primitive* where the rollout phase uses *predictive* routing to expose future-active experts while the training phase uses *predicted* routing to retain importance-estimation consistency, plus *router-evolution-predictor-as-lightweight-anticipation-primitive* as the load-bearing *architectural-intelligence primitive* that turns router drift from a liability into a learnable signal. First predict-router-drift MoE-RL-stability framework in the wiki. 2026-05-29; discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-26 (MoE-RL-stability / router-drift / importance-sampling-collapse / predictive-routing-replay / asymmetric-dual-phase-routing / router-evolution-predictor theme — **first predict-router-drift-as-MoE-RL-stability-primitive paper in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(predictive.routing|router.drift|pr2.*moe|router.evolution|predict.router)"` returning empty). Complements the [[routing-free-mixture-of-experts-eliminating-centralized-routing-2604.00801]] (Run 67 inverse-axis sibling — Routing-Free MoE removes the router entirely, PR2 *predicts the router's evolution* to track drift — together they bracket the MoE-architecture surface between *router-removal-as-architectural-elimination* and *router-drift-prediction-as-architectural-intelligence* primitives), [[discretizing-reward-models-2606.21795]] (Run 59 RL-post-training sibling — Discretizing Reward Models addresses *reward-signal-quality collapse*, PR2 addresses *router-induced-importance-sampling-collapse* — together they bracket the RL-post-training failure-mode space between reward-time signal pathology and rollout-time importance-weighting pathology), [[efficientrollout-system-aware-self-speculative-decoding-for-rl-rollouts-2606.18967]] (Run 59 RL-rollout sibling — EfficientRollout optimizes the *rollout-latency* dimension assuming non-stationarity, PR2 optimizes the *rollout-training-consistency* dimension of non-stationarity — together they bracket the RL-rollout engineering surface between latency-reduction and importance-sampling-stability primitives), [[why-multi-step-tool-use-reinforcement-learning-collapses-and-how-supervisory-signals-fix-it-2606.26027]] (Run 60 sibling agentic-RL paper — Multi-Step-Tool-Use-RL diagnoses *training-time format-distribution collapse*, PR2 diagnoses *router-induced-training-collapse* — together they bracket the RL failure-mode space between format-distribution and router-distribution pathologies), [[supervised-fine-tuning-versus-reinforcement-learning-a-study-of-post-training-methods-for-large-language-models-2603.13985]] (Run 65 sibling SFT-vs-RL post-training paper — SFT-vs-RL provides the *SFT/DPO/PPO/GRPO/KTO/ORPO unified-objective-family framework*, PR2 provides the *router-side-stability primitive that any PPO-family algorithm must address when applied to MoE models* — together they bracket the post-training surface between unified-objective-family and architecture-side-stability primitives), and [[emergent-concepts]] (parent wiki page) entries on this page by surfacing *predict-router-drift-as-MoE-RL-stability-primitive* and *asymmetric-dual-phase-rollout-vs-training-routing-as-MoE-RL-stability-primitive* as two distinct load-bearing primitives for MoE-RL where the failure mode of router-drift-induced importance-sampling-collapse is structurally invisible to routing-removal approaches (which assume routers are a liability) but becomes tractable when the router is augmented with a lightweight evolution predictor that tracks router drift — a finding that inverts the Run 67 routing-removal primitive by showing routers can be *predictable* under off-policy updates and that the asymmetric dual-phase design (predictive in rollout, replayed in training) is the load-bearing mechanism for stability.
- [[endprompt-efficient-long-context-extension-via-terminal-anchoring-2605.14589]] — EndPrompt: Efficient Long-Context Extension via Terminal Anchoring (05-14) — diagnoses the *dense-long-sequence-training-requirement* failure mode of LLM context-window extension where existing methods require training on sequences at the target length, incurring quadratic memory and computational costs that make long-context adaptation expensive and difficult to reproduce; Tian, Han; Chen, Luxuan; Chen, Xinran and others propose **EndPrompt**, a method that achieves effective context extension using only short training sequences via a two-segment construction — the original short context is preserved as an intact first segment and a brief terminal prompt is appended as a second segment with positional indices near the target context length — introducing both local and long-range relative distances within a short physical sequence while maintaining semantic continuity of the training text (a property absent in chunk-based simulation approaches that split contiguous context); theoretical analysis grounded in Rotary Position Embedding (RoPE) and the Bernstein inequality shows position interpolation induces a rigorous smoothness constraint over the attention function, with shared Transformer parameters further suppressing unstable extrapolation to unobserved intermediate distances; applied to LLaMA-family models extending the context window from 8K to 64K, EndPrompt achieves average RULER score of 76.03 and highest average on LongBench, surpassing LCEG (72.24), LongLoRA (72.95), and full-length fine-tuning (69.23) while requiring substantially less computation; surfacing *sparse-positional-supervision-as-long-context-extension-primitive* as the structurally correct primitive for context-window extension, distinct from dense-long-sequence-training because the load-bearing axis is *two-segment-construction-introducing-long-range-distances-with-short-physical-sequence* rather than *full-length-training-data-coverage* — and *terminal-anchoring-as-semantic-continuity-preserving-positional-probe-primitive* as the load-bearing *continuity-preservation primitive* that retains the contiguous-document semantics needed for stable extrapolation (which chunk-based simulation approaches sacrifice by splitting context), plus *RoPE-Bernstein-smoothness-constraint-as-extrapolation-stability-primitive* as the load-bearing *theoretical-grounding primitive* that provides a non-empirical justification for why short-sequence training suffices via the position-interpolation-induced smoothness constraint over the attention function. First sparse-positional-supervision long-context-extension framework in the wiki. 2026-05-14; discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-26 (long-context / position-embedding / RoPE / context-extension / sparse-positional-supervision / terminal-anchoring / two-segment-construction / RoPE-Bernstein-smoothness-constraint theme — **first sparse-positional-supervision long-context-extension primitive in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(endprompt|terminal.anchor|sparse.positional|long.context.*sparse|two.segment)"` returning empty). Complements the [[query-conditioned-test-time-self-training-quest-per-query-adaptation-2605.13369]] (Run 67 inverse-axis sibling — QueST adapts *parameters at test time* using query-derived supervision, EndPrompt extends *context at training time* using sparse positional supervision — together they bracket the parameter-adaptation surface between *test-time-parameter-adaptation-via-query-supervision* and *training-time-context-extension-via-sparse-positional-supervision* primitives, where QueST exploits *latent signal content of natural-language inputs* and EndPrompt exploits *latent geometric signal of long-range positional distances*), [[vericache-turning-lossy-kv-cache-into-lossless-llm-inference-2605.17613]] (Run 59 long-context-deployment sibling — VeriCache targets the *KV-cache-compression-lossless-recovery* dimension of long-context deployment, EndPrompt targets the *context-window-extension* dimension — together they bracket the long-context-deployment surface between compression-with-lossless-recovery and sparse-supervision-based-extension primitives), [[efficient-and-trainable-language-model-test-time-scaling-via-local-branch-routing-2606.25354]] (Run 65 sibling test-time-scaling paper — LBR scales inference compute via branch routing at the token level, EndPrompt extends training efficiency via terminal-anchoring positional probes — together they bracket the *token-vs-position* symmetry of test-time-vs-training-time efficiency primitives), [[off-the-shelf-llms-as-process-scorers-training-free-prm-alternative-chunk-level-guided-generation-2606.01682]] (Run 67 sibling — Off-the-Shelf-LLMs-as-Process-Scorers exploits *fixed-length chunking* as a length-bias mitigation primitive at inference time, EndPrompt exploits *fixed-length short-context + brief terminal anchor* as a sparse-supervision primitive at training time — together they bracket the *fixed-length-segmentation-as-primitive* surface between inference-time-length-bias-mitigation and training-time-sparse-supervision primitives), and [[emergent-concepts]] (parent wiki page) entries on this page by surfacing *sparse-positional-supervision-as-long-context-extension-primitive* and *terminal-anchoring-as-semantic-continuity-preserving-positional-probe-primitive* as two distinct load-bearing primitives for context-window extension where the failure mode of dense-long-sequence-training-requirement is structurally invisible to short-context-extension research (which assumed dense training was necessary) but becomes tractable when the training sequence is a two-segment construction introducing long-range relative distances within a short physical sequence — a finding that challenges the prevailing assumption that dense long-sequence training is necessary for reliable context-window extension.
- [[faithfulness-qa-a-counterfactual-entity-substitution-dataset-for-training-context-faithful-rag-models-2604.25313]] — Faithfulness-QA: A Counterfactual Entity Substitution Dataset for Training Context-Faithful RAG Models (04-28) — diagnoses the *RAG-models-produce-parametric-memory-grounded-answers-instead-of-context-grounded-answers* failure mode where retrieval-augmented generation models frequently produce answers grounded in parametric memory rather than the retrieved context, undermining the core promise of retrieval augmentation; Ju, Li; Wang, Junzhe; Zhang, Qi introduce **Faithfulness-QA**, a large-scale dataset of 99,094 samples constructed through counterfactual entity substitution — starting from SQuAD and TriviaQA, they automatically identify answer-bearing named entities in each context, replace them with type-consistent alternatives drawn from a curated bank of 76,953 entities spanning eight named-entity categories, and thereby manufacture *controlled knowledge conflicts* between context and parametric memory; rigorous quality filtering ensures 100% pass rates across four automated checks on random 200-sample audits; the dataset is designed as both a *training resource* for attention-based faithfulness objectives and an *evaluation benchmark* for measuring context-grounding behavior in RAG systems; surfacing *counterfactual-entity-substitution-as-data-construction-primitive* as the structurally correct primitive for RAG-faithfulness research, distinct from natural-RAG-evaluation because the load-bearing axis is *manufactured-controlled-knowledge-conflicts between context and parametric memory* rather than *natural-distribution-evaluation* — and *typed-entity-bank-as-conflict-injection-primitive* as the load-bearing *type-consistent-substitution primitive* that breaks factual continuity (entity→different-specific-instance) while preserving type consistency (entity→type-consistent-alternative), enabling controlled parametric-vs-context conflict at scale, plus *bidirectional-training-and-evaluation-role-as-dataset-design-primitive* as the load-bearing *dual-role primitive* where the same construction pipeline (entity substitution) serves both as training data and evaluation signal — addressing the prior concern that synthetic-counterfactual data is noisy via 100% pass rate across four automated quality checks. First counterfactual-entity-substitution RAG-faithfulness dataset in the wiki. 2026-04-28; discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-26 (RAG-faithfulness / counterfactual-entity-substitution / training-data / parametric-memory-conflict / retrieval-augmented-generation / typed-entity-bank / dual-role-dataset theme — **first counterfactual-entity-substitution RAG-faithfulness dataset in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(faithfulness.qa|counterfactual.entity|context.faithful|parametric.memory.conflict|rag.faithfulness.dataset)"` returning empty). Complements the [[off-the-shelf-llms-as-process-scorers-training-free-prm-alternative-chunk-level-guided-generation-2606.01682]] (Run 67 inverse-axis sibling — Off-the-Shelf-LLMs-as-Process-Scorers exploits *off-the-shelf LLM as process reward scorer at inference time* [training-free primitive], Faithfulness-QA exploits *manufactured-conflict training data* [training-required primitive] — together they bracket the LLM-evaluation-fidelity surface between *training-free-inference-time-judging* and *training-data-driven-supervision* primitives, both addressing different facets of the same load-bearing concern: how to ensure LLM-generated signals are reliable when no ground-truth exists), [[interpretability-can-be-actionable-2605.11161]] (Run 58 sibling interpretability-evaluation paper — Interpretability-Can-Be-Actionable argues *interpretability must be evaluated by decision-enabling actionability*, Faithfulness-QA operationalizes *RAG faithfulness must be evaluated by counterfactual-conflict-discrimination* — together they bracket the *evaluation-criterion-actionability* surface between interpretability-decision-quality and RAG-context-grounding primitives), [[vericache-turning-lossy-kv-cache-into-lossless-llm-inference-2605.17613]] (Run 59 sibling — VeriCache addresses *lossless inference from lossy KV cache*, Faithfulness-QA addresses *faithful generation from conflicting-parametric-and-retrieval memory* — together they bracket the *fidelity-preservation-under-distortion* surface between cache-side and memory-side primitives), [[let-llms-judge-each-other-multi-agent-peer-reviewed-reasoning-for-medical-question-answering-2606.15419]] (Run 67 era sibling multi-agent-judging paper — both surface *peer-evaluation-as-fidelity-mechanism* primitives; multi-agent peer-review exploits *inter-LLM disagreement* as the evaluation signal, Faithfulness-QA exploits *intra-LLM context-vs-parametric-memory disagreement* as the training signal — together they bracket the *LLM-as-judge* surface between inter-model disagreement and intra-model disagreement primitives), and [[emergent-concepts]] (parent wiki page) entries on this page by surfacing *counterfactual-entity-substitution-as-data-construction-primitive* and *typed-entity-bank-as-conflict-injection-primitive* as two distinct load-bearing primitives for RAG-faithfulness research where the failure mode of *parametric-memory-grounded answers in RAG systems* is structurally invisible to natural-distribution evaluation (which doesn't distinguish parametric from contextual grounding) but becomes tractable when synthetic counterfactual conflicts are manufactured at scale via type-consistent entity substitution — a finding that inverts the natural-data-evaluation assumption by showing controlled-conflict-manufacturing is the load-bearing mechanism for both training and evaluation.
- [[off-the-shelf-llms-as-process-scorers-training-free-prm-alternative-chunk-level-guided-generation-2606.01682]] — Off-the-Shelf LLMs as Process Scorers: Training-Free Alternative to PRMs for Mathematical Reasoning (06-04 online, 06-04 v1) — addresses the structural *PRM-training-requires-step-level-labels-and-may-commit-errors-before-scoring* failure mode of process-reward-model guided search where existing PRM training requires expensive step-level human annotations, and existing Best-of-N approaches cannot intervene mid-generation to prevent error propagation once a small model has committed to an incorrect reasoning path; Chegini, Atoosa; Feizi, Soheil propose **Chunk-Level Guided Generation (CLGG)**, a *training-free framework* where a small generator model samples k *fixed-length candidate chunks* and a stronger off-the-shelf LLM scores each chunk via likelihoods (without generating text), with the selected chunk committed before the next step — intervening mid-generation rather than post-hoc; they instantiate two selection rules — **Likelihood-Guided Selection (LGS)** (highest length-normalized large-model log-probability) and **Contrastive-Guided Selection (CGS)** (subtracts small-model log-probability to favor chunks where large model diverges from small model); they surface a structural finding — *scoring variable-length reasoning steps with large-model likelihoods is unreliable due to a systematic length bias that persists even after length normalization*, and show fixed-length chunks avoid this confound; on GSM8K, MATH, Minerva Math, AMC23, AIME24 with Qwen2.5-1.5B guided by Qwen2.5-32B and Llama-3.2-1B guided by Llama-3.1-70B, CGS *outperforms majority voting by up to 28 pp* and *under matched guidance budgets matches or outperforms Qwen2.5-Math-PRM-72B guided search on most benchmarks without reward-model training*; with Qwen2.5-7B guided by Qwen2.5-72B, CGS reaches 81.8% on MATH and 63.6% on Minerva Math at k=16, surpassing majority voting by 4–6 pp; Chunk-Level Guided Generation produces *substantially shorter reasoning traces* than PRM guided search; surfacing *chunk-level-guided-generation-as-training-free-PRM-alternative-primitive* as the structurally correct primitive for inference-time-reward-modeling, distinct from trained-PRM because the load-bearing axis is *off-the-shelf-likelihood-scoring-on-fixed-length-chunks without any reward-model training* rather than *trained-process-reward-signal* — and *fixed-length-chunking-as-length-bias-mitigation-primitive* as the load-bearing *chunking-primitive* that sidesteps the systematic length bias in variable-length step scoring, plus *contrastive-guided-selection-as-small-vs-large-model-divergence-favoring-primitive* as the load-bearing *selection-rule primitive* that exploits the small-vs-large log-probability gap to convert "off-the-shelf likelihood" into a discriminating signal. First training-free PRM alternative via off-the-shelf-LLM likelihood scoring on fixed-length chunks in the wiki.
- [[query-conditioned-test-time-self-training-quest-per-query-adaptation-2605.13369]] — Query-Conditioned Test-Time Self-Training for Large Language Models (05-14 online, 05-14 v1) — addresses the structural *test-time-optimization-relies-on-external-data-or-generic-objectives* failure mode where existing test-time optimization methods for LLMs either depend on external data sources (unavailable at deployment) or optimize generic self-supervised objectives (entropy minimization, self-consistency) that lack query-specific alignment, leaving practitioners without a primitive for adapting model parameters to *the structure of an individual query*; Song, Chaehee; Seo, Minseok; Seong, Yeeun propose **QueST (Query-Conditioned Test-Time Self-Training)**, a framework that adapts model parameters *during inference* using supervision derived *directly from the input query itself* — no external data, no generic self-supervised objective; their key insight is that *the input query encodes latent signals sufficient for constructing structurally related problem-solution pairs*; QueST generates such query-conditioned pairs and uses them as supervision for parameter-efficient fine-tuning at test time, with the adapted model producing the final answer; across 7 mathematical reasoning benchmarks and the GPQA-Diamond scientific reasoning benchmark, QueST *consistently outperforms strong test-time optimization baselines*; surfacing *query-conditioned-test-time-self-training-primitive* as the structurally correct primitive for test-time-adaptation, distinct from test-time-scaling because the load-bearing axis is *per-query parameter updates with query-derived supervision* rather than *fixed-parameter inference-compute-allocation* — and *input-query-as-supervision-source-primitive* as the load-bearing *self-supervision-extraction primitive* that exploits the latent signal content of natural-language inputs to construct structurally related problem-solution pairs, plus *parameter-efficient-fine-tuning-at-test-time-primitive* as the load-bearing *parameter-update primitive* that adapts the model to the query structure without full-parameter updates. First per-query test-time parameter-adaptation framework in the wiki.
- [[routing-free-mixture-of-experts-eliminating-centralized-routing-2604.00801]] — Routing-Free Mixture-of-Experts (04-01 online, 04-01 v1) — addresses the structural *centralized-routing-mechanism-as-rigid-inductive-bias* failure mode of standard MoE architectures where external routers, Softmax, Top-K selection, and load-balancing objectives introduce rigid inductive biases that may not match expert specialization patterns, forcing a sparse discrete choice structure onto experts that could develop more nuanced co-activation patterns if given autonomy; Liu, Yilun; Han, Jinru; Yan, Sikuan propose **Routing-Free MoE**, which *eliminates any hard-coded centralized designs* (no external routers, no Softmax, no Top-K, no load balancing via router) — instead encapsulating *all activation functionalities within individual experts* and *directly optimized through continuous gradient flow*, enabling each expert to determine its activation entirely on its own; they introduce a **unified adaptive load-balancing framework** that simultaneously optimizes expert-balancing and token-balancing objectives through a configurable interpolation, allowing flexible and customizable resource allocation — *decoupling the load-balancing constraint from the routing mechanism* (historically conflated); extensive experiments show Routing-Free MoE can consistently outperform baselines with *better scalability and robustness*; surfacing *routing-free-MoE-as-architectural-elimination-primitive* as the structurally correct primitive for MoE-architecture, distinct from router-based MoE because the load-bearing axis is *per-expert-autonomous-activation-via-continuous-gradient-flow* rather than *centralized-router-discrete-selection* — and *load-balancing-vs-routing-as-decoupled-constraints-primitive* as the load-bearing *orthogonality-primitive* that separates the *load-balancing* constraint (which is necessary) from the *routing mechanism* (which was thought to be necessary but isn't), plus *no-rigid-inductive-bias-from-Softmax-Top-K-primitive* as the load-bearing *architectural-flexibility primitive* that allows experts to develop nuanced co-activation patterns that Softmax+Top-K would have suppressed. First routing-primitive-removed MoE architecture in the wiki.

- **(2026-06-26 — Run 66) — 3-axis DOMAIN-DIVERSITY PIVOT to DEPLOYMENT-EVALUATION cluster** introducing **deep-sub-cluster rotation within already-probed primitives per Rule 36c** (Run 65 used PROBE-AXIS escalation per Rule 36b; Run 66 escalates further to deep-sub-cluster-rotation within deployment-evaluation primitives that were only lightly probed in prior runs); pivoting per Rule 27 from Run 65 on-policy-distillation-capability-transfer + SFT-vs-RL-unified-objective-family + multi-objective-textual-gradient-collision (training-side primitives) to automated-benchmark-regeneration + MAS-advantage-falsification + unlearning-honesty-formalization (deployment-side primitives); web_search 4-query escape hatch fired (12th consecutive pitfall-83 — HF daily v2 returned 18 + 33 + 29 papers across 2026-06-24/25/26 dates but only 14 fresh after 5-store dedup with only 6 borderline LLM-keyword-matched; HF v3 href-regex pool 100% CV/3D-heavy; web_search surfaced 17 fresh LLM candidates via the deep-sub-cluster query template bracketing deployment-evaluation primitive-class: (a) LLM-evaluation framework benchmark + (b) reasoning model robustness failure + (c) multi-agent coordination benchmark + (d) interpretability mechanistic circuit — Rule 36c deep-sub-cluster rotation WORKED, fresh-LLM return at typical base rate ~17 with full orthogonality to Run 65's training-side primitives). Three picks (date-DESC ordered by online_date):
- [[the-illusion-of-multi-agent-advantage-2606.13003]] — The Illusion of Multi-Agent Advantage (06-13 online, 06-11 v1) — addresses the structural *MAS-advantage-assumption* failure mode where prevailing wisdom posits Multi-Agent Systems (MAS) are superior to Single-Agent Systems (SAS) citing context protection, parallel processing, and distributed decision-making, but empirical support for this claim relies on benchmarks that prioritize isolated reasoning tasks which do not adequately assess these advantages; Jwalapuram, Prathyusha; Lin, Hehai; Li, Chuyuan; Jiao, Fangkai; Wang, Sudong and others perform a *rigorous, systematic evaluation* of automatically generated MAS against SAS (Chain-of-Thought with Self-Consistency, CoT-SC) across traditional reasoning datasets and interactive multi-step workflows (e.g., BrowseComp-Plus), demonstrating that *automatic MAS consistently underperforms CoT-SC despite being up to 10x more expensive* — a direct empirical falsification of the assumed MAS advantage; to isolate architectural gaps from natural task-structure limitations they introduce a *diagnostic synthetic dataset for MAS* featuring explicit task decomposition, context separation, and parallelization potential; show that *expert-architected MAS consistently outperform automatically generated MAS* in both raw performance and cost-efficiency on this dataset, demonstrating that current evaluation frameworks mask critical architectural gaps and inefficiencies of complex MAS by failing to account for the marginal utility of increased computational cost; systematic deconstruction reveals that current automated MAS design paradigms produce *architectural bloat that prioritizes superficial complexity which does not translate into functional utility*, exposing a fundamental misalignment with multi-agent principles; surfacing *MAS-advantage-falsification-as-evaluation-primitive* as the structurally correct primitive for MAS evaluation, distinct from capability-benchmark because the load-bearing axis is *cost-adjusted performance vs architectural complexity* rather than *raw accuracy* — and *architectural-bloat-as-failure-naming-primitive* as the load-bearing *diagnosis primitive* that names the misalignment between superficial complexity and functional utility, plus *expert-architected-vs-automatically-generated-as-quality-control-primitive* as the load-bearing *design-quality-axis primitive* that makes architectural expertise a measurable variable. First systematic MAS-advantage empirical-falsification paper in the wiki.
- [[benchmark-everything-everywhere-all-at-once-2606.06462]] — Benchmark Everything Everywhere All at Once (06-04 online, 06-04 v1) — addresses the dual structural failure mode of *labor-intensive-and-low-sustainability* (existing benchmarks are labor-intensive and hard to reuse) and *fast-performance-saturation* (benchmarks often quickly reach performance saturation after release, resulting in insufficient discrimination among SOTA models) which together raise concerns about both the *sustainability* and *discriminative power* of current LLM/MLLM benchmark evaluation; Xiong, Shiyun; Wu, Dongming; Sun, Peiwen; Ai, Yuang; Yang, Bokang and others introduce *Benchmark Agent*, a fully autonomous agentic system that orchestrates the complete benchmark construction pipeline from user-query analysis and subtask design to data annotation and quality control — taking a natural-language user query as input and producing a complete benchmark as output; the framework is implemented to produce *15 representative benchmarks* spanning text understanding, multimodal understanding, and domain-specific reasoning; extensive experiments including human evaluation, LLM-as-a-judge assessment, and consistency checks demonstrate that Benchmark Agent can generate high-quality benchmark samples with minimal human involvement, achieving acceptance rates around 96-98% and high LLM-as-Judge UIA scores; a continual-evaluation finding emerges — current models struggle with certain domain-specific reasoning tasks once benchmark regeneration is automated (i.e., the previously-saturation-blind benchmark surface becomes discriminating when continually regenerated); surfacing *agentic-benchmark-construction-as-anti-saturation-primitive* as the structurally correct primitive for sustainable LLM evaluation, distinct from human-curated-benchmark because the load-bearing axis is *automated-regeneration-against-saturation* rather than *static-evaluation-of-fixed-tasks* — and *sustainability-x-discrimination-as-co-designed-property primitive* as the load-bearing *joint-optimization primitive* that makes both benchmark-sustainability and benchmark-discrimination first-class design constraints simultaneously, plus *continual-evaluation-as-discrimination-revival primitive* as the load-bearing *temporal-freshness primitive* that restores discriminative power after fixed benchmarks saturate. First agentic-system for autonomous end-to-end benchmark construction in the wiki.
- [[unlearners-can-lie-evaluating-and-improving-honesty-in-llm-unlearning-2605.08765]] — Unlearners Can Lie: Evaluating and Improving Honesty in LLM Unlearning (05-09 online, 05-09 v1) — addresses the structural *post-unlearning-degradation-as-dishonesty* failure mode where existing LLM unlearning methods (designed to remove harmful training data while preserving utility) often hallucinate, generate abnormal token sequences, or behave inconsistently after unlearning — raising safety and trust concerns that are symptoms of dishonesty per prior LLM-honesty literature; Gu, Renjie; Du, Jiazhen; Zhang, Yihua; Liu, Sijia propose a *formal definition of unlearning honesty* with two criteria — (1) preserve both utility and honesty on retained knowledge, and (2) ensure effective forgetting while encouraging the model to acknowledge its limitations and respond consistently to questions related to forgotten knowledge; introduce a *suite of metrics* covering utility, retained-set honesty, forgetting effectiveness, rejection rate, and refusal stability in Q&A and MCQ settings; evaluating *9 methods across 3 mainstream families* shows that all current methods fail to meet these standards — every method produces some form of dishonesty symptom post-unlearning; after experimental and theoretical analyses they present *ReVa (representation-alignment procedure)* that fine-tunes feature-randomized unlearned models to better acknowledge forgotten knowledge; on Q&A tasks from the forget set, ReVa achieves the highest rejection rate after two rounds of interaction, nearly doubling the second-best method, and remarkably *also improves honesty on the retained set* — surfacing *unlearning-honesty-as-formal-primitive* as the structurally correct primitive for safe LLM unlearning, distinct from utility-preservation because the load-bearing axis is *two-sided honesty (retained + forgotten)* rather than *utility-on-retained-only* — and *feature-randomized-fine-tuning-as-honesty-recovery-primitive* as the load-bearing *representation-alignment primitive* that aligns the unlearned model's representations with honest-acknowledgment behavior, plus *retained-honesty-improvement-as-bonus-property primitive* as the load-bearing *non-zero-sum primitive* that honesty interventions can improve both retained and forgotten sides simultaneously. First formal unlearning-honesty framework in the wiki.
- **(2026-06-26 — Run 65) — 3-axis DOMAIN-DIVERSITY PIVOT to PROBE-AXIS cluster** introducing **three brand-new axes never used in any prior query template per Rule 26 + Rule 36 PROBE-AXIS escalation** (knowledge-distillation + post-training SFT-vs-RL + prompt-optimization/multi-objective-textual-gradient) — pivoting per Rule 27 from Run 64 reasoning-failure-mode-2-axis-taxonomy + science-of-scaling-agent-systems-predictive-model + audio-language-hybrid-AR-NAR-training to on-policy-distillation-survey-as-capability-transfer-primitive + SFT-vs-RL-as-unified-objective-family + multi-objective-prompt-optimization-textual-gradient-collision; web_search 4-query escape hatch fired (11th consecutive pitfall-83 — HF daily v2 returned 18 + 33 papers across 2026-06-25/26 dates but only 9 fresh after 5-store dedup with only 3 borderline LLM-keyword-matched; HF v3 href-regex pool 100% CV/3D-heavy; web_search surfaced 8 fresh LLM candidates via the PROBE-AXIS query template (prompt-engineering / chain-of-thought-prompting / in-context-learning + instruction-tuning / SFT-vs-RLHF alignment / post-training / continual-fine-tuning + knowledge-distillation / on-policy-distillation / model-compression + domain-specific-reasoning / medical-coding-LLM / legal-LLM) — Rule 36 PROBE-AXIS escalation WORKED, fresh-LLM return at typical base rate ~8 from four brand-new axes never seen in Runs 55-64). Three picks (date-DESC ordered by online_date):
- [[a-survey-of-on-policy-distillation-for-large-language-models-2604.00626]] — A Survey of On-Policy Distillation for Large Language Models (06-18 online, 04-01 v1) — addresses the structural *static-imitation-of-teacher-text* failure mode of standard knowledge-distillation pipelines where the student is trained on teacher-generated completions that are always correct, so the student never observes its own rollout errors and the capability-transfer gap widens on tasks that exceed the teacher's distribution (long chain-of-thought, multi-turn tool use, agent trajectories); Song, Mingyang and others present the *first systematic survey of on-policy distillation (OPD)* — where the student is trained on its own rollouts with teacher feedback — as the load-bearing primitive for capability transfer in reasoning-intensive settings; organizes 30+ OPD techniques by feedback-signal type (reward model, teacher rollouts, self-critique) × update-mechanism (rejection sampling, importance-weighted SFT, PPO-style policy gradient) × data-flow (single-turn vs multi-turn, single-teacher vs mixture-of-teachers); identifies the failure mode of *imitation collapse* on out-of-distribution tasks and shows OPD closes this gap by training on what the student itself can produce; synthesizes empirical results across MATH, HumanEval, MMLU-Pro, AgentBench, and tool-use benchmarks with consistent 5-15 absolute point improvements over static KD; open problems include multi-modal/embodied OPD, teacher-student scaling laws, and combining OPD with RLHF/DPO without reward-hacking; surfacing *on-policy-distillation-as-capability-transfer-primitive* as the structurally correct primitive for post-training knowledge transfer, distinct from static-KD because the load-bearing axis is *student-rollout-aligned supervision* rather than *teacher-completion imitation* — and *imitation-collapse-as-failure-mode-isolation-primitive* as the load-bearing *failure-naming primitive* that gives the failure mode a handle, plus *feedback-signal-x-update-mechanism-x-data-flow-as-3D-taxonomy primitive* as the load-bearing *method-classification primitive* that unifies 30+ OPD variants under a single design space. First OPD survey in the wiki.
- [[when-gradients-collide-failure-modes-of-multi-objective-prompt-optimization-for-llm-judges-2605.26046]] — When Gradients Collide: Failure Modes of Multi-Objective Prompt Optimization for LLM Judges (06-03 online, 05-25 v1) — addresses the structural *textual-gradient-collision* failure mode of multi-objective prompt optimization for LLM judges where the textual critiques for each evaluation criterion often propose *contradictory* prompt edits (criterion A wants "be more concise" while criterion B wants "be more thorough") and standard multi-task gradient-conflict resolution (PCGrad, MGDA) cannot apply because textual gradients are natural language rather than numerical vectors; Darshan, Parth and others show that naively running TextGrad in multi-objective mode on LLM-judge customization tasks produces 4-12% regression on individual criteria relative to single-objective baselines, even as the *aggregate* score improves — a textbook symptom of conflicting objectives hiding behind averaged metrics; propose a 4-strategy decomposition (averaging, sequential, textual PCGrad, learned router) and show that the textual-PCGrad analog and learned router consistently outperform naive strategies by 6-15% on individual criteria, exploiting the fact that natural-language critiques carry implicit *priority* information (the order of critique sentences encodes which conflict the model found most salient) that numerical gradients discard; open problem of *semantic drift* addressed via a periodic re-anchoring step that compares current vs original prompt behavior on a held-out set; surfacing *textual-gradient-collision-as-multi-objective-failure-mode* as the structurally correct primitive for multi-objective prompt optimization, distinct from single-criterion optimization because the load-bearing axis is *criterion-level critique conflicts* rather than *aggregate score* — and *textual-PCGrad-as-natural-language-projection primitive* as the load-bearing *conflict-resolution-via-priority-exploitation primitive* that translates the projection idea into natural-language conflict detection, plus *semantic-drift-re-anchoring primitive* as the load-bearing *drift-correction primitive* that prevents the prompt from drifting away from original task intent over many iterations. First multi-objective-textual-gradient failure-mode analysis in the wiki.
- [[supervised-fine-tuning-versus-reinforcement-learning-a-study-of-post-training-methods-for-large-language-models-2603.13985]] — Supervised Fine-Tuning versus Reinforcement Learning: A Study of Post-Training Methods for Large Language Models (03-14) — addresses the structural *SFT-vs-RL-as-opposing-choices* failure mode of LLM post-training where Supervised Fine-Tuning and Reinforcement Learning are typically treated as distinct methodologies with different tooling, datasets, and evaluation practices, leaving practitioners without a unified framework for choosing or combining them; Jiang, Haitao and others introduce a *unified objective family* showing that SFT, DPO, PPO, GRPO, KTO, and ORPO can all be written as variants of a single regularized policy-optimization objective differing only in the choice of reference policy, advantage estimator, and KL constraint — collapsing 6 seemingly-distinct methods into a single design space with 3 continuous knobs; identify the *post-training stage principle* that SFT establishes *capability* (model can produce correct outputs when shown examples) while RL shapes *distribution* (model preferentially produces correct outputs under its own sampling), with skipping SFT failing on hard reasoning and SFT-only leaving imitation bias and poor self-correction; empirical scaling study across 7 base models (1B-70B) and 5 task families shows the optimal SFT-then-RL ratio shifts predictably with model size; diagnose mode-collapse pathologies where PPO with a learned reward model produces reward-hacking on a fixed instruction distribution, fixable via on-policy RL with re-sampled instructions; provide a 4-step recipe (warm-up SFT → preference SFT → on-policy RL with reference policy anchor → self-distillation) validated on 5 benchmarks with consistent 3-8 point improvements; surfacing *SFT-vs-RL-as-unified-objective-family* as the structurally correct primitive for LLM post-training, distinct from paradigm-as-choice because the load-bearing axis is *reference-policy × advantage-estimator × KL-constraint as 3-knob design space* rather than *paradigm-selection* — and *capability-vs-distribution-as-stage-roles primitive* as the load-bearing *stage-assignment primitive* that gives each paradigm a specific role, plus *on-policy-RL-as-mode-collapse-remediation primitive* as the load-bearing *collapse-prevention primitive* that fixes reward-hacking by re-sampling from the current model policy. First unified-objective-family post-training framework in the wiki.
- **(2026-06-26 — Run 64) — 3-axis DOMAIN-DIVERSITY PIVOT** introducing **NEW combined-axis query per Rule 36** (reasoning-failure-mode + agent-scaling + audio/multimodal — bracketing agent-reasoning-in-deployment primitive-class) — pivoting per Rule 27 from Run 63 attribution-guided-continual-learning + uncertainty-quantification-for-hallucination-detection + revisiting-parameter-based-knowledge-editing to reasoning-failures-2-axis-taxonomy + science-of-scaling-agent-systems-predictive-model + audio-language-hybrid-AR-NAR-training; web_search 4-query escape hatch fired (10th consecutive pitfall-83 — HF daily v2 returned 18 + 33 papers across 2026-06-25/26 but only 9 fresh after 5-store dedup with only 2 borderline LLM-keyword-matched; HF v3 href-regex pool 100% CV/3D-heavy; web_search surfaced 13 fresh LLM candidates after 5-store dedup via the Rule 36 combined-axis query template (reasoning-failure-mode + agent-scaling + embodied-agent + text-to-X multimodal) — Rule 36 combined-axis query WORKED, fresh-LLM return at typical base rate ~13 vs prior cycle's 3-14). Three picks:
- [[large-language-model-reasoning-failures-2602.06176]] — Large Language Model Reasoning Failures (02-05) — provides the *first comprehensive survey dedicated to reasoning failures in LLMs*, addressing the structural *fragmentation-primitive* failure mode where the field's understanding of reasoning failures is scattered across isolated studies with no unified taxonomy, no consistent definitions, and no mapping between diagnosis and remediation; Song, Peiyang; Han, Pengrui; Goodman, Noah introduce a novel *2-axis categorization framework* distinguishing *embodied* (grounded in physical/digital action) from *non-embodied* (pure symbolic reasoning) reasoning, with the latter further subdivided into *informal* (intuitive) and *formal* (logical), and classifying reasoning failures along a complementary axis into three types: *fundamental failures* intrinsic to LLM architectures (broadly affecting downstream tasks), *application-specific limitations* (manifest in particular domains), and *robustness issues* (inconsistent performance across minor variations); for each reasoning failure provides clear definition, root-cause analysis, and mitigation strategies, arguing that sustained attention to failure modes is essential to ensure future LLMs not only perform better on reasoning tasks but *fail better* (gracefully, transparently, recoverably); surfacing *reasoning-modality-as-taxonomy-axis primitive* × *failure-scope-as-orthogonal-primitive* as the structurally correct primitive for LLM failure studies, distinct from single-axis classifications because the load-bearing axes are *what kind of reasoning* (embodied/non-embodied/informal/formal) AND *what kind of failure* (fundamental/application/robustness) independently — and *diagnosis-to-mitigation-mapping primitive* as the load-bearing *actionable-primitive* that distinguishes survey from mere literature catalog, plus *graceful-failure-as-design-goal primitive* as the load-bearing *failure-quality primitive* that complements performance metrics. 2026-02-05; discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-26 (reasoning-failure / llm-survey / embodied-reasoning / robustness / fundamental-failure / application-failure / graceful-failure theme — **first 2-axis embodied-vs-non-embodied × fundamental-vs-application-vs-robustness reasoning-failure taxonomy survey in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(reasoning.failures.*survey|llm.reasoning.failures|embodied.*reasoning.*taxonomy|fundamental.*application.*robustness)"` returning empty). Complements the [[reasoning-models-struggle-to-control-their-chains-of-thought-2603.05706]] (sibling Run 61 CoT-monitorability work — Reasoning-Models-Struggle-to-Control measures *CoT-vs-output controllability asymmetry* (23× for Claude Sonnet 4.5), LLM-Reasoning-Failures surveys *the broader landscape of reasoning failures* with embodied/non-embodied × fundamental/application/robustness axes — together they bracket the reasoning-failure surface between *verbalization-side controllability measurement* (Reasoning-Models-Struggle-to-Control) and *broad reasoning-failure taxonomy* (LLM-Reasoning-Failures)), [[when-the-chain-of-thought-knows-better-failure-modes-in-multi-turn-reasoning-models-2606.10740]] (sibling Run 60 multi-turn-reasoning-failure work — When-the-Chain-of-Thought-Knows-Better diagnoses *CoT-Output-2x2-matrix failure modes* in multi-turn reasoning models, LLM-Reasoning-Failures provides *the broad taxonomy* of reasoning failures across reasoning types — together they bracket the reasoning-failure surface between *per-turn multi-turn failure attribution* (When-the-Chain-of-Thought-Knows-Better) and *cross-domain failure taxonomy* (LLM-Reasoning-Failures)), [[why-reasoning-fails-to-plan-a-planning-centric-analysis-of-long-horizon-decision-making-in-llm-agents-2601.22311]] (sibling Run 60 planning-failure work — Why-Reasoning-Fails-to-Plan isolates *reasoning-vs-planning-orthogonal-axes failure mode* with FLARE future-aware-lookahead, LLM-Reasoning-Failures provides *the broader reasoning-failure taxonomy* — together they bracket the reasoning-failure surface between *planning-specific failure mechanism* (Why-Reasoning-Fails-to-Plan) and *general reasoning-failure taxonomy* (LLM-Reasoning-Failures)), [[a-theoretical-model-for-task-routing-in-mixture-of-expert-transformers-2606.14398]] (sibling Run 62 MoE-theoretical work — Task-Routing-Theory shows MoE *task-expert specialization as a theorem*, LLM-Reasoning-Failures provides *the failure-mode taxonomy* against which successful reasoning must be measured — together they bracket the reasoning surface between *theoretical-task-routing-guarantees* (Task-Routing-Theory) and *empirical-failure-mode-catalog* (LLM-Reasoning-Failures)), [[reasoninglens-hierarchical-visualization-and-diagnostic-auditing-for-large-reasoning-models-2606.23404]] (sibling Run 60 trace-auditing work — ReasoningLens provides *hierarchical-trace-strategy-vs-execution diagnostics*, LLM-Reasoning-Failures provides *the failure-mode taxonomy* that diagnostics need to identify — together they bracket the reasoning-diagnostics surface between *per-trace audit* (ReasoningLens) and *per-failure-mode classification* (LLM-Reasoning-Failures)), and [[emergent-concepts]] (parent wiki page) entries on this page by surfacing *reasoning-modality-as-taxonomy-axis* × *failure-scope-as-orthogonal-primitive* as the structurally correct primitive for LLM failure studies where the failure mode of *plausible-but-flawed reasoning* is structurally invisible to single-axis classifications but becomes tractable when reasoning type (embodied/non-embodied/informal/formal) and failure scope (fundamental/application/robustness) are independently classified — bridging fragmented research efforts into a unified framework with diagnosis-to-mitigation mappings.
- [[towards-a-science-of-scaling-agent-systems-2512.08296]] — Towards a Science of Scaling Agent Systems (12-09) — addresses the structural *no-predictive-model-for-agent-systems* failure mode where the field's understanding of how LLM-based agent systems scale across dimensions (coordination, model capability, system/task factors) is empirically fragmented with no quantitative framework for predicting when coordination helps, hurts, or saturates; Kim, Yubin; Gu, Ken; Park, Chanwoo; Park, Chunjong; Schmidgall, Samuel; Heydari, A. Ali; Yan, Yao; Zhang, Zhihan; Zhuang, Yuchen; Liu, Yun; Malhotra, Mark; Liang, Paul Pu; Park, Hae Won; Yang, Yuzhe; Xu, Xuhai; Du, Yilun; Patel, Shwetak; Althoff, Tim; McDuff, Daniel; Liu, Xin introduce *quantitative scaling principles for agent systems* as a *predictive model*, capturing how performance varies with coordination, model capability, and measurable system/task factors; perform *controlled evaluations across 260 configurations* spanning six agentic benchmarks, five canonical architectures (Single-Agent and four Multi-Agent: Independent, Centralized, Decentralized, Hybrid), and three LLM families, with standardized tools, prompts, and compute to isolate architectural effects; achieving a *cross-validated R²=0.373* across all six benchmarks (R²=0.413 with task-grounded capability metric); identifying a robust *capability-saturation effect* (coordination yields diminishing returns once single-agent baselines exceed certain performance), *task-modality-dependence* (tool-heavy tasks appear to incur multi-agent overhead), and *centralized-verification-effect* (architectures without centralized verification tend to propagate errors more than those with centralized verification); surfacing *agent-scaling-science-as-predictive-discipline primitive* as the structurally correct primitive for agent-system research, distinct from empirical-observation because the load-bearing axis is *cross-validated predictive power over 260 controlled configurations* rather than *single-configuration benchmark results* — and *capability-saturation-effect primitive* as the load-bearing *diminishing-returns primitive* that explains when multi-agent architectures help vs. hurt, plus *centralized-verification-as-error-suppression primitive* as the load-bearing *architectural-feature-as-error-control primitive*. 2025-12-09; discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-26 (agent-systems / scaling-laws / multi-agent / coordination / single-agent-baseline / capability-saturation / centralized-verification theme — **first quantitative scaling-science-for-agent-systems paper with 260 controlled configurations and cross-validated R²=0.373 predictive model in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(scaling.agent.systems|agent.scaling.science|quantitative.agent.scaling|capability.saturation|coordination.*diminishing.returns)"` returning empty). Complements the [[benchmarking-open-ended-multi-agent-coordination-in-language-agents-2606.08340]] (sibling multi-agent-coordination-benchmark work — Open-Ended-Multi-Agent-Coordination-Benchmark evaluates *open-ended multi-agent coordination in language agents*, Towards-a-Science-of-Scaling-Agent-Systems provides *the quantitative scaling principles* that coordination benchmarks must satisfy — together they bracket the multi-agent-coordination surface between *empirical coordination-benchmark design* (Open-Ended-Multi-Agent-Coordination-Benchmark) and *quantitative scaling-science foundations* (Towards-a-Science-of-Scaling-Agent-Systems)), [[deep-research-in-physical-sciences-a-multi-agent-framework-and-comprehensive-benchmark-2606.18648]] (sibling multi-agent-physical-sciences work — Deep-Research-in-Physical-Sciences provides a *multi-agent framework* for physical sciences research, Towards-a-Science-of-Scaling-Agent-Systems provides *the predictive scaling model* that such domain-specific multi-agent systems must scale within — together they bracket the MAS-application surface between *domain-specific multi-agent frameworks* (Deep-Research-in-Physical-Sciences) and *general quantitative scaling-science* (Towards-a-Science-of-Scaling-Agent-Systems)), [[let-llms-judge-each-other-multi-agent-peer-reviewed-reasoning-for-medical-question-answering-2606.15419]] (sibling multi-agent-peer-review work — Multi-Agent-Peer-Reviewed-Reasoning applies *peer review* across LLM agents for medical QA, Towards-a-Science-of-Scaling-Agent-Systems provides *the quantitative scaling framework* that peer-review architectures instantiate — together they bracket the multi-agent-coordination surface between *peer-review coordination protocols* (Multi-Agent-Peer-Reviewed-Reasoning) and *quantitative scaling-science* (Towards-a-Science-of-Scaling-Agent-Systems)), [[memory-r2-fair-credit-assignment-for-long-horizon-memory-augmented-llm-agents-2605.21768]] (sibling Run 56 memory-credit-assignment work — Memory-R2 isolates *memory-credit-assignment fairness* primitives, Towards-a-Science-of-Scaling-Agent-Systems provides *the agent-system-scaling-science framework* where memory credit assignment is one scaling dimension — together they bracket the long-horizon-agent surface between *credit-assignment-isolation-primitive* (Memory-R2) and *quantitative-system-scaling* (Towards-a-Science-of-Scaling-Agent-Systems)), [[a-theoretical-model-for-task-routing-in-mixture-of-expert-transformers-2606.14398]] (sibling Run 62 MoE-theoretical work — Task-Routing-Theory shows MoE *task-expert specialization as a theorem*, Towards-a-Science-of-Scaling-Agent-Systems provides *the agent-system-level scaling model* where MoE-style task-routing is one architectural choice — together they bracket the scaling surface between *intra-model task-routing theoretical guarantees* (Task-Routing-Theory) and *inter-agent system scaling* (Towards-a-Science-of-Scaling-Agent-Systems)), and [[emergent-concepts]] (parent wiki page) entries on this page by surfacing *agent-scaling-science-as-predictive-discipline* as the load-bearing primitive for agent systems where the failure mode of *unpredictable-coordination-benefits* is structurally invisible to single-configuration evaluations but becomes tractable when 260 controlled configurations are used to derive a cross-validated predictive model with capability-saturation and centralized-verification patterns — bridging the gap between empirical observation and quantitative prediction in multi-agent system design.
- [[audio-language-model-needs-non-autoregressive-joint-training-2509.20072]] — From Text to Talk: Audio-Language Model Needs Non-Autoregressive Joint Training (09-24) — addresses the structural *modality-relation-uniformity* failure mode of existing multimodal training where models handling interleaved audio and text rely on a single autoregressive (AR) method, overlooking that *text depends on target-target relations* whereas *audio depends mainly on source-target relations* — a structural asymmetry in relation-dependency that uniform-AR methods fail to model; Liu, Tianqiao; Li, Xueyi; Wang, Hao; Li, Haoxuan; Chen, Zhichao; Luo, Weiqi; Liu, Zitao propose *Text-to-Talk (TtT)*, a unified audio-text framework that integrates AR text generation with NAR audio diffusion in a *single Transformer*, leveraging the *any-order AR property of absorbing discrete diffusion* to provide a unified training objective; design a *modality-aware attention mechanism* that enforces *causal decoding for text* while allowing *bidirectional modeling within audio spans*, and introduce *three training strategies* that reduce train-test discrepancies; employ *block-wise diffusion* during inference to synthesize audio in parallel while flexibly handling variable-length outputs; comprehensive experiments on Audio-QA, ASR, AA benchmarks; surfacing *modality-relation-asymmetry-as-load-bearing-primitive* as the structurally correct primitive for multimodal training, distinct from uniform-AR because the load-bearing axis is *text-vs-audio relation structure (target-target vs source-target)* rather than *single-relation-type AR modeling* — and *absorbing-discrete-diffusion-as-unifying-formalism primitive* as the load-bearing *training-objective-unification primitive* that bridges AR and NAR paradigms, plus *modality-aware-attention-as-decoupling-primitive* as the load-bearing *per-position causal-vs-bidirectional selection primitive* that decouples per-position decoding direction from modality. 2025-09-24; discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-26 (audio-language-model / non-autoregressive / multimodal-llm / absorbing-diffusion / modality-aware-attention / speech-to-speech theme — **first AR-text + NAR-audio hybrid-training paradigm with modality-aware attention and absorbing-discrete-diffusion in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(audio.language.*non.autoregressive|text.to.talk|modality.aware.attention|absorbing.discrete.diffusion|hybrid.ar.nar)"` returning empty). Complements the [[look-light-think-heavy-what-multimodal-chain-of-thought-reasoning-can-and-cannot-do-2606.22565]] (sibling multimodal-CoT work — Look-Light-Think-Heavy evaluates *multimodal chain-of-thought reasoning*, TtT provides *the unified AR+NAR training paradigm* for multimodal models that such reasoning operates within — together they bracket the multimodal-LLM surface between *multimodal-CoT reasoning evaluation* (Look-Light-Think-Heavy) and *hybrid audio+text training* (TtT)), [[causal-rcm-a-unified-teacher-forcing-and-self-forcing-open-recipe-for-autoregressive-diffusion-distillation-in-streaming-video-generation-and-interactive-world-models-2606.25473]] (sibling autoregressive-diffusion-distillation work — Causal-rCM unifies *teacher-forcing and self-forcing recipes for autoregressive diffusion distillation*, TtT provides *the AR+NAR hybrid training paradigm* such recipes target — together they bracket the AR+NAR-diffusion surface between *unified training recipes for AR-diffusion* (Causal-rCM) and *hybrid AR-text + NAR-audio training* (TtT)), [[shuttermuse-capture-time-photography-guidance-with-mllms-2606.25763]] (sibling MLLM-application work — ShutterMuse applies *MLLMs* for capture-time photography guidance, TtT provides *the AR+NAR hybrid training paradigm* for multimodal conversational systems — together they bracket the multimodal-LLM surface between *photography-guidance application* (ShutterMuse) and *hybrid audio+text training* (TtT)), [[a-latent-computational-mode-in-large-language-models-2601.08058]] (sibling Run 61 SAE-reasoning work — Latent-Computational-Mode identifies *latent-reasoning features* with single-feature causal steering, TtT provides *the AR+NAR hybrid training paradigm* where such latent features can also surface in audio — together they bracket the LLM-internals surface between *single-feature-reasoning-steering* (Latent-Computational-Mode) and *hybrid-modality-training* (TtT)), [[dart-diffusion-inspired-speculative-decoding-for-fast-llm-inference-2601.19278]] (sibling Run 61 diffusion-parallel-decoding work — DART uses *diffusion-inspired parallel logit prediction* for fast inference, TtT uses *block-wise diffusion* for parallel audio synthesis — together they bracket the diffusion-decoding surface between *parallel-text-decoding* (DART) and *parallel-audio-synthesis* (TtT)), and [[emergent-concepts]] (parent wiki page) entries on this page by surfacing *modality-relation-asymmetry-as-load-bearing-primitive* as the structurally correct primitive for multimodal training where the failure mode of *forcing AR-on-audio or NAR-on-text* is structurally invisible to single-modality evaluations but becomes tractable when text-vs-audio relation structure (target-target vs source-target) is recognized and *modality-aware attention* with *absorbing-discrete-diffusion* is used to enforce causal decoding for text and bidirectional modeling within audio spans — bridging AR and NAR paradigms under a single unified training objective with parallel block-wise inference.
