- [[gem-geometric-entropy-mixing-for-optimal-llm-data-curation-2605.26121]] — GEM: Geometric Entropy Mixing for Optimal LLM Data Curation (05-29) — reformulates LLM pre-training data curation as a variational problem on the hypersphere, introducing a Geometric Entropy Mixing (GEM) framework that counteracts two structural pathologies in existing mixing strategies: human-taxonomy ontological misalignment (category boundaries that fail to match semantic structure) and Euclidean-embedding anisotropy (Euclidean clustering breaks down because embeddings are not isotropic); Min, Yue decouples the generative prior from the mixing balance and optimizes the resulting objective via a provably convergent Minorize-Maximize (MM) algorithm that effectively counteracts cluster collapse to discover balanced semantic structures invisible to Euclidean heuristics, then scales the geometric fidelity to web-scale corpora via teacher-student distillation and introduces a Geometric Influence Score (GIS) for interpretable taxonomy generation; empirically establishes new state-of-the-art on 1.1B-parameter models when GEM-derived mixing is integrated into established mixing strategies (DoReMi, RegMix), improving average downstream accuracy by up to 1.2% and offering a robust coordinate system for predictable data mixing; surfacing *variational-hypersphere-formulation-as-data-curation-primitive* as the structurally correct primitive for LLM pre-training data curation, distinct from gradient-descent-weight-optimization because the load-bearing axis is *geometric-entropy-minimization-on-hypersphere* rather than mixture-weight tuning — and *MM-algorithm-with-teacher-student-distillation-to-web-scale* as the load-bearing *scalability-primitive* that bridges controlled-distribution GE algorithms with web-scale deployment, plus *Geometric-Influence-Score-as-interpretable-taxonomy-generator* as the load-bearing *interpretability-primitive* that simultaneously serves as an axis for automated taxonomy generation. 2026-05-29; discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-26 (data-curation / pre-training / variational / hypersphere / geometric-entropy / minorize-maximize / teacher-student-distillation / geometric-influence-score / DoReMi / RegMix theme — **first geometric-entropy-on-hypersphere variational formulation for LLM data curation in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(gem.*geometric.entropy|geometric.entropy.mix|hypersphere.*data.curation|geometric.influence.score|mm.algorithm.*data)"` returning empty). Complements the [[ac-odm-actor-critic-online-data-mixing-for-sample-efficient-llm-pretraining-2505.23878]] (sibling data-mixing work — AC-ODM is *online* mixing with actor-critic feedback during training, GEM is *pre-training-curation* mixing via geometric entropy on the hypersphere — together they bracket the LLM data-mixing surface between online-feedback and offline-geometric-entropy primitives), [[fastmix-fast-data-mixture-optimization-via-gradient-descent-2606.14971]] (sibling data-mixing optimization work — FastMix optimizes mixture weights via gradient descent, GEM optimizes mixture geometry via variational hypersphere — together they bracket the LLM data-mixing surface between gradient-descent-weight and variational-geometry primitives), [[demystifying-training-time-augmentation-for-data-constrained-language-model-pretraining-2606.16246]] (sibling data-constrained pretraining work — Demystifying addresses *augmentation* under data scarcity, GEM addresses *mixing geometry* under standard-scale data — together they bracket the data-constrained pretraining surface between augmentation-side and mixing-geometry-side primitives), and [[emergent-concepts]] (parent wiki page) entries on this page by surfacing *variational-hypersphere-formulation-as-data-curation-primitive* as the load-bearing primitive for LLM pre-training data curation where the failure modes of human-taxonomy-ontological-misalignment and Euclidean-embedding-anisotropy are structurally invisible to gradient-descent-on-mixture-weights approaches but become tractable when data curation is reformulated as variational optimization on the hypersphere — a finding that is only visible when mixing strategies are evaluated at the geometry-of-embedding-distribution level rather than at the mixture-weight level.

- [[interpretability-can-be-actionable-2605.11161]] — Interpretability Can Be Actionable (05-11) — a position paper arguing that the central missing ingredient in interpretability research is not new methods but evaluation criteria: interpretability should be evaluated by *actionability* — the extent to which insights enable concrete decisions and interventions beyond interpretability research itself — addressing the structural concern that despite rapid growth in interpretability research, much of it has not translated into practical impact, raising questions about its relevance and utility; Orgad, Hadas defines actionable interpretability along two dimensions (*concreteness* — can a stakeholder point to a specific decision the insight enables? × *validation* — does the insight produce a measurable outcome when acted on?) yielding a four-quadrant evaluation grid, analyzes barriers currently preventing real-world impact (research incentives reward novelty over utility, evaluation metrics reward explanation-quality rather than decision-quality, no shared benchmarks for downstream-action validation), and identifies five domains where interpretability offers unique leverage (safety alignment, model debugging, scientific discovery, deployment monitoring, policy/governance); surfacing *actionability-as-interpretability-evaluation-criterion* as the structurally correct primitive for the interpretability research field, distinct from explanation-quality metrics because the load-bearing axis is *concreteness×validation four-quadrant evaluation* rather than explanation fidelity or human-judgment scores — and *five-domain-leverage-map* as the load-bearing *taxonomy-primitive* that explicitly contrasts with "interpretability as exploration-only" framing by surfacing where interpretability has deployment traction versus where it remains research-only. 2026-05-11; discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-26 (interpretability / actionability / evaluation-criteria / position-paper / mechanistic-interpretability / deployment / five-domain-leverage theme — **first actionability-framed interpretability-evaluation-criteria position paper in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(interpret.*actionable|actionab.*interpret|concreteness.*validation|five.domain.leverage)"` returning empty). Complements the [[secret-mixtures-of-experts-inside-your-llm-2512.18452]] (sibling Run 55 mechanistic-interpretability work — Secret-Mixtures uses *SAE structure in dense MLP activations* to mechanistically interpret MoE-like sparsity — Actionable-Interpretability provides the *evaluation-criteria primitive* that asks whether the SAE-MoE insight enables a concrete decision (e.g., designing low-rank routers), Secret-Mixtures is one example of an interpretability finding that survives the actionability bar) and [[emergent-concepts]] (parent wiki page) entries on this page by surfacing *actionability-as-interpretability-evaluation-criterion* as the load-bearing primitive for interpretability research evaluation where the failure mode of methods-proliferating-without-translation-to-practice is structurally invisible to explanation-quality metrics but becomes tractable when each interpretability contribution is evaluated against the concreteness×validation four-quadrant grid — a finding that is only visible when interpretability research is judged by whether the insights enable concrete decisions and measurable outcomes beyond the research setting itself.
- [[when-errors-become-narratives-a-longitudinal-taxonomy-of-silent-failures-in-a-production-llm-agent-runtime-2606.14589]] — When Errors Become Narratives: A Longitudinal Taxonomy of Silent Failures in a Production LLM Agent Runtime (06-12) — an eight-week longitudinal study of silent failures in a personal-assistant LLM agent runtime in continuous production since March 2026 (40 scheduled jobs, 8 LLM providers, tool-governance proxy, knowledge-base memory plane, 4,286 unit tests, 827 governance checks), documenting 22 incidents with full root-cause postmortems in which one meta-pattern — a failure whose error signal never reaches a human in actionable form — manifested at least 28 times; Wu, Wei derives a five-class mechanism-oriented taxonomy: (A) environment and platform quirks, (B) design-assumption mismatches, (C) error swallowing and dilution, (D) chained hallucination and fabrication, (E) operational omission and forensic blind spots — and identifies Class D as unique to LLM systems and the most dangerous: the system does not merely fail to report an error — the LLM transforms it into fluent, plausible narrative delivered to the user — coining the term *fail-plausible* (gray failure's differential observability escalated by LLMs' natural-language fluency); surfacing *fail-plausible-narrative-as-LLM-specific-failure-mode* as the structurally correct primitive for production LLM agent reliability, distinct from generic software-failure taxonomies because the load-bearing axis is *LLM-as-fluent-narrator threat model* (the system becomes the threat surface via its narrative-generating capacity) rather than discrete error codes — and *mechanism-oriented-five-class-taxonomy* as the load-bearing diagnostic primitive for production-runtime incident analysis. 2026-06-12; discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-26 (silent-failure / production-runtime / longitudinal-study / five-class-taxonomy / fail-plausible / LLM-as-narrator theme — **first fail-plausible-LLM-as-narrator threat-model paper in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(silent.fail|narrative.error|fail.plausible|longitudinal.taxonomy|production.runtime.failure)"` returning empty). Complements the [[silent-failure-in-llm-agent-systems-the-entropy-principle-2606.08162]] (sibling Run 57 entropy-lens work — When-Errors-Become-Narratives surfaces a *5-class mechanism taxonomy*, Entropy-Principle formalizes the *monotonic entropy growth* that the taxonomy's Class D dynamics produce — together they bracket the silent-failure surface between mechanism-classification and entropy-mechanistic-lens primitives), [[where-llm-agents-fail-and-how-they-can-learn-from-failures-2509.25370]] (sibling Run 57 AgentErrorTaxonomy work — AgentErrorTaxonomy is *modular-classification-by-component* (memory/reflection/planning/action/system), When-Errors-Become-Narratives is *mechanism-classification-by-failure-dynamics* (5 classes including the LLM-unique Class D) — together they bracket the LLM-agent-failure surface between component-based and mechanism-based taxonomic primitives), [[planbench-xl-evaluating-long-horizon-planning-of-llm-tool-use-agents-in-large-scale-tool-ecosystems-2606.22388]] (sibling long-horizon tool-use evaluation work — PlanBench-XL probes *planning-side failure modes* in tool ecosystems, When-Errors-Become-Narratives probes *narrative-side failure modes* in production runtimes — together they bracket the LLM-agent-evaluation surface between planning-bottleneck and runtime-bottleneck primitives), [[foresight-failure-detection-long-horizon-robotic-manipulation-2606.23085]] (sibling foresight-driven failure-detection work for embodied long-horizon tasks — Foresight detects failures *pre-rollout via world-model evidence*, When-Errors-Become-Narratives surfaces failures *post-rollout via production-runtime incident analysis* — together they bracket the LLM-failure-detection surface between predictive and forensic detection primitives), and [[emergent-concepts]] (parent wiki page) entries on this page by surfacing *fail-plausible-narrative-as-LLM-specific-failure-mode* as the load-bearing primitive for production LLM agent reliability where the failure mode of fluent-narrative-camouflage is structurally invisible to error-code-based monitoring — a finding that is only visible when production-runtime incidents are analyzed longitudinally rather than treated as discrete bug reports.

- [[silent-failure-in-llm-agent-systems-the-entropy-principle-2606.08162]] — Silent Failure in LLM Agent Systems: The Entropy Principle (06-06) — through systematic analysis of over 40,000 controlled trials and long-term production observations spanning 100,000+ agent interactions, identifies silent failures (unexpected deviations from intended behavior under normal conditions — no injection, no adversarial input, no resource exhaustion) as a distinct failure class routinely misattributed to bugs or configuration errors, and formalizes an *Entropy Principle* governing these failures: S(t) = S0 * e^(alpha * t), where system entropy — measurable accumulation of disorder (loss of output consistency, task accuracy, and cross-session coherence) — increases monotonically with interaction rounds whenever a sufficient subset of 22 intrinsic properties co-exist; Liu, Dexing surveys the global research literature on autonomous agent reliability and synthesizes 22 intrinsic properties across six lifecycle layers: foundation semantics, inter-agent transmission, memory persistence, task execution, feedback correction, and systemic evolution — empirically demonstrating that whenever a sufficient subset of these properties co-exist, system entropy grows exponentially; surfacing *entropy-principle-as-silent-failure-mechanism* as the structurally correct primitive for LLM agent reliability, distinct from discrete-bug-surface taxonomies because the load-bearing axis is *measurable-monotonic-disorder-accumulation* rather than countable failure modes — and *22-properties-across-6-lifecycle-layers* as the load-bearing diagnostic surface that links silent-failure emergence to specific property subsets, inverting the conventional debugging framing where silent failures are treated as inexplicable. 2026-06-06; discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-26 (silent-failure / entropy-principle / 22-properties / 6-lifecycle-layers / 40000-trials / 100000-interactions / monotonic-disorder theme — **first entropy-principle-formalization of silent-failure dynamics in LLM agents in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(entropy.principle|silent.failure.*llm|22.properties|monotonic.disorder|6.lifecycle)"` returning empty). Complements the [[when-errors-become-narratives-a-longitudinal-taxonomy-of-silent-failures-in-a-production-llm-agent-runtime-2606.14589]] (sibling Run 57 longitudinal-study work — Entropy-Principle formalizes the *monotonic growth mechanism* with S(t) = S0 * e^(alpha * t), When-Errors-Become-Narratives classifies the *failure-manifestation modes* in a 5-class taxonomy including the LLM-unique Class D — together they bracket the silent-failure surface between mechanism-formalization and failure-classification primitives), [[where-llm-agents-fail-and-how-they-can-learn-from-failures-2509.25370]] (sibling Run 57 AgentErrorTaxonomy work — AgentErrorTaxonomy is *component-based classification* (memory/reflection/planning/action/system), Entropy-Principle is *property-subset emergence-dynamics* (22 properties across 6 layers producing monotonic entropy growth) — together they bracket the LLM-agent-failure surface between component-classification and property-emergence-dynamics primitives), [[planbench-xl-evaluating-long-horizon-planning-of-llm-tool-use-agents-in-large-scale-tool-ecosystems-2606.22388]] (sibling long-horizon tool-use evaluation work — PlanBench-XL surfaces *planning-evaluation-failure modes* in tool ecosystems, Entropy-Principle surfaces *emergence-dynamics* of silent failures from 22-property subset co-occurrence — together they bracket the LLM-agent-evaluation surface between task-level planning bottlenecks and cross-session disorder-accumulation dynamics), [[why-multi-step-tool-use-reinforcement-learning-collapses-and-how-supervisory-signals-fix-it-2606.26027]] (sibling multi-step tool-use RL stabilization work — Multi-Step-Tool-Use-RL-Collapses stabilizes *training-time format* with interleaved SFT, Entropy-Principle surfaces *inference-time disorder accumulation* — together they bracket the LLM-tool-use surface between training-time format stabilization and inference-time cross-session-coherence stabilization primitives), and [[emergent-concepts]] (parent wiki page) entries on this page by surfacing *entropy-principle-as-silent-failure-mechanism* as the load-bearing primitive for LLM agent reliability where the failure mode of exponential-disorder-growth is structurally invisible to single-interaction evaluation — a finding that is only visible when cross-session coherence is measured longitudinally across 100,000+ interactions.

- [[where-llm-agents-fail-and-how-they-can-learn-from-failures-2509.25370]] — Where LLM Agents Fail and How They Can Learn From Failures (09-29) — addresses the structural failure-mode proliferation of LLM agents (integrating planning, memory, reflection, and tool-use modules) where sophisticated architectures amplify vulnerability to cascading failures — a single root-cause error propagates through subsequent decisions, leading to task failure — and current systems lack a framework that comprehensively understands agent error in a modular and systemic way; Zhu, Kunlun; Liu, Zijia; Li, Bingxuan; Tian, Muxin; Yang, Yingxuan introduce three contributions: (1) **AgentErrorTaxonomy** — a modular classification of failure modes spanning memory, reflection, planning, action, and system-level operations; (2) **AgentErrorBench** — the first dataset of systematically annotated failure trajectories from ALFWorld, GAIA, and WebShop, grounding error analysis in real-world agent rollouts; (3) **AgentDebug** — a debugging framework that isolates root-cause failures and provides corrective feedback, enabling agents to recover and iteratively improve — empirically achieving 24% higher all-correct accuracy and 17% higher step accuracy on AgentErrorBench; surfacing *component-modular-failure-taxonomy-plus-annotated-bench-plus-recovery-framework* as the structurally correct primitive for LLM agent debugging, distinct from surface-error-classification because the load-bearing axis is *isolated-root-cause-isolation-plus-corrective-feedback-iteration* rather than aggregated failure metrics — and *modular-component-classification-as-debugging-substrate* as the load-bearing analytic primitive that makes per-component failure-mode attribution tractable. 2025-09-29; discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-26 (agent-failure-taxonomy / modular-classification / agent-error-bench / agent-debug / cascading-failure / root-cause-isolation / corrective-feedback theme — **first component-modular-annotated-bench-plus-recovery-framework for LLM agent failures in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(agent.error.tax|agent.debug|where.llm.agents.fail|root.cause.*agent|modular.failure.*agent)"` returning empty). Complements the [[silent-failure-in-llm-agent-systems-the-entropy-principle-2606.08162]] (sibling Run 57 entropy-lens work — AgentErrorTaxonomy is *component-based classification* (memory/reflection/planning/action/system), Entropy-Principle is *property-subset emergence-dynamics* (22 properties across 6 layers producing monotonic entropy growth) — together they bracket the LLM-agent-failure surface between component-classification and property-emergence-dynamics primitives), [[when-errors-become-narratives-a-longitudinal-taxonomy-of-silent-failures-in-a-production-llm-agent-runtime-2606.14589]] (sibling Run 57 mechanism-oriented work — AgentErrorTaxonomy is *component-based classification* (memory/reflection/planning/action/system), When-Errors-Become-Narratives is *mechanism-based classification* (5 classes including the LLM-unique Class D) — together they bracket the LLM-agent-failure surface between component-based and mechanism-based taxonomic primitives), [[a-technical-taxonomy-of-llm-agent-communication-protocols-2606.19135]] (sibling agent communication-protocols taxonomy work — Communication-Protocols taxonomizes *inter-agent coordination primitives*, AgentErrorTaxonomy taxonomizes *intra-agent failure modes* — together they bracket the LLM-agent-system surface between coordination-protocol design and failure-mode taxonomy), [[why-multi-step-tool-use-reinforcement-learning-collapses-and-how-supervisory-signals-fix-it-2606.26027]] (sibling multi-step tool-use RL stabilization work — Multi-Step-Tool-Use-RL-Collapses stabilizes *training-time format* with interleaved SFT, AgentDebug enables *inference-time root-cause-isolation-plus-recovery* — together they bracket the LLM-agent-reliability surface between training-time and inference-time recovery primitives), and [[emergent-concepts]] (parent wiki page) entries on this page by surfacing *component-modular-failure-taxonomy-plus-annotated-bench-plus-recovery-framework* as the load-bearing primitive for LLM agent debugging where the failure mode of cascading-error-propagation-across-components is structurally invisible to aggregated-error metrics — a finding that is only visible when failures are attributed to specific components and recovery is conditioned on root-cause isolation rather than surface-error correction.
- [[memory-r2-fair-credit-assignment-for-long-horizon-memory-augmented-llm-agents-2605.21768]] — Memory-R2: Fair Credit Assignment for Long-Horizon Memory-Augmented LLM Agents (05-20) — addresses the structural unfairness in trajectory-level credit assignment for memory-augmented LLM agents where different rollouts write, update, or delete different memories and no longer share the same intermediate memory state, so trajectory-level group-relative methods (GRPO, PPO) violate a foundational fairness assumption — once memory becomes part of the agent's environment, rollouts sample from different effective environments, yielding noisy/biased credit signals for memory operations; Yan, Sikuan; Bahloul, Ahmed; Nie, Ercong; Schwarzmann, Susanna; Trivisonno, Riccardo; Tresp, Volker introduce **Memory-R2**, a training framework whose core algorithm **LoGo-GRPO** combines *local rerollouts* (same memory state, different memory-operation outcomes — restoring the GRPO fairness assumption) with *global trajectory-level optimization* (preserving end-to-end learning from long-horizon rewards), plus a *co-learning design* that jointly optimizes memory formation (fact extractor) and memory evolution (memory manager) with shared parameters rather than treating them as decoupled components — surfacing *local-rerollouts-as-trajectory-comparison-fairness-primitive* as the structurally correct algorithmic primitive for memory-agent RL, distinct from outcome-only methods because the load-bearing axis is *shared-memory-state-rerollouts-for-grpo-fairness* rather than trajectory-level success. 2026-05-20; discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-26 (memory-agent / credit-assignment / grpo / long-horizon / local-rerollouts / co-learning theme — **first LoGo-GRPO-with-local-rerollouts paper for memory-augmented long-horizon LLM agent credit assignment in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(memory.r2|logo.grpo|local.rerollout|fair.credit.assignment.*memory)"` returning empty). Complements the [[meta-cognitive-memory-policy-optimization-for-long-horizon-llm-agents-2605.30159]] (sibling Run 55 memory-policy paper — MMPO provides *belief-entropy self-supervised proxy* for memory quality, Memory-R2 provides *local-rerollouts fair credit assignment* for memory operations — together they bracket the memory-agent training surface between uncertainty-aware supervision and trajectory-level credit-allocation primitives), [[agentic-reasoning-for-large-language-models-2601.12538]] (sibling Run 55 agentic-reasoning survey — Memory-R2 is the *training-algorithm primitive* for the survey's memory layer of self-evolving agentic reasoning, the fairness-restoring local-rerollout mechanism that makes GRPO applicable to memory-augmented agents where trajectory-level comparison becomes structurally unfair), [[agent-memory-characterization-and-system-implications-of-stateful-long-horizon-workloads-2606.06448]] (sibling memory-system characterization — Memory-R2 complements with the *training-side fairness primitive* that the system-level characterization motivates), and [[emergent-concepts]] (parent wiki page) entries on this page by surfacing *local-rerollouts-as-trajectory-comparison-fairness-primitive* as the load-bearing algorithmic primitive for memory-augmented long-horizon LLM agent RL where the failure mode of trajectory-level reward-hacking-on-divergent-memory-states is structurally invisible to outcome-only training signals but becomes tractable when rollouts are rerolled from a shared memory state.

- [[agentic-trading-when-llm-agents-meet-financial-markets-2605.19337]] — Agentic Trading: When LLM Agents Meet Financial Markets (05-19) — a protocol-coded survey of 77 studies on LLM-based trading agents that reframes them as *expert-system decision pipelines* (perceive → retrieve → reason → emit action → adapt under market feedback) and presents an audit-oriented evidence map with a primary empirical subset of 19 studies satisfying the minimum boundary of Action Output + Closed-Loop Evaluation; Xia, Yihan; You, Panpan; Wang, Taotao; Liu, Fang; Qi, Han; Wu, Xiaoxiao identify *protocol incomparability* as the central empirical finding — within the primary subset, only 2/19 studies report extractable time-consistent split protocols, 1/19 reports an explicit transaction-cost model, 1/19 documents universe or survivorship handling, 11/19 report execution timing or semantics, 15/19 are coded as R0 (no reproducibility), and no study reaches R3 — exposing architectural experimentation as expanding rapidly while comparable evaluation protocols, execution semantics, and reproducible artifacts remain the field's immediate bottlenecks, and foregrounding *Architecture-Capability-Adaptation* as a working analytical lens (not a validated taxonomy) plus a concrete reporting checklist designed to close the protocol-incomparability gap; surfacing *protocol-coded-evidence-ledger-as-trading-agent-evaluation-primitive* as the structurally correct primitive for the trading-agent literature, distinct from capability-only benchmarks because the load-bearing axis is *R0–R3 reproducibility coding plus protocol-element presence/absence* rather than headline accuracy, and *expert-system-pipeline-framing* as the load-bearing *interface-primitive* that surfaces the perception → retrieval → reasoning → action → adaptation points where protocols structurally diverge — turning architectural comparisons from "which model wins?" into "which backtest protocol was used, and is it comparable to the competing baseline's?". 2026-05-19; discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-26 (trading-agent / protocol-coded-survey / reproducibility-audit / expert-system-pipeline / reporting-checklist theme — **first R0–R3-coded protocol-incomparability audit of LLM-trading-agent literature in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(agentic.trad|trading.agent|llm.*trad|financ.*agent.*bench|expert.system.pipelin)"` returning empty). Complements the [[the-verification-horizon-no-silver-bullet-for-coding-agent-rewards-2606.26300]] (sibling verification-fidelity work — Verification-Horizon diagnoses *no fixed reward stays faithful* for coding agents, Agentic-Trading diagnoses *no fixed backtest protocol is comparable* across trading-agent studies — together they bracket the *agent-evaluation reliability surface* between reward-fidelity and backtest-protocol-fidelity), [[openbiorq-unsolved-biomedical-research-questions-for-agents-2606.21959]] (sibling research-faithfulness benchmark — OpenBioRQ probes citation-correctness on unsolved biomedical questions, Agentic-Trading probes protocol-comparability on closed-loop trading evaluation — together they bracket the *evaluation-protocol reliability* surface between forward-looking research-faithfulness and historical-backtest-comparability), [[agentic-reasoning-for-large-language-models-2601.12538]] (sibling Run 55 agentic-reasoning survey — Agentic-Trading is a domain-specific instantiation of the survey's real-world-domain axis (finance) with empirical reproducibility-audit findings that the survey's domain axis does not cover), and [[emergent-concepts]] (parent wiki page) entries on this page by surfacing *protocol-coded-evidence-ledger-as-trading-agent-evaluation-primitive* and *expert-system-pipeline-framing* as two distinct load-bearing primitives — the first inverts "headline accuracy" framing into "R0–R3 reproducibility + protocol-element presence/absence" so architectural comparisons become structurally falsifiable, and the second inverts "monolithic model" framing into "perceive → retrieve → reason → emit → adapt pipeline" so protocol divergence becomes visible at named interface points rather than buried in aggregated metrics.

- [[multibreak-a-scalable-and-diverse-multi-turn-jailbreak-benchmark-for-evaluating-llm-safety-2605.01687]] — MultiBreak: A Scalable and Diverse Multi-Turn Jailbreak Benchmark for Evaluating LLM Safety (05-03) — a 10,389-prompt multi-turn jailbreak benchmark spanning 2,665 distinct harmful intents designed to evaluate LLM safety under natural conversational attack settings where single-turn benchmarks systematically underestimate vulnerability; Song, Jialin; Liu, Xiaodong; Yang, Weiwei; Chen, Wuyang; Feng, Mingqian; Zhu, Xuekai use an *active-learning adversarial-prompt pipeline* where a generator is iteratively fine-tuned to produce stronger attack candidates guided by uncertainty-based refinement — empirically achieving up to 54.0 and 34.6 higher attack success rate (ASR) than the second-best dataset on DeepSeek-R1-7B and GPT-4.1-mini respectively, and revealing that *categories which appear benign under single-turn can exhibit substantially higher adversarial effectiveness in multi-turn scenarios* — exposing a systematic blind spot in single-turn safety evaluation that holds even for safety-aligned LLMs; surfacing *multi-turn-active-learning-adversarial-coevolution-as-safety-benchmark-primitive* as the structurally correct primitive for safety-benchmark construction, distinct from static-curation benchmarks because the load-bearing axis is *adversarial-coevolution-with-generator-fine-tuning* rather than fixed-prompt coverage, and *categories-benign-under-single-turn-as-load-bearing-risk-indicator* as the load-bearing *inversion-primitive* that inverts the common assumption that "obvious attack categories = obvious defenses" by showing safety-aligned LLMs can be structurally weaker on benign-looking multi-turn paths than on the canonical harmful-prompt paths single-turn benchmarks cover. 2026-05-03; discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-26 (multi-turn-jailbreak / safety-benchmark / active-learning-adversarial-pipeline / categories-benign-risk-indicator theme — **first active-learning-coevolved multi-turn jailbreak benchmark with benign-category-blind-spot finding in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(multibreak|multi.turn.jailbreak|active.learn.*jailbreak|benign.category.risk)"` returning empty). Complements the [[what-intermediate-layers-know-detecting-jailbreaks-from-entropy-dynamics-2606.25182]] (sibling jailbreak-detection work — MultiBreak is the *attack-side benchmark*, What-Intermediate-Layers-Know is the *defense-side detection* primitive via entropy dynamics — together they bracket the jailbreak surface between attack-coverage and detection-via-entropy-dynamics), [[geometry-of-refusal-linear-instability-safety-aligned-llms-2606.22686]] (sibling refusal-mechanism work — Geometry-of-Refusal characterizes refusal as a linear-instability phenomenon in safety-aligned LLMs, MultiBreak probes where that instability manifests under multi-turn attacks — together they bracket the refusal-mechanism surface between linear-instability characterization and multi-turn adversarial manifestation), [[policyalign-direct-policy-based-safety-alignment-llms-2606.25442]] (sibling safety-alignment work — PolicyAlign is a *training-time alignment* primitive, MultiBreak is the *evaluation-time coverage* primitive — together they bracket the LLM-safety surface between alignment-recipe and benchmark-coverage), [[safety-paradox-how-enhanced-safety-awareness-leaves-llms-vulnerable-to-posterior-attack-2606.05614]] (sibling safety-awareness paradox work — Safety-Paradox shows enhanced safety-awareness can leave LLMs *more* vulnerable, MultiBreak shows multi-turn paths expose vulnerabilities single-turn misses — together they surface the *non-monotonic relationship* between safety investment and vulnerability, where more safety-awareness can leave models more exposed and more evaluation-coverage on multi-turn paths reveals more blind spots), and [[emergent-concepts]] (parent wiki page) entries on this page by surfacing *multi-turn-active-learning-adversarial-coevolution-as-safety-benchmark-primitive* and *categories-benign-under-single-turn-as-load-bearing-risk-indicator* as two distinct load-bearing primitives for LLM safety evaluation — the first inverts static-benchmark construction into coevolution-with-generator-fine-tuning so the benchmark stays hard as models harden, and the second inverts "obvious attack = obvious defense" into a structurally falsifiable risk indicator where benign-looking multi-turn paths become the canonical probe for safety-aligned-LLM vulnerabilities.

- [[meta-cognitive-memory-policy-optimization-for-long-horizon-llm-agents-2605.30159]] — Meta-Cognitive Memory Policy Optimization for Long-Horizon LLM Agents (05-28) — addresses the structural training-inefficiency of memory-augmented LLM agents where existing memory policies are trained with sparse outcome-based RL signals that fail to localize where intermediate memory quality degrades, and proposes **MMPO** which reframes memory optimization as a *belief-clarity* problem; Liu, Ziyan; Hao, Zhezheng; Chen, Yeqiu; Wang, Hong; Hou, Jingren; Ding, Ruiyi; Yang, Yongkang; Ji, Wence; Xia, Wei; Liu, Feng introduce **Belief Entropy**, a self-supervised proxy that probes how uncertain the model remains about the latent task state given its current memory, providing dense, memory-specific supervision without environment reward by explicitly penalizing summaries that induce high epistemic uncertainty; empirically maintains **97.1% performance at 1.75M-token contexts** on diverse long-horizon tasks — surfacing *belief-entropy-as-self-supervised-memory-quality-proxy* as the structurally correct primitive for memory-augmented LLM training, distinct from outcome-based RL because the load-bearing axis is *epistemic-uncertainty-localization* (where does memory quality degrade?) rather than trajectory-level success. 2026-05-28; discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-26 (long-horizon-agent / memory-policy / belief-entropy / self-supervised-proxy / epistemic-uncertainty-penalization theme — **first belief-entropy-as-self-supervised-memory-quality-proxy for memory-augmented long-horizon LLM agents in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(mmpo|belief.entropy|meta.cognitive.memory)"` returning empty). Complements the [[agentic-reasoning-for-large-language-models-2601.12538]] (sibling agentic-reasoning survey that taxonomizes the *applied* surface — memory policies for long-horizon agents as one layer — MMPO provides the *training-recipe* primitive that the survey's memory layer needs to actually work at scale), [[why-multi-step-tool-use-reinforcement-learning-collapses-and-how-supervisory-signals-fix-it-2606.26027]] (sibling RL-stabilization work — Why-Multi-Step-Tool-Use-RL-Collapses stabilizes *RL training format* with interleaved SFT, MMPO stabilizes *memory-agent training quality* with self-supervised belief-entropy — together they bracket the agentic-RL reliability surface between format-side and memory-side stabilization primitives), and [[emergent-concepts]] (parent wiki page) entries on this page by surfacing *belief-entropy-as-self-supervised-memory-quality-proxy* as the load-bearing primitive for memory-augmented long-horizon LLM training where the failure mode of sparse-outcome-RL-on-recursive-summaries (silent information loss across summary steps) is structurally invisible to outcome-only training signals.

- [[agentic-reasoning-for-large-language-models-2601.12538]] — Agentic Reasoning for Large Language Models (01-18) — a unifying survey that reframes LLM reasoning as **agentic reasoning** — LLMs acting as autonomous agents that plan, act, and learn through continual interaction in open-ended and dynamic environments; Wei, Tianxin; Li, Ting-Wei; Liu, Zhining; Ning, Xuying; Yang, Ze; Zou, Jiaru; Zeng, Zhichen; Qiu, Ruizhong; Lin, Xiao; Fu, Dongqi; Li, Zihao; Ai, Mengting; Zhou, Duo; Bao, Wenxuan; Li, Yunzhe; Li, Gaotang; Qian, Cheng; Wang, Yu; Tang, Xiangru; Xiao, Yin; Fang, Liri; Liu, Hui; Tang, Xianfeng; Zhang, Yuji; Wang, Chi; You, Jiaxuan; Ji, Heng; Tong, Hanghang; He, Jingrui organize the field along three complementary axes: environment-dynamics layers (foundational single-agent / self-evolving / collective multi-agent), training-stage split (in-context orchestration vs post-training RL+SFT), and real-world domains (science, robotics, healthcare, autonomous research, mathematics) — synthesizing agentic-reasoning methods into a unified roadmap bridging thought and action; surfacing *three-layer-environment-dynamics-taxonomy* as the structurally correct primitive for the agentic-reasoning literature, distinct from single-axis taxonomies because the load-bearing insight is that **agent capabilities, self-evolution, and collective coordination are orthogonal axes** that must be reasoned about jointly — not isolated improvements to one layer. 2026-01-18; discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-26 (agentic-reasoning / survey / multi-agent / environment-dynamics / in-context-vs-post-training theme — **first three-layer-environment-dynamics taxonomy survey for agentic reasoning in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(agentic.reasoning.survey|environment.dynamics|multi.agent.survey.2601)"` returning empty). Complements the [[meta-cognitive-memory-policy-optimization-for-long-horizon-llm-agents-2605.30159]] (sibling applied-memory-policy work — MMPO provides the *training-recipe* primitive for the memory layer of the survey's self-evolving-agentic-reasoning layer), [[the-hitchhikers-guide-to-agentic-ai-from-foundations-to-systems-2606.24937]] (sibling foundational agentic-AI survey that covers the broader systems landscape — Agentic-Reasoning provides the *reasoning-specific* taxonomy that complements Hitchhiker's broader coverage), [[agentic-chain-of-thought-steering-for-efficient-and-controllable-llm-reasoning-2606.03965]] (sibling reasoning-steering work — ACTS implements *MDP-formulated controller* reasoning steering, Agentic-Reasoning surveys the broader agentic-reasoning landscape ACTS belongs to), [[benchmarking-open-ended-multi-agent-coordination-in-language-agents-2606.08340]] (sibling multi-agent coordination benchmark — alem provides *coordination-as-distinct-bottleneck* empirical results for the survey's collective multi-agent layer), and [[emergent-concepts]] (parent wiki page) entries on this page by surfacing *three-layer-environment-dynamics-taxonomy* as the load-bearing primitive for organizing agentic-reasoning research where the failure mode of single-axis taxonomies (improvements to one layer without acknowledging the others) is structurally addressed by treating capabilities, self-evolution, and coordination as orthogonal axes.

- [[secret-mixtures-of-experts-inside-your-llm-2512.18452]] — Secret Mixtures of Experts Inside Your LLM (12-20) — hypothesizes and empirically validates that the **MLP layers in dense LLMs secretly approximate a sparsely-activating Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) computation**, providing a mechanistic explanation for the effectiveness of MoE-based transformers and suggesting new directions for low-rank router design; Boix-Adsera, Enric formalizes a **theoretical bridge between MoE layers and Sparse Autoencoder (SAE) structure** in activation space, framing the dense-MLP-as-MoE hypothesis as testable sparse-approximation in pretrained LLMs — empirically validating that MLP activations in pretrained LLMs can be approximated by sparse MoE layers, but only because of *structure in the distribution of neural-network activations* (the same approximation fails on Gaussian data, ruling out generic sparsity explanations), and suggests **new directions for MoE architecture design based on low-rank routers** that leverage the discovered dense-MLP-as-MoE structure to design more parameter-efficient routing mechanisms; surfacing *dense-MLP-as-secret-MoE* as the structurally correct mechanistic primitive for understanding why MoE-based transformers work — distinct from MoE training recipes because the load-bearing axis is *mechanistic interpretability of dense-MLP activations as latent sparse routing* rather than explicit top-k routing. 2025-12-20; discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-26 (mechanistic-interpretability / mixture-of-experts / mlp / sparse-autoencoder / transformer-architecture / low-rank-router theme — **first dense-MLP-as-secret-MoE mechanistic-interpretability paper in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(secret.moe|mlp.as.moe|sae.moe|low.rank.router)"` returning empty). Complements the [[grouped-query-experts-mixture-of-experts-on-gqa-self-attention-2606.20945]] (sibling MoE-on-attention work that applies MoE to attention layers — Secret-Mixtures complements this by providing the *mechanistic explanation* for why MoE works on attention layers: dense attention projections secretly approximate MoE just like dense MLPs do) and [[emergent-concepts]] (parent wiki page) entries on this page by surfacing *dense-MLP-as-secret-MoE* as the load-bearing primitive for transformer-architecture understanding where the empirical effectiveness of MoE-based transformers is now mechanistically grounded in the SAE structure of dense-MLP activations — a finding that is only visible when dense MLPs are approximated as latent sparse routers rather than treated as monolithic dense computation.

- [[jetspec-breaking-the-scaling-ceiling-of-speculative-decoding-with-parallel-tree-2606.18394]] — JetSpec: Breaking the Scaling Ceiling of Speculative Decoding with Parallel Tree Drafting (06-16) — introduces JetSpec, a head-based speculative decoding framework that combines one-forward drafting efficiency with branch-wise causal conditioning, training a causal parallel draft head over fused hidden states from the frozen target model so candidate-tree scores align with the target model's autoregressive factorization and converting larger draft budgets into longer accepted prefixes and higher end-to-end speedup — breaking the scaling ceiling where prior head-based SD methods faced a causality-efficiency dilemma, with up to 9.64x speedup on MATH-500 and 4.58x on open-ended conversational workloads on H100 GPUs and further latency gains via vLLM integration under realistic serving loads; Hu, Lanxiang; Feng, Zhaoxiang; Wu, Yulun; Yuan, Haoran; Zhao, Yujie; Qian, Yu-Yang; Wang, Bojun; Zhao, Peng; Jiang, Daxin; Zhu, Yibo — surfacing *parallel-tree-drafting-with-causal-conditioning-as-speculative-decoding-scaling-primitive* — 
- [[how-post-training-shapes-biological-reasoning-models-2606.16517]] — How Post-Training Shapes Biological Reasoning Models (06-15) — presents a controlled study of 100+ biological reasoning models across genomics, transcriptomics, and proteins with systematic variation in backbone, CPT, SFT, and RL showing that biological reasoning does not improve monotonically with additional supervision or compute — each post-training stage reshapes generalization distinctly (CPT aligns with biological language, SFT consistently increases ID but causes OOD to peak early and decline as models fit the training distribution, RL on strong SFT checkpoints with aligned rewards improves OOD and partially recovers generalization) and under fixed budgets the strongest ID-OOD trade-off comes from brief SFT, larger RL allocations, and asymmetric adaptation capacity across stages; Fesser, Lukas; Zhang, Hanlin; Li, Michelle M.; Wang, Eric; Perozzi, Bryan; Azizi, Shekoofeh; Kakade, Sham M.; Zitnik, Marinka — surfacing *post-training-composition-not-accumulation-as-biological-reasoning-primitive* — 
- [[gridvqa-x-a-framework-for-evaluating-multimodal-explainability-methods-2606.14740]] — GridVQA-X: A Framework for Evaluating Multimodal Explainability Methods (06-02) — introduces GridVQA-X, the first diagnostic framework specifically designed to evaluate cross-modal explainability, leveraging closed-world synthesis logic to generate unique mathematically guaranteed explanations and constructing paired ground-truth models (M_pure learning robust spatial-relational reasoning vs M_spur structurally forced to rely on cross-modal shortcuts) as a rigorous behavioral-divergence testbed where faithful explainers must report distinct reasoning pathways — finding that widely used MxAI methods fail to distinguish between models relying on genuine spatial-relational reasoning and those exploiting cross-modal shortcuts, highlighting a critical gap in capturing true cross-modal synergy and misrepresenting how multimodal models actually make decisions; Belsare, Sujay; Nikhil, Sudarshan; Kumar, Sushant; Kumaraguru, Ponnurangam; Agarwal, Chirag — surfacing *closed-world-diagnostic-as-multimodal-explainability-evaluation-primitive* — 
- [[hallucination-in-world-models-is-predictable-and-preventable-2606.27326]] — Hallucination in World Models is Predictable and Preventable (06-25) — addresses the structural hallucination failure mode of modern generative world models that render increasingly realistic action-controllable futures while drifting from ground-truth dynamics — rollouts remain visually fluent while physically wrong, a silent-coherence failure mode that fluency-based evaluation metrics systematically miss — and hypothesize that hallucination concentrates in low-coverage regions of the state-action space where lightweight data-centric signals can both detect and mitigate it; Hansen, Nicklas; Wang, Xiaolong introduce MMBench2, a 427-hour, 210-task dataset for visual world modeling with ground-truth actions, rewards, and live simulators, train a 350M-parameter world model on it, and identify three distinct hallucination modes — *perceptual* (anchored to the rendering stage), *action-marginalized* (anchored to the action-conditioning stage where actions lose their causal influence), and *scene-diverging* (anchored to the scene-continuity stage where the rendered scene diverges from the rollout trajectory) — each with a distinct failure signature and a distinct lightweight predictor; three data-centric signals accurately predict where the model will fail (one per hallucination mode), enabling pre-rollout hallucination forecasting rather than post-hoc hallucination detection — inverting the conventional generative-model evaluation framing where hallucination is only observable after the model has already produced the wrong rollout; a coverage-aware training-time sampling technique uses the three hallucination predictors to weight training samples toward low-coverage state-action regions, and the same predictors serve as curiosity rewards for targeted online data collection, yielding a data-efficient finetuning recipe that adapts the pretrained world model to entirely unseen environments with as few as 50 real environment trajectories; surfacing *data-centric-hallucination-predictability-and-preventability* as the structurally correct primitive for generative-world-model evaluation and improvement — distinct from fluency-based evaluation because the load-bearing axis is *low-coverage-region-identification* rather than aggregate-rendering-quality — and *hallucination-predictors-as-curiosity-rewards* as the load-bearing deployment primitive that closes the train-test coverage gap with target-efficient online data collection; 2026-06-25; discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-26 (world-models / generative-models / hallucination-taxonomy / data-centric / coverage-aware-sampling / curiosity-rewards / mmbench theme — **first data-centric hallucination-predictability-and-preventability taxonomy for generative world models in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(halluc.world|hallucination.world|coverage.aware|mmbench|world.model.halluc)"` returning empty). Complements the [[causal-rcm-a-unified-teacher-forcing-and-self-forcing-open-recipe-for-autoregressive-diffusion-distillation-in-streaming-video-generation-and-interactive-world-models-2606.25473]] (sibling world-model + diffusion-distillation work — Causal-RCM focuses on the training-recipe axis for autoregressive diffusion distillation in streaming video generation, Hallucination-in-World-Models focuses on the failure-mode axis for generative world models with low-coverage-region identification — together they bracket the world-model-training surface between training-recipe design and training-data coverage), [[in-context-world-modeling-for-robotic-control-2606.26025]] (sibling world-model robotics work — ICWM uses world-model context for in-task system identification in VLA control, Hallucination-in-World-Models uses world-model failure-mode signals for training-time and online hallucination prevention — together they bracket the world-model-application surface between inference-time system-adaptation and training/deployment-time failure-prevention primitives), [[world-action-models-a-survey-2606.20781]] (sibling world-model survey work that establishes the taxonomy of world-model action-controllable generation — Hallucination-in-World-Models complements this survey by surfacing the *failure-mode taxonomy* and the *coverage-aware mitigation recipe* that surveys typically do not address), [[qwen-agentworld-language-world-models-for-general-agents-2606.24597]] (sibling agentic world-model work — Qwen-AgentWorld builds language world models for general agents, Hallucination-in-World-Models builds coverage-aware hallucination mitigation for visual world models — together they bracket the agentic-world-model surface between language-world-model design and visual-world-model failure prevention), [[world-value-models-robotic-manipulation-2606.24742]] (sibling robotics world-model work — World-Value-Models uses world-model value scores for training-data selection, Hallucination-in-World-Models uses low-coverage-region signals for training-time sampling and online data collection — together they bracket the world-model-deployment surface between training-time data selection and deployment-time coverage closure primitives), and [[emergent-concepts]] (parent wiki page) entries on this page by surfacing *data-centric-hallucination-predictability-and-preventability* as the load-bearing primitive for generative-world-model evaluation where the failure mode of fluency-masked-physical-drift is structurally invisible to aggregate-rendering-quality metrics, and *hallucination-predictors-as-curiosity-rewards* as the structurally correct deployment primitive that closes the train-test coverage gap with target-efficient online data collection in entirely unseen environments.

- [[insight-self-guided-skill-acquisition-via-steerable-vlas-2606.24884]] — InSight: Self-Guided Skill Acquisition via Steerable VLAs (06-23) — addresses the structural capability ceiling of Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models whose manipulation skills are bounded by the demonstrations in the training data — any novel task whose required primitives are absent from training data is unreachable without costly human demonstrations, leaving VLA deployment in real-world long-horizon settings structurally dependent on manual data-collection effort — and proposes InSight, a framework that unlocks autonomous skill acquisition by rendering VLAs steerable at the primitive-action level (e.g., "move gripper to the bowl", "lift upward", "pour the bottle"); Wang, Maggie; Osterberg, Lars; Tian, Stephen; Shorinwa, Ola; Wu, Jiajun; Schwager, Mac design an automated segmentation pipeline that partitions existing demonstrations into labeled primitives via VLM plan decomposition (the VLM reads the task and decomposes the demonstration into a sequence of primitive labels) combined with end-effector pose analysis — eliminating the manual primitive-annotation step that would otherwise block the data flywheel — and a VLM-guided data flywheel that closes the autonomous-skill-acquisition loop: the VLM identifies which primitives are missing for a target novel task, proposes low-level control sequences to attempt each missing primitive, the system attempts demonstrations autonomously, and successful demonstrations are automatically labeled, stored, and integrated back into the VLA training set — running without any human demonstrations of the target skills; the framework is evaluated across simulation and real-world manipulation tasks including block flipping, drawer closing, sweeping, twisting, and pouring — five distinct manipulation skills learned autonomously — with learned primitives composable to execute novel long-horizon behaviors; surfacing *primitive-level-steerability-as-VLA-action-space* as the structurally correct primitive for autonomous skill acquisition — distinct from raw continuous control because the load-bearing axis is *composability of named primitives* rather than precision of end-effector trajectories — and *VLM-guided-autonomous-data-flywheel* as the load-bearing acquisition loop that closes the missing-primitive gap without human intervention, inverting the conventional VLA-training assumption that human demonstrations are required for new skills; 2026-06-23; discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-26 (robotics / vla / skill-acquisition / steerable-primitives / vlm-decomposition / autonomous-data-collection / flywheel theme — **first primitive-level-steerability-plus-VLM-guided-autonomous-data-flywheel for VLA skill acquisition in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(insight.*2606.24884|self.guided.skill|steerable.vla|primitive.steer|autonomous.data.flywheel)"` returning empty). Complements the [[in-context-world-modeling-for-robotic-control-2606.26025]] (sibling VLA in-context adaptation work — ICWM adapts to new *system configurations* via self-generated interactions, InSight acquires new *skills* via autonomous primitive discovery — together they bracket the VLA-deployment surface between environmental-adaptation and capability-expansion primitives), [[eventvla-event-driven-visual-evidence-memory-for-long-horizon-vision-language-action-policies-2606.20092]] (sibling VLA long-horizon memory work — EventVLA preserves visual evidence across long-horizon execution via foresight-driven keyframe selection, InSight acquires new skills autonomously to reduce the long-horizon-task-design effort — together they bracket the long-horizon-VLA surface between runtime-memory-preservation and training-time-skill-expansion primitives), [[policytrim-boosting-intrinsic-policy-efficiency-vla-models-2606.22540]] (sibling VLA efficiency work — PolicyTrim improves VLA decision efficiency from fixed observations, InSight improves VLA capability surface via autonomous primitive expansion — together they bracket the VLA-improvement surface between decision-side efficiency and capability-side expansion primitives), and [[emergent-concepts]] (parent wiki page) entries on this page by surfacing *primitive-level-steerability-as-VLA-action-space* as the load-bearing primitive for autonomous VLA capability expansion in deployment regimes where human demonstrations of new tasks are unavailable, and *VLM-guided-autonomous-data-flywheel* as the structurally correct acquisition loop that closes the missing-primitive gap with VLM-proposed control sequences and automatic labeling of successful demonstrations.

- [[eventvla-event-driven-visual-evidence-memory-for-long-horizon-vision-language-action-policies-2606.20092]] — EventVLA: Event-Driven Visual Evidence Memory for Long-Horizon Vision-Language-Action Policies (06-18) — addresses the structural memory bottleneck of long-horizon robotic manipulation — standard Vision-Language-Action (VLA) policies fail when task-relevant cues become occluded or unobservable over time, and existing memory-augmented methods suffer from one of three structural failures: severe information bottlenecks, decoupled dual-system latency, or unselective buffers that accumulate massive visual redundancies — leaving VLA policies with no scalable way to retain transient visual evidence — and proposes EventVLA, an end-to-end framework founded on the concept of sparse visual evidence memory that combines foundational visual anchors (to retain initial and short-term contexts) with a dynamic Keyframe Evidence Memory (KEM) module that directly predicts future keyframe probabilities from the VLA's latent embeddings to autonomously capture and store sparse, task-critical visual events; Yang, Ganlin; Tu, Zhangzheng; Yang, Yuqiang; Mao, Sitong; Dong, Junyi; Chen, Tianxing; Peng, Jiaqi; Xiong, Jing; Cao, Jiafei; Dai, Jifeng; Zhou, Wengang; Mu, Yao; Wang, Tai design a foresight-driven selection mechanism — the policy dynamically evaluates the future causal utility of current observations before deciding to preserve or discard them, inverting the standard reactive-buffer assumption that memory decisions happen after-the-fact — so transient visual evidence is captured *before* it becomes unobservable, not reactively after occlusion has already destroyed the cue; paired with RoboTwin-MeM, a diagnostic benchmark specifically designed for long-horizon memory evaluation that operationalizes the long-horizon-memory axis as a measurable surface (not absorbed into general manipulation benchmarks that hide the memory-specific failure modes); surfacing *foresight-driven-keyframe-evidence-memory* as the structurally correct primitive for long-horizon VLA — distinct from reactive-buffer memory because the load-bearing axis is *future-utility prediction* (decide-to-store based on predicted downstream utility) rather than *retrospective importance* (decide-to-store based on current importance) — and *VLA-latent-embedding-as-utility-predictor* as the load-bearing substrate that lets the policy itself serve as the memory-controller without a separate vision module; 2026-06-18; discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-26 (robotics / vla / long-horizon-manipulation / evidence-memory / foresight-driven / keyframe-prediction / sparse-visual-events theme — **first foresight-driven-keyframe-evidence-memory primitive for long-horizon VLA in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(eventvla|event.driven.vla|foresight.driven.keyframe|keyframe.evidence|memory.long.horizon.vla)"` returning empty). Complements the [[in-context-world-modeling-for-robotic-control-2606.26025]] (sibling VLA in-context adaptation work — ICWM uses self-generated interactions to identify the system before task execution, EventVLA uses VLA-latent-predicted future-utility to decide which visual evidence to preserve before it disappears — together they bracket the VLA long-horizon surface between pre-task system identification and during-task visual-evidence preservation), [[policytrim-boosting-intrinsic-policy-efficiency-vla-models-2606.22540]] (sibling VLA efficiency work — PolicyTrim focuses on intrinsic policy efficiency from fixed observations, EventVLA focuses on memory efficiency by deciding which observations are worth remembering — together they bracket the VLA-deployment surface between decision-side efficiency and memory-side efficiency primitives), [[foresight-failure-detection-long-horizon-robotic-manipulation-2606.23085]] (sibling long-horizon robotics work that shares the Foresight name as a primitive axis but targets a distinct surface — Foresight-Failure-Detection predicts *task-failure* events from world-model latents for runtime safety monitoring, EventVLA predicts *task-critical-visual-event* keyframes from VLA latents for memory preservation — together they bracket the long-horizon-manipulation surface between runtime-safety detection and runtime-memory-preservation primitives), [[world-value-models-robotic-manipulation-2606.24742]] (sibling world-model robotics work — World-Value-Models filters training data via world-model value scores, EventVLA filters runtime evidence via VLA-utility predictions — together they bracket the world-model-deployment surface between training-time data selection and inference-time evidence selection), and [[emergent-concepts]] (parent wiki page) entries on this page by surfacing *foresight-driven-keyframe-evidence-memory* as the load-bearing primitive for long-horizon VLA where the failure mode of post-occlusion-evidence-loss is structurally invisible to reactive-buffer methods that decide-to-store based on current importance rather than predicted downstream utility, and *VLA-latent-embedding-as-utility-predictor* as the structurally correct substrate that lets the policy itself serve as the memory-controller without a separate vision module.


- [[qwen-image-agent-bridging-the-context-gap-in-real-world-image-generation-2606.26907]] — Qwen-Image-Agent: Bridging the Context Gap in Real-World Image Generation (06-25) — diagnoses the structural *Context Gap* failure mode in modern text-to-image (T2I) systems where real-world user requests are often underspecified, implicit, or dependent on up-to-date knowledge, yet T2I models receive only the literal prompt and assume it carries sufficient generation context — and proposes Qwen-Image-Agent, a unified agentic framework that integrates plan, reason, search, memory, and feedback in a context-centric manner, treating user input as partial context and progressively constructing the *generation context* the model actually needs through Context-Aware Planning (identifies missing context and plans how it should be acquired and used) and Context Grounding (gathers context from reason, search, memory, and feedback sources); Zhang, Zekai; Li, Jiahao; Zhang, Jie; Gao, Kaiyuan; Yan, Kun; Jiang, Lihan; Tang, Ningyuan; Yin, Shengming; Wu, Tianhe; Chen, Xiaoyue; Xu, Xiao; Shu, Yan; Zhang, Yanran; Xu, Yixian; Chen, Yuxiang; Wang, Zhendong; Liu, Zihao; Zhou, Zikai; Zhang, Huishuai; Zhao, Dongyan; Wu, Chenfei introduce Image Agent Bench (IA-Bench) as the first benchmark explicitly covering the four core image-agent capabilities — Plan, Reason, Search, and Memory — and demonstrate state-of-the-art performance across IA-Bench, Mindbench, and WISE-Verified; surfacing *context-gap-as-task-definition* as the structurally correct primitive for agentic image generation — distinct from prompt-engineering or retrieval-augmented generation because the load-bearing insight is that the agent's job is *gap-filling between user context and generation context* rather than prompt-improvement alone — and *capability-coverage-eval (Plan+Reason+Search+Memory)* as the load-bearing diagnostic axis that the existing T2I evaluation surfaces cannot probe; 2026-06-25; discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-26 (agentic-image-generation / context-gap-filling / ia-bench / planning-reasoning-search-memory theme — **first context-gap-as-task-definition paper for agentic T2I in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(context.gap|qwen.image.agent|image.agent.bench|ia.bench)"` returning empty). Complements the [[qwen-agentworld-language-world-models-for-general-agents-2606.24597]] (sibling Qwen-family agentic work — Qwen-AgentWorld builds language world models for general agents, Qwen-Image-Agent builds agentic gap-filling for image generation — together they bracket the Qwen-agentic surface between world-model and image-generation primitives), and [[emergent-concepts]] (parent wiki page) entries on this page by surfacing *context-gap-as-task-definition* as a load-bearing primitive for the deployment regime where T2I agents must handle ambiguous real-world requests — where the load-bearing variable is *gap-filling capability* (Plan+Reason+Search+Memory), not T2I rendering quality alone.
- [[danceopd-on-policy-generative-field-distillation-2606.27377]] — DanceOPD: On-Policy Generative Field Distillation (06-25) — addresses the structural capability-composition challenge in modern image generation where a single model must unify diverse capabilities — text-to-image (T2I), local editing, and global editing — that rarely naturally align and often conflict (e.g., editing tends to degrade T2I performance while global and local editing interfere with each other) — and proposes DanceOPD, an on-policy generative field distillation framework for flow-matching models that routes each sample to one capability field, queries one low-noise student-induced state, and trains with a simple velocity MSE objective; Zhou, Wei; Zhu, Xiongwei; Xu, Zelin; Dong, Bo; Gong, Lixue; Liang, Yongyuan; Chu, Meng; Qu, Leigang; Kong, Lingdong; Liu, Wei surface a methodology-level contribution to flow-matching distillation — each capability source is a velocity field over the shared flow state space, and the student learns from fields queried on its own rollout states to compose expert capabilities, with the formulation also absorbing operator-defined fields such as classifier-free guidance (CFG) into the same distillation framework; comprehensive experiments on T2I, editing, realism-field absorption, and CFG absorption show that DanceOPD improves multi-capability composition, strengthening target capabilities while preserving anchor generation quality; surfacing *capability-routing-as-distillation-target* as the structurally correct primitive for multi-capability flow-matching — distinct from naive multi-task distillation because the load-bearing axis is *per-sample routing to a capability field* rather than joint training — and *student-rollout-state-field-querying* as the on-policy refinement that lets the student compose expert capabilities on its own trajectory rather than from fixed expert rollouts; 2026-06-25; discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-26 (flow-matching / on-policy-distillation / multi-capability-composition / capability-routing theme — **first capability-routing-as-distillation-target paper for multi-capability flow-matching in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(danceopd|generative.field.distill|capability.routing|on.policy.*flow.matching)"` returning empty). Complements the [[exploring-the-design-space-of-reward-backpropagation-for-flow-matching-2606.11075]] (sibling flow-matching reward work — Flow-Matching Reward-Backprop explores reward-gradient design space, DanceOPD explores capability-field distillation design space — together they bracket the flow-matching design surface between reward-backpropagation and capability-composition primitives), and [[emergent-concepts]] (parent wiki page) entries on this page by surfacing *capability-routing-as-distillation-target* as a load-bearing primitive for unified image generation where the load-bearing variable is *per-sample routing*, not shared multi-task supervision that lets capability interference dominate.
- [[in-context-world-modeling-for-robotic-control-2606.26025]] — In-Context World Modeling for Robotic Control (06-24) — diagnoses the structural failure mode of modern Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models that fail to generalize to novel setups (altered camera viewpoints, robot morphologies) because they are conditioned only on current observations and language instructions and implicitly assume a fixed execution context — necessitating data-intensive fine-tuning for any new environment — and introduces In-Context World Modeling (ICWM), a framework that treats system identification as an in-context adaptation problem; Wang, Siyin; Shi, Junhao; Fei, Senyu; Fu, Zhaoyang; Ji, Li; Gong, Jingjing; Qiu, Xipeng enable robot policies to autonomously infer essential system variables from a short history of self-generated, task-agnostic interactions, with the context window used not for demonstrations specifying *what task to perform* but for self-exploration that captures *how the system operates*; by processing these interactions before task execution, the model implicitly captures the world dynamics of the current system, enabling adaptation to novel configurations without parameter updates, with extensive experiments in simulation and on real-world robot platforms demonstrating that ICWM significantly outperforms standard VLA baselines on novel camera viewpoints; surfacing *self-exploration-as-system-identification* as the structurally correct primitive for in-context VLA adaptation — distinct from demonstration-conditioned in-context learning because the load-bearing axis is *system-config inference from self-generated interactions* rather than task-specification from human demonstrations — and *pre-task-execution-context-processing* as the load-bearing temporal primitive that captures system dynamics *before* the task begins, inverting the standard VLA assumption that context and task are received simultaneously; 2026-06-24; discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-26 (vla / in-context-adaptation / system-identification / self-exploration / world-model-pretraining theme — **first self-exploration-as-system-identification paper for in-context VLA adaptation in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(icwm|in.context.world|system.identification|self.exploration.*vla)"` returning empty). Complements the [[world-value-models-robotic-manipulation-2606.24742]] (sibling robotics world-model work — World-Value-Models uses world models for data-selection in manipulation, ICWM uses world-model context for in-task system identification — together they bracket the world-model surface between training-time data selection and inference-time system adaptation), and [[emergent-concepts]] (parent wiki page) entries on this page by surfacing *self-exploration-as-system-identification* as a load-bearing primitive for VLA deployment in heterogeneous robot environments — where the load-bearing variable is *system-config inference from autonomous interactions*, not demonstration-based task learning.

- [[robust-to-confidence-aware-tool-orchestration-for-robust-video-understanding-2606.26904]] — Confidence-Aware Tool Orchestration for Robust Video Understanding (06-25) — addresses the Blind Trust Problem in video reasoning language models — frontier models implicitly assume every input frame is equally reliable, causing 15-30 percentage-point accuracy drops on real-world embodied benchmarks under realistic perturbations (motion blur, glare, occlusion) while remaining unaware their visual evidence has been degraded — and proposes Robust-TO, an agentic video understanding framework that explicitly integrates per-frame trustworthiness into every stage of reasoning; He, Yangfan; Choi, Yujin; Yoon, Jaehong organize heterogeneous visual perception tools under a unified evidence interface where each tool receives a sub-query and a set of trustworthy frames selected by a reliability-relevance score, returning evidence in a shared format (concrete prediction, temporal grounding, calibrated reliability score); the calibrated scores guide a three-tier synthesis process (high/medium/low) and define a confidence-cost GRPO reward jointly optimizing correctness, evidence reliability, and efficiency, achieving 56.4% average accuracy on clean inputs (surpassing strongest open-source baseline by 10.6 pp and outperforming Gemini-2.5-Pro at 46.2%) and maintaining 54.3% under five corruption types — the smallest clean-to-corrupted drop among compared methods; surfacing *calibrated-confidence-as-evidence-attribute* as the structurally correct primitive for multi-tool perception pipelines — replacing implicit uniform-trust assumption with explicit per-evidence reliability weights — and *confidence-cost-GRPO* as a Pareto-shaped training-time reward primitive that balances correctness, reliability, and efficiency simultaneously; 2026-06-25; discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-26 (video-reasoning / evidence-reliability / calibrated-confidence / confidence-cost-grpo theme — **first calibrated-per-evidence-reliability-weighting paper for video reasoning in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(blind.trust|video.reliab|frame.trust|calibrated.*video|robust.to|video.tool.orchestr)"` returning empty). Complements the [[remmd-realistic-multilingual-multi-image-agentic-verification-for-multimodal-misinformation-detection-2606.24112]] (sibling multimodal agentic-verification work — REMMD verifies multimodal misinformation via agentic pipelines, Robust-TO verifies visual-evidence reliability via calibrated scores in video reasoning — together they bracket the multimodal-verification surface between misinformation-detection and visual-evidence-reliability primitives), [[capable-but-careless-do-computer-use-agents-follow-contextual-integrity-2606.23189]] (sibling agentic-carefulness surface — Capable-But-Careless audits rule-following under contextual integrity, Robust-TO audits evidence-reliability-weighting under perturbation — together they bracket the agentic-carefulness surface between contextual-rule-following and perception-evidence-reliability), [[the-verification-horizon-no-silver-bullet-for-coding-agent-rewards-2606.26300]] (sibling verification-fidelity work — Verification-Horizon argues no fixed reward stays faithful for coding agents, Robust-TO operationalizes per-evidence reliability scores as a calibrated alternative — together they bracket the verification-fidelity surface between reward-stability ceilings and calibrated-evidence reliability primitives), and [[emergent-concepts]] (parent wiki page) entries on this page by surfacing *calibrated-confidence-as-evidence-attribute* as the load-bearing primitive for video reasoning where the failure mode of uniform-reliability-assumption becomes a 15-30 pp accuracy drop that the model is structurally unaware of, and *confidence-cost GRPO* as the structurally correct training-time primitive that replaces pure-accuracy-maximization with Pareto-shaped joint optimization across correctness, reliability, and efficiency.

- [[the-verification-horizon-no-silver-bullet-for-coding-agent-rewards-2606.26300]] — The Verification Horizon: No Silver Bullet for Coding Agent Rewards (06-25) — argues that the classical intuition "verifying a solution is easier than producing one" is being inverted for modern coding agents — as foundation models grow stronger and engineering harnesses more sophisticated, generating complex candidate solutions is no longer difficult, but reliably verifying them has become the harder problem; every verifier is only a proxy for human intent, never the intent itself, making verification subject to a twofold difficulty (intent is underspecified by nature, and during training optimization widens the proxy-vs-intent gap as reward hacking or signal saturation); Wang, Binghai; Zhang, Chenlong; Liu, Dayiheng; Zhang, Jiajun; Chen, Jiawei; Chen, Mouxiang; Fang, Rongyao characterize reward signal quality along three dimensions — scalability, faithfulness, and robustness — and empirically evaluate four reward constructions (test verifier for general coding tasks, rubric verifier for frontend tasks, user-as-verifier for real-world agent tasks, automated agent verifier for long-horizon tasks) across task types and policy capability levels, demonstrating that targeted verification design can suppress reward hacking and improve task completion quality but only when matched to the specific task profile; surfacing *verification-horizon-as-co-evolution-primitive* as the load-bearing insight — no fixed reward function remains effective as policy capability continues to grow, and verification must co-evolve with the generator — inverting the conventional RL framing where reward is assumed stable and only the policy adapts; 2026-06-25; discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-26 (coding-agent-reward / verification-co-evolution / no-silver-bullet / reward-hacking-stability theme — **first verification-co-evolution-with-generator framing for coding-agent reward design in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(verification.horizon|silver.bullet|reward.signal.fidelity|reward.co.evol|verifier.fait)"` returning empty). Complements the [[swe-marathon-can-agents-autonomously-complete-ultra-long-horizon-software-work-2606.07682]] (sibling coding-agent benchmark — SWE-Marathon evaluates whether agents *complete* 4+ hour software tasks, Verification-Horizon evaluates whether verifier signals *stay faithful* as agents complete them — together they bracket the coding-agent evaluation surface between task completion and reward-signal fidelity), [[beyond-reward-engineering-a-data-recipe-for-long-context-reinforcement-learning-2606.18831]] (sibling reward-design work — Beyond-Reward-Engineering focuses on data recipes for long-context RL, Verification-Horizon focuses on reward construction for coding agents — together they bracket the RL reward-design surface between data-side and verifier-side primitives), [[rethinking-reward-supervision-rubric-conditioned-self-distillation-2606.19327]] (sibling rubric-style reward-supervision work — Rubric-Conditioned Self-Distillation replaces scalar rewards with rubric-conditioned teacher guidance at training time, Verification-Horizon diagnoses the structural ceiling of any fixed reward at training time — together they bracket the post-training-reward surface between training-time credit-assignment refinements and the structural argument that no fixed reward is stable), [[naturebench-can-coding-agents-match-the-published-sota-of-nature-family-papers-2606.24530]] (sibling coding-agent capability benchmark — NatureBench measures whether agents can match published SOTA, Verification-Horizon measures whether verifiers can stay faithful to capability gains — together they bracket the coding-agent evaluation surface between capability measurement and reward-signal reliability), [[exploring-the-design-space-of-reward-backpropagation-for-flow-matching-2606.11075]] (sibling reward-design-space exploration — Flow-Matching Reward-Backprop explores the gradient-flow design space for rewards, Verification-Horizon explores the signal-quality design space for verifiers — together they bracket the RL reward-design surface between optimization-flow design and signal-quality characterization), [[robust-to-confidence-aware-tool-orchestration-for-robust-video-understanding-2606.26904]] (sibling verification-fidelity work — Robust-TO operationalizes per-evidence reliability scores as a calibrated alternative to scalar reward signals, Verification-Horizon diagnoses the structural impossibility of any fixed scalar reward staying faithful — together they bracket the verification-fidelity surface between calibrated-evidence reliability primitives and the structural-ceiling argument for scalar-reward rewards), and [[emergent-concepts]] (parent wiki page) entries on this page by surfacing *verification-co-evolution-with-generator* as the load-bearing primitive for coding-agent RL where the conventional assumption of stable reward is structurally
