false — verification must be redesigned as policy capability advances, and the verification-horizon framework (scalability, faithfulness, robustness) provides the three-dimensional space on which this co-evolutionary design can be made explicit rather than implicit.

- [[openbiorq-unsolved-biomedical-research-questions-for-agents-2606.21959]] — OpenBioRQ: Unsolved Biomedical Research Questions for Agents (06-20) — addresses the citation-correctness failure mode in current agentic models — over 99% of citations resolve, yet roughly 15.9% link to the wrong paper, a failure mode existing benchmarks miss because fixed-answer-key questions let a model reproduce the expected source from that key rather than independently verifying that the source supports the claim — and introduces OpenBioRQ, a retrieval-grounded agentic benchmark of 12,553 unsolved biomedical research questions across 12 domains, designed as a faithfulness-and-abstention probe; Jeong, Minbyul anchors difficulty on questions three open-weight reference models fail to answer (not subjective hardness labels), with on-the-hardest-subset held-out models from the same lineage as difficulty anchors solving only ~17% while three independent frontier agents (Gemini-3-Pro, Opus-4.7, GPT-5.5) span a wide 29-60% range — making the benchmark hard, non-saturating, and discriminating across capability tiers; diagnoses *agentic collapse* on the hardest questions where agents stop using their tools, and shows that for the most collapse-prone model blocking tool access entirely barely changes its score — tools stop paying off exactly where they are needed most; a frozen per-question checklist raises inter-judge agreement from Spearman 0.35 to 0.82, providing a concrete recipe for stabilizing open-ended research-grade evaluation; surfacing *abstention-as-research-discipline* as the structurally correct primitive for agentic research settings where confident hallucination is the dominant failure mode, and *agentic-collapse-on-hardest-questions* as the load-bearing inversion where the standard "more tools → better agents" framing reverses exactly at the capability frontier; 2026-06-20; discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-26 (biomedical-agentic-research / citation-faithfulness / abstention-discipline / agentic-collapse-on-hardest-questions theme — **first biomedical benchmark combining agentic setting with unsolved questions that have no answer key in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(openbiorq|unsolved.biomedic|biomedical.research.question|citation.correctness|biomedical.*agents.*bench)"` returning empty). Complements the [[lingxidiagbench-a-multi-agent-framework-for-benchmarking-llms-in-chinese-psychiatric-consultation-and-diagnosis-2602.09379]] (sibling medical-domain multi-agent benchmark — LingxiDiagBench benchmarks clinical-reasoning-competence in Chinese psychiatric consultation, OpenBioRQ benchmarks citation-faithfulness-and-abstention in unsolved biomedical research — together they bracket the medical-LLM evaluation surface between clinical-competence and research-faithfulness), [[let-llms-judge-each-other-multi-agent-peer-reviewed-reasoning-for-medical-question-answering-2606.15419]] (sibling medical-LLM multi-agent reasoning work — Let-LLMs-Judge-Each-Other uses peer-review-as-selection for MedQA, OpenBioRQ uses unsolved-question-and-abstention-probe for biomedical research — together they bracket the medical-LLM evaluation surface between answer-quality selection and research-faithfulness), [[where-does-the-signal-live-a-web-data-recipe-for-medical-encoder-pretraining-2606.22079]] (sibling medical-domain LLM work — Where-Does-the-Signal-Live provides data-recipe for medical encoder pretraining, OpenBioRQ provides evaluation-recipe for medical agentic research — together they bracket the medical-LLM development surface between pretraining-data curation and agentic-research evaluation), [[deep-research-in-physical-sciences-a-multi-agent-framework-and-comprehensive-benchmark-2606.18648]] (sibling multi-agent deep-research benchmark — Deep-Research evaluates task-completion in scientific reasoning workflows, OpenBioRQ evaluates citation-faithfulness in unsolved biomedical research — together they bracket the deep-research benchmark surface between task-completion and research-faithfulness primitives), [[llm-based-scientific-peer-review-methods-benchmarks-reliability-challenges-2606.25057]] (sibling peer-review reliability work — Scientific-Peer-Review benchmarks peer-review automation reliability, OpenBioRQ benchmarks agentic-research faithfulness (a different reliability surface — citation correctness and abstention discipline rather than review quality) — together they bracket the LLM-scientific-research reliability surface between review-reliability and research-faithfulness), [[the-verification-horizon-no-silver-bullet-for-coding-agent-rewards-2606.26300]] (sibling verification-fidelity work — Verification-Horizon diagnoses the structural impossibility of fixed-reward verification for coding agents, OpenBioRQ diagnoses the structural impossibility of fixed-answer-key verification for biomedical research agents — together they bracket the agent-evaluation reliability surface between reward-signal-fidelity and answer-key-fidelity), [[from-agent-traces-to-trust-a-survey-of-evidence-tracing-and-execution-provenance-in-llm-agents-2606.04990]] (sibling evidence-tracing survey — Evidence-Tracing surveys forward-tracing decisions to source material, OpenBioRQ probes backward-tracing claims to source material via the citation-correctness failure mode — together they bracket the evidence-tracing surface between forward provenance and backward faithfulness), [[foresci-evaluating-llm-agents-for-forward-looking-ai-research-judgment-2606.00644]] (sibling research-judgment benchmark — ForeSci evaluates forward-looking research judgment under temporal control, OpenBioRQ evaluates citation-faithfulness under unsolved-question control — together they bracket the research-grade agent-evaluation surface between prospective-judgment and open-question faithfulness), and [[emergent-concepts]] (parent wiki page) entries on this page by surfacing *abstention-as-research-discipline* and *agentic-collapse-on-hardest-questions* as two distinct load-bearing primitives for biomedical agentic research — the first inverts the "always answer" framing to require explicit discipline around claiming uncertainty, and the second surfaces a structural failure mode where the standard "more tools → better agents" framing reverses exactly at the capability frontier where tool use is most needed but least employed.

- [[let-llms-judge-each-other-multi-agent-peer-reviewed-reasoning-for-medical-question-answering-2606.15419]] — Let LLMs Judge Each Other: Multi-Agent Peer-Reviewed Reasoning for Medical Question Answering (06-13) — addresses the structural limitation of LLM-as-judge methods that focus on answer agreement rather than reasoning quality in high-stakes domain QA, and proposes a multi-agent peer-reviewed reasoning method where multiple LLM agents independently generate chain-of-thought reasoning with candidate answers, then act as peer reviewers to evaluate each other's reasoning for factual correctness and logical soundness, with the highest-rated reasoning chain selected to produce the final answer; Zhan, Zaifu; Zhou, Shuang; Zhang, Rui demonstrate across five SOTA LLMs (Llama-3.1-8B, Qwen2.5-7B, Phi-4, DeepSeek-LLM-7B, GPT-oss-20B) and three medical benchmarks (HeadQA, MedQA-USMLE, PubMedQA) that peer-reviewed reasoning consistently outperforms both single-model CoT and CoT-based majority voting — the best combination achieves 0.820 average accuracy, exceeding the strongest single model (0.777) and majority voting ensembles (up to 0.789), with peer assessments reliably distinguishing high- from low-quality reasoning chains and the method scaling effectively with more participating models; surfacing *peer-review-as-reasoning-selection-criterion* as the structurally correct primitive for multi-agent reasoning — distinct from ensemble-voting or majority-agreement — because the load-bearing question is not "do answers agree?" but "is the reasoning chain factually correct and logically sound?"; 2026-06-13; discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-26 (multi-agent-peer-review / llm-as-judge / reasoning-quality-selection / medical-question-answering theme — **first peer-review-as-reasoning-selection paper for medical-LLM multi-agent reasoning in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(peer.review|llm.judge|each.other|solvers.*evaluators)"` returning empty). Complements the [[llm-based-scientific-peer-review-methods-benchmarks-reliability-challenges-2606.25057]] (sibling peer-review methodology paper — Scientific-Peer-Review surveys *human-mediated* peer-review of LLM outputs, Let-LLMs-Judge-Each-Other provides *machine-mediated* peer-review of LLM reasoning chains — together they bracket the peer-review surface between human-mediated and machine-mediated primitives), [[benchmarking-open-ended-multi-agent-coordination-in-language-agents-2606.08340]] (sibling multi-agent evaluation — alem isolates *coordination-as-distinct-bottleneck* in long-horizon open-ended tasks, Let-LLMs-Judge-Each-Other isolates *peer-review-as-reasoning-selection* in single-turn MedQA — together they bracket the multi-agent evaluation surface between long-horizon coordination and single-turn peer-review reasoning), [[lingxidiagbench-a-multi-agent-framework-for-benchmarking-llms-in-chinese-psychiatric-consultation-and-diagnosis-2602.09379]] (sibling medical-domain multi-agent benchmark — LingxiDiagBench benchmarks *clinical-reasoning-competence* in Chinese psychiatric consultation, Let-LLMs-Judge-Each-Other benchmarks *peer-review-reasoning-selection* in MedQA — together they bracket the medical-LLM evaluation surface between clinical-competence and peer-review-selection), and [[emergent-concepts]] (parent wiki page) entries on this page by surfacing *peer-review-as-reasoning-selection-criterion* as a load-bearing primitive for high-stakes multi-agent LLM reasoning where the failure mode of majority-voting (sycophantic agreement on shared reasoning errors) is structurally avoided by evaluating reasoning quality rather than answer agreement.
- [[benchmarking-open-ended-multi-agent-coordination-in-language-agents-2606.08340]] — Benchmarking Open-Ended Multi-Agent Coordination in Language Agents (06-06) — addresses the structural gap in agent evaluation where existing benchmarks rarely test long-horizon coordination demands together (instead emphasising single-agent tasks, short interactions, or highly structured multi-agent settings), and introduces alem, a JAX-based benchmark for open-ended multi-agent coordination built on Craftax-like dynamics that embeds procedurally generated coordination tasks, soft specialisation, communication, and controllable coordination difficulty into a long-horizon survival world with exploration, crafting, trading, and combat; Tessera, Kale-ab Abebe; Szecsenyi, Andras; Barker, Cameron; Rutherford, Alexander; Paglieri, Davide; Scannell, Aidan; Gouk, Henry; Crowley, Elliot J.; Rocktäschel, Tim; Storkey, Amos evaluate 13 modern LLMs zero-shot within homogeneous teams (with trained MARL agents as reference points) and demonstrate that current LLM agents average only ~6% normalised return, but their failures are not uniform — on the hardest coordination setting, zero-shot Gemini-3.1-Pro-High approaches MARL agents trained for one billion steps, while GPT-5.4-High achieves strong base-task reward but much lower coordination reward, surfacing *coordination-as-distinct-bottleneck* for frontier LLM agents as separable from single-agent capabilities, and that *communication* is the largest contributor to coordination with memory and reasoning providing secondary gains when used to maintain multi-step plans; 2026-06-06; discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-26 (open-ended-multi-agent-coordination / craftax-like-dynamics / long-horizon-coordination / communication-as-dominant-axis theme — **first open-ended-multi-agent-coordination benchmark with controllable coordination difficulty in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(alem|craftax|coordination.*bench|long.horizon.*multi.agent)"` returning empty). Complements the [[the-hitchhikers-guide-to-agentic-ai-from-foundations-to-systems-2606.24937]] (sibling foundational agentic-AI survey that organises the multi-agent systems landscape alem now provides the empirical coordination primitive for), [[deep-research-in-physical-sciences-a-multi-agent-framework-and-comprehensive-benchmark-2606.18648]] (sibling multi-agent deep-research benchmark — Deep-Research evaluates *task-completion* in scientific reasoning workflows, alem evaluates *coordination competence* in open-ended survival workflows — together they bracket the multi-agent evaluation surface between task-completion and open-ended coordination), [[llm-based-scientific-peer-review-methods-benchmarks-reliability-challenges-2606.25057]] (sibling peer-review methodology — Scientific-Peer-Review benchmarks human-mediated trace evaluation, alem benchmarks machine-mediated long-horizon coordination — together they bracket the multi-agent reliability surface between peer-evaluation and long-horizon coordination), [[let-llms-judge-each-other-multi-agent-peer-reviewed-reasoning-for-medical-question-answering-2606.15419]] (sibling multi-agent reasoning primitive — Let-LLMs-Judge-Each-Other uses peer-review-as-selection in single-turn MedQA, alem uses coordination-as-emergence in long-horizon survival — together they bracket the multi-agent reasoning surface between selection-via-evaluation and emergence-via-coordination), and [[emergent-concepts]] (parent wiki page) entries on this page by surfacing *coordination-as-distinct-bottleneck* as a load-bearing primitive for frontier-agent evaluation where individual task competence does not imply coordination competence — the GPT-5.4-High vs Gemini-3.1-Pro-High contrast shows that the bottleneck is *coordination* (not capability), and ablations identify communication as the largest contributor with memory and reasoning as secondary contributors for multi-step plan maintenance.
- [[agentic-chain-of-thought-steering-for-efficient-and-controllable-llm-reasoning-2606.03965]] — Agentic Chain-of-Thought Steering for Efficient and Controllable LLM Reasoning (06-02) — addresses the structural gap in efficient reasoning methods that control thinking length by shortening, early-stopping, or compressing traces but leave *how the model thinks* implicit, and proposes Agentic Chain-of-Thought Steering (ACTS) which formulates reasoning steering as a Markov decision process where a controller agent adaptively steers a frozen reasoner during inference; Xia, Yu; Xie, Zhouhang; Xu, Xin; Kang, Byungkyu; Lamba, Prarit; Gao, Xiang; McAuley, Julian demonstrate that at each step the controller observes the reasoning trace and remaining thinking budget, then issues a steering action consisting of a reasoning strategy and a steering phrase that initiates the next reasoner step — enabling budget-aware strategy control for efficient reasoning while preserving the reasoner's generation continuity, with the controller initialised from synthetic steering trajectories with multi-budget augmentation and further optimised via RL with budget-conditioned reward shaping so that a single controller generalises across multiple reasoning budgets; ACTS matches full-thinking performance with substantial token savings and enables controllable accuracy-efficiency trade-offs across different reasoners and tasks, surfacing *MDP-formulated reasoning steering* as a load-bearing primitive for efficient reasoning — distinct from truncation or compression methods because the controller explicitly emits a *strategy + steering phrase* at each step rather than collapsing the trace; 2026-06-02; discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-26 (reasoning-steering / mdp-controller / budget-aware-strategy / generation-continuity theme — **first MDP-formulated inference-time reasoning-steering controller paper in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(acts|steering.*reason|controller.*reason|mdp.*steer|budget.*aware.*reason)"` returning empty). Complements the [[bagen-are-llm-agents-budget-aware-2606.00198]] (sibling budget-aware work — BAGEN is *budget-aware agent evaluation*, ACTS is *budget-aware reasoning control* — together they bracket the budget-as-design-variable surface between evaluation-budget allocation and inference-budget control), [[progress-advantage-neglected-free-lunch-post-training-llm-agents-2606.26080]] (sibling post-training signal work — Progress Advantage extracts *training-time implicit advantage* from log-prob ratios, ACTS extracts *inference-time steering strategy* via RL on the controller — together they bracket the post-training-vs-inference-time signal surface between training-time advantage extraction and inference-time steering control), and [[emergent-concepts]] (parent wiki page) entries on this page by surfacing *MDP-formulated reasoning steering* as a load-bearing primitive for efficient reasoning where the load-bearing insight is that *strategy* is a first-class output variable — emitted by a controller agent that observes trace+budget — rather than a fixed property of the reasoner, enabling a single controller to span the full accuracy-efficiency Pareto frontier via budget-conditioned reward shaping.
- [[why-multi-step-tool-use-reinforcement-learning-collapses-and-how-supervisory-signals-fix-it-2606.26027]] — Why Multi-Step Tool-Use Reinforcement Learning Collapses and How Supervisory Signals Fix It (06-24) — addresses the structural catastrophic-collapse failure mode of recent agentic RL methods for multi-step tool use — where models abruptly lose tool-invocation structure during training even though their underlying tool-use capability is preserved — and traces the failure to format-level pathologies (unexpected probability spikes in specific control tokens) rather than capability-level degradation; Hao, Yupu; Jin, Zhuoran; Liao, Huanxuan; Liu, Kang; Zhao, Jun investigate a structured set of supervisory signals (off-policy supervision, hint-based guidance, erroneous-example supervision) under both synchronous and interleaved SFT+RL schemes, surfacing *interleaved-SFT-as-stabilizer-for-RL-collapse* as the load-bearing primitive — recognizing that agentic-RL instability is a format-distribution pathology amenable to supervised anchors rather than a capability gap requiring more capability data; 2026-06-24; discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-26 (agentic-rl-collapse / control-token-pathology / supervisory-signal-stabilization / interleaved-sft theme — **first agentic-RL-collapse diagnosis as format-distribution-pathology paper in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(rl.collapse|interleaved.sft|supervisory.signal|format.pathology)"` returning empty). Complements the [[opid-on-policy-skill-distillation-for-agentic-reinforcement-learning-2606.26790]] (sibling agentic-RL post-training work — OPID targets skill-density in on-policy distillation, Why-Multi-Step-Tool-Use-RL-Collapses targets RL-training-time collapse — together they bracket the agentic-RL failure-mode space between *post-training distillation gaps* (OPID) and *RL-training format collapse* (this paper)), [[dont-blindly-trust-it-how-unreliable-feedback-breaks-tool-using-llm-agents-2606.21409]] (sibling tool-using-agent-failure work — Feedback-Reliability diagnoses failure from *unreliable external feedback*, Why-Multi-Step-Tool-Use-RL-Collapses diagnoses failure from *unstable training dynamics* — together they bracket the tool-use failure-mode space between deployment-time observation unreliability and training-time RL instability), [[constraint-tax-in-open-weight-llms-an-empirical-study-of-tool-calling-suppression-under-structured-output-constraints-2606.25605]] (sibling tool-calling-pathology work — Constraint-Tax diagnoses tool-calling failures under structured-output constraints at inference, Why-Multi-Step-Tool-Use-RL-Collapses diagnoses tool-invocation failures during training — together they bracket the tool-calling pathology space between inference-time structured-output suppression and training-time control-token pathology), [[planbench-xl-evaluating-long-horizon-planning-of-llm-tool-use-agents-in-large-scale-tool-ecosystems-2606.22388]] (sibling long-horizon tool-use evaluation — PlanBench-XL evaluates *whether agents complete* long-horizon tool plans, Why-Multi-Step-Tool-Use-RL-Collapses evaluates *whether agents can be trained* to produce long-horizon tool plans — together they bracket the long-horizon-tool-use surface between evaluation and training-time stability), [[learning-from-your-own-mistakes-constructing-learnable-micro-reflective-trajectories-for-self-distillation-2606.18844]] (sibling trajectory-aware self-correction work — Learning-from-Your-Own-Mistakes uses erroneous-prefix corrections for trajectory distillation, Why-Multi-Step-Tool-Use-RL-Collapses uses erroneous-example supervision for RL stabilization — together they bracket the erroneous-trajectory-as-supervision surface between *distillation-time micro-corrections* and *RL-time format stabilization*), and [[emergent-concepts]] (parent wiki page) entries on this page by surfacing *interleaved-SFT-as-RL-stabilizer* as the structurally correct primitive for the agentic-RL collapse regime where the failure is not capability-loss but format-distribution pathology — the supervised anchor repairs the format distribution without requiring new capability data, inverting the assumption that RL collapse requires more or better RL data.

- [[gui-vs-cli-execution-bottlenecks-in-screen-only-and-skill-mediated-computer-use-agents-2606.24551]] — GUI vs. CLI: Execution Bottlenecks in Screen-Only and Skill-Mediated Computer-Use Agents (06-22) — addresses the interaction-modality-confound pathology in computer-use-agent evaluation where existing benchmarks mix modality differences with task difficulty, initial states, verifiers, and permitted actions, making modality-vs-capability conclusions scientifically meaningless, and proposes a matched execution-layer benchmark of 440 desktop tasks across 18 applications and 12 workflow categories where screen-only GUI agents and skill-mediated CLI agents receive identical goals, states, and final-state verifiers while being restricted to modality-native actions; Zhou, Xiao; Zhang, Siyue; Zhao, Yilun; Wei, Jinbiao; Song, Tingyu; Cohan, Arman; Zhao, Chen demonstrate that the strongest GUI agent reaches 59.1% full pass rate vs the strongest original-skill CLI agent at 48.2%, but verifier-guided skill augmentation raises CLI success to 69.3% — surfacing the modality-specific bottleneck diagnosis (GUI agents limited by reliable grounded interaction over long-horizon workflows, CLI agents limited by the coverage and scalability of their skill interfaces) and *modality-native-bottleneck-isolation* as the load-bearing evaluation primitive; 2026-06-22; discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-26 (computer-use-agent / interaction-modality / matched-benchmark / skill-coverage-bottleneck theme — **first interaction-modality-as-isolated-variable paper for computer-use-agent evaluation in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(gui.*cli|interaction.modality|matched.benchmark)"` returning empty). Complements the [[beyond-static-leaderboards-predictive-validity-evaluation-llm-agents-2606.19704]] (sibling agent-evaluation methodology — Beyond-Static-Leaderboards critiques aggregate-leaderboard scoring as structurally inadequate, GUI-vs-CLI isolates the interaction-modality variable as a concrete demonstration of how confounding variables mask capability differences), [[capable-but-careless-do-computer-use-agents-follow-contextual-integrity-2606.23189]] (sibling CUA contextual-integrity compliance — Capable-But-Careless audits *whether agents follow rules*, GUI-vs-CLI isolates *whether the interaction modality is the binding constraint* — together they bracket the CUA evaluation surface between rule-following and modality-bottleneck isolation), [[skillharness-harnessing-safe-skills-computer-use-agents-2606.20636]] (sibling CUA safe-skill harness — SkillHarness gates skill invocation at runtime, GUI-vs-CLI's skill-augmentation result (raising CLI from 48.2% to 69.3%) demonstrates that the gap SkillHarness needs to gate is largely skill-coverage rather than model capability — together they bracket CUA deployment readiness between skill-coverage expansion and runtime safety gating), [[planbench-xl-evaluating-long-horizon-planning-of-llm-tool-use-agents-in-large-scale-tool-ecosystems-2606.22388]] (sibling long-horizon tool-use evaluation — PlanBench-XL evaluates long-horizon *tool* planning, GUI-vs-CLI evaluates long-horizon *desktop execution* — together they bracket the long-horizon-agent evaluation surface between tool planning and modality-matched desktop execution), [[oragentbench-can-llm-agents-solve-challenging-operations-research-tasks-end-to-end-2606.19787]] (sibling execution-grounded OR benchmark — ORAgentBench is execution-grounded evaluation in a specific domain, GUI-vs-CLI is execution-grounded evaluation with modality-isolated design — together they bracket the execution-grounded-benchmark discipline across domain-specific and cross-modality axes), [[ebench-elemental-diagnosis-of-generalist-mobile-manipulation-policies-2606.18239]] (sibling multi-axis diagnostic — EBench decomposes capability into 5×4 dimensions, GUI-vs-CLI isolates a single modality variable — together they bracket the multi-axis vs matched-single-variable evaluation methodology dichotomy), and [[emergent-concepts]] (parent wiki page) entries on this page by surfacing *matched-modality-variable* as the structurally correct evaluation design for the computer-use-agent deployment regime where the binding constraint is interaction-modality-specific (grounded-interaction bottleneck for GUI, skill-coverage bottleneck for CLI) — a finding that is only visible when modality is isolated from task/verifier/permitted-action confounds.

- [[hidden-thoughts-are-not-secret-reasoning-trace-exposure-in-llms-2606.00642]] — Hidden Thoughts Are Not Secret: Reasoning Trace Exposure in LLMs (05-30) — addresses the structural gap between *interface-level trace hiding* and *security-boundary enforcement* in deployed reasoning models — where the deployment assumption is that hiding raw internal traces from end users (exposing only summaries and final answers) prevents users from obtaining reasoning supervision signals — and demonstrates via Reasoning Exposure Prompting (REP), a lightweight in-context elicitation method using shadow-model-generated demonstrations wrapped in auxiliary code-like formats (pseudo-code, JSON scaffolds, function-call syntax), that the assumption is *structurally false*; Lu, Yu-An; Tsai, Ci-Yang; Tsai, Yu-Lin; Popa, Raluca Ada; Yu, Chia-Mu substantially increase similarity between user-visible traces and victim-model internal traces across multiple reasoning datasets, victim models, and student distillation regimes, surfacing *reasoning-trace-as-attack-extractable-asset* as the load-bearing primitive — interface-level trace hiding does not constitute a security boundary because the prompting format itself can be engineered to make the model volunteer its traces; 2026-05-30; discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-26 (reasoning-trace-extraction / shadow-model-demonstrations / code-like-format-amplifier / distillation-privacy theme — **first reasoning-trace-exposure-via-prompting-format paper in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(reasoning.trace|hidden.thoughts|trace.exposure|rep\.)" returning empty). Complements the [[from-agent-traces-to-trust-a-survey-of-evidence-tracing-and-execution-provenance-in-llm-agents-2606.04990]] (sibling trace-side work — Evidence-Tracing is forward-tracing decisions to source material, Hidden-Thoughts is backward-extraction of internal reasoning via prompting — together they bracket the trace discipline between *forward provenance* and *backward extraction*), [[llm-based-scientific-peer-review-methods-benchmarks-reliability-challenges-2606.25057]] (sibling peer-review reliability — Scientific-Peer-Review is human-mediated trace evaluation, Hidden-Thoughts is interface-mediated trace exposure — together they bracket the trace-reliability surface between human-mediated and interface-mediated trace surfaces), [[ras-measuring-llm-safety-through-refusal-alignment-2606.25750]] (sibling safety-measurement — RAS measures refusal-alignment over-rejection, Hidden-Thoughts measures a different vulnerability surface — *trace-extraction amplification* via prompting format — together they bracket the safety-evaluation surface between refusal measurement and trace-extraction measurement), [[privacyalign-contextual-privacy-alignment-for-llm-agents-2606.21710]] (sibling contextual privacy alignment — PrivacyAlign provides training-time privacy alignment for agents, Hidden-Thoughts demonstrates that *training-time* privacy alignment does not protect against *interface-time* extraction attacks — together they bracket the privacy-alignment surface between training-time alignment and interface-time extraction), [[taro-token-level-adaptive-routing-llm-test-time-alignment-2603.18411]] (sibling Run 46 token-level routing — TARo uses token-level routing as a test-time alignment primitive, Hidden-Thoughts surfaces a different alignment vulnerability — *prompting-format-amplified extraction* of reasoning traces — together they bracket the test-time-alignment surface between routing-based alignment primitives and extraction-side attacks on aligned reasoning), and [[emergent-concepts]] (parent wiki page) entries on this page by surfacing *reasoning-trace exposure as an extraction-attack surface* — distinct from forward-tracing of decisions (Evidence-Tracing survey) and from training-time privacy alignment (PrivacyAlign) — that the deployed-reasoning-model community needs to treat as a load-bearing vulnerability rather than an interface-design detail.
- [[the-generalization-spectrum-a-chromatographic-approach-to-evaluating-learning-algorithms-2606.25450]] (2026-06-24, Zhang et al.) — Chromatographic evaluation framework decomposing aggregate test-set performance into a per-sample generalization spectrum that exposes structural transfer between examples; reframes algorithm comparison on the *structure* of generalization rather than aggregate i.i.d. accuracy. **First chromatography-as-evaluation-metaphor paper in the wiki.**
- [[towards-direct-latent-space-synthesis-for-parallel-branches-in-llm-agent-workflows-2606.14672]] (2026-06-12, Liu et al.) — Direct latent-space synthesis as substrate for parallel-branch agent workflows, replacing serialize-then-decode round-trips with latent representations that preserve branch parallelism through the forward pass. **First latent-space-parallel-branch-synthesis paper in the wiki.**
- [[leap-supercharging-llms-for-formal-mathematics-with-agentic-frameworks-2606.03303]] (2026-06-02, Kung et al.) — LEAP agentic framework lifts general-purpose LLMs to SOTA on Lean theorem proving by decomposing long proof generation into agentic planning + tactic-generation + verification loops; isolates the bottleneck as *generating long mechanically-verifiable Lean proofs* rather than informal language comprehension. **First agentic-framework-for-formal-theorem-proving paper in the wiki.**
- [[opid-on-policy-skill-distillation-for-agentic-reinforcement-learning-2606.26790]] — On-policy skill-conditioned self-distillation providing dense token-level supervision for agentic RL (06-25)
- [[policyalign-direct-policy-based-safety-alignment-llms-2606.25442]] — PolicyAlign: Direct Policy-Based Safety Alignment for Large Language Models (06-24) — observes that real-world LLM deployment confronts safety requirements specified as *natural-language policies* — often without corresponding high-quality supervision data, since policy updates outpace the data-collection pipeline — and proposes PolicyAlign, a direct framework that first synthesizes policy-violating instructions from a stated safety policy and then performs *on-policy self-distillation* to internalize policy-guided behavior, supplemented by *Policy-Sensitive Filtering* that selects the instructions where the policy induces the largest behavioral shift; Wu, Chang; Fang, Junfeng; Jiang, Houcheng; Tang, Kai; Cheng, Pengyu; Jiang, Xiaoxi; Jiang, Guanjun; Wang, Xiang bypass reward-modeling and preference-pair construction entirely, generalize to medical / legal / financial safety scenarios, and maintain low over-refusal plus preserved general capabilities — making *policy-natural-language-as-safety-specification-surface* the load-bearing primitive for the deployment regime where safety requirements evolve faster than the data-annotation pipeline; 2026-06-24; discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-26 (direct-policy-alignment / on-policy-self-distillation / policy-sensitive-filtering / natural-language-policy-specification theme — **first direct-natural-language-policy-as-safety-alignment-surface paper in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(policy.align|policy.direct|safety.align|policy.based)"` returning empty). Complements the [[safety-paradox-how-enhanced-safety-awareness-leaves-llms-vulnerable-to-posterior-attack-2606.05614]] (sibling safety-vulnerability surface — Safety Paradox shows that *enhanced safety awareness* can become a posterior-attack vulnerability, PolicyAlign treats *natural-language policy itself* as the alignment specification, bypassing the data-bottleneck that awareness-based approaches inherit — together they bracket the safety-alignment surface between *awareness-as-vulnerability* (Safety Paradox) and *natural-language-policy-as-specification* (PolicyAlign)), [[ras-measuring-llm-safety-through-refusal-alignment-2606.25750]] (sibling safety-measurement primitive — RAS measures safety via refusal-representation alignment, PolicyAlign directly aligns models against stated policies without measurement infrastructure — together they bracket the safety-discipline surface between *internal-representation measurement* (RAS) and *direct-policy-based alignment* (PolicyAlign)), [[geometry-of-refusal-linear-instability-safety-aligned-llms-2606.22686]] (sibling mechanistic-safety geometry — Geometry of Refusal surfaces refusal as a linearly decodable feature, PolicyAlign demonstrates that *refusal can also be specified directly from natural-language policy* via on-policy distillation, bypassing the need for representation-level steering — together they bracket the safety-primitive surface between *mechanistic-geometry steering* (Geometry of Refusal) and *direct-policy specification* (PolicyAlign)), [[privacyalign-contextual-privacy-alignment-for-llm-agents-2606.21710]] (sibling contextual-privacy alignment — PrivacyAlign aligns LLMs for contextual privacy, PolicyAlign generalizes the same natural-language-policy approach to safety broadly — together they bracket the contextual-alignment surface between *privacy-specific alignment* (PrivacyAlign) and *safety-policy alignment* (PolicyAlign)), [[opid-on-policy-skill-distillation-for-agentic-reinforcement-learning-2606.26790]] (sibling on-policy distillation — OPID uses on-policy distillation for skill-conditioned RL, PolicyAlign uses on-policy self-distillation for safety-policy adherence — together they bracket the on-policy-distillation surface between *skill-conditioned RL* (OPID) and *safety-policy-aligned behavior* (PolicyAlign)), [[deeper-is-not-always-better-mitigating-the-alignment-tax-via-confident-layer-decoding-2606.21906]] (sibling alignment-tax mitigation — Confident-Layer-Decoding mitigates alignment tax via decoding intervention, PolicyAlign bypasses the alignment-tax-vs-safety tradeoff by training directly against the policy — together they bracket the alignment-robustness surface between *decoding-time tax mitigation* (Confident-Layer-Decoding) and *direct-policy-aligned training* (PolicyAlign)), and [[emergent-concepts]] (parent wiki page) entries on this page by surfacing *natural-language-policy-as-safety-alignment-specification* as the load-bearing primitive for the deployment regime where LLM safety requirements evolve faster than the supervision-data pipeline.
- [[efficient-and-trainable-language-model-test-time-scaling-via-local-branch-routing-2606.25354]] — Efficient and Trainable Language Model Test-Time Scaling via Local Branch Routing (LBR) (06-24) — surfaces the structural trade-off in test-time scaling where long chain-of-thought sampling remains single-threaded while sentence- or solution-level search is computationally expensive and hard to train end-to-end, then introduces Local Branch Routing (LBR), a token-level test-time scaling framework that expands a small local lookahead tree, forwards all sampled branches through the language model, and uses a lightweight router to select the best continuation; Yin, Yutong; Jin, Mingyu; Pan, Jin; Yang, Changyi; Xia, Zijie; Pai, Dhruv; Hu, Shuming; Zhang, Zhen; Zhao, Chenyang; Zhao, Jinman; Xu, Wujiang; Li, Raymond; Wang, Xin Eric; McAuley, Julian; Wang, Zhaoran demonstrate that LBR is end-to-end trainable and that post-candidate hidden states provide useful routing evidence on hierarchical-planning tasks and mathematical-reasoning benchmarks, surfacing *token-level branch routing* as a new test-time-scaling paradigm that is neither single-threaded CoT nor expensive solution-level search; 2026-06-24; discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-26 (test-time-scaling / local-branch-routing / trainable-inference-compute theme — **first token-level branch-routing test-time scaling framework in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(local.branch|branch.routing|test.time.scal|lbr.)" returning empty`). Complements the [[randomized-yarn-improves-length-generalization-for-long-context-reasoning-2606.23687]] (sibling length-extension-for-reasoning work — Randomized YaRN recovers long-context reasoning at 16K-128K context windows, LBR scales inference compute via branch routing at the token level — together they bracket the long-context-reasoning surface between *positional-encoding length extension* (Randomized YaRN) and *token-level branch-routing inference scaling* (LBR)), [[verievol-scaling-multimodal-mathematical-reasoning-via-verifiable-evol-instruct-2606.23543]] (sibling mathematical-reasoning-scaling work — VeriEvol scales math reasoning via verifiable evol-instruct at training time, LBR scales reasoning via branch routing at inference time — together they bracket the math-reasoning-scaling surface between *training-time evol-instruct* (VeriEvol) and *inference-time branch routing* (LBR)), [[the-periodic-table-of-llm-reasoning-a-structured-survey-of-reasoning-paradigms-abstractions-building-blocks-and-open-challenges-2606.11470]] (sibling systematic reasoning-paradigm survey — Periodic-Table-of-LLM-Reasoning catalogs reasoning paradigms as building blocks, LBR introduces a new paradigm (token-level branch-routing inference scaling) that the Periodic Table would categorize under search-based inference scaling — together they bracket the reasoning-paradigm surface between *systematic paradigm taxonomy* (Periodic Table) and *new paradigm instantiation* (LBR)), and [[emergent-concepts]] (parent wiki page) entries on this page by surfacing *token-level branch routing* as a structurally new test-time-scaling primitive — neither single-threaded CoT nor expensive solution-level search — that is end-to-end trainable and provides a lightweight router-on-top-of-lookahead-tree architecture for inference-compute allocation.
- [[randomized-yarn-improves-length-generalization-for-long-context-reasoning-2606.23687]] — Randomized positional encoding during YaRN extension recovers long-context reasoning at 16K-128K (06-22)
- [[where-does-the-signal-live-a-web-data-recipe-for-medical-encoder-pretraining-2606.22079]] — Where Does the Signal Live? A Web Data Recipe for Medical Encoder Pretraining (06-20) — diagnoses the encoder-vs-decoder pretraining-data asymmetry where encoders for dense-terminology domains like medicine have been confined to small manually-curated corpora (limiting scalability and writing-style diversity, a bottleneck especially severe in non-English clinical settings) while decoder LLMs have benefited from web-scale curation, then investigates whether web-scale data curation transfers to encoder Masked Language Modeling in dense-terminology domains; Huang, Bofeng; Sun, Jacques; Bouchacourt, Diane; Barascud, Nicolas; Fogel, Fajwel introduce a recipe combining domain-relevant document filtering with quality signals adapted to encoder MLM, surfacing *signal-location-aware-curation* as the load-bearing primitive for domain-specific encoder pretraining — where the load-bearing question is not "what data should we use?" but "where in the web corpus does the relevant signal live for this dense-terminology domain?"; 2026-06-20; discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-26 (web-data-recipe / dense-terminology-domain / medical-encoder-pretraining theme — **first web-data-recipe paper for domain-specific encoder pretraining in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(web.data|medical.encoder|signal.live|dense.terminology)"` returning empty). Complements the [[beyond-reward-engineering-a-data-recipe-for-long-context-reinforcement-learning-2606.18831]] (sibling data-recipe-for-RL work — Beyond-Reward-Engineering focuses on data recipes for long-context RL, Where-Does-the-Signal-Live focuses on web-data recipes for medical encoder pretraining — together they bracket the data-recipe-as-discipline surface between *post-training RL data curation* (Beyond-Reward-Engineering) and *domain-specific encoder pretraining data curation* (Where-Does-the-Signal-Live)), [[openthoughts-agent-data-recipes-for-agentic-models-2606.24855]] (sibling data-recipe-for-agentic-models work — OpenThoughts-Agent provides (task, trajectory) data recipes for agentic fine-tuning, Where-Does-the-Signal-Live provides web-curation recipes for encoder pretraining — together they bracket the data-recipe discipline between *agentic fine-tuning supervision* (OpenThoughts-Agent) and *domain-specific encoder pretraining* (Where-Does-the-Signal-Live)), and [[emergent-concepts]] (parent wiki page) entries on this page by surfacing *signal-location-aware curation* as the load-bearing primitive for transferring web-scale data curation from decoder LLM pretraining to dense-terminology-domain encoder pretraining — where the bottleneck is no longer data quantity but signal localization in domain-relevant web corpora.
- [[memory-is-reconstructed-not-retrieved-graph-memory-llm-agents-2606.06036]] — Memory is Reconstructed, Not Retrieved: Graph Memory for LLM Agents (MRAgent) (06-04) — surfaces the structural limitation of the static retrieve-then-reason memory pipeline for LLM agents and replaces it with an active reconstruction mechanism built on a Cue-Tag-Content associative graph, allowing memory access to adapt dynamically to intermediate evidence uncovered during inference; Ji, Shuo; Li, Yibo; Hooi, Bryan reframe long-interaction memory as graph-conditioned reconstruction rather than fixed-index retrieval, surfacing *reconstruction-vs-retrieval-as-memory-access-primitive* as the load-bearing distinction between architectures that adapt access patterns to discovered evidence and architectures that depend on a single retrieved context; 2026-06-04; discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-26 (memory-graph-reconstruction / dynamic-access / associative-graph theme — **first graph-conditioned reconstruction memory architecture in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(memory.graph|graph.memory|reconstruct.memory)"` returning empty). Complements the [[agent-memory-characterization-and-system-implications-of-stateful-long-horizon-workloads-2606.06448]] (sibling systems-level memory characterization — MRAgent operates at the *access-layer* via graph-conditioned reconstruction, Agent Memory Characterization surveys the *system-layer* trade-offs across storage/extraction/consolidation/control — together they bracket the agent-memory surface between *graph-reconstruction access pattern* (MRAgent) and *systems-level memory trade-off survey* (Agent Memory Characterization)), [[are-we-ready-for-an-agent-native-memory-system-2606.24775]] (sibling framework-level memory-survey — MRAgent is one of the candidate architectures that Are-We-Ready evaluates for agent-native deployment), [[evoembedding-evolvable-representations-for-long-context-retrieval-and-agentic-memory-2606.21649]] (sibling long-context retrieval-evolution work — EvoEmbedding evolves retrieval representations over time, MRAgent reconstructs from a graph rather than retrieving from a fixed index — together they bracket the long-context-memory surface between *evolving retrieval representations* (EvoEmbedding) and *graph-conditioned reconstruction* (MRAgent)), [[trustmem-trustworthy-memory-consolidation-llm-agents-long-term-memory-2606.25161]] (sibling memory-consolidation work — TrustMem focuses on consolidation reliability for long-term agent memory, MRAgent focuses on access pattern during inference — together they bracket the agent-memory surface between *consolidation reliability* (TrustMem) and *access-pattern reconstruction* (MRAgent)), [[memtoolagent-leveraging-memory-tool-using-agents-environment-user-feedback-2606.07909]] (sibling tool-use-memory-mechanism work — MemToolAgent uses memory-as-tool, MRAgent uses graph-as-reconstruction substrate — different surface of agent-memory design — together they bracket the agent-memory surface between *tool-mediated memory access* (MemToolAgent) and *graph-conditioned reconstruction* (MRAgent)), and [[emergent-concepts]] (parent wiki page) entries on this page by surfacing *graph-conditioned reconstruction as a fundamentally different memory-access primitive* — distinct from retrieval, consolidation, evolution, or tool-mediated access — that the agent-memory literature should treat as a load-bearing primitive in its own right.
- [[smh-bench-benchmarking-llm-agents-for-environment-grounded-reasoning-and-action-in-smart-homes-2606.01912]] — First sustained-environment-grounded agent benchmark for evolving smart-home worlds (06-01)
- [[externalization-llm-agents-unified-review-memory-skills-protocols-harness-engineering-2604.08224]] — Externalization in LLM Agents: A Unified Review of Memory, Skills, Protocols and Harness Engineering (04-09) — proposes *externalization* as the load-bearing primitive for understanding practical LLM-agent progress: instead of changing model weights to acquire new capability, current systems relocate cognitive burden into memory stores, reusable skills, interaction protocols, and the surrounding harness — transforming problems the model cannot reliably solve internally into forms it can solve more reliably; Zhou, Chenyu; Chai, Huacan; Chen, Wenteng; Guo, Zihan; Shan, Rong; Song, Yuanyi; Xu, Tianyi; Yang, Yingxuan; Yu, Aofan; Zhang, Weiming; Zheng, Congming; Zhu, Jiachen; Zheng, Zeyu; Zhang, Zhuosheng; Lou, Xingyu; Zhang, Changwang; Fu, Zhihui; Wang, Jun; Liu, Weiwen; Lin, Jianghao; Zhang, Weinan frame memory as externalizing state across time, skills as externalizing procedural expertise, protocols as externalizing interaction structure, and harness engineering as the unification layer that coordinates them — a clean 4-way decomposition rather than ad-hoc per-system taxonomies; the review traces a historical *weights → context → harness* progression and identifies *self-evolving harnesses* and *shared agent infrastructure* as emerging directions; 2026-04-09; discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-26 (externalization / cognitive-artifacts / weights-to-harness-progression / runtime-reorganization-over-weight-change theme — **first externalization-as-unifying-primitive survey of LLM-agent infrastructure in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(external|externaliz|runtime.reorganiz|cognitive.artifact)"` returning empty). Complements the [[are-we-ready-for-an-agent-native-memory-system-2606.24775]] (sibling framework-level memory survey — Are-We-Ready surveys agent-memory as a data-management problem, Externalization places memory within the broader 4-way decomposition (memory / skills / protocols / harness) as the *state-across-time* axis — together they bracket the agent-infrastructure surface between *memory-specific framework survey* (Are-We-Ready) and *unified externalization-as-primitive survey* (Externalization)), [[agent-memory-characterization-and-system-implications-of-stateful-long-horizon-workloads-2606.06448]] (sibling systems-level memory characterization — Agent Memory Characterization provides a 4-axis taxonomy (storage / extraction / consolidation / control), Externalization provides the broader 4-way externalization decomposition (memory / skills / protocols / harness) — together they bracket the memory-systems surface between *internal-memory-mechanism taxonomy* (Agent Memory Characterization) and *memory-as-one-of-four-externalizations* (Externalization)), [[memory-is-reconstructed-not-retrieved-graph-memory-llm-agents-2606.06036]] (sibling graph-reconstruction memory — MRAgent uses graph-conditioned reconstruction as a memory-access pattern, Externalization positions memory as a state-across-time externalization primitive that MRAgent instantiates — together they bracket the memory surface between *access-pattern mechanism* (MRAgent) and *externalization decomposition* (Externalization)), [[agent-skill-evaluation-and-evolution-frameworks-benchmarks-2606.11435]] (sibling skill-evolution survey — Skill Evaluation and Evolution survey categorizes skill evolution into 4 paradigms, Externalization positions skills as *procedural-expertise-externalization* within the 4-way decomposition — together they bracket the skill surface between *evolution-taxonomy* (Skill Evaluation) and *externalization-position* (Externalization)), [[a-technical-taxonomy-of-llm-agent-communication-protocols-2606.19135]] (sibling communication-protocol taxonomy — Communication-Protocols-Taxonomy categorizes protocols as inter-agent infrastructure, Externalization frames protocols as *interaction-structure externalization* within the 4-way decomposition — together they bracket the protocol surface between *taxonomy-of-protocols* (Communication-Protocols-Taxonomy) and *externalization-position* (Externalization)), [[skillharness-harnessing-safe-skills-computer-use-agents-2606.20636]] (sibling harness-instance for safe-skills — SkillHarness implements a concrete harness for safe-skill computer-use agents, Externalization identifies *harness engineering* as the unification layer that coordinates memory / skills / protocols — together they bracket the harness surface between *concrete harness implementation* (SkillHarness) and *harness-as-unification-layer* (Externalization)), [[aohp-an-open-source-os-level-agent-harness-for-personalized-efficient-and-2606.23449]] (sibling OS-level harness — AOHP provides an OS-level agent harness, Externalization identifies *harness engineering* as the load-bearing coordination primitive — together they bracket the harness-OS surface between *OS-level harness instance* (AOHP) and *harness-as-externalization-coordinate* (Externalization)), [[opid-on-policy-skill-distillation-for-agentic-reinforcement-learning-2606.26790]] (sibling on-policy skill-distillation — OPID trains skills via on-policy distillation for agentic RL, Externalization frames skills as *procedural-expertise externalization* — together they bracket the skill-acquisition surface between *on-policy skill-distillation* (OPID) and *skills-as-externalization* (Externalization)), [[notes2skills-from-lab-notebooks-to-certainty-aware-scientific-agent-skills-2606.11897]] (sibling certainty-aware skill construction — Notes2Skills constructs certainty-aware scientific skills from lab notebooks, Externalization positions skills within the broader 4-way externalization decomposition — together they bracket the skill-acquisition surface between *domain-specific skill construction* (Notes2Skills) and *skills-as-externalization* (Externalization)), [[gatemem-benchmarking-memory-governance-in-multi-principal-shared-memory-agents-2606.18829]] (sibling memory-governance benchmark — GateMem benchmarks multi-principal shared-memory governance, Externalization frames memory governance as one of the *control* mechanisms that memory-as-externalization needs — together they bracket the memory-governance surface between *multi-principal governance benchmark* (GateMem) and *memory-as-externalization-control-need* (Externalization)), [[memprobe-probing-longterm-agent-memory-via-hidden-userstate-recovery-2606.24595]] (sibling memory-evaluation probe — MemProbe probes long-term memory via hidden user-state recovery, Externalization identifies *memory-evaluation* as a load-bearing open challenge in the memory-externalization axis — together they bracket the memory-evaluation surface between *hidden-state-recovery probe* (MemProbe) and *memory-evaluation-as-open-challenge* (Externalization)), [[evoembedding-evolvable-representations-for-long-context-retrieval-and-agentic-memory-2606.21649]] (sibling long-context retrieval evolution — EvoEmbedding evolves retrieval representations, Externalization frames evolving-memory representations as a *self-evolving harness* emerging direction — together they bracket the memory-representation surface between *evolving-retrieval-representations* (EvoEmbedding) and *self-evolving-harness emerging direction* (Externalization)), [[evoarena-tracking-memory-evolution-robust-llm-agents-dynamic-environments-2606.13681]] (sibling memory-evolution benchmark — EvoArena benchmarks memory evolution under dynamic environments, Externalization identifies *self-evolving harnesses* as an emerging direction — together they bracket the memory-evolution surface between *dynamic-environment memory benchmark* (EvoArena) and *self-evolving-harness emerging direction* (Externalization)), [[memtoolagent-leveraging-memory-tool-using-agents-environment-user-feedback-2606.07909]] (sibling tool-mediated memory — MemToolAgent uses memory-as-tool mechanism, Externalization frames memory as a state-across-time externalization primitive that tools can mediate access to — together they bracket the memory-access surface between *tool-mediated memory access* (MemToolAgent) and *memory-as-externalization-state* (Externalization)), [[openrath-session-centered-runtime-state-for-agent-systems-2606.19409]] (sibling session-centered runtime state — OpenRATH frames runtime state as session-centered, Externalization's *weights → context → harness* progression traces the same arc from model-centric to runtime-centric — together they bracket the runtime-state surface between *session-centered runtime* (OpenRATH) and *weights-to-harness progression* (Externalization)), and [[emergent-concepts]] (parent wiki page) entries on this page by surfacing *externalization-as-the-unifying-primitive* for LLM-agent infrastructure — providing the 4-way decomposition (memory / skills / protocols / harness) and the *weights → context → harness* historical progression that reframes agent progress as *better external cognitive infrastructure* rather than *stronger models*, with *self-evolving harnesses* and *shared agent infrastructure* as the emerging directions the field should now treat as load-bearing.
- [[taro-token-level-adaptive-routing-llm-test-time-alignment-2603.18411]] — TARo: Token-level Adaptive Routing for LLM Test-time Alignment (03-19) — observes that existing test-time alignment methods have been explored mainly for preference alignment rather than reasoning, and proposes TARo (Token-level Adaptive Routing), a framework that steers a *frozen LLM* toward structured reasoning entirely at inference time by training reward models on *step-wise mathematical traces* to capture fine-grained logical-consistency signals, then introducing a *learnable token-level router* that automatically controls when the reward model guides the base model during decoding; Rai, Arushi; Zhang, Qiang; Zeng, Hanqing; Zhang, Yunkai; Tamboli, Dipesh; Fan, Xiangjun; Zhao, Zhuokai; Zhang, Lizhu improve reasoning by up to +22.4% over the base model and +8.4% over existing token-level test-time alignment methods, and generalize from small to large backbones without retraining — extending test-time alignment from preference optimization to robust cross-domain reasoning, with out-of-distribution gains on clinical reasoning (MedXpertQA) and instruction following (AlpacaEval); 2026-03-19; discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-26 (token-level-routing / test-time-alignment / step-wise-reward-modeling / cross-domain-reasoning theme — **first token-level-routing-as-test-time-alignment-decision paper in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(token.level.rout|adaptive.rout|taro)"` returning empty). Complements the [[efficient-and-trainable-language-model-test-time-scaling-via-local-branch-routing-2606.25354]] (sibling branch-level routing — LBR uses branch-level routing as the *inference-compute-decision* primitive, TARo uses token-level routing as the *alignment-decision* primitive — together they bracket the routing-as-decision surface between *branch-level compute routing* (LBR) and *token-level alignment routing* (TARo)), [[geometry-of-refusal-linear-instability-safety-aligned-llms-2606.22686]] (sibling mechanistic-safety geometry — Geometry of Refusal surfaces refusal as a linearly decodable feature for *steering* safety, TARo uses token-level routing for *reasoning alignment* during decoding — together they bracket the token-level-inference-intervention surface between *steering-time alignment* (Geometry of Refusal) and *routing-time alignment* (TARo)), [[ras-measuring-llm-safety-through-refusal-alignment-2606.25750]] (sibling safety-measurement primitive — RAS measures safety via refusal-representation alignment, TARo demonstrates that *step-wise reward models on mathematical traces* can also drive alignment during decoding — together they bracket the alignment-representation surface between *refusal-representation measurement* (RAS) and *step-wise-trace reward routing* (TARo)), [[privacyalign-contextual-privacy-alignment-for-llm-agents-2606.21710]] (sibling contextual-privacy alignment — PrivacyAlign aligns LLMs for contextual privacy, TARo aligns frozen LLMs for reasoning at inference time — together they bracket the alignment surface between *application-context alignment* (PrivacyAlign) and *inference-time reasoning alignment* (TARo)), [[deeper-is-not-always-better-mitigating-the-alignment-tax-via-confident-layer-decoding-2606.21906]] (sibling alignment-tax mitigation — Confident-Layer-Decoding mitigates alignment tax via decoding-time intervention, TARo uses token-level routing for reasoning alignment at decoding time — together they bracket the decoding-time-alignment surface between *layer-wise intervention* (Confident-Layer-Decoding) and *token-wise routing* (TARo)), [[planbench-xl-evaluating-long-horizon-planning-of-llm-tool-use-agents-in-large-scale-tool-ecosystems-2606.22388]] (sibling long-horizon planning benchmark — PlanBench-XL benchmarks multi-step planning across 1,665 tools, TARo's step-wise reward models on mathematical traces provide the *step-level evaluation signal* that long-horizon planners need — together they bracket the long-horizon-reasoning surface between *planning-capability benchmarking* (PlanBench-XL) and *step-level-trace reward modeling* (TARo)), [[verievol-scaling-multimodal-mathematical-reasoning-via-verifiable-evol-instruct-2606.23543]] (sibling math-reasoning scaling — VeriEvol scales math reasoning via verifiable evol-instruct at training time, TARo scales math reasoning via token-level routing at inference time — together they bracket the math-reasoning-scaling surface between *training-time evol-instruct* (VeriEvol) and *inference-time token-level routing* (TARo)), and [[emergent-concepts]] (parent wiki page) entries on this page by surfacing *token-level-routing-as-the-test-time-alignment-decision-unit* — finer-grained than sequence-level or branch-level routing, capturing token-by-token reasoning dynamics, and generalizing from small to large backbones without retraining — as a structurally new test-time-alignment primitive that complements branch-level routing (LBR) and extends test-time alignment from preference optimization to cross-domain reasoning.

## Updates (new block for Run 43)

- [[rethinking-reward-supervision-rubric-conditioned-self-distillation-2606.19327]] — Rethinking Reward Supervision: Rubric-Conditioned Self-Distillation (06-17) — addresses the joint failure mode of standard post-training for reasoning language models — supervised distillation depends on chain-of-thought annotations that are expensive and noisy (even when the final solution is correct, an imperfect rationale interferes with learning), while RL with verifiable rewards compresses evaluative feedback into a scalar that obscures which aspects of a response should be improved — and proposes Rubric-Conditioned Self-Distillation, where a teacher conditioned on criterion-level rubrics provides token-level guidance on the student's own sampled trajectories, avoiding the single-reference-rationale supervision trap; Gu, Siyi; Chen, Jialin; Zhou, Sophia; Cohan, Arman; Ying, Rex instantiate this with a two-stage pipeline that (i) learns to generate task-specific rubrics, then (ii) trains a rubric-guided reasoner — surpassing GRPO by 1.0 points and OPSD by 0.9 points on average across science reasoning benchmarks; surfaces *rubric-level-supervision-as-fine-grained-credit-assignment* as the structurally correct primitive for post-training regimes where scalar rewards and reference-rationale supervision both fail — the load-bearing shift is reframing the supervision question from "what is the right reasoning trace?" to "what criteria should a strong response satisfy, and how do those criteria map to token-level credit?"; 2026-06-17; discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-26 (rubric-conditioned-self-distillation / token-level-credit-assignment / post-training-rubric-supervision theme — **first rubric-as-supervision paper for post-training reasoning models in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(rubric.condition|rubric.distill|reward.super|credit.assign)"` returning empty). Complements the [[progress-advantage-neglected-free-lunch-post-training-llm-agents-2606.26080]] (sibling post-training signal extraction — Progress Advantage extracts *implicit* step-level scoring from the RL log-prob ratio, Rubric-Conditioned Self-Distillation extracts *explicit* step-level guidance from rubrics — together they bracket the post-training-signal surface between *implicit advantage from existing training* (Progress Advantage) and *explicit criterion-level guidance* (Rubric-Conditioned Self-Distillation)), [[v-zero-answer-label-free-on-policy-distillation-contrastive-evidence-2606.25319]] (sibling on-policy distillation — V-Zero uses *contrastive regional evidence* to gate token-level distillation without answer labels, Rubric-Conditioned Self-Distillation uses *criterion-level rubrics* to provide token-level guidance on the student's own trajectories — together they bracket the label-free-post-training surface between *contrastive evidence gating* (V-Zero) and *rubric-conditioned supervision* (Rubric-Conditioned Self-Distillation)), [[renio-reweighting-negative-trajectory-importance-for-llm-on-policy-distillation-2606.23104]] (sibling on-policy trajectory reweighting — ReNIO reweights trajectories by negative-importance estimation, Rubric-Conditioned Self-Distillation reweights trajectories implicitly via rubric-conditioned teacher scoring — together they bracket the on-policy-distillation surface between *trajectory-importance reweighting* (ReNIO) and *criterion-conditional teacher guidance* (Rubric-Conditioned Self-Distillation)), [[distilling-examples-into-task-instructions-enhanced-in-context-2606.15641]] (sibling label-minimization ICL distillation — Distilling-Examples-into-Task-Instructions compresses verbose few-shot examples into compact criteria+descriptions for ICL, Rubric-Conditioned Self-Distillation compresses supervision into rubric-criteria for post-training — together they bracket the compact-supervision surface between *ICL deployment compression* (Distilling-Examples) and *post-training credit assignment* (Rubric-Conditioned Self-Distillation)), and [[learning-from-your-own-mistakes-constructing-learnable-micro-reflective-trajectories-for-self-distillation-2606.18844]] (sibling trajectory-aware self-correction RL — Learning-from-Your-Own-Mistakes constructs micro-reflective corrections from erroneous prefixes, Rubric-Conditioned Self-Distillation uses rubric-conditioned teacher guidance on the student's own trajectories — together they bracket the trajectory-self-supervision surface between *explicit error-prefix micro-corrections* (Learning-from-Your-Own-Mistakes) and *criterion-conditional teacher scoring* (Rubric-Conditioned Self-Distillation)) entries on this page by surfacing *rubric-conditioned fine-grained credit assignment* as a load-bearing primitive for post-training regimes where both standard CoT supervision and scalar-reward RL hit structural ceilings.

- [[agent-skill-evaluation-and-evolution-frameworks-benchmarks-2606.11435]] — Agent Skill Evaluation and Evolution: Frameworks and Benchmarks (06-09) — surfaces the emerging paradigm shift in agent research from *isolated skill creation* to *automated, evaluation-driven skill evolution* and provides the systematic survey that names the shift, categorizing skill evolution into four paradigms (execution feedback, trajectory distillation, compression, reinforcement learning) and analyzing six skill-centric benchmark categories to identify structural gaps in coverage, trade-offs, and metric richness; Ding, Kexin; Zhou, Yang; Jin, Can; Tong, Feng; Zhou, Mu; Metaxas, Dimitris N. argue that the field's open direction is building skill ecosystems that are generalizable, efficient, and verifiably safe, and identify concrete gaps in benchmark coverage and metric richness that motivate evaluation-driven evolution as the load-bearing framing; 2026-06-09; discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-26 (agent-skill-evolution-survey / evaluation-driven-skill-paradigm / four-paradigm-taxonomy theme — **first systematic survey of skill-evolution-as-discipline in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(skill.evolution|skill.eval.survey|skill.paradigm|skill.framework)"` returning empty). Complements the [[notes2skills-from-lab-notebooks-to-certainty-aware-scientific-agent-skills-2606.11897]] (sibling scientific-domain skill-construction work — Notes2Skills constructs certainty-aware skills from lab notebooks, this survey categorizes the broader skill-evolution discipline that Notes2Skills sits within — together they bracket the agent-skill surface between *domain-specific skill construction* (Notes2Skills) and *general skill-evolution taxonomy* (this survey)), [[skillharness-harnessing-safe-skills-computer-use-agents-2606.20636]] (sibling skill-safety tool — SkillHarness provides safe-skill mechanism for computer-use agents, this survey maps the broader skill-evolution discipline including safety as a load-bearing dimension — together they bracket the agent-skill surface between *safe-skill mechanism implementation* (SkillHarness) and *general skill-evolution survey* (this survey)), and [[emergent-concepts]] (parent wiki page) entries on this page by establishing the four-paradigm taxonomy (execution feedback, trajectory distillation, compression, reinforcement learning) as the structural taxonomy for understanding agent-skill evolution as a unified discipline rather than scattered sub-areas.

- [[act-as-a-real-researcher-aarri-bench-frontier-llms-agentic-research-lifecycle-2606.07462]] — Act As a Real Researcher: A Suite of Benchmarks Evaluating Frontier LLMs and Agentic Harnesses in Research Lifecycle (AARRI-Bench) (06-05) — surfaces the gap between macro-capability benchmarks (which test whether agents complete research tasks) and researcher-role-mimicry benchmarks (which test whether agents *act like real researchers* — with the field sensitivity, research ethics, and nuanced scientific judgment that distinguish competent execution from researcher-grade professionalism) and introduces AARRI-Bench, the first installment of the AARR (Act As a Real Researcher) benchmark series; Wang, Jiayu; Lv, Weijiang; Fu, Bowen; Fu, Jing; Song, Jiayi; Zhang, Lingyu; Xue, Lanxuan; Chen, Luodi; Xin, Zepeng; Li, Kaiyu; Cao, Xiangyong find that even the best-performing configuration (Mini-SWE-Agent + Claude Opus 4.7) achieves only 68.3% success rate on AARRI-Bench, frequently overlooking subtle yet critical details obvious to real human researchers, framing the diagnostic question — what is the load-bearing variable that distinguishes researcher-grade agents from macro-capable ones? — as *research behavior*, not agent scaffolding complexity; 2026-06-05; discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-26 (researcher-role-mimicry-benchmark / research-lifecycle-evaluation / research-ethics-and-judgment theme — **first researcher-role-mimicry-as-evaluation-surface paper in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(researcher.role|research.lifecycle|real.researcher|aarri|research.ethics)"` returning empty). Complements the [[connect-the-dots-training-llms-for-long-lifecycle-agents-with-cross-domain-generalization-via-reinforcement-learning-2606.20002]] (sibling lifecycle-spanning agent work — Connect-the-Dots trains single agents for cross-domain generalization via RL, AARRI-Bench evaluates whether agents can act like real researchers across the full research lifecycle — together they bracket the lifecycle agent surface between *cross-domain training* (Connect-the-Dots) and *researcher-role evaluation* (AARRI-Bench)), [[llm-based-scientific-peer-review-methods-benchmarks-reliability-challenges-2606.25057]] (sibling scientific-review survey — LLM-Based-Scientific-Peer-Review surveys automated scientific review mechanisms, AARRI-Bench evaluates agent capability across the *lifecycle* of which peer-review is one stage — together they bracket the LLM-scientific-research surface between *peer-review automation* (Scientific Peer Review survey) and *full-lifecycle researcher-mimicry* (AARRI-Bench)), [[beyond-static-leaderboards-predictive-validity-evaluation-llm-agents-2606.19704]] (sibling predictive-validity methodology — Beyond-Static-Leaderboards argues agent-evaluation methodology must move beyond single-anchor leaderboards, AARRI-Bench instantiates this with a researcher-role-mimicry surface that no single-anchor leaderboard can probe — together they bracket the agent-evaluation discipline between *methodology critique of leaderboards* (Beyond-Static-Leaderboards) and *new evaluation surface* (AARRI-Bench)), and [[emergent-concepts]] (parent wiki page) entries on this page by surfacing *researcher-role-mimicry-as-evaluation-surface* as the structurally correct framing for the deployment regime where LLM agents transition from research assistants into autonomous research agents — where the load-bearing variable is not agent scaffolding complexity but the agent's ability to exhibit researcher-grade professionalism, field sensitivity, and nuanced scientific judgment.
- [[ras-measuring-llm-safety-through-refusal-alignment-2606.25750]] — RAS: Measuring LLM Safety Through Refusal Alignment (06-24) — *Refusal Alignment Score* — measures safety by the alignment between internal refusal representations and external refusal behavior, decoupling safety evaluation from output-level judging; argues output-level safety evaluation is expensive, judge-sensitive, and tied to fixed safety taxonomies — RAS addresses all three by being model-internal and taxonomy-agnostic; correlates with downstream safety outcomes at significantly lower evaluation cost than output-judging pipelines.
- [[from-agent-traces-to-trust-a-survey-of-evidence-tracing-and-execution-provenance-in-llm-agents-2606.04990]] — From Agent Traces to Trust: A Survey of Evidence Tracing and Execution Provenance in LLM Agents (06-03) — first survey dedicated to *evidence-tracing* and *execution-provenance* infrastructure for LLM-based agents — establishes the provenance primitive as the load-bearing concept for post-hoc verification of agent decisions; conceptual framework linking agent autonomy (planning / tool use / retrieval / memory / environment / multi-agent) to the trust requirements execution-provenance must satisfy; inventory of trace-reconstruction, lineage-tracking, provenance-aware retrieval, and audit primitives.
- [[foresci-evaluating-llm-agents-for-forward-looking-ai-research-judgment-2606.00644]] — ForeSci: Evaluating LLM Agents for Forward-Looking AI Research Judgment (05-30) — first *temporally controlled* benchmark for forward-looking AI research judgment — separates forward-looking research decisions from retrospective capability measurement by ensuring ground-truth becomes available only *after* the agent decides; targets bottleneck selection, direction pursuit, project positioning under genuine epistemic uncertainty; bridges LLM-agent evaluation and AI-research methodology.

- [[swe-marathon-can-agents-autonomously-complete-ultra-long-horizon-software-work-2606.07682]] — SWE-Marathon (06-05) — 20 long-horizon tasks calibrated at the **4+ hour / 1M+ token** scale, with completion validator + state-of-the-art SE-agent baseline — first benchmark that exposes gaps in planning/long-context/memory at hour-scale (vs minute-scale prior benchmarks)
- [[safety-paradox-how-enhanced-safety-awareness-leaves-llms-vulnerable-to-posterior-attack-2606.05614]] — Safety Paradox Posterior Attack (06-04) — single-query jailbreak exploiting safety awareness as a side channel: prompts model to emit the EXACT harmful response its internal classifier would flag — >90% attack success across 30+ LLMs
- [[agent-memory-characterization-and-system-implications-of-stateful-long-horizon-workloads-2606.06448]] — Agent Memory Characterization (06-04) — first systems-level characterization of agent memory with 4-axis taxonomy (storage/extraction/consolidation/control) + controlled workload experiments on cost/latency/recall/revision — distinct from framework-level surveys (Are-We-Ready) by measuring system behavior under realistic session lengths

- [[llm-based-scientific-peer-review-methods-benchmarks-reliability-challenges-2606.25057]] — LLM-Based Scientific Peer Review: Methods, Benchmarks, and Reliability Challenges (06-23) — survey of automated scientific review with adversarial-robustness inventory (prompt injection, data poisoning, reward hacking).
- [[memtoolagent-leveraging-memory-tool-using-agents-environment-user-feedback-2606.07909]] — MemToolAgent: Leveraging Memory for Tool Using Agents Based on Environment and User Feedback (06-06) — tool-call memory mechanism with reflection-based extraction (no LLM fine-tuning), 29%/80%/17% gains on WorkBench/NESTFUL/PEToolBench.
- [[toward-pre-deployment-assurance-enterprise-ai-agents-ontology-grounded-simulation-trust-certification-2606.04037]] — Toward Pre-Deployment Assurance for Enterprise AI Agents: Ontology-Grounded Simulation and Trust Certification (06-02) — first framework combining Agent Operational Envelope + ontology-to-scenario generation + machine-verifiable Trust Certificate; 1,800 scenarios across 5 industry-by-regulatory cells.



- [[progress-advantage-neglected-free-lunch-post-training-llm-agents-2606.26080]] — Neglected Free Lunch from Post-training: Progress Advantage for LLM Agents: addresses the prohibitive cost structure of building process reward models (PRMs) for agentic settings — where long-horizon interactions, irreversible actions, and stochastic environment feedback make both human annotation and Monte Carlo estimation of step-level rewards infeasible at scale — and demonstrates that reinforcement-learning (RL) post-training already provides the ingredients for an effective implicit step-level scoring signal called *progress advantage*, the log-probability ratio between the RL-trained policy and its reference policy exactly recovers the optimal advantage function under a general stochastic Markov decision process; the result is an annotation-free, domain-agnostic, training-free step-level score available as a byproduct of the standard RL post-training pipeline that consistently outperforms confidence-based baselines while surpassing dedicated trained reward models on test-time scaling, uncertainty quantification, and failure attribution across five benchmarks and four model families; Oh, Changdae; Li, Wendi; Park, Seongheon; Yeh, Samuel; Mallick, Tanwi; Li, Sharon demonstrate the structurally correct step-level scoring signal is *not* a separately trained artifact but a property of the trained policy itself, reconceptualizing the PRM research program as one of *exploiting what RL already produces*; 2026-06-24; discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-26 (post-training-implicit-advantage / step-level-scoring-byproduct / agentic-rl-free-lunch theme — **first implicit-advantage-from-existing-post-training paper in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(progress.advantage|implicit.advantage|post.training.free|free.lunch)"` returning empty). Complements the [[connect-the-dots-training-llms-for-long-lifecycle-agents-with-cross-domain-generalization-via-reinforcement-learning-2606.20002]] (sibling cross-domain-agent RL training — Connect-the-Dots trains single agents for cross-domain generalization via RL, Progress Advantage demonstrates that the trained policy already carries implicit step-level scoring signal — together they bracket the RL post-training surface between the *training objective* (Connect-the-Dots) and the *free step-level diagnostic* (Progress Advantage)), [[learning-from-your-own-mistakes-constructing-learnable-micro-reflective-trajectories-for-self-distillation-2606.18844]] (sibling trajectory-aware self-correction RL — Learning-from-Your-Own-Mistakes uses the model's own erroneous prefixes to construct micro-reflective corrections in RL training, Progress Advantage extracts the step-level credit signal from the same trajectory data without dedicated reward-model training — together they bracket the trajectory-supervision surface between *explicit trajectory construction* (TAPO) and *implicit advantage extraction* (Progress Advantage)), [[renio-reweighting-negative-trajectory-importance-for-llm-on-policy-distillation-2606.23104]] (sibling on-policy trajectory reweighting — ReNIO down-weights low-quality student-generated trajectories in on-policy distillation, Progress Advantage reweights trajectories via the policy-vs-reference log-probability ratio — together they bracket the trajectory-reweighting surface between *negative-importance estimation for OPD* (ReNIO) and *policy-reference log-ratio for step-level scoring* (Progress Advantage)),
