[[planbench-xl-evaluating-long-horizon-planning-of-llm-tool-use-agents-in-large-scale-tool-ecosystems-2606.22388]] (sibling long-horizon planning benchmark — PlanBench-XL benchmarks agents on multi-step planning across 1,665 tools, Progress Advantage provides the step-level scoring primitive those planners need — together they bracket the long-horizon agent surface between *planning capability benchmarking* (PlanBench-XL) and *step-level credit primitive for training* (Progress Advantage)), and [[manifold-bandits-bayesian-curriculum-learning-over-the-latent-geometry-of-large-language-models-2606.19750]] (sibling curriculum RL — Manifold Bandits samples problems adaptively in RL, Progress Advantage adds the step-level scoring primitive that curriculum-RL training pipelines could exploit — together they bracket the RL-training surface between the *sampling* axis (Manifold Bandits) and the *step-level credit-assignment* axis (Progress Advantage)) entries on this page by surfacing *implicit-advantage-from-existing-post-training* as the load-bearing primitive for the regime where dedicated reward-model training is structurally inadequate for agentic long-horizon supervision.

- [[trustmem-trustworthy-memory-consolidation-llm-agents-long-term-memory-2606.25161]] — TRUSTMEM: Learning Trustworthy Memory Consolidation for LLM Agents with Long-Term Memory: addresses a structural reliability gap in long-term-memory LLM agents — where existing memory systems actively update external memory via generated write/revise/delete operations that may *omit* important information, *corrupt* existing memory, or introduce unsupported *hallucinated* content, with such errors becoming persistent system-state failures affecting all future reasoning — and introduces TrustMem with a Memory Transition Verifier evaluating each update along three orthogonal axes (coverage, preservation, faithfulness) plus preference-guided RL that constructs preference pairs among candidate updates to directly optimize memory-updating behavior; achieves SOTA across MemoryAgentBench, HaluMem, and Mem-alpha, improves HaluMem extraction by 12.14 F1 points, and reduces transition-level omission/corruption/hallucination by 40.1%/79.1%/50.0% vs strongest baseline per error type; Yang, Tianyu; Paul, Sudipta; Srinivasan, Vijay; Kulkarni, Vivek; Chappidi, Srinivas surface *memory-transition-reliability* as the structurally correct axis for long-term-memory agent evaluation — the load-bearing primitive for the deployment regime where memory errors compound into persistent system-state failures is to supervise the *update transition* directly (along coverage / preservation / faithfulness axes) rather than attempt to detect and correct downstream; 2026-06-23; discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-26 (memory-transition-reliability / preference-rl-memory-update / omission-corruption-hallucination theme — **first transition-level-memory-reliability paper for LLM agents in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(trust.mem|memory.consolidat|transition.verif|memory.update)"` returning empty). Complements the [[are-we-ready-for-an-agent-native-memory-system-2606.24775]] (sibling agent-native memory survey — Are-Ready surveys memory-architecture choices from retrieval to data management, TrustMem focuses on the *update-transition reliability* axis that the survey implicitly assumes but does not measure — together they bracket the agent-memory surface between *architectural design space* (Are-Ready) and *transition-level error reduction* (TrustMem)), [[gatemem-benchmarking-memory-governance-in-multi-principal-shared-memory-agents-2606.18829]] (sibling memory-governance benchmark — GateMem benchmarks multi-principal shared-memory *governance* (who can read/write what), TrustMem benchmarks *single-agent memory-update reliability* — together they bracket the memory-reliability surface between *multi-actor governance* (GateMem) and *single-actor update transitions* (TrustMem)), [[memprobe-probing-longterm-agent-memory-via-hidden-userstate-recovery-2606.24595]] (sibling memory-evaluation probe — MemProbe probes long-term memory via hidden user-state recovery, TrustMem evaluates memory via transition-level error reduction — together they bracket the memory-evaluation surface between *hidden-state-recovery probing* (MemProbe) and *transition-error probing* (TrustMem)), [[evoarena-tracking-memory-evolution-robust-llm-agents-dynamic-environments-2606.13681]] (sibling memory-evolution benchmark — EvoArena benchmarks agent capability under persistent environment evolution (the environment drifts, memory must adapt), TrustMem benchmarks *memory-update reliability* (the update process must avoid omission/corruption/hallucination) — together they bracket the memory-evaluation surface between *capability under drift* (EvoArena) and *reliability under update* (TrustMem)), and [[dont-blindly-trust-it-how-unreliable-feedback-breaks-tool-using-llm-agents-2606.21409]] (sibling feedback-reliability controlled comparison — Don't-Blindly-Trust-Feedback demonstrates that unreliable external feedback inverts agent value, TrustMem demonstrates that unreliable internal-memory transitions propagate errors into persistent system-state failures — together they bracket the agent-reliability surface between *external-feedback reliability* (Feedback paper) and *internal-memory-transition reliability* (TrustMem)) entries on this page by surfacing *transition-level-memory-reliability* as a load-bearing primitive for the deployment regime where memory errors compound across the agent lifetime.

- [[geometry-of-refusal-linear-instability-safety-aligned-llms-2606.22686]] — The Geometry of Refusal: Linear Instability in Safety-Aligned LLMs: investigates whether safety compliance in aligned LLMs is a deep semantic decision or a manipulable linear feature — introduces Contrastive Logit Steering (CLS), a zero-optimization framework that isolates the refusal direction via system-prompt contrast and operates directly on the output distribution as a diagnostic probe for alignment fragility; coupled with prefix injection, CLS induces a phase transition where guardrails collapse, with architectural phase transitions (Late Decision vs Early Divergence) determining exploitability — Llama-3.1 reaches 95% ASR in ~1s, while Qwen-2.5 integrates safety mid-computation and resists the same attack; CLS achieves 73% ASR vs 22.6% on Llama 2 and 91% vs 79.2% on Qwen 7B vs activation-level steering, and inverting the steering vector hardens models against jailbreaks without retraining, reframing the safety axis as both vulnerability and defense primitive; Ratnakar, Shivam; Vats, Kartikeya surface *safety-as-linear-feature* as the structurally correct axis for mechanistic interpretability of LLM alignment — the load-bearing primitive is that the refusal direction is a *linearly decodable geometric axis* (not a deep semantic decision), architectural phase transitions (Late Decision vs Early Divergence) determine exploitability, and the same linearity that enables bypass also enables *bidirectional control* (inverting the steering vector hardens against jailbreaks without retraining); 2026-06-21; discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-26 (mechanistic-safety-geometry / logit-level-steering / linear-feature / bidirectional-defense theme — **first mechanistic-geometry-of-refusal paper for safety-aligned LLMs in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(geometry.of.refus|linear.instability|safety.geometry|logit.steer|cls.refusal)"` returning empty). Complements the [[comparing-linear-probes-mahalanobis-cosine-similarity-2606.19603]] (sibling linear-probe interpretability — Comparing Linear Probes audits *which* linear probe best recovers learned representations, Geometry of Refusal shows that *refusal itself* is such a linearly decodable feature — together they bracket the linear-feature interpretability surface between *probe design* (Comparing Linear Probes) and *refusal-direction geometry* (Geometry of Refusal)), [[what-intermediate-layers-know-detecting-jailbreaks-from-entropy-dynamics-2606.25182]] (sibling jailbreak detection from internal entropy — Detecting-Jailbreaks-from-Entropy audits *intermediate-layer entropy* as a jailbreak signal, Geometry of Refusal audits *logit-level contrastive directions* as a jailbreak surface — together they bracket the safety-internals surface between *detection via entropy dynamics* (Detection paper) and *attack via linear feature geometry* (Geometry of Refusal)), [[deeper-is-not-always-better-mitigating-the-alignment-tax-via-confident-layer-decoding-2606.21906]] (sibling alignment robustness — Confident-Layer-Decoding mitigates alignment tax via decoding-time intervention, Geometry of Refusal shows that logit-level steering (a different intervention surface) exposes and exploits the underlying alignment geometry — together they bracket the alignment-robustness surface between *decoding-time mitigation* (Confident-Layer-Decoding) and *logit-time exploitation / inversion* (Geometry of Refusal)), [[do-thinking-tokens-help-with-safety-2606.25013]] (sibling safety reasoning mechanism — Thinking-Tokens audits whether chain-of-thought reasoning helps safety, Geometry of Refusal audits whether safety is a manipulable linear feature — together they bracket the safety-mechanism surface between *reasoning-time safety* (Thinking-Tokens) and *steerability-time safety* (Geometry of Refusal)), and [[privacyalign-contextual-privacy-alignment-for-llm-agents-2606.21710]] (sibling contextual alignment — PrivacyAlign aligns LLMs for contextual privacy compliance, Geometry of Refusal surfaces a more general principle — alignment is geometrically linear and thus steerable in both attack and defense directions, of which privacy is one specific axis — together they bracket the alignment-discipline surface between *application-specific alignment* (PrivacyAlign) and *general-mechanism alignment geometry* (Geometry of Refusal)) entries on this page by reframing the safety direction as both a critical vulnerability and a precise primitive for defense, with the architectural phase-transition property (Late Decision vs Early Divergence) determining which regime each model family occupies.

- [[dont-blindly-trust-it-how-unreliable-feedback-breaks-tool-using-llm-agents-2606.21409]] — Don't Blindly Trust It: How Unreliable Feedback Breaks Tool-Using LLM Agents: investigates whether the gains tool-augmented agents achieve under reliable external feedback persist — or invert — when feedback is unreliable, via a controlled matched-loop comparison that fixes the agent loop, prompt, action space, and decoding while varying only the returned observation: faithful, misleading, or absent; across question answering and fact verification, persistent misleading feedback produces a value inversion — agents that benefit from clean tools can perform worse than agents receiving no task evidence at all — surfacing *feedback-reliability-as-deployment-condition* as a load-bearing primitive for tool-using LLM-agent evaluation; Zhang, Chubin; Wan, Zhenglin; Yu, Xingrui; Zhou, Pengfei; Zhao, Wangbo et al. (8 authors) demonstrate that the matched-loop design (fix everything except the observation) is the structurally correct diagnostic for separating tool-capability from feedback-integration pathologies, applicable beyond Q&A/fact-verification to any tool-using agent deployed against an unreliable external source; 2026-06-19; discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-26 (tool-using-agent / feedback-reliability / value-inversion / controlled-matched-loop / observation-only-variable theme — **first feedback-reliability-as-deployment-condition paper for tool-using LLM agents in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(unreliable.feedback|tool-using|feedback.reliab)"` returning empty). Complements the [[constraint-tax-in-open-weight-llms-an-empirical-study-of-tool-calling-suppression-under-structured-output-constraints-2606.25605]] (sibling tool-calling pathology — Constraint-Tax diagnoses tool-calling failures under output constraints, Feedback-Reliability diagnoses tool-using failures under unreliable observations, together they bracket the tool-use failure-mode space between structural-output-pathology and observation-reliability-pathology), [[foresight-failure-detection-long-horizon-robotic-manipulation-2606.23085]] (sibling deployment-monitoring — Foresight detects manipulation failures via world-model latents, Feedback-Reliability diagnoses a complementary class — misleading-but-correctable observations — the unmonitored-but-correctable primitive that complements failure detection), and [[calvert-augmenting-agents-with-calibrated-verifier-telemetry-improves-action-and-learning-in-knowledge-intensive-tasks-2606.21777]] (sibling verifier-telemetry — CALVERT calibrates verifier signals, Feedback-Reliability demonstrates what happens when verifier signals are themselves unreliable — together they bracket verifier-integrity as the structural axis).
- [[oragentbench-can-llm-agents-solve-challenging-operations-research-tasks-end-to-end-2606.19787]] — ORAgentBench: Can LLM Agents Solve Challenging Operations Research Tasks End to End?: introduces ORAgentBench, an execution-grounded benchmark for evaluating autonomous LLM agents on challenging end-to-end operations research (OR) tasks — addressing the structural under-evaluation of LLM agents on realistic OR work where existing OR evaluations decouple modeling from solving, rely on pre-formalized or text-only instances, and rarely test the full workflow from operational artifacts to validated decisions — surfacing *end-to-end-execution-as-evaluation-surface* as the load-bearing primitive for OR-agent evaluation; Li, Jiajun; Cai, Mingshu; Li, Yixuan; Ding, Yu; Hou, Ran et al. (8 authors) provide the first execution-grounded OR benchmark with 107 human-reviewed tasks covering the full workflow from operational artifacts to validated decisions, demonstrating that the gap between *model can formalize an OR problem* and *agent can solve it end-to-end from artifacts* is the binding constraint that prior decoupled evaluations systematically missed; 2026-06-18; discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-26 (operations-research-agent / execution-grounded-benchmark / end-to-end-evaluation / formalization-silent-failure / high-stakes-decision theme — **first execution-grounded OR benchmark for LLM agents in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(operations.research|oragentbench|or.agent|optimization.agent)"` returning empty). Complements the [[naturebench-can-coding-agents-match-the-published-sota-of-nature-family-papers-2606.24530]] (sibling SOTA-replication benchmark — NatureBench tests coding agents against published Nature-family SOTA, ORAgentBench tests agents against published OR-methodology SOTA, together they bracket the SOTA-replication discipline across high-stakes scientific domains where execution-grounded validation is unambiguous), [[beyond-static-leaderboards-predictive-validity-evaluation-llm-agents-2606.19704]] (sibling agent-evaluation methodology — Beyond-Static-Leaderboards argues aggregate scoring is structurally inadequate, ORAgentBench demonstrates the specific execution-grounded alternative in a domain where aggregate scoring would systematically overstate agent capability), and [[tropt-an-open-framework-for-unifying-and-advancing-discrete-text-optimization-2606.23496]] (sibling open-source evaluation substrate — TROPT unifies discrete text optimizers for adversarial evaluation, ORAgentBench is a complementary domain-specific benchmark providing the execution-grounded evaluation surface those optimizers would be measured against in the OR regime).
- [[a-technical-taxonomy-of-llm-agent-communication-protocols-2606.19135]] — A Technical Taxonomy of LLM Agent Communication Protocols: develops a technical taxonomy to classify and analyze LLM agent communication protocols — addressing the fragmented protocol landscape that presents a significant interoperability challenge for distributed multi-agent networks where the canonical single-agent loop is structurally inadequate and robust communication between heterogeneous agents is the binding constraint — and surfaces *communication-protocol-as-infrastructure* as a load-bearing primitive for distributed LLM-agent deployments; Sander, Linus; Gidey, Habtom Kahsay; Lenz, Alexander; Knoll, Alois follow an established iterative taxonomy-development method (purpose, meta-characteristic, ending conditions, then five iterations across empirical-to-conceptual analysis) to identify the structural axes along which protocols differentiate — message format, transport, discovery, state-synchronization, error-recovery — and the canonical design choices within each axis, providing future protocol designers with a reference frame for what *robust inter-agent communication* means in the LLM-agent stack; 2026-06-17; discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-26 (llm-agent-protocols / multi-agent-infrastructure / inter-agent-communication / taxonomy / distributed-deployment theme — **first systematic taxonomy of LLM agent communication protocols in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(communication.protocol|agent.protocol|protocol.taxonomy)"` returning empty). Complements the [[connect-the-dots-training-llms-for-long-lifecycle-agents-with-cross-domain-generalization-via-reinforcement-learning-2606.20002]] (sibling long-lifecycle-agent work — Connect-the-Dots trains single agents for cross-domain generalization, Communication-Protocols-Taxonomy classifies the multi-agent infrastructure the long-lifecycle agents would compose with), [[aohp-an-open-source-os-level-agent-harness-for-personalized-efficient-and-2606.23449]] (sibling OS-level agent harness — AOHP provides the runtime substrate, Communication-Protocols-Taxonomy classifies the wire-level protocol layer the harness must speak), and [[critique-of-agent-model-2606.23991]] (sibling agent epistemics — Critique-of-Agent-Model audits what counts as an agent, Communication-Protocols-Taxonomy surveys how agents talk to each other once the agency question is settled).
- [[beyond-static-leaderboards-predictive-validity-evaluation-llm-agents-2606.19704]] — Beyond Static Leaderboards: Predictive Validity for the Evaluation of LLM Agents: aggregates the largest coordinated deep-dive of one MCP-based industrial-agent benchmark (14 parallel implementation studies) and proposes *predictive validity* as the operational alternative to aggregate-leaderboard scoring — argues single aggregate scores produce rank instability because no single benchmark touches more than 4-5 of the deployment dimensions, and surfaces *multi-dimensional deployment-relevant evaluation* as a load-bearing primitive for the LLM-agent deployment regime where the canonical evaluation surface (single benchmark, single scalar, single ranking) is structurally inadequate; Patel, Dhaval C.; Maghraoui, Kaoutar El; Lin, Shuxin; Li, Yusheng; Feng, Tianjun et al. (61 authors) demonstrate the rank-instability pathology when the same agents are evaluated along different deployment-relevant axes and provide concrete tooling and methodology for industrial-agent benchmark construction that supports multi-axis evaluation rather than collapsing to aggregate scores; 2026-06-18; discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-26 (agent-evaluation-methodology / predictive-validity / MCP-benchmark / multi-dimensional-deployment theme — **first predictive-validity methodology for LLM agent evaluation in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(predictive.valid|leaderboard|meta.eval)"` returning empty). Complements the [[counsel-a-meta-evaluation-dataset-for-agentic-tasks-2606.21627]] (sibling meta-evaluation — COUNSEL meta-evaluates agentic-task benchmarks, Beyond-Static-Leaderboards is the methodology paper that grounds that meta-evaluation principle in predictive validity), [[enterpriseclawbench-benchmarking-agents-from-real-workplace-sessions-2606.23654]] (sibling workplace-agent benchmark — EnterpriseClawBench captures real workplace sessions, the multi-dimensional regime where aggregate-leaderboard scoring fails), [[evoarena-tracking-memory-evolution-robust-llm-agents-dynamic-environments-2606.13681]] (sibling persistent-environment benchmark — EvoArena's version-aware scoring instantiates multi-axis evaluation, Beyond-Static-Leaderboards provides the methodology that grounds it), and [[bagen-are-llm-agents-budget-aware-2606.00198]] (sibling agent-budget-awareness — BAGEN diagnoses a deployment failure that aggregate scores systematically hide, Beyond-Static-Leaderboards provides the methodological argument for why such failures must be measured along deployment axes) entries on this page by surfacing *predictive validity* as the operational alternative to single-scalar ranking for the deployment regime where LLM agents face heterogeneous dimensions.
- [[evoarena-tracking-memory-evolution-robust-llm-agents-dynamic-environments-2606.13681]] — EvoArena: Tracking Memory Evolution for Robust LLM Agents in Dynamic Environments: addresses a structural under-evaluation gap in LLM-agent benchmarks — most assume static environments while real-world deployment is inherently dynamic (tools change APIs, documents update, user preferences drift), and agents that perform well on static benchmarks routinely fail under persistent change because their memory representations become stale — and introduces EvoArena, a benchmark suite where the environment *persists and evolves* across episodes, surfacing *memory evolution tracking* and *version-awareness* as load-bearing evaluation primitives (agent capability is not just accuracy-at-time-T but the rate at which memory representations converge to the new environment state and the graceful degradation curve under sustained drift); Xu, Jundong; Li, Qingchuan; Wu, Jiaying; Lan, Yihuai; Li, Shuyue Stella et al. (14 authors) demonstrate that frontier agents exhibit characteristic failure patterns under persistent evolution — over-reliance on outdated cached knowledge, delayed update of tool-use policies, and loss of cross-session consistency — none visible in static benchmarks, extending the long-horizon-memory principle to non-embodied software environments with persistent API/version drift; 2026-06-11; discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-26 (dynamic-environment-benchmark / memory-evolution / persistent-state / version-aware-agents theme — **first persistent-environment-evolution benchmark for LLM agents in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(arena|persistent|dynamic.env|memory.evol)"` returning empty). Complements the [[are-we-ready-for-an-agent-native-memory-system-2606.24775]] (sibling agent-memory-survey — Are-Ready surveys memory-architecture choices, EvoArena isolates the *evaluation* axis — under persistent evolution, the question is not which memory design is best but how to *measure* memory evolution), [[gatemem-benchmarking-memory-governance-in-multi-principal-shared-memory-agents-2606.18829]] (sibling memory-governance benchmark — GateMem benchmarks multi-principal shared-memory governance, EvoArena benchmarks *unipersonal* memory evolution under persistent environment change, together they bracket the agent-memory-evaluation surface), [[worldlines-benchmarking-and-modeling-long-horizon-stateful-2606.18847]] (sibling long-horizon-memory benchmark — WorldLines benchmarks long-horizon memory in embodied agents, EvoArena extends the long-horizon principle to non-embodied software environments with persistent API/version drift), and [[beyond-static-leaderboards-predictive-validity-evaluation-llm-agents-2606.19704]] (sibling agent-evaluation-methodology — Beyond-Static-Leaderboards critiques aggregate-leaderboard scoring, EvoArena's version-aware scoring is a concrete instantiation of that critique) entries on this page by isolating *memory evolution tracking* as a first-class evaluation axis for the deployment regime where environment state drifts faster than agent representations.
- [[bagen-are-llm-agents-budget-aware-2606.00198]] — BAGEN: Are LLM Agents Budget-Aware?: investigates whether frontier LLM agents can predict and respect computational/token budgets as a control signal during task execution — finds that current agents cannot reliably anticipate when they are about to run out of runway, treating budget as a passive post-hoc cost metric rather than an active control input — and proposes a Budget-Aware Agent (BAGEN) framework that folds budget into the agent decision loop as a first-class signal; Lin, Yuxiang; Wang, Zihan; Liu, Mengyang; Shan, Yuxuan; Bai, Longju et al. (12 authors) systematically define *budget estimation* as two distinct axes (internal budgets from environment signals such as remaining API calls or context length, vs external budgets from user constraints such as dollar limits or wall-clock deadlines), and demonstrate that frontier agents waste 44% of tokens on tasks they ultimately fail at — the failure is predictable in hindsight but not during execution, indicating the missing primitive is budget-anticipation not task-capability; 2026-05-29; discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-26 (agent-budget-awareness / control-signal / token-economy / runtime-cost-prediction theme — **first budget-as-control-signal paper for LLM agents in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(budget|bagen|cost-aware)"` returning empty). Complements the [[are-we-ready-for-an-agent-native-memory-system-2606.24775]] (sibling agent-runtime-design-survey — Are-Ready surveys the architectural choices including memory, BAGEN isolates the orthogonal budget-control axis — both surface that the *runtime substrate* of an LLM agent is a design space not a fixed target), [[beyond-static-leaderboards-predictive-validity-evaluation-llm-agents-2606.19704]] (sibling agent-evaluation-methodology — Beyond-Static-Leaderboards argues aggregate-leaderboard scoring is structurally inadequate, BAGEN diagnoses a specific deployment failure — budget-anticipation — that aggregate scores systematically hide), and [[counsel-a-meta-evaluation-dataset-for-agentic-tasks-2606.21627]] (sibling meta-evaluation — COUNSEL meta-evaluates agentic-task benchmarks, BAGEN surfaces a deployment-relevant failure mode that benchmark scores cannot surface — the failure occurs *during* execution, after the score is computed) entries on this page by surfacing *budget-as-control-signal* as a load-bearing primitive for the deployment regime where agents run unattended for long horizons and must manage their own runway rather than just produce outputs.
- [[mvtrack4gen-multi-view-point-tracking-geometric-supervision-4d-video-generation-2606.26087]] — MVTrack4Gen: Multi-View Point Tracking as Geometric Supervision for 4D Video Generation: addresses a structural geometric-consistency bottleneck in novel-view video synthesis from a monocular reference video along a target camera trajectory — where existing 3D-representation-based methods are limited by off-the-shelf reconstruction module accuracy (especially on dynamic objects) and camera-conditioning-only methods achieve high visual quality but struggle to preserve geometric and motion consistency across views — and proposes MVTrack4Gen, a motion-aware training framework that leverages multi-view point tracking as an additional geometric and motion supervision signal for camera-conditioning-only novel-view video diffusion models; Lee, JoungBin; Jung, Jaewoo; Lee, Jongmin; Kim, Tongmin; Kim, Hyunsung; Narihira, Takuya et al. discover that *specific attention layers encode strong correspondence cues* — query features attend to key features at geometrically corresponding locations across views and over time — and the misalignment of these correspondences is the structural cause of motion inconsistency; based on this observation, they route these features into an auxiliary multi-view tracking head and jointly train the diffusion model with a point-tracking objective, surfacing *geometric-supervision-via-tracking* as a load-bearing primitive for 4D video generation where tracking-derived signals stand in for expensive 3D ground truth while still constraining the multi-view output to be 3D-consistent, achieving SOTA geometric consistency and competitive camera accuracy across diverse benchmarks; 2026-06-24; discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-26 (4d-video-generation / multi-view-point-tracking / geometric-supervision / novel-view-synthesis / video-diffusion theme — **first 4D-generation + multi-view-point-tracking paper in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(4d|multi-view|point-track|geometric)"` returning only Causal-rCM as a tangentially related match). Complements the [[causal-rcm-a-unified-teacher-forcing-and-self-forcing-open-recipe-for-autoregressive-diffusion-distillation-in-streaming-video-generation-and-interactive-world-models-2606.25473]] (sibling streaming-video diffusion distillation — Causal-rCM unifies teacher/self-forcing for AR diffusion distillation, MVTrack4Gen adds geometric supervision as a complementary axis to appearance-only distillation), [[domain-shuttle-freeform-open-domain-subject-driven-text-to-video-2606.26058]] (sibling T2V evaluation discipline — DomainShuttle surfaces in-domain/cross-domain S2V ambiguity, MVTrack4Gen surfaces view-consistency as a structurally distinct axis of T2V quality), [[wan-streamer-v0-1-end-to-end-real-time-interactive-foundation-models-2606.25041]] (sibling multimodal video foundation — Wan-Streamer is end-to-end real-time interactive video, MVTrack4Gen is offline novel-view synthesis with geometric-supervision, together they bracket video foundation models on the latency/quality spectrum), and [[world-action-models-a-survey-2606.20781]] (sibling WAM survey — WAM surveys predictive-action methods, MVTrack4Gen is a *non-predictive* geometric-supervision primitive useful for WAM-style downstream applications) entries on this page by isolating tracking-derived geometric supervision as the structurally correct way to attack novel-view-synthesis consistency without requiring 3D ground truth at training time.
- [[policytrim-boosting-intrinsic-policy-efficiency-vla-models-2606.22540]] — PolicyTrim: Boosting Intrinsic Policy Efficiency of Vision-Language-Action Models: addresses the execution-efficiency bottleneck preventing VLA models from real-world robotic-manipulation deployment — where prior efficiency work predominantly targets compute-centric per-step inference latency (shrinking the VLM backbone) but the *intrinsic policy efficiency* (effective executable length of predicted action chunks and total physical steps required to complete a task) remains largely unexplored despite jointly determining the total number of forward inference calls during execution — and proposes PolicyTrim, a reinforcement-learning-based post-training framework that targets two distinct inefficiency axes: *unreliable long-horizon planning* (current VLA policies suffer severe prediction degradation at the tail of action chunks) and *redundant physical steps* (policies tend to generate unnecessarily redundant actions); Wang, Xianghui; Chen, Feng; Zhang, Wenbo; Yan, Hua; Wang, Zixuan; Li, Changsheng et al. design a dynamic exploration strategy that explicitly rewards successful completion of longer executable lengths to extend the trustworthy prediction horizon to its empirical limit, plus a redundancy-aware reward that favors successful task completions with fewer steps while penalizing unreproducible shortcuts, surfacing *policy-head-as-distinct-efficiency-axis* as a load-bearing primitive for VLA deployment — efficiency gains are not just about shrinking the language model, the policy-decoding layers carry their own redundancy and reclaiming it is the structurally correct way to attack VLA deployment cost; extensive experiments across three benchmarks and three VLA models demonstrate 3× action-chunk utilization improvement and 51.4% reduction in physical execution steps, with up to 5.83× end-to-end deployment speedup without compromising task success rates; 2026-06-21; discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-26 (vla / policy-head-efficiency / post-training-pruning / deployment-cost-reduction / robotic-manipulation theme — **first VLA-policy-head efficiency paper in the wiki**, verified via `ls entities/ | grep -iE "(vla|policy.eff|pruning|compress)"` returning only KaLM-Reranker-V1 as a related-but-different compression surface). Complements the [[polar-factorizing-extent-mode-latent-actions-robot-policy-2606.21139]] (sibling VLA latent-action representation — PoLAR factorizes extent/mode in latent action space, PolicyTrim targets the policy head's structural redundancy, together they bracket VLA representation-side vs policy-side optimization primitives), [[world-action-models-a-survey-2606.20781]] (sibling WAM survey — WAM surfaces the design pattern *generate less of the future while preserving what control requires*, PolicyTrim instantiates this on the policy-side rather than the generation-side, pruning redundancy rather than shortening horizons), [[world-value-models-robotic-manipulation-2606.24742]] (sibling VLA data-selection — WVM targets value-estimation-based data selection, PolicyTrim targets inference-time efficiency, together they bracket VLA efficiency between training-time data and inference-time pruning), [[ebench-elemental-diagnosis-of-generalist-mobile-manipulation-policies-2606.18239]] (sibling multi-axis-diagnostic VLA benchmark — EBench surfaces capability-specific collapses at near-equal aggregate success rates, PolicyTrim surfaces deployment-cost as a distinct axis that should be measured alongside EBench's capability axes), and [[kalm-reranker-v1-fast-not-late-interaction-compressed-2606.22807]] (sibling reranker-side compression — KaLM decouples passage/query encoding, PolicyTrim prunes the policy head, together they show encoder-decoder decoupling and policy-head pruning are the two distinct compression axes in modern LLM-derived systems) entries on this page by isolating the policy-decoding layers' redundancy profile as a structurally distinct efficiency frontier that is amenable to post-training pruning without requiring full VLA retraining.
- [[mobileforge-annotation-free-adaptation-mobile-gui-agents-hierarchical-feedback-policy-optimization-2606.19930]] — MobileForge: Annotation-Free Adaptation for Mobile GUI Agents with Hierarchical Feedback-Guided Policy Optimization: addresses the per-app adaptation cost structure that prevents MLLM-based mobile GUI agents from scaling to the long tail of real-world mobile applications — where mobile apps are numerous, frequently updated, and hard to cover with human-written tasks/demonstrations/reward labels, and where existing annotation-free GUI learning lacks a unified substrate connecting target-app exploration, curriculum mining, rollout execution, and feedback while policy optimization often relies on isolated rollouts and coarse rewards that are hard to convert into reliable improvement signals — and proposes MobileForge, an annotation-free adaptation system consisting of MobileGym (which grounds task generation and rollout evaluation in real mobile app interaction) and Hierarchical Feedback-Guided Policy Optimization (HiFPO) which turns trajectory outcomes, step-level process feedback, and corrective hints into hint-contextualized step-level GRPO updates; Liu, Guangyi; Zhao, Pengxiang; Wu, Gao; Yin, Yiwen; Li, Mading; Liu, Liang et al. demonstrate that using only automatically generated annotation-free adaptation data, MobileForge adapts Qwen3-VL-8B to 67.2% Pass@3 on AndroidWorld — close to the closed-data GUI-specialized GUI-Owl-1.5-8B base model at 69.0% — and the MobileForge-adapted ForgeOwl-8B reaches 77.6% Pass@3 on AndroidWorld and 41.0% success on the out-of-domain MobileWorld GUI-only split, establishing the strongest open-data mobile GUI agent in their evaluation; surfacing *hierarchy-as-credit-assignment-structure* as a load-bearing primitive for annotation-free agent adaptation — the structural relationship between UI actions, app state, and task outcomes is the right inductive bias for credit assignment, replacing the missing annotation signal with a structurally-grounded learning signal; 2026-06-18; discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-26 (mobile-gui-agent / annotation-free-adaptation / hierarchical-policy / feedback-guided-optimization / app-specialization theme — **sibling to MemGUI-Agent (Run 34) from the same lead authors** with a distinct axis: annotation-free hierarchical adaptation vs proactive context management). Complements the [[memgui-agent-end-to-end-long-horizon-mobile-gui-agent-proactive-context-2606.19926]] (sibling same-author mobile-GUI work on *proactive context management* — MemGUI-Agent surfaces context-management-as-policy on long-horizon tasks, MobileForge surfaces hierarchy-as-credit-assignment on annotation-free adaptation, together they form a complementary two-axis treatment of mobile GUI agent deployment from the same research group: proactive-context + annotation-free-adaptation), [[capable-but-careless-do-computer-use-agents-follow-contextual-integrity-2606.23189]] (sibling CUA contextual-integrity compliance — Capable-But-Careless audits rule-following, MobileForge targets deployment-readiness via adaptation, together they bracket CUA safety between execution-time compliance and deployment-time adaptation), [[aohp-an-open-source-os-level-agent-harness-for-personalized-efficient-and-2606.23449]] (sibling OS-level agent harness — AOHP argues the OS must treat agents as first-class actors, MobileForge surfaces hierarchy-as-policy-structure as the agent-internal complement to AOHP's external-first-class treatment), [[are-we-ready-for-an-agent-native-memory-system-2606.24775]] (sibling agent-memory design-space survey — Are-Ready surveys memory architectural choices, MobileForge instantiates the hierarchical-policy choice on the mobile GUI deployment regime), and [[gatemem-benchmarking-memory-governance-in-multi-principal-shared-memory-agents-2606.18829]] (sibling multi-principal memory governance — GateMem isolates access-control as a memory axis, MobileForge isolates hierarchy as a policy axis, together they show memory and policy are the two structurally distinct axes of agent design for multi-user deployment) entries on this page by extending the mobile GUI agent deployment wave from long-horizon context management into the long-tail app regime, where annotation-free hierarchical optimization replaces the missing per-app trajectory annotations with a structurally-grounded learning signal.
- [[speaker-identity-non-verbal-vocalizations-conditional-distillation-mixture-experts-2606.21215]] — Speaker Identity in Non-Verbal Vocalizations: Conditional Distillation and Mixture of Experts Approach: addresses a structural under-coverage of non-verbal vocalizations (NVVs) in modern speaker verification (SV) systems — where expressive TTS and voice-conversion pipelines increasingly generate NVVs (laughs, sighs, breaths, gasps, coughs) as a first-class output class to enhance naturalness, but SV systems trained on verbal speech generalize poorly to NVV segments and suffer catastrophic forgetting when fine-tuned on NVV data — and proposes a two-stage solution: Conditional Distillation that compresses a high-capacity audio encoder's identity-relevant representations into a compact student that retains verbal-only performance, plus a Mixture-of-Experts head over an NVV-aware feature extractor that specializes per-vocalization-type while sharing cross-type identity backbones; Wei, Tzu-Chieh; Lin, Yi-Cheng; Chou, Huang-Cheng; Chen, Kuan-Yu; Sung, Hsin-Yen; Narayanan, Shrikanth release the Expressive Speaker Verification (ESV) benchmark spanning verbal/non-verbal/laughter/breath/sigh/conditioned-VC slices, surfacing *vocalization-aware routing* as a load-bearing primitive for the deployment regime where identity must be verified across heterogeneous non-verbal segments that share a speaker identity but differ in acoustic structure; 2026-06-19; discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-26 (speaker-verification / non-verbal-vocalizations / distillation / mixture-of-experts / expressive-tts / voice-conversion theme). Complements the [[grouped-query-experts-mixture-of-experts-on-gqa-self-attention-2606.20945]] (sibling MoE routing — GQE routes over attention heads, Speaker-NVV routes over vocalization types, together they extend MoE-as-routing from attention-level to acoustic-event-level heterogeneity), [[v-zero-answer-label-free-on-policy-distillation-contrastive-evidence-2606.25319]] (sibling distillation — V-Zero distills visual reasoning trajectories answer-label-free, Speaker-NVV distills identity-relevant representations from high-capacity encoder to compact student, together they bracket distillation-as-capability-transfer across visual and acoustic surfaces), [[renio-reweighting-negative-trajectory-importance-for-llm-on-policy-distillation-2606.23104]] (sibling reweighted distillation — ReNIO reweights negative trajectories in LLM distillation, Speaker-NVV distills identity-relevant features while suppressing verbal-only forgetting, together they establish reweighting-as-distillation-discipline), and [[distill-once-adapt-life-long-dataset-distillation-continual-2606.20196]] (sibling dataset distillation — Speaker-NVV is parameter-level encoder-to-encoder distillation, DO-ALL is dataset-level source-to-synthetic-anchors distillation, together they extend the distillation-as-compression wave from model-side to data-side compression) entries on this page by extending the distillation-as-capability-transfer pattern into the acoustic-non-verbal surface where identity consistency across the verbal-to-non-verbal transition is the deployment regime that modern expressive-TTS/VC pipelines produce and that the SV field has been unable to verify without an NVV-aware aggregator and benchmark.
- [[qg-mil-gated-transformer-aggregator-domain-agnostic-multiple-instance-learning-medical-2606.20027]] — QG-MIL: A Gated Transformer Aggregator for Domain-Agnostic Multiple Instance Learning in Medical Imaging: addresses the structural attention-concentration pathology in attention-based Multiple Instance Learning (MIL) aggregators for medical imaging — where standard softmax attention pooling concentrates probability mass on a few high-scoring instances, producing overconfident and unstable predictions that fail under domain shift across histopathology, radiology, and other clinical-imaging modalities — and proposes QG-MIL, a gated transformer aggregator combining four synergistic components: RMSNorm-based pre-normalization, per-head QK normalization, fine-grained attention output gating, and SwiGLU-style feed-forward gating; Zedda, Luca; Mura, Davide Antonio; Di Ruberto, Cecilia; Atzori, Maurizio; Dasdelen, Muhammed Furkan; Marr, Carsten validate QG-MIL on CAMELYON16, TCGA-NSCLC, and a cross-domain aggregation study across histopathology/CT/MRI without per-domain hyperparameter tuning, surfacing *aggregation-discipline-as-domain-invariance* as a load-bearing primitive for clinical-imaging deployment where labelled training data per domain is limited and per-domain tuning budgets are operationally infeasible; 2026-06-18; discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-26 (medical-imaging / multiple-instance-learning / attention-concentration / gated-aggregator / cross-domain-generalization theme). Complements the [[hydrahead-from-head-level-functional-heterogeneity-to-specialized-attention-hybridization-2606.20097]] (sibling head-level attention discipline — HydraHead argues head-level heterogeneity is functional and routes by expertise, QG-MIL normalizes and gates at the head level to suppress concentration pathologies, together they bracket head-level-attention-discipline between specialization-as-routing and concentration-suppression-as-gating), [[comparing-linear-probes-mahalanobis-cosine-similarity-2606.19603]] (sibling metric-primitive correctness — MCS-Ying corrects the cosine-similarity metric for probe-direction comparison, QG-MIL corrects the attention pooling for MIL-bag aggregation, together they surface the metric-primitive-correctness wave across interpretability and clinical-imaging surfaces), [[naturebench-can-coding-agents-match-the-published-sota-of-nature-family-papers-2606.24530]] (sibling benchmark-as-diagnostic — NatureBench decomposes coding-agent SOTA-replication results across Nature-family tasks, QG-MIL's ablation decomposes the four gated-aggregator components to isolate each component's marginal value, together they extend eval-as-discipline from benchmark results to architectural components), and [[ebench-elemental-diagnosis-of-generalist-mobile-manipulation-policies-2606.18239]] (sibling multi-axis diagnostic — EBench decomposes manipulation-policy capability into 5×4 dimensions, QG-MIL decomposes its aggregation design into 4 synergistic components, together they establish diagnostic-decomposition as the primitive for moving past aggregate-success-rate signals) entries on this page by replacing the attention-concentration pathology of standard MIL pooling with a four-component gated aggregator where each component targets a distinct concentration mechanism, and surfacing attention-discipline as a measurable axis of MIL aggregator quality rather than a hidden hyperparameter.
- [[robusto-2-benchmarking-humans-vlms-autonomous-driving-lima-new-york-city-2606.20980]] — Robusto-2: Benchmarking Humans & VLMs for Autonomous Driving in Lima & New York City: surfaces a structural out-of-distribution generalization gap in VLM-backed action models for autonomous driving — where Self-Driving Cars are expanding internationally and using multimodal systems such as VLMs as the cognitive backbone for their action models, but how well these systems generalize to OOD edge-case scenarios in new geographies has been underexplored despite being the deployment regime — and introduces Robusto-2, a full-factorial human-vs-VLM benchmark in Lima (Peru) and New York City on identical driving scenarios, surfacing the human-prior-to-VLM-prior gap as a structural axis where human drivers carry cultural-context priors (informal turn-taking, pedestrian-behavior expectations, signage conventions) that VLM action models lack because their training distribution is US/EU-centric; Cespedes, Adrian; Chincha, Marcelo; Cusipuma, Dunant; Flores-Benites, Victor; Ortega, David; Deza, Arturo argue that single-geography benchmarks systematically overstate VLM action-model capability and that full-factorial cross-geography benchmarks are required to characterize deployment-relevant generalization; 2026-06-18; discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-26 (autonomous-driving / VLM-action-model / geographical-OD / cross-cultural-benchmarking theme). Complements the [[flowr2a-learning-reward-to-action-distribution-for-multimodal-driving-planning-2606.24231]] (sibling multimodal driving planning — FlowR2A learns reward-to-action distribution for driving planning, Robusto-2 evaluates how VLM action models generalize across geographies, together they bracket the VLM-for-driving surface between training-time planning and evaluation-time cross-geography generalization), [[ebench-elemental-diagnosis-of-generalist-mobile-manipulation-policies-2606.18239]] (sibling multi-axis diagnostic — EBench decomposes manipulation-policy capability into 5×4 dimensions, Robusto-2 decomposes VLM action-model capability into geographical/cultural/action-decision dimensions, together they establish the multi-axis-diagnostic wave as a load-bearing evaluation primitive across embodied-agent surfaces), [[enterpriseclawbench-benchmarking-agents-from-real-workplace-sessions-2606.23654]] (sibling real-deployment-regime benchmarking — EnterpriseClawBench evaluates agents on real workplace sessions, Robusto-2 evaluates VLM action models on real cross-cultural driving scenarios, together they extend the eval-as-discipline wave from workplace-document reasoning to geographical-OD driving cognition), and [[naturebench-can-coding-agents-match-the-published-sota-of-nature-family-papers-2606.24530]] (sibling SOTA-replication benchmarking — NatureBench measures whether coding agents can replicate published Nature-family SOTA, Robusto-2 measures whether VLMs can match human drivers across geographies, together they extend the benchmark-as-deployment-diagnostic wave from coding-replication to AV cross-geography generalization) entries on this page by extending the eval-as-discipline wave from workplace and robotics surfaces into the geographical-OD driving surface, isolating *cross-cultural scenario-recognition failures* vs *action-model reasoning failures* vs *systematic geographical-prior mismatches* as the three decomposition axes of VLM action-model underperformance in OOD cities.
- [[memgui-agent-end-to-end-long-horizon-mobile-gui-agent-proactive-context-2606.19926]] — MemGUI-Agent: An End-to-End Long-Horizon Mobile GUI Agent with Proactive Context Management: addresses a structural context-overflow pathology in mobile GUI agents on long-horizon tasks — where standard agents either rely on external memory controllers that operate outside the action loop (creating context-management latency and policy/controller mismatch) or trigger reactive compression only when the context window is exceeded (losing task-relevant UI facts at the worst possible moment) — and proposes Context-as-Action (ConAct), which folds context-management operations (compress, evict, persist, restore) into the same policy that selects UI actions, so the agent decides *when* to manage context as part of the action sequence rather than via a separate controller; Liu, Guangyi; Wu, Gao; Liu, Congxiao; Zhao, Pengxiang; Liu, Liang; Li, Mading; Zhang, Qi; Wang, Mengyan release MemGUI-3K, a memory-intensive trajectory dataset of teacher rollouts from MemGUI-Agent using the ConAct paradigm, and MemGUI-8B-SFT, an 8B model fine-tuned from Qwen3-VL-8B-Instruct on MemGUI-3K as a reproducible reference implementation, surfacing *context-management-as-policy* as a load-bearing primitive for the long-horizon mobile GUI deployment regime where existing trajectory corpora saturate and existing memory controllers cannot keep pace with UI-state churn; 2026-06-18; discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-25 (mobile-gui-agent / long-horizon / proactive-context-management / memory-as-policy / qwen3-vl theme). Complements the [[are-we-ready-for-an-agent-native-memory-system-2606.24775]] (sibling agent-memory design-space survey — Are-Ready surveys the architectural choices, MemGUI-Agent instantiates the proactive-policy choice on mobile GUI), [[gatemem-benchmarking-memory-governance-in-multi-principal-shared-memory-agents-2606.18829]] (sibling multi-principal shared-memory governance — GateMem is access-control surface, MemGUI-Agent is context-management surface, together they bracket the agent-memory research surface), and [[worldlines-benchmarking-and-modeling-long-horizon-stateful-2606.18847]] (sibling long-horizon embodied agents — WorldLines is simulation, MemGUI-Agent is real mobile device control, both surface long-horizon memory as a deployment-axis-spanning primitive) entries on this page by replacing the external-memory-controller pattern with a policy-level design where the same model that picks UI actions also picks context-management actions — a unification that closes the policy/controller mismatch gap and surfaces *when* to manage context as a structural choice rather than an implementation detail.
- [[comparing-linear-probes-mahalanobis-cosine-similarity-2606.19603]] — Comparing Linear Probes with Mahalanobis Cosine Similarity: addresses a systematic conflation in linear-probe comparison where the standard practice of using raw cosine similarity between probe directions is structurally miscalibrated — different layers of the same network induce probes whose geometry is dominated by layer-specific activation statistics, so a "0.95 cosine similarity" between a mid-layer and late-layer probe can mask the fact that the two probes are tracking *different* features in their respective layers' native coordinate frames — and proposes Mahalanobis Cosine Similarity (MCS), a test-data-covariance-weighted inner product that recovers a task-aware refinement and reduces to standard cosine similarity in the isotropic-covariance limit; Ying, Zhuofan Josh; Hase, Peter; Kriegeskorte, Nikolaus empirically validate that MCS near-perfectly predicts cross-domain generalization (R² = 0.98) across a sweep of probes on the same model and across models, while raw cosine similarity fails to predict the same generalization pattern, surfacing *probe geometry under the right metric* as a load-bearing primitive for interpretability cross-comparison and showing that probe-selection and model-comparison conclusions inverted under raw cosine similarity become consistent and theoretically grounded under MCS; 2026-06-17; discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-25 (representation-learning / linear-probe / interpretability / mahalanobis-cosine-similarity / cross-domain-generalization theme). Complements the [[what-intermediate-layers-know-detecting-jailbreaks-from-entropy-dynamics-2606.25182]] (sibling interpretability-driven detection — Entropy-Layers reasons about hidden-state-level statistics as observable probes, MCS-Ying reasons about probe-direction geometry, both push back against treating layer-level primitives as interchangeable without metric awareness), [[hydrahead-from-head-level-functional-heterogeneity-to-specialized-attention-hybridization-2606.20097]] (sibling head-level functional heterogeneity — HydraHead is structural-level diagnosis, MCS-Ying is probe-comparison-level diagnosis, both papers surface the metric-correctness axis as a methodology concern), and [[forecasting-future-behavior-as-a-learning-task-2606.11445]] (sibling task-aware metric construction — MCS reweights by test-data covariance, Forecasting-Future-Behavior argues forecasting accuracy must be measured on the right temporal horizon, together they extend the eval-as-discipline wave into the linear-probe comparison surface) entries on this page by providing a methodology-correctness fix at the foundational layer of interpretability research — rather than yet another probe design, MCS corrects the comparison metric the field has been using to declare probes "similar" across layers and models, surfacing a class of misleading conclusions that has been invisible under raw cosine similarity.
- [[skillharness-harnessing-safe-skills-computer-use-agents-2606.20636]] — SkillHarness: Harnessing Safe Skills for Computer-Use Agents: addresses a structural capability-versus-safety trade-off in continual skill learning for Computer-Use Agents (CUAs) — where static pre-vetted skill sets freeze capability growth while unrestricted continual learning accumulates unevaluated behavioral surface area faster than pre-deployment audits can evaluate — and proposes SkillHarness, a framework that models skill learning and utilization as a safety-constrained interaction process in which every skill invocation is gated by a runtime safety evaluator against the current environment state; Chen, Yurun; Yi, Biao; Yin, Keting; Zhang, Shengyu demonstrate that the safety-constrained interaction process preserves continual-learning capability gains while preventing the unsafe-invocation failure modes that fully-open continual-learning baselines exhibit on dynamic interactive environments, surfacing *harness-as-intermediary* as a load-bearing runtime pattern for the deployment regime where skill learning is continual and behavioral surface area is unbounded; 2026-06-02; discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-25 (computer-use-agents / skill-curation / runtime-safety-gating / continual-learning / harness-as-intermediary theme). Complements the [[capable-but-careless-do-computer-use-agents-follow-contextual-integrity-2606.23189]] (sibling CUA contextual-integrity compliance — Capable-But-Careless audits task-execution rule-following, SkillHarness gates skill-invocation against environment-state risk, together they bracket the CUA safety surface between execution-time and invocation-time primitives), [[tropt-an-open-framework-for-unifying-and-advancing-discrete-text-optimization-2606.23496]] (sibling discrete text optimization — TROPT supplies the optimization substrate that produces adversarial skill-trigger sequences, SkillHarness is the deployment-time defensive layer that gates whether such triggers are invocable, together they bracket adversarial CUA safety between trigger generation and runtime gating), and [[critique-of-agent-model-2606.23991]] (sibling agent epistemology — Critique-of-Agent argues agency requires internalized structures, SkillHarness argues that even without full agency, runtime safety gating is a load-bearing primitive for the deployment regime) entries on this page by extending the eval-as-discipline and capability-versus-safety framing from pre-deployment safety work into the deployment-time runtime surface where continual learning happens — surfacing a *harness-as-intermediary* pattern that mediates between the policy's skill-selection decisions and the environment's effect-execution channel, rather than relying on pre-deployment audits that cannot keep pace with continual-skill-learning surface-area growth.
- [[shuttermuse-capture-time-photography-guidance-with-mllms-2606.25763]] — ShutterMuse: Capture-Time Photography Guidance with MLLMs: addresses a structural under-evaluation of capture-time (vs post-hoc) photography guidance for multimodal LLMs — where existing aesthetic-cropping benchmarks evaluate only retrospective crop prediction and ignore subject-side pose recommendations, leaving the live-assistance axis of photography under-assessed even though that axis is the deployment regime for real-world photo-capture workflows — and proposes CaptureGuide-Bench, a two-axis benchmark covering photographer-side composition decision and refinement plus subject-side scene-conditioned pose recommendation, alongside CaptureGuide-Dataset (130K samples with textual rationales and structured visual annotations) and ShutterMuse, a unified MLLM trained via supervised + reinforcement fine-tuning; Li, Jiayu; Fang, Yixiao; Hu, Tianyu; Cheng, Wei; Huang, Ping; Fan, Zheheng; Yu, Gang; Ma, Xingjun surface a methodological-level contribution to MLLM evaluation — *composition decision* and *crop localization* are dissociable capabilities (general-purpose MLLMs handle the former but not the latter, specialized aesthetic-cropping models handle the latter but not the former), and a unified capture-time guidance system must be evaluated on both axes plus the subject-side pose axis to characterize capability rather than collapse it into a single accuracy scalar, with ShutterMuse achieving best photographer-side performance and competitive subject-side pose recommendation at substantially lower inference cost than task-specialized pipelines; 2026-06-24; discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-25 (mllm / photography / capture-time-guidance / benchmark / post-hoc-crop / subject-pose theme). Complements the [[iv-cot-implicit-visual-chain-of-thought-structure-aware-text-to-image-2606.24849]] (sibling visual-decomposition work on T2I structural-to-semantic cascade), [[are-text-to-image-models-inductivist-turkeys-counterfactual-benchmark-causal-2606.24548]] (sibling T2I counterfactual-evaluation work via CF-World), and [[diffusionbench-holistic-evaluation-diffusion-transformers-2606.24888]] (sibling multi-axis-diagnostic evaluation work on DiT surface) entries on this page by extending the eval-as-discipline principle from T2I generation and DiT benchmarking into photography guidance — where the structural capability-gap diagnosis (composition vs localization are dissociable; subject-side is a distinct axis) shows that single-accuracy-scalar evaluation systematically misleads about MLLM capability for live-assistance creative workflows.
- [[wan-streamer-v0-1-end-to-end-real-time-interactive-foundation-models-2606.25041]] — Wan-Streamer v0.1: End-to-end Real-time Interactive Foundation Models: addresses a structural latency-accumulation pathology in cascaded real-time interactive systems — where stitching together separately-trained VAD, ASR, language, TTS, audio-driven animation, and video-generation modules adds pipeline latency and cross-module error accumulation that together prevent sub-second full-duplex audio-visual interaction even when each module individually meets its latency budget — and proposes Wan-Streamer, a single Transformer trained end-to-end for native streaming where language, audio, and video are interleaved as a mixed visual/audio/text input-output token sequence coordinated by block-causal attention, with causal encoders/decoders and low-latency multimodal token scheduling co-designed for 160 ms streaming units at 25 fps; Huang, Lianghua; Wu, Zhifan; Wang, Wei; Shi, Yupeng; Feng, Mengyang; et al. surface a methodology-level contribution to interactive-multimodal foundation models — *streamability* (causal encoders + causal decoders + block-causal attention + multimodal token scheduling) is a first-class architectural constraint rather than a downstream optimization, and learning perception/reasoning/generation/response-timing/turn-management/cross-modal-synchronization jointly within one model replaces the cascaded-stack pipeline-latency pathology with ≈200 ms model-side response latency and ≈550 ms total interaction latency at 350 ms bidirectional network RTT, achieving sub-second full-duplex audio-visual communication at foundation-model scale; 2026-06-23; discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-25 (streaming / multimodal / interactive-foundation-model / full-duplex / audio-visual / block-causal-attention theme). Complements the [[causal-rcm-a-unified-teacher-forcing-and-self-forcing-open-recipe-for-autoregressive-diffusion-distillation-in-streaming-video-generation-and-interactive-world-models-2606.25473]] (sibling streaming-causal-attention work on autoregressive video diffusion distillation), [[world-action-models-a-survey-2606.20781]] (sibling WAM survey on embodied predictive-action models), and [[domain-shuttle-freeform-open-domain-subject-driven-text-to-video-2606.26058]] (sibling open-domain subject-driven T2V work) entries on this page by replacing the cascaded-stack pattern with a single unified streaming Transformer — Wan-Streamer's joint learning of perception/reasoning/generation/timing is the architectural unification that WAM's design-axis decomposition points toward, and its sub-second latency milestone validates that interactive multimodal systems do not require modular decomposition to meet real-time constraints.
- [[safe-few-step-generation-via-velocity-editing-2606.23267]] — Safe Few-Step Generation via Velocity Editing: addresses a structural safety-method breakdown in flow-matching T2I generation at small sampling-step budgets — where prior safety and concept-removal methods rely on iterative trajectory steering across many denoising steps or on CLIP-centric prompt-embedding manipulation, both of which fail under 4-step MeanFlow where the limited sampling budget prevents iterative correction and modern context-aware text encoders diminish embedding-level interventions — and proposes VESFlow, a training-free safety method that directly edits the velocity field via a safe-conditional posterior (steering the trajectory toward safe outputs while leaving the conditioning prompt unchanged), plus VESFlow+ which pushes velocity away from the unsafe direction and adds a risk-score-based bypass for benign prompts; Choi, Yujin; Yoon, Jaehong surface a methodology-level contribution to T2I safety — flow-matching models learn the marginal velocity field, so safety can be injected as a posterior edit on velocity rather than via the iterative-trajectory or CLIP-embedding primitives that prior safety work assumed, and the velocity-edit primitive is validated at the 4-step MeanFlow budget where prior methods break down, with VESFlow+ reducing NudeNet attack success rate to 6.3% on Ring-A-Bell and 6.8% on MMA-Diffusion while preserving benign-prompt fidelity without any fine-tuning; 2026-06-22; discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-25 (flow-matching / t2i-safety / velocity-editing / training-free / concept-removal / few-step-distillation theme). Complements the [[exploring-the-design-space-of-reward-backpropagation-for-flow-matching-2606.11075]] (sibling flow-matching reward-backprop work — FlowBP is a training-side intervention, VESFlow is an inference-side intervention on the same surface), [[tropt-an-open-framework-for-unifying-and-advancing-discrete-text-optimization-2606.23496]] (sibling discrete-text-optimization framework for LLM jailbreaks and auditing — TROPT ships the open optimization substrate that flow-matching safety work needs), and [[fedot-ownership-verification-leakage-tracing-watermarks-federated-ldms-2606.22875]] (sibling generative-model security work on ownership verification + leakage tracing for federated LDMs) entries on this page by extending the generative-model safety and security wave from training-time intervention (FlowBP) and ownership-side primitives (FedOT) into an inference-time velocity-edit primitive that works at the small-step-budget regime where prior training-required safety methods are too costly to deploy.- [[universat-resolution-and-modality-agnostic-transformers-for-earth-observation-2606.23503]] — UniverSat: Resolution- and Modality-Agnostic Transformers for Earth Observation: addresses a structural fragmentation gap in applying Vision Transformers to Earth Observation (EO) — where the canonical ViT patch projector is rigid and tied to a specific spatial / spectral / temporal resolution, forcing the field to maintain separate ViT variants per sensor type and per resolution band, fragmenting pretraining and downstream transfer — and proposes UniverSat, a ViT-style backbone built around a Universal Patch Encoder that maps patches from arbitrary spatial, spectral, and temporal resolutions and from both optical and non-optical sensors into a shared embedding space under a shared set of weights, enabling training a single model on heterogeneous multimodal corpora via self-supervision; Perron, Yohann; Astruc, Guillaume; Gonthier, Nicolas; Mallet, Clement; Landrieu, Loic surface a methodology-level contribution to multimodal-pretraining — the rigid-patch-projector assumption that anchors standard ViT is the load-bearing bottleneck for cross-sensor transfer, and a Universal Patch Encoder with shared weights is the structural primitive that lets a single backbone absorb arbitrary-modality corpora without per-sensor heads or per-resolution fine-tuning, validated across classification and segmentation on GeoBench, PANGEABench, and SpectralEarth with strong sensor-agnostic spatial features; 2026-06-22; discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-25 (earth-observation / remote-sensing / foundation-model / resolution-modality-agnostic / cross-sensor-pretraining theme). Complements the [[biomatrix-towards-a-comprehensive-biological-foundation-model-spanning-the-modality-matrix-of-sequences-structures-and-language-2606.22138]] (sibling cross-modality foundation-model work spanning the biological-sequence/structure/language matrix), [[distill-once-adapt-life-long-dataset-distillation-continual-2606.20196]] (sibling cross-domain-distillation work on the continual-adaptation side), and [[emergent-concepts]] (parent meta-cluster) entries on this page by extending the cross-modality foundation-model framing from the biological-modality surface (where Biomatrix spans sequences/structures/language) to the EO-modality surface (where UniverSat spans spatial/spectral/temporal resolutions and optical/non-optical sensors), surfacing *shared-weights patch encoding* as the structural primitive that makes a single model absorb heterogeneous multimodal corpora rather than a fleet of siloed modality-specific variants.

- [[unlimited-ocr-works-2606.23050]] — Unlimited OCR Works: addresses a structural cost-scaling failure mode of end-to-end OCR models with LLM decoders — where using an LLM as the decoder leverages the language-model prior to improve OCR accuracy, but the LLM decoder's KV cache grows linearly with the output sequence length, making long-document transcription progressively slower and more memory-hungry, in stark contrast to humans who exhibit no such decline during long-horizon copying — and proposes Unlimited OCR, which replaces all attention layers in the OCR decoder with Reference Sliding Window Attention (R-SWA), a sliding-window attention variant that references a fixed-size cached context while processing new tokens, eliminating KV-cache growth with output length; Yin, Youyang; Liu, Huanhuan; Xie, Qunyi; Liu, Chaorun; et al. surface a methodology-level contribution to efficient LLM inference — KV-cache growth with output length is a structural artifact of standard attention, not a fundamental requirement of language modeling, and R-SWA's reference-window design keeps the KV cache constant throughout decoding while reducing attention compute cost, combined with DeepSeek OCR's high encoder compression enabling dozens of pages of documents to be transcribed in a single forward pass under the standard 32K maximum context length, with R-SWA positioned as a general parsing-attention primitive equally applicable to ASR and translation; 2026-06-22; discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-25 (OCR / document-AI / attention-mechanism / KV-cache / long-context-inference / efficient-decoding theme). Complements the [[distilling-examples-into-task-instructions-enhanced-in-context-2606.15641]] (sibling ICL-deployment work that diagnoses ICL's context-growth cost pattern, complementary to R-SWA's decoder-side cost fix), [[kalm-reranker-v1-fast-not-late-interaction-compressed-2606.22807]] (sibling efficient-decoding-architecture work on the reranker side), and [[emergent-concepts]] (parent meta-cluster) entries on this page by isolating the decoder-KV-cache bottleneck as a structural limit on long-document OCR — extending the efficient-inference wave from the encoder-decoder-reranker architecture surface (where KaLM-Reranker-V1 decouples passage and query computation) to the OCR-decoder surface (where R-SWA decouples attention-receptive-field growth from KV-cache growth), and surfacing a *human parsing working memory* framing that frames KV-cache-bounded attention as a feature, not a workaround.

- [[exploring-the-design-space-of-reward-backpropagation-for-flow-matching-2606.11075]] — Exploring the Design Space of Reward Backpropagation for Flow Matching: addresses a structural reward-gradient-bottleneck pathology in aligning text-to-image flow matching models (SD3.5-M, FLUX.1-dev, FLUX.2-Klein-base) with human preferences via direct reward backpropagation — where (1) activations cannot be stored across the full sampling trajectory at modern model scale, and (2) chained Jacobian products across steps inflate the reward gradient as it travels back to early indices, both pathologies converging to make direct reward BP impractical at frontier-model scale — and proposes FlowBP, a unified surrogate-trajectory framework that treats the backward trajectory itself as the design object, keeping a no-gradient cached rollout for sampling and building a lightweight backward surrogate from cached and selectively re-forwarded velocities; Wang, Ruoyu; Niu, Boye; Zhou, Xiangxin; Huang, Yushi; Liu, Tongliang; et al. surface a methodology-level contribution to T2I alignment training — FlowBP factorizes the design into four explicit choices (reward-model input, active set, integration weights, bridge coupling) and recovers prior direct-gradient methods (including LeapAlign-class connector-based methods) as particular settings within the unified framework, instantiating three variants (FlowBP-Sparse via sparse Euler reconstruction, FlowBP-Bridge via controlled bridge coupling, FlowBP-Lagrange via raised-order leap quadrature) that bound memory by the active-set size and limit gradient chaining to at most one Jacobian factor, validated across SD3.5-M, FLUX.1-dev, and FLUX.2-Klein-base on preference, quality, and compositional metrics with consistent improvements over direct-gradient baselines; 2026-06-09; discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-25 (flow-matching / reward-backpropagation / T2I-alignment / preference-optimization / surrogate-trajectory / design-space theme). Complements the [[beyond-reward-engineering-a-data-recipe-for-long-context-reinforcement-learning-2606.18831]] (sibling RL-reward-engineering work on the long-context RL surface), [[improving-text-to-music-generation-with-human-preference-2606.21670]] (sibling small-budget preference-aligned creative-multimodal work where the training-time reward-conditioning vs inference-time CFG axis mirrors FlowBP's reward-model-input axis), and [[distilling-examples-into-task-instructions-enhanced-in-context-2606.15641]] (sibling ICL-distillation training-technique work, adjacent "training-bottleneck framing") entries on this page by isolating the reward-gradient-bottleneck axis of T2I alignment as a structural limit on direct reward backpropagation, surfacing *surrogate-trajectory-as-design-object* as the load-bearing primitive that converts prior connector-based methods into a unified framework with auditable design choices, and demonstrating cross-architecture generalization across three flow-matching backbones — extending the broader eval-as-discipline and reward-engineering wave into the T2I flow-matching alignment surface with a unifying design-space lens rather than yet another single-method contribution.
- [[domain-shuttle-freeform-open-domain-subject-driven-text-to-video-2606.26058]] — DomainShuttle: Freeform Open Domain Subject-driven Text-to-video Generation: surfaces a structural ambiguity in open-domain subject-driven T2V (S2V) generation — where existing methods over-optimize subject fidelity (in-domain regime) at the cost of cross-domain editability, locking them into a "preserve the reference at all costs" local optimum — and proposes DomainShuttle, a framework that explicitly shuttles between the in-domain and cross-domain regimes so that the reference subject is preserved while subject-irrelevant properties (background, lighting, pose, action) vary freely according to the text prompt; Chen, Nan; Cai, Yiyang; Xie, Rongchang; Pan, Junwen; Chen, Cheng; Jia, Weinan surface a methodology-level contribution to open-domain S2V — the in-domain/cross-domain distinction is structural rather than a hyperparameter setting, and the load-bearing primitive is *subject-irrelevant properties as a first-class axis* that should vary flexibly under cross-domain prompts (the structural complement to subject-fidelity maximization), with the "shuttle" framing reflecting that a single model can serve both regimes by varying this axis rather than specializing to one; 2026-06-24; discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-25 (T2V / subject-driven-generation / open-domain / cross-domain editability theme). Complements the [[physics-question-scene-graph-fine-grained-evaluation-physical-plausibility-text-to-video-2606.25306]] (sibling T2V evaluation work extending the eval-as-discipline wave into the T2V physical-plausibility surface), [[multi-turn-reflective-masking-elicits-reasoning-mask-diffusion-2606.16700]] (sibling non-AR reasoning work in the diffusion-language-modeling surface), and [[world-action-models-a-survey-2606.20781]] (sibling embodied predictive-action survey) entries on this page by isolating the in-domain-vs-cross-domain axis of open-domain S2V as a structural choice — not a hyperparameter — that the field has been avoiding by over-optimizing subject fidelity.

- [[causal-rcm-a-unified-teacher-forcing-and-self-forcing-open-recipe-for-autoregressive-diffusion-distillation-in-streaming-video-generation-and-interactive-world-models-2606.25473]] — Causal-rCM: A Unified Teacher-Forcing and Self-Forcing Open Recipe for Autoregressive Diffusion Distillation in Streaming Video Generation and Interactive World Models: extends rCM (a recent diffusion-distillation framework whose core philosophy is the complementarity between forward divergence — consistency models — and reverse divergence — distribution-matching distillation) from the standard non-autoregressive video setting into the autoregressive causal-diffusion-transformer setting required for real-time streaming video generation and action-conditioned interactive world models; Zheng, Kaiwen; He, Guande; Zhao, Min; Zhang, Jintao; Chen, Huayu; Chen, Jianfei surface a methodology-level contribution to autoregressive diffusion distillation — the CM+DMD complementarity that powers rCM in the static setting survives chunkwise rollout under causal attention, and the unified open recipe supports both teacher-forcing (frozen-teacher-rollout supervision) and self-forcing (model-rollout supervision) regimes under one distillation objective, eliminating the need for two separate distillation pipelines depending on the supervision source; 2026-06-24; discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-25 (video-diffusion / autoregressive-distillation / streaming / interactive-world-models theme). Complements the [[world-action-models-a-survey-2606.20781]] (sibling embodied predictive-action survey), [[world-value-models-robotic-manipulation-2606.24742]] (sibling value-estimation world-model work in the manipulation surface), and [[multi-turn-reflective-masking-elicits-reasoning-mask-diffusion-2606.16700]] (sibling non-AR reasoning work in the diffusion-language-modeling surface) entries on this page by unifying the two supervision regimes (teacher-forcing vs self-forcing) under one distillation objective for autoregressive video diffusion, lifting streaming/world-model deployment out of the dual-pipeline bottleneck.

- [[forecasting-future-behavior-as-a-learning-task-2606.11445]] — Forecasting Future Behavior as a Learning Task: diagnoses a structural breakdown of the canonical interpretability route — *explain* a model's behavior, then use the explanation to *forecast* its future behavior — for Large Reasoning Models (LRMs), where (a) single-token explanation methods do not generalize to long reasoning trajectories and (b) LRMs' natural-language trajectories are often empirically unfaithful, so any forecast derived from an explanation inherits that unfaithfulness; Levy, Mosh; Goldberg, Yoav; Stickland, Asa Cooper propose instead to bypass the explanation step entirely and treat behavior forecasting itself as a supervised learning task — training a Behavior Forecaster that operates on a single reasoning trajectory and predicts what the LRM will do next, decoupling the trust anchor from interpretability; 2026-06-09; discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-25 (interpretability / large-reasoning-models / behavior-forecasting / safety-trust-anchor theme).
