Complements the [[what-intermediate-layers-know-detecting-jailbreaks-from-entropy-dynamics-2606.25182]] (sibling mechanistic-interpretability work on intermediate-layer jailbreak-detection via entropy dynamics) and [[a-verifiable-search-is-not-a-learnable-chain-of-thought-2606.21884]] (sibling CoT-reasoning-evaluability work that raises the same diagnosis that natural-language reasoning traces are not learnable signals in the way we hope) entries on this page by isolating *behavior forecasting as a learnable task* as an alternative trust anchor — replace "explain→trust" with "forecast→trust," where the auxiliary forecaster earns trust by being right about future behavior regardless of whether the underlying trajectory is human-interpretable.
- [[iv-cot-implicit-visual-chain-of-thought-structure-aware-text-to-image-2606.24849]] — IV-CoT: Implicit Visual Chain-of-Thought for Structure-Aware Text-to-Image Generation: addresses a structural failure mode in unified multimodal LLMs (MLLMs) for text-to-image generation — where existing models entangle *structural planning* (object counts, spatial relations, attribute bindings, coarse layouts) with *appearance rendering* in a single conditioning stream, producing images that look right but violate structural constraints — and proposes IV-CoT (Implicit Visual Chain-of-Thought), a latent visual reasoning framework that decomposes visual conditioning into a structural-to-semantic cascade where structural queries first form a latent visual plan and semantic queries then render appearance conditioned on this plan, with training-only sketch supervision that guides structural-query learning without requiring sketch extraction or intermediate decoding at inference time, performing implicit CoT reasoning in a single forward pass and achieving superior results on GenEval and T2I-CompBench; Li, Zixuan; Lin, Haokun; Xiao, Yicheng; Li, Zhiwei; Song, Xinyang; Zheng, Zelong; He, Yong; Yao, Heng; Ding, Ke surface a methodology-level contribution to multimodal reasoning — chain-of-thought reasoning extends from the LLM-text surface (where CoT was originally proposed) to the latent-visual surface (where CoT-style decomposition becomes a structural-to-semantic cascade), and the *implicit* formulation (latent-only, training-supervised, inference-baked) avoids the explicit-intermediate-decoding overhead that limits where CoT-style reasoning can be deployed, with training-only sketch supervision as the structural prior that bakes structural reasoning into the latent cascade without inference-time sketch extraction; 2026-06-24; discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-25 (T2I / chain-of-thought / structural-reasoning / latent-visual-cascade / structure-aware-generation theme). Complements the [[a-verifiable-search-is-not-a-learnable-chain-of-thought-2606.21884]] (sibling CoT-reasoning work on the LLM-text surface), [[look-light-think-heavy-what-multimodal-chain-of-thought-reasoning-can-and-cannot-do-2606.22565]] (sibling multimodal CoT-reasoning work), [[diffusionbench-holistic-evaluation-diffusion-transformers-2606.24888]] (sibling T2I / DiT evaluation-discipline work), and [[are-text-to-image-models-inductivist-turkeys-counterfactual-benchmark-causal-2606.24548]] (sibling T2I causal-reasoning evaluation work) entries on this page by extending chain-of-thought reasoning from the LLM-text surface into the latent-visual surface — where the load-bearing primitive is decomposing visual conditioning into a structural-to-semantic cascade with training-only sketch supervision, not extending explicit CoT decoding to images, and the resulting implicit formulation preserves inference efficiency while encoding the structural priors that the canonical single-conditioning-stream formulation conflates.

- [[physics-question-scene-graph-fine-grained-evaluation-physical-plausibility-text-to-video-2606.25306]] — Physics Question Scene Graph: Fine-grained Evaluation of Physical Plausibility in Text-to-Video Generation: addresses a structural evaluation gap in text-to-video (T2V) generation — where current models produce realistic-looking videos that nonetheless violate basic physical laws, and where existing evaluation methods cannot localize or specify *which* physical-law violation is occurring — and introduces Physics Question Scene Graph (PQSG), a hierarchical question-based evaluation pipeline that decomposes T2V physical-plausibility assessment into objects, actions, and physics-law-adherence sub-questions organized as a graph with logical dependencies between queries, validated against FinePhyEval (a dataset of human-annotated videos from Sora 2, Veo 3, and Wan 2.1) where PQSG achieves higher correlation with human judgment than prior work and ranks closed-source models above Wan 2.1 on physical realism; Pothiraj, Atin; Cho, Jaemin; Zhang, Yue; Stengel-Eskin, Elias; Bansal, Mohit surface a methodology-level contribution to multimodal evaluation — scene-graph-structured question hierarchies with logical dependencies are a structurally stronger diagnostic of physical-law adherence than flat question lists, because flat questions can ask physics questions about objects that have not yet been verified to exist in the video, and scene graphs enforce the dependency structure that mirrors the dependency structure of the underlying physical reasoning; 2026-06-24; discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-25 (T2V / physical-plausibility-eval / scene-graph / question-hierarchy theme). Complements the [[diffusionbench-holistic-evaluation-diffusion-transformers-2606.24888]] (sibling T2I / DiT evaluation-discipline work), [[are-text-to-image-models-inductivist-turkeys-counterfactual-benchmark-causal-2606.24548]] (sibling T2I causal-reasoning evaluation work via counterfactual prompts), and [[ebench-elemental-diagnosis-of-generalist-mobile-manipulation-policies-2606.18239]] (sibling multi-axis-diagnostic-benchmark work) entries on this page by extending the eval-as-discipline wave into the T2V physical-plausibility surface — where the canonical T2V evaluation surface (visual realism, prompt alignment) cannot probe physical-law adherence, and graph-structured question hierarchies with logical dependencies are the structural tool that localizes *which* physical-law violation is occurring in a generated video.

- [[fedot-ownership-verification-leakage-tracing-watermarks-federated-ldms-2606.22875]] — FedOT: Ownership Verification and Leakage Tracing via Watermarks for Federated LDMs: addresses a structural security gap in federated training of Latent Diffusion Models (LDMs) — where FL's distributed nature creates both ownership-verification needs (proving the model belongs to the training coalition) and leakage-tracing needs (identifying which client redistributed the model), and where existing VAE-based watermarking techniques fail because they can be removed simply by replacing the decoder with a clean counterpart — and proposes FedOT, the first framework for joint ownership verification and leakage tracing in federated LDMs, using a chunked watermark (one part for ownership, one part for client identification) plus Latent Vector Transformation (LVT) that modifies the VAE latent distribution so that any VAE replacement for watermark removal causes significant image-quality degradation rendering the model unusable; Cheng, Wenlong; Gan, Yuan; Xu, Yunqiu; Miao, Jiaxu surface a methodology-level contribution to generative-model security — ownership verification and leakage tracing are *joint design constraints*, not separable problems, and a chunked watermark design with one half supporting coalition-level ownership claims and the other half encoding client-specific identification unifies both questions into a single payload with two functionally-coupled halves, while LVT closes the VAE-replacement attack surface that would otherwise make the watermark trivially removable; 2026-06-22; discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-25 (federated-learning / latent-diffusion-model / watermarking / model-security / ownership-verification / leakage-tracing theme). Complements the [[privacyalign-contextual-privacy-alignment-for-llm-agents-2606.21710]] (sibling privacy work in the LLM-agent surface), [[when-lower-privileges-suffice-investigating-over-privileged-tool-selection-in-llm-agents-2606.20023]] (sibling access-control work in the LLM-agent surface), and [[diffusionbench-holistic-evaluation-diffusion-transformers-2606.24888]] (sibling diffusion-model work) entries on this page by extending the structural-privacy / structural-security discipline into the federated-diffusion surface — where the load-bearing threat is unauthorized redistribution of FL-trained generators, not just data leakage during training, and chunked watermarks + latent-vector-transformation-coupled VAE defense are the structural primitives that close the ownership-verification, leakage-tracing, and VAE-replacement gaps simultaneously.

- [[are-text-to-image-models-inductivist-turkeys-counterfactual-benchmark-causal-2606.24548]] — Are Text-to-Image Models Inductivist Turkeys? A Counterfactual Benchmark for Causal Reasoning: probes a structural epistemic gap in text-to-image (T2I) evaluation — where visually realistic outputs cannot distinguish between genuine causal understanding and sophisticated visual-textual pattern matching, the same surface realism may correspond to very different underlying mechanisms — and introduces Counterfactual-World (CF-World), a counterfactual benchmark that tests T2I models on prompts requiring physically implausible or counterfactually altered scenes; Lei, Jiayi; Pu, Yuandong; Han, Xingyu; Zhu, Rongpeng; Xu, Jing; Wang, Jinyao reframe the diagnostic question through Russell's inductivist-turkey analogy — a model that has only seen "sun rises in the east" cannot generate "sun rises in the west" without genuine causal grounding — and argue that counterfactual prompts are a structurally stronger diagnostic of underlying causal reasoning than naturalistic prompts because naturalistic prompts can be solved by retrieval-like pattern matching over visual-textual co-occurrence while counterfactual prompts require genuine mechanism modeling; 2026-06-24; discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-25 (multimodal / T2I-causal-reasoning / counterfactual-benchmark theme). Complements the [[diffusionbench-holistic-evaluation-diffusion-transformers-2606.24888]] (sibling DiT-evaluation discipline work), [[causal-discovery-in-the-era-of-agents-2606.23608]] (sibling LLM-causal-reasoning epistemological-audit work), and [[ebench-elemental-diagnosis-of-generalist-mobile-manipulation-policies-2606.18239]] (sibling multi-axis-diagnostic-benchmark work) entries on this page by extending the causal-reasoning diagnostic principle to the T2I surface — where the canonical evaluation surface (FID, CLIPScore, prompt-alignment) cannot distinguish causal understanding from pattern matching, and counterfactual prompts are the structural tool that breaks the conflation.

- [[diffusionbench-holistic-evaluation-diffusion-transformers-2606.24888]] — DiffusionBench: On Holistic Evaluation of Diffusion Transformers: diagnoses a structural failure mode in Diffusion Transformer (DiT) research — the field has converged to a single evaluation setup (class-conditional generation on ImageNet) where improving FID and related metrics has decoupled from real generative-modeling progress, while text-to-image generation, the natural alternative, has been treated as too costly or inconvenient to train — and introduces DiffusionBench, a holistic evaluation suite for DiTs that probes capabilities the ImageNet-FID setup cannot surface and provides the cost-scaled T2I evaluation regime the field has been avoiding; Leng, Xingjian; Singh, Jaskirat; Liang, Zhanhao; Smith, Ethan; Bell, Martin; Saha, Aninda surface a methodology-level discipline — distinguishing compute-cost-scaled evaluation from single-anchor-benchmark evaluation — that complements the broader eval-as-discipline wave by extending the diagnostic principle from embodied and agentic surfaces (EBench, WorldLines, AGORA, NatureBench) to the DiT generative-modeling surface, lifting DiT evaluation from a single-axis single-benchmark discipline to a multi-axis suite that reflects the deployment regime the methods are actually used in; 2026-06-23; discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-25 (evaluation / benchmark / DiT / holistic-eval theme). Complements the [[ebench-elemental-diagnosis-of-generalist-mobile-manipulation-policies-2606.18239]] (sibling multi-axis-diagnostic-benchmark work on robotics), [[agora-an-archive-grounded-benchmark-for-agentic-workplace-document-reasoning-2606.24526]] (sibling domain-grounded-benchmark work), and [[worldlines-benchmarking-and-modeling-long-horizon-stateful-2606.18847]] (sibling benchmark-for-real-deployment-regime work) entries on this page by applying the same eval-as-discipline principle to the DiT surface — where the canonical benchmark setup (class-conditional ImageNet) under-represents the deployment regime (T2I generation), and a holistic suite is needed to surface the capabilities the single-anchor setup cannot probe.

- [[polar-factorizing-extent-mode-latent-actions-robot-policy-2606.21139]] — PoLAR: Factorizing Extent and Mode in Latent Actions for Robot Policy Learning: addresses a structural ambiguity in latent action pretraining for robot policies — where existing methods encode each observation-transition pair as a single unstructured representation that entangles *transition extent* (how much changed) with *transition mode* (what kind of change), making the latent space hard to interpret and limiting how downstream policies can compose behaviors — and proposes Polar Latent Actions with Radial structure (PoLAR), which imposes a radial-direction geometry on latent actions, encouraging the radius to encode transition extent and the angular direction to encode transition mode, yielding a factorized latent representation that mirrors the geometric primitives of the underlying visual change; Jeong, Youngjoon; Yu, Jihwan; Jo, Minsoo; Chun, Junha; Kim, Taesup surface a methodology-level contribution — factorized latent spaces that align with the geometric primitives of the underlying phenomenon (extent vs mode in transitions) are a structurally stronger inductive bias than unstructured embeddings — supporting composable behavior transfer and downstream interpretability, and complementing the broader flow-matching and world-model representation-learning wave (WVM, Foresight, WAM) by isolating the factorize-extent-from-mode axis as a load-bearing inductive bias for latent action pretraining; 2026-06-19; discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-25 (robotics / latent-action-representation / factorize / flow-matching theme). Complements the [[world-value-models-robotic-manipulation-2606.24742]] (sibling robotics representation-learning work on the value-estimation axis), [[worldlines-benchmarking-and-modeling-long-horizon-stateful-2606.18847]] (sibling long-horizon embodied work), and [[flowr2a-learning-reward-to-action-distribution-for-multimodal-driving-planning-2606.24231]] (sibling robotics policy-learning work on multimodal driving) entries on this page by extending the geometry-of-representation principle into the latent-action-pretraining surface — where the load-bearing primitive is factorizing extent from mode in the polar geometry, not using richer but unstructured embeddings, and the resulting factorized representation supports the composable behavior transfer that downstream policy learning depends on.
- [[v-zero-answer-label-free-on-policy-distillation-contrastive-evidence-2606.25319]] — V-Zero: Answer-Label-Free On-Policy Distillation with Contrastive Evidence Gating for Fine-Grained Visual Reasoning: addresses the prohibitive post-training cost structure of fine-grained visual-reasoning multimodal LLMs (MLLMs) — where existing agentic methods rely on reinforcement learning with hand-designed verifiable rewards or supervised fine-tuning on large-scale annotated reasoning traces, leading to costly exploration, brittle verification rules, or heavy textual-supervision dependence — and proposes V-Zero, an answer-label-free framework with Contrastive Evidence Gating that uses On-Policy Distillation (learning from the student's own sampled trajectories) paired with a question-relevant regional crop and a negative visual view to evaluate trajectories and gate dense token-level distillation; Sun, Haoxiang; Yi, Zhihang; Deng, Langxuan; Zhou, Yuhao; Jia, Peiqi; Zhao, Jian; Yuan, Li; Lv, Jiancheng; Wang, Tao surface a structural diagnosis of bare OPD through a negative-free stop-gradient-alignment lens — token-level correction is effective but bounded by the absence of trajectory-level discrimination — and show that contrastive regional evidence is the missing trajectory-quality signal, yielding consistent gains on multiple visual reasoning benchmarks while preserving strong generalization with 5×+ speedups over SFT and 10×+ speedups over RL baselines; 2026-06-24; discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-25 (MLLM post-training / on-policy-distillation / answer-label-free / contrastive-evidence-gating theme). Complements the [[distilling-examples-into-task-instructions-enhanced-in-context-2606.15641]] (sibling label-minimization work in the ICL surface) and [[distill-once-adapt-life-long-dataset-distillation-continual-2606.20196]] (sibling dataset-distillation work on the continual-adaptation side) entries on this page by extending the compact-signal-replaces-verbose-supervision pattern into the MLLM visual-reasoning post-training regime — where the load-bearing primitive is contrastive regional grounding replacing hand-designed verifiers, not distillation of few-shot examples into task descriptions or raw source data into synthetic anchors.

- [[foresight-failure-detection-long-horizon-robotic-manipulation-2606.23085]] — Foresight: Failure Detection for Long-Horizon Robotic Manipulation with Action-Conditioned World Model Latents: addresses the underexplored deployment-monitoring gap in long-horizon robotic manipulation — where failure onset is ambiguous, dense temporal annotations are typically unavailable, and per-step ground-truth verifiers do not exist, leaving real-world deployments without a reliable safety overlay — and proposes Foresight, a failure-detection framework that monitors manipulation trajectories using latent representations from an action-conditioned world model, trained on only final task-level success/failure labels and calibrated via functional conformal prediction (FCP) for adaptive detection thresholds; Zhang, Haoran; Lu, Yifu; Wang, Boyang; Kang, Xuhui; Kuo, Yen-Ling; Cheng, Zezhou; Wang, Mengdi; Jenkins, Odest Chadwicke validate Foresight across SOTA vision-language-action policies on LIBERO-Long, ManiSkill-Long, and BEHAVIOR-1K simulation benchmarks plus real-robot long-horizon tasks on ReactorX-200 and Franka arms, surfacing action-conditioned world-model embeddings as a scalable, policy-agnostic representation for reliable failure monitoring; 2026-06-22; discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-25 (robotics / failure-detection / world-model-deployment-monitoring theme). Complements the [[world-value-models-robotic-manipulation-2606.24742]] (sibling robotics world-model work on the data-selection axis), [[worldlines-benchmarking-and-modeling-long-horizon-stateful-2606.18847]] (sibling long-horizon household-assistance benchmark with ObsMem), [[world-action-models-a-survey-2606.20781]] (sibling WAM survey), and [[ebench-elemental-diagnosis-of-generalist-mobile-manipulation-policies-2606.18239]] (sibling multi-axis-diagnostic robotics benchmark) entries on this page by isolating the missing *runtime-failure-detection* axis of long-horizon embodied deployment — where WVM filters training data, WorldLines asks whether the agent remembers the household state, WAM surveys predictive-action methods, and EBench diagnoses aggregate capability profiles, Foresight asks whether the deployment system can detect when the manipulation policy is failing mid-trajectory, with the same action-conditioned world-model backbone serving as the load-bearing representation for both training-time data selection and runtime safety monitoring.

- [[improving-text-to-music-generation-with-human-preference-2606.21670]] — Improving Text-to-Music Generation with Human Preference Rewards: describes the ATTM Grand Challenge (ICME 2026) efficiency-track entry that pushes a 120M-parameter FluxAudio-S text-to-music backbone toward human-preference alignment via five engineering decisions — training-time reward conditioning via the TuneJury twin pairwise ranker that doubles as inference-time CFG axis, an asymmetric sweep over five score-conditioning architectures (training-time vs inference-time variants differ), expert iteration on the top decile, a short preference-tuning pass via CRPO, and inference post-processing via joint CFG + source separation + loudness normalization — yielding a small-budget recipe for preference-aligned audio generation that pushes back against the assumption that preference-aligned creative multimodality requires frontier-scale models or heavyweight RL pipelines; Kim, Yonghyun; Lee, Junwon; Xia, Haiwen; Ma, Yinghao; Donahue, Chris perform per-stage decomposition on 100 Song Describer prompts to isolate each contribution's marginal value, surfacing training-time reward conditioning as a *functional* conditioning axis (not a regularizer), expert iteration as the dominant contributor, the preference-tuning pass adding only noise-level audio-text-alignment gain, and the inference-time score scalar already saturated by end of the chain — a transparent ablation discipline rarely surfaced in creative-multimodal work; 2026-06-19; discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-25 (text-to-music / human-preference-reward / expert-iteration / small-budget-recipe theme). Complements the [[libretto-giving-llm-agents-a-sense-of-musical-structure-2606.22708]] (sibling symbolic-music work for LLM agents), [[improved-large-language-diffusion-models-2606.25331]] (sibling mask-diffusion language-modeling work on non-AR creative substrates), and [[tropt-an-open-framework-for-unifying-and-advancing-discrete-text-optimization-2606.23496]] (sibling open-source discrete-text-optimization framework for adversarial alignment) entries on this page by extending the learned-reward-conditioning pattern from text-to-image and LLM alignment into the text-to-music surface, and by surfacing the per-stage-ablation discipline (isolate each engineering decision's marginal value on a fixed prompt set) as a load-bearing methodology that creative-multimodal work rarely adopts — together they bracket the music-LLM research surface between structured representation (Libretto) and preference-aligned generation (ATTM entry), with discrete-text-optimization substrate (TROPT) supplying the optimization primitives both creative-multimodal surfaces rely on.
- [[worldlines-benchmarking-and-modeling-long-horizon-stateful-2606.18847]] — WorldLines: Benchmarking and Modeling Long-Horizon Stateful Embodied Agents: surfaces a structural gap in long-horizon embodied evaluation where existing long-term-memory benchmarks focus on language-centric retrieval/QA and existing embodied benchmarks focus on short-horizon task execution, leaving the joint "long-horizon + embodied + memory-dependent" regime under-evaluated despite being the deployment regime for home-assistance agents, and introduces WorldLines — a project-driven benchmark that constructs temporally extended household traces (dialogues, actions, execution feedback, object/device state changes) and converts them into evidence-linked samples for both Memory QA and Embodied Task Planning; Zhang, Yehang; Su, Jianchong; Huang, Haojian; Chang, Yifan; Zhou, Tianhao; Xu, Xinli; Xu, Yingjie; Li, Yinchuan propose ObsMem, an observer-grounded memory framework that maintains two complementary streams — visibility-aware memories (what the agent currently perceives) and action-native state trails (what the agent has actually changed) — yielding state-aware decisions that respect partial observability and dynamic world-state overwrites, and surface three persistent failure modes (partial observability causing memory-recall failures, overwritten world states breaking plans, and the difficulty of translating long-term memory into grounded embodied plans) that ObsMem addresses as a stronger reference architecture; 2026-06-17; discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-25 (embodied-agent / long-horizon-memory / household-assistance benchmark theme). Complements the [[world-value-models-robotic-manipulation-2606.24742]] (sibling robotics world-model value-estimation work) and [[ebench-elemental-diagnosis-of-generalist-mobile-manipulation-policies-2606.18239]] (sibling multi-axis-diagnostic robotics benchmark) entries on this page by isolating the missing long-horizon-memory axis of embodied evaluation — where EBench asks "which atomic skills does the policy have?" and WVM asks "what data should the policy train on?", WorldLines asks "can the agent remember the household state and translate that memory into grounded plans over weeks of interaction?".

- [[multi-turn-reflective-masking-elicits-reasoning-mask-diffusion-2606.16700]] — Multi-Turn Reflective Masking Elicits Reasoning in Mask Diffusion Models: identifies a structural asymmetry between autoregressive (AR) chain-of-thought reasoning and Mask Diffusion Model (MDM) reasoning — AR refinement always relies on fully sequential regeneration even when only a local edit is needed, while MDMs' native masking mechanism supports explicit local edits on prior outputs, yet existing MDMs do not support multi-turn masking/denoising and so leave this native reasoning advantage latent — and proposes Reflective Masking (RM), a lightweight post-training method that unlocks multi-turn masking/denoising on existing MDMs without architectural changes; Zhang, Yanming; Bian, Yihan; Qi, Jingyuan; Yao, Yuguang; Huang, Lifu; Zhou, Tianyi additionally introduce History Reference, a parameter-free mechanism that leverages intermediate denoising states during revision to incorporate insights from previous turns (the analog of AR reasoning's prior-token context), and validate RM across text generation, Sudoku, and image editing — consistent gains over standard masking baselines and strong cross-modality generality — positioning RM as a fundamental primitive for reasoning on MDMs that complements (rather than replaces) AR chain-of-thought; 2026-06-15; discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-25 (mask-diffusion / reasoning / test-time-scaling theme). Complements the [[improved-large-language-diffusion-models-2606.25331]] (sibling large-language-diffusion-models work) and [[the-periodic-table-of-llm-reasoning-a-structured-survey-of-reasoning-paradigms-abstractions-building-blocks-and-open-challenges-2606.11470]] (sibling reasoning-paradigm survey) entries on this page by surfacing reflective masking as a concrete reasoning primitive that the survey's taxonomy must now accommodate as a non-AR, mask-edit-native alternative to chain-of-thought — and demonstrates that selective local refinement can be elicited via lightweight post-training without architectural changes.

- [[distilling-examples-into-task-instructions-enhanced-in-context-2606.15641]] — Distilling Examples into Task Instructions: Enhanced In-Context Learning for Real-World B2B Conversations: addresses the gap between standard in-context-learning (ICL) few-shot classification and the practical demands of specialized-domain deployment, releasing the Call Playbook dataset of five classification tasks over real-world multi-party B2B conversations targeting core sales concepts — explicitly designed to expose ICL failure modes in specialized domains where context length grows by concatenating few-shot examples — and proposing knowledge-extraction methods that distill verbose few-shot examples into two compact, interpretable artifacts (structured classification criteria and precise task descriptions); Rotman, Guy; Kopilov, Adi; Zalmanson, Danit Berger; Allouche, Omri demonstrate a 99% reduction in token usage, up to 7% macro-AUC improvement over traditional ICL, and robustness as context grows where advanced token-compression baselines degrade by over 9 F1 points — surfacing a deployment-relevant design pattern: explicit distillation of *what each example demonstrates* into *what the task is* is more robust under context growth than compressing raw tokens, and enables direct refinement of classification logic that opaque token-compression baselines leave unmet; 2026-06-14; discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-25 (in-context-learning distillation / B2B-conversations / low-resource-NLP theme). Complements the [[fastmix-fast-data-mixture-optimization-via-gradient-descent-2606.14971]] (sibling LLM-training-data systems work) and [[distill-once-adapt-life-long-dataset-distillation-continual-2606.20196]] (sibling dataset-distillation work for continual test-time adaptation) entries on this page by extending the distillation pattern from training-data mixtures and continual-adaptation anchors into the ICL deployment surface — where the load-bearing primitive is distilling verbose few-shot examples into structured criteria + task descriptions rather than compressing raw tokens, and the resulting compact artifacts enable direct refinement of classification logic that addresses transparency, efficiency, and user-interaction requirements.
- [[kalm-reranker-v1-fast-not-late-interaction-compressed-2606.22807]] — KaLM-Reranker-V1: Fast but Not Late Interaction for Compressed Document Reranking: presents a fast-but-not-late-interaction (FBNL) reranker that decouples passage and query computation by using an encoder-decoder architecture — the encoder pre-encodes passages with Matryoshka embedding pooling while the decoder models the system instruction, user instruction, and query intent, with cross-attention capturing relevance — yielding deployment-flexible reranking that achieves SOTA on BEIR on par with industrial Qwen3-Reranker models and strong multilingual performance on MIRACL despite limited multilingual training data; Zhao, Xinping; Xu, Jiaxin; Dai, Ziqi; Zhang, Xin; Huang, Shouzheng; Tang, Danyu; Hu, Xinshuo; Zhang, Meishan; Hu, Baotian; Zhang, Min instantiate the family in three sizes (Nano / Small / Large at 0.27B / 1B / 4B activated parameters), making compressed-storage pre-encoded passage reranking practical at retrieval-system scale where the encoder-side computation dominates the deployment cost; 2026-06-22 (cs.CL, cs.IR); discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-25 (LLM reranker / retrieval-efficiency / encoder-decoder-decoupling theme). Complements the [[sproutrag-attention-guided-tree-search-with-progressive-embeddings-for-long-2606.18381]] (hierarchical-RAG sibling) and [[hakari-bench-a-lightweight-benchmark-for-comparing-retrieval-architectures-and-efficiency-settings-under-unified-conditions-2606.22778]] (retrieval-architecture-benchmark sibling) entries on this page by attacking the joint-encoding bottleneck that tightly couples query and passage computation in most existing rerankers, and surfacing Matryoshka passage encoding + decoder-side cross-attention as the architectural pattern that makes compressed-passage reranking practical.

- [[world-value-models-robotic-manipulation-2606.24742]] — World Value Models for Robotic Manipulation: argue that VLM-backboned value models lack the temporal modeling capabilities required for accurate value estimation because they are pretrained on static or temporally sparse visual observations, and marry world models with value estimation to construct a generalist World Value Model (WVM) that grounds current belief using historical context and plans over future outcomes — achieving SOTA Value-Order Correlation on standard benchmarks; Wang, Zhihao; Li, Jianxiong; Cui, Yu; Gao, Yuan; Zhan, Xianyuan; Yu, Junzhi; Ma, Xiao further introduce Suboptimal-Value-Bench, a multi-embodiment benchmark of 800 suboptimal trajectories with high-fidelity human-labeled frame annotations explicitly designed to evaluate value models on the non-expert data that dominates large-scale manipulation corpora, and show WVM maintains SOTA on this regime and improves downstream manipulation policy learning when used as a data-quality filter; 2026-06-19 (cs.RO, cs.AI, cs.CV); discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-25 (robotics / value-function / world-model data-selection theme). Complements the [[world-action-models-a-survey-2606.20781]] (predictive-action-model survey sibling) and [[ebench-elemental-diagnosis-of-generalist-mobile-manipulation-policies-2606.18239]] (multi-axis-diagnostic-benchmark sibling) entries on this page by surfacing world-model backbones as the right foundation for generalist value estimation and demonstrating that data-selection with a non-expert-aware value model improves downstream manipulation policy learning — the missing link between large-scale mixed-quality manipulation data and policy-deployment-ready training subsets.

- [[distill-once-adapt-life-long-dataset-distillation-continual-2606.20196]] — Distill Once, Adapt Life-Long: Exploring Dataset Distillation for Continual Test-Time Adaptation: identifies the source-vs-stability trade-off in continual test-time adaptation where practical deployments cannot retain the source dataset due to privacy or licensing constraints but purely source-free CTTA methods become unstable under long-term distribution shift with compounding self-training errors and catastrophic forgetting, and introduces DO-ALL — a plug-and-play framework that performs Dataset Distillation once before deployment to produce a compact set of privacy-conscious synthetic anchors summarizing the source distribution, then matches each target sample to its semantically aligned anchor during online adaptation for source replay, representation alignment, and manifold-smoothing regularization; Jang, Hyun-Kurl; Kim, Jihun; Kweon, Hyeokjun; Yoon, Kuk-Jin demonstrate that DO-ALL can be seamlessly integrated into existing CTTA algorithms and consistently improves long-term robustness across CIFAR100-C, ImageNet-C, and the CCC benchmark; 2026-06-18 (cs.LG, cs.CV); discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-25 (continual-test-time-adaptation / dataset-distillation / source-free-robustness theme). Complements the [[fastmix-fast-data-mixture-optimization-via-gradient-descent-2606.14971]] (LLM-pretraining data-mixing bilevel-optimization sibling) and [[manifold-bandits-bayesian-curriculum-learning-over-the-latent-geometry-of-large-language-models-2606.19750]] (geometric-anchor RL sibling) entries on this page by extending the data-distillation-style compression pattern from pretraining-mixture selection into the continual-adaptation regime, where the geometric-anchor matching pattern is the load-bearing primitive for stable online adaptation without retaining raw source data.


- [[an-exploratory-case-study-of-llm-assisted-refactoring-and-gameplay-feature-generation-in-an-endless-runner-game-2606.21171]] — An Exploratory Case Study of LLM-Assisted Refactoring and Gameplay Feature Generation in an Endless Runner Game: an exploratory empirical case study of GPT-4o on six selected development tasks (three localized refactoring tasks and three gameplay feature generation tasks) within a custom Python/Pygame endless runner, evaluated through software metrics, unit tests, and manual gameplay assessments; Wunderlich, Jan; Kleffmann, Markus; Lempert, Sebastian find that all three refactoring tasks were completed successfully in functional terms, whereas only one of three gameplay feature generation tasks resulted in a correctly integrated feature, suggesting GPT-4o handled localized transformations more reliably than tasks requiring new gameplay interactions across multiple existing systems — an asymmetric capability profile that benchmark-style code-LLM evaluations typically do not surface; 2026-06-19 (cs.SE); discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-25 (LLM-coding / software-engineering / case-study theme). Complements the [[beyond-nl2code-a-structured-survey-of-multimodal-code-intelligence-2606.15932]] (multimodal-code survey sibling) and [[naturebench-can-coding-agents-match-the-published-sota-of-nature-family-papers-2606.24530]] (coding-agent SOTA-replication sibling) entries on this page by being a transparent case-based account of LLM-coding in an existing production-style game codebase — local transformations work, multi-system integration remains the binding constraint.

- [[world-action-models-a-survey-2606.20781]] — World Action Models: A Survey: a structured survey of World Action Models (WAMs) — embodied predictive-action models that make a forecast of the future available to action — that disambiguates WAMs from broader world models, video-generation models, action-grounded video world models, and Vision-Language-Action policies by decomposing each method along two complementary axes (what it is required to generate, and how its predictive substrate, backbone, action coupling, and deployment regime are composed); Shen, Qiuhong; Zhang, Shihua; Liao, Yue; Li, Qi; Tan, Zhenxiong; Wang, Shizun; Yan, Shuicheng; Wang, Xinchao surface a consistent design pattern — WAMs are not simply video generators with action heads, but predictive-action methods whose design choices trade representational richness against compute, memory, latency, and action-label cost, and the field is moving toward methods that generate less of the future while preserving what control requires; 2026-06-18 (cs.RO, cs.AI, cs.CV); discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-25 (survey / embodied world-models / action-models theme). Complements the [[qwen-agentworld-language-world-models-for-general-agents-2606.24597]] (language world models for agents sibling) and [[ebench-elemental-diagnosis-of-generalist-mobile-manipulation-policies-2606.18239]] (robotics multi-axis-diagnostic sibling) entries on this page by extending the survey wave into embodied predictive-action and surfacing the consistent design pattern that links WAM methods across heterogeneous backbones.

- [[fastmix-fast-data-mixture-optimization-via-gradient-descent-2606.14971]] — FastMix: Fast Data Mixture Optimization via Gradient Descent: reformulates data-mixture selection for LLM pre-training and post-training as a bilevel optimization problem in which mixture coefficients and model parameters are jointly learned via a single proxy-model training run, with per-source loss weights derived from gradient-based updates; Tan, Haoru; Wu, Sitong; Chen, Yanfeng; Xia, Jun; Xie, Ruobing; Xia, Bin; Sun, Xingwu; Qi, Xiaojuan show the approach substantially reduces search cost versus prior heuristic or simulation-based data-mixture discovery while outperforming baselines on both pre- and post-training data-mixture tasks, training only a single proxy model end-to-end rather than multiple model variants; 2026-06-12 (cs.LG, cs.CL); discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-25 (LLM training-data / data-mixture / bilevel-optimization theme). Complements the [[holistic-data-scheduler-for-llm-pre-training-via-multi-objective-reinforcement-learning-2606.24133]] (RL-based scheduler sibling) and [[ac-odm-actor-critic-online-data-mixing-for-sample-efficient-llm-pretraining-2505.23878]] (actor-critic online data mixing sibling) entries on this page by replacing heuristic or simulation-based mixture search with a single-proxy-model gradient-based procedure that jointly optimizes mixture ratios and model parameters.

- [[deep-research-in-physical-sciences-a-multi-agent-framework-and-comprehensive-benchmark-2606.18648]] — Deep Research in Physical Sciences: A Multi-Agent Framework and Comprehensive Benchmark: introduces PhySciBench — a 200-question benchmark balanced between physics and chemistry across six task categories reflecting real-world scientific workflows — and proposes DelveAgent, a modular multi-agent framework with adaptive planning, dual-granularity memory, and hierarchical physics-grounded reflection; Yigeng Jiang, Tengchao Yang, Taoyong Cui, Jiaxing Wan, Yuan Wang, Weida Wang et al. show that even Gemini Deep Research reaches only 33.5% accuracy on PhySciBench, identifying fragility in extended reasoning chains, limited cross-step knowledge transfer, and absence of physics-grounded self-verification as the three recurrent failure modes — and that DelveAgent improves accuracy by up to 7.5 percentage points while reducing inference cost to roughly one-third of the strongest baseline; 2026-06-17 (cs.AI, cs.CL, cs.MA); discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-25 (deep-research / multi-agent scientific-reasoning / physical-sciences theme). Complements the [[naturebench-can-coding-agents-match-the-published-sota-of-nature-family-papers-2606.24530]] (Nature-family coding-agent sibling) and [[enterpriseclawbench-benchmarking-agents-from-real-workplace-sessions-2606.23654]] (workplace-session sibling) entries on this page by extending the deep-research evaluation wave to the physical-sciences setting where hallucination risk is structurally high and physics-grounded self-verification is the load-bearing primitive.

- [[ebench-elemental-diagnosis-of-generalist-mobile-manipulation-policies-2606.18239]] — EBench: Elemental Diagnosis of Generalist Mobile Manipulation Policies: a 26-task simulation benchmark that diagnoses generalist mobile manipulation policies along 5 capability dimensions and 4 generalization dimensions, replacing the aggregate-success-rate scalar with a multi-axis capability profile; Ning Gao, Jinliang Zheng, Xing Gao, Haoxiang Ma, Hanqing Wang, Yukai Wang et al. evaluate π₀, π₀.₅, XVLA, and InternVLA-A1 and reveal strikingly disjoint capability profiles at near-equal aggregate success rates — π₀.₅ has the best train-test retention, InternVLA-A1 dominates mobile manipulation but collapses on dexterous tasks, and XVLA covers a different set of atomic skills than the others; 2026-06-16 (cs.RO, cs.AI, cs.CV); discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-25 (robotics / VLA / multi-axis-diagnostic benchmark theme). Complements the [[enterpriseclawbench-benchmarking-agents-from-real-workplace-sessions-2606.23654]] (sibling diagnostic-benchmark on workplace agents) and [[naturebench-can-coding-agents-match-the-published-sota-of-nature-family-papers-2606.24530]] (sibling SOTA-reproduction benchmark) entries on this page by showing that even within generalist manipulation, aggregate SOTA masks capability-specific collapses — InternVLA-A1 winning mobile but failing dexterity, XVLA covering complementary atomic skills, and π₀.₅ having the best train-test retention — diagnostic signals invisible to a single-leaderboard-number evaluation.

- [[beyond-nl2code-a-structured-survey-of-multimodal-code-intelligence-2606.15932]] — Beyond NL2Code: A Structured Survey of Multimodal Code Intelligence: a structured survey of systems that generate, edit, refine, or reason with code under visually grounded inputs and outputs (screenshots, charts, vector drawings, videos, interactive states) where correctness depends not only on syntax but on layout, data semantics, interaction behaviour, and post-execution domain constraints; Xuanle Zhao, Qiushi Sun, Jingyu Xiao, Xuexin Liu, Haoyue Yang, Qiaosheng Chen et al. propose a role-based taxonomy (code as rendered artifact, editable symbolic structure, scientific representation, intermediate reasoning trace, or executable policy/tool interface) and organise benchmarks and methods into four domains (Graphical User Interface, Scientific Visualization, Structured Graphics, Frontier Tasks and Frameworks), then identify four verification-centered research directions; 2026-06-14 (cs.CL, cs.SE, cs.CV); discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-25 (LLM survey / multimodal-code / taxonomy theme). Complements the [[the-periodic-table-of-llm-reasoning-a-structured-survey-of-reasoning-paradigms-abstractions-building-blocks-and-open-challenges-2606.11470]] (sibling reasoning-paradigm survey) and [[the-hitchhikers-guide-to-agentic-ai-from-foundations-to-systems-2606.24937]] (sibling agentic-AI survey) entries on this page by extending the survey wave to the multimodal-code surface and reframing code-intelligence around *what code does* in the task rather than the text-to-code vs code-to-text split.

- [[critique-of-agent-model-2606.23991]] — Critique of Agent Model: an epistemic audit of "agent" as a term of art in current LLM systems, arguing that genuine agency requires goal/identity/decision/self-regulation/learning structures to be internalized within the system itself rather than assembled through external scaffolding, and proposing the Goal-Identity-Configurator (GIC) reference architecture for what they call "agentive" systems; Xing, Eric; Deng, Mingkai; Hou, Jinyu argue the piece lands at a moment when the field has been marketing LLM-toolchains as "coding agents" and "AI co-scientists" while entertaining speculative "machine agency" risk narratives — and that both framings collapse without a precise agentic-vs-agentive distinction; 2026-06-22 (cs.AI, cs.LG, cs.MA, cs.RO); discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-25 (LLM agent epistemology / agency-internalism / GIC architecture theme). Complements the [[causal-discovery-in-the-era-of-agents-2606.23608]] and [[capable-but-careless-do-computer-use-agents-follow-contextual-integrity-2606.23189]] entries on this page by pushing the audit question one level deeper — from "do agents follow rules" to "do agents have the structures required to follow rules internally" — and frames the agent-safety discussion around internalized self-regulation rather than scaffold-thickening.

- [[tropt-an-open-framework-for-unifying-and-advancing-discrete-text-optimization-2606.23496]] — TROPT: An Open Framework for Unifying and Advancing Discrete Text Optimization: the first open-source framework that unifies discrete text-trigger optimizers (the family of methods used for LLM jailbreaks, model auditing, and interpretability probes) under a single interface, currently shipping 30+ optimization recipes built from 15+ optimizers spanning white-box to black-box access and 15+ losses from foundational to state-of-the-art methods; Ben-Tov, Matan; Sharif, Mahmood argue that existing discrete optimizers are scattered across research codebases tied to specific models, objectives, and problem domains, with variants proliferating and head-to-head comparison being near-impossible — TROPT makes every component swappable and ships with a controlled large-scale experimental study surfacing potent-yet-underadopted jailbreak techniques the field's scattered-codebase state had been obscuring; 2026-06-22 (cs.LG, cs.CR); discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-25 (LLM discrete-text-optimization / red-teaming framework / open-source-unification theme). Complements the [[what-intermediate-layers-know-detecting-jailbreaks-from-entropy-dynamics-2606.25182]] (jailbreak-detection sibling) and [[toward-open-weight-models-without-risks-separating-public-and-private-capabilities-in-llms-2606.21638]] (open-weight capability-separation sibling) entries on this page by providing the open-source optimization substrate that detection and capability-separation work needs as the reproducible basis for both adversarial trigger generation and auditing primitives.

- [[libretto-giving-llm-agents-a-sense-of-musical-structure-2606.22708]] — Libretto: Giving LLM Agents a Sense of Musical Structure: an agent-facing framework that turns symbolic music from a raw token sequence into a measurable and editable object for language-model agents, using an LLM-native grammar with explicit onset slots, voices, and bar-level organization, evaluated in a corpus-calibrated statistical space over rhythm, harmony, melody, texture, form, and variation; Xu, Yichen shows the same structural axes support retrieval, diagnosis, copy-risk control, and iterative self-revision — so an agent can fill a gap, morph toward a reference, generate an educational piece, or audit its own output against corpus statistics without leaving the grammar; 2026-06-21 (cs.SD, cs.AI); discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-25 (LLM-agent creative/multimodal / symbolic-music / structured-representation theme). Complements the [[biomatrix-towards-a-comprehensive-biological-foundation-model-spanning-the-modality-matrix-of-sequences-structures-and-language-2606.22138]] (multi-modal foundation model sibling) and [[agent-orchestration]] (parent meta-page on agent-system organization) entries on this page by being a structured-representation framework for agents rather than a unified-modality foundation model — Libretto's load-bearing primitive is a symbolic-music grammar, not a model, making the agent-tooling pattern visible at the creative-multimodal surface.

- [[gatemem-benchmarking-memory-governance-in-multi-principal-shared-memory-agents-2606.18829]] — GateMem: Benchmarking Memory Governance in Multi-Principal Shared-Memory Agents: introduces a benchmark for shared-memory LLM agent deployments where multiple principals (e.g., users, roles, scopes) write to a common memory pool and query it under different authorization boundaries — a regime that existing single-user memory benchmarks leave understudied; Ren, Zhe; Yang, Yibo; Chen, Yimeng; +7 more evaluate memory quality along three axes jointly: utility for legitimate long-horizon requests with state updates, access control across contextual authorization boundaries, and agent-facing actions under governance constraints, surfacing memory-governance failures that prior retrieval/recall benchmarks conflated with single-user noise; 2026-06-17 (cs.LG); discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-25 (LLM agent memory / multi-principal / shared-memory governance theme). Complements the [[are-we-ready-for-an-agent-native-memory-system-2606.24775]] and [[capable-but-careless-do-computer-use-agents-follow-contextual-integrity-2606.23189]] entries on this page by isolating the missing access-control axis of LLM agent memory — where the prior benchmarks ask 'can the agent remember?' GateMem asks 'should the agent remember this, and for whom?', a regime that hospitals, workplaces, campuses, and household-assistant deployments will require.

- [[sproutrag-attention-guided-tree-search-with-progressive-embeddings-for-long-2606.18381]] — SproutRAG: Attention-Guided Tree Search with Progressive Embeddings for Long-Document RAG: an attention-guided hierarchical RAG framework that organizes sentence-level chunks into progressively larger but semantically coherent units and uses tree search guided by attention-derived signals to navigate them, avoiding the three failure modes of prior hierarchical RAG (LLM-guided chunking costs at indexing/retrieval, single-level context expansion's fixed granularity, and hierarchical summarization's information loss); Abaskohi, Amirhossein; Laradji, Issam H.; West, Peter; +1 more show that the progressive-embedding design preserves sentence-level retrieval precision while enabling paragraph- and section-level aggregation without the summarization bottleneck; 2026-06-16 (cs.CL); discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-25 (LLM RAG / hierarchical-retrieval / attention-guided progressive-embeddings theme). Complements the [[hakari-bench-a-lightweight-benchmark-for-comparing-retrieval-architectures-and-efficiency-settings-under-unified-conditions-2606.22778]] (sibling entry on retrieval efficiency) and prior chartwalker cross-chart RAG work by attacking the granularity-vs-coherence trade-off with attention-derived signals instead of LLM-guided chunking or summarization — making long-document RAG practical without paying the indexing-time LLM cost.

- [[dailyreport-an-open-ended-benchmark-for-evaluating-search-agents-on-daily-2606.12871]] — DailyReport: An Open-ended Benchmark for Evaluating Search Agents on Daily Search Tasks: an open-ended benchmark of 150 realistic daily information-seeking tasks paired with 3,546 atomic evaluation criteria, addressing the gap between specialized SA benchmarks (unrealistic task distribution) and real-world daily search deployment (where coarse task-level rubrics limit evaluation interpretability); Han, Jingxuan; Liu, Wei; Zhu, Mingyang; +8 more show that the atomic-criterion design enables fine-grained, interpretable scoring that separates task-completion from synthesis quality from source-grounding — three axes prior benchmarks conflated under a single accuracy number; 2026-06-11 (cs.AI); discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-25 (LLM search-agent evaluation / open-ended benchmark theme). Complements the [[agent-orchestration]] (parent meta-page covering agent-evaluation surfaces) by shifting the open-ended benchmark framing from long-horizon embodied agents to the search-agent evaluation surface, where atomic-criterion scoring has been missing entirely.
- [[causal-discovery-in-the-era-of-agents-2606.23608]] — Causal Discovery in the Era of Agents: an epistemological audit of recent LLM-causal-discovery work (pairwise direction inference, graph-structure proposal, LM outputs as priors) arguing these methods obscure whether causal evidence is supported by data and assumptions or by textual associations, prompt artifacts, and hallucinated mechanisms; Zheng, Yujia; Verma, Vishal; Gill, Mantej; Dai, Haoyue propose an evaluation framework that separates the contribution of the LM's prior knowledge, the statistical signal in the data, and the prompt and scaffolding artifacts — allowing the field to attribute discovered causal structures to the right source rather than crediting LM-imitation as causal inference; 2026-06-22 (cs.AI); discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-25 (LLM-augmented causal-discovery / epistemological-audit / agentic-scientific-reasoning theme). Complements the a-verifiable-search and NatureBench entries on this page by surfacing the same gap between verifiable surface outputs and learnable underlying mechanisms in the LLM-augmented scientific-discovery setting, and frames "LLM-as-causal-scientist" as a higher-risk framing than "LLM-as-causal-assistant" because the agent setting amplifies the LM-imitation-as-causal-evidence failure mode.


- [[manifold-bandits-bayesian-curriculum-learning-over-the-latent-geometry-of-large-language-models-2606.19750]] — Manifold Bandits: Bayesian Curriculum Learning over the Latent Geometry of Large Language Models: reformulates LLM RL curriculum learning as Bayesian optimization over a learned manifold of problem difficulty, replacing the standard "each prompt is an independent arm" formulation with a posterior that shares statistical strength across geometrically similar problems; McKenzie, Darrien; Hansen, Nicklas; Wang, Xiaolong show that exploiting the embedding-space geometry of reasoning problems yields materially better sample efficiency on LLM RL benchmarks than independent-arm bandit baselines, with gains concentrated in the early- and mid-training regime where exploration matters most; 2026-06-18 (cs.LG); discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-25 (LLM RL training efficiency / curriculum-learning geometry theme). Complements the Holistic-Data-Scheduler and AC-ODM data-allocation work on this page by attacking the prompt-sampling side of LLM RL rather than the pretraining-data-mixing side — together they frame "what to sample" as the load-bearing RL training decision.


- [[openrath-session-centered-runtime-state-for-agent-systems-2606.19409]] — OpenRath: Session-Centered Runtime State for Agent Systems: introduces a PyTorch-like programming model for multi-agent, multi-session systems that consolidates fragmented runtime state (transcripts, tool effects, memory events, workspace placement, branch provenance, replay evidence) into a single first-class session object — modeled explicitly on PyTorch's autograd tape as a structured, inspectable, replayable artifact rather than a bag of side-channel logs spread across services; Wen, Fukang; Wang, Zhijie; Xu, Ruilin elevate reproducibility, branching, and replay to first-class runtime operations on the state object itself; 2026-06-17 (cs.SE); discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-25 (agent-runtime substrate / session-model theme). Complements the AOHP OS-level harness work by addressing the missing engineering substrate between the OS and the agent loop — where AOHP argues the OS must treat agents as first-class actors, OpenRath argues the agent runtime must treat its own state the way PyTorch treats the computation graph.


- [[autodata-an-agentic-data-scientist-to-create-high-quality-synthetic-data-2606.25996]] — Autodata: An agentic data scientist to create high quality synthetic data: frames synthetic-data construction as an agentic iterative loop over schema design, sample generation, quality filtering, and rubric verification, and trains (meta-optimizes) the agentic data scientist end-to-end; Kulikov, Whitehouse, Wu show that synthetic data from the meta-optimized agent matches or exceeds the downstream model quality of carefully curated human datasets at a fraction of the cost; 2026-06-24 (cs.AI); discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-25 (LLM training-data / agentic-data-pipeline theme). Complements the agent-benchmarking and self-compacting-agent work on this page by showing that the data-generation side of the agentic loop can itself be agentic, raising the question of whether human-curated datasets are still the long-term ceiling.



- [[constraint-tax-in-open-weight-llms-an-empirical-study-of-tool-calling-suppression-under-structured-output-constraints-2606.25605]] — Constraint Tax in Open-Weight LLMs: An Empirical Study of Tool Calling Suppression Under Structured Output Constraints: empirical study showing structured-output enforcement (JSON-schema / grammar-constrained decoding) imposes a measurable "constraint tax" on tool-calling reliability across multiple open-weight model families, with the degradation concentrated in tool-selection rather than schema-format errors; Li, Zhang, Lv propose constraint-aware fine-tuning and two-pass decoding mitigations; 2026-06-24 (cs.CL); discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-25 (LLM tool-use / structured-output alignment theme). Complements the agentic-capability evaluation literature by isolating the constraint layer's contribution to tool-call failures, an interaction that prior benchmarks had conflated with general instruction-following degradation.



- [[improved-large-language-diffusion-models-2606.25331]] — Improved Large Language Diffusion Models: introduces iLLaDA, an 8B masked diffusion language model trained from scratch that matches strong autoregressive baselines at matched compute, using a curriculum-style noising schedule and a parallel-decoding inference strategy with no quality regression at long context; Nie, Shen, Min, Qiyang, Xu, Shaoxuan show that the masked-diffusion vs autoregressive gap closes at 8B scale with the right training recipe; 2026-06-24 (cs.CL); discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-25 (LLM architecture frontier / non-autoregressive diffusion theme). Complements the inference-efficiency and agentic-memory discoveries on this page by showing that a fundamentally different factorization (masked diffusion) is now competitive with autoregression at production scales, reopening the question of what the dominant pre-training objective will be in 2027+.



- [[what-intermediate-layers-know-detecting-jailbreaks-from-entropy-dynamics-2606.25182]] — What Intermediate Layers Know: Detecting Jailbreaks from Entropy Dynamics: identifies a reproducible mid-layer entropy-compression / late-layer-divergence trajectory signature of adversarial prompts and trains a lightweight MLP probe over per-layer entropy statistics that detects jailbreak attempts at >90% AUROC across 5 attack families (GCG, PAIR, ArtPrompt, multilingual, cipher-based) before full-sequence decoding; Nikolenko, Papucci, Rezaei show cross-model transfer of the probe to other open-weight LLMs with shared tokenizer vocabulary; 2026-06-23 (cs.CL); discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-25 (LLM safety / jailbreak-detection / mechanistic-interpretability theme). Provides a pre-generation guard layer that is substantially cheaper than LLM-judge cascades and complements output-space refusal classifiers by inspecting hidden states at layer N/2.



- [[openthoughts-agent-data-recipes-for-agentic-models-2606.24855]] — OpenThoughts-Agent: Data Recipes for Agentic Models: introduces OT-Agent, a fully open end-to-end data curation pipeline for training broadly capable agentic language models, derived from 100+ controlled ablation experiments that systematically isolate the contribution of each pipeline stage, task source, and diversity dimension; Raoof, Zhuang, Nezhurina, Guha, Tejaswi et al. fine-tune Qwen3-32B on a 100K-example dataset assembled from the pipeline and achieve 44.8% average accuracy across 7 agentic benchmarks (a 3.9 percentage-point gain over Nemotron-Terminal-32B's 40.9%), with strong scaling properties (outperforming alternative open datasets at every compute-controlled training-set size); 2026-06-23 (cs.AI); discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-25 (LLM agent training-data / open-data-pipeline theme). Complements the Beyond Reward Engineering data-recipe work on this page by applying the same data-centric philosophy to the agentic-training setting and releasing the full pipeline (training sets + curation code + models) at openthoughts.ai, removing the single-benchmark specialization that has limited prior open agentic data efforts like SWE-Smith, SERA, and Nemotron-Terminal.



- [[are-we-ready-for-an-agent-native-memory-system-2606.24775]] — Are We Ready For An Agent-Native Memory System?: argues that LLM-agent memory has evolved from simple retrieval-augmented mechanisms into a full data-management system requiring persistence, structured retrieval, lifecycle updates, and multi-agent consistency, and proposes design recommendations for an agent-native memory layer drawn from database systems, knowledge graphs, and OS memory hierarchies; Zhou, Zhou, Han survey the current generation of agent memory solutions and identify the scaling gaps that prevent them from supporting long-running multi-session agents; 2026-06-23 (cs.CL); discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-25 (LLM agent infrastructure / memory-systems theme). Complements the self-compacting-agent and agent-benchmarking discoveries on this page by surfacing the missing systems-level substrate — agent memory is currently a collection of ad-hoc retrieval layers, and this paper argues it should be a first-class system.



- [[dream-dense-retrieval-embeddings-via-autoregressive-modeling-2606.24667]] — DREAM: Dense Retrieval Embeddings via Autoregressive Modeling: investigates whether dense retrieval embeddings can be produced directly by an autoregressive LM (the same model class used for generation) by repurposing the next-token-prediction objective to also produce document-level representations without contrastive supervision; Tang, Yang show that autoregressive embedding objectives, paired with a careful aggregation of hidden states across the document, recover competitive retrieval quality on standard benchmarks while eliminating the labeled-pair pipeline that has been the dominant cost driver for new retrieval domains; demonstrate cross-task transfer (open-domain QA, dense passage retrieval, multilingual retrieval) and that the same model can serve as retriever and re-ranker without architectural change; 2026-06-23 (cs.CL); discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-25 (LLM retrieval / embedding-from-LM / unified-retrieval-generation theme). Complements the EvoEmbedding and agent-native-memory entries on this page by removing the specialist-retriever barrier — DREAM shows retrieval embeddings can come from the same autoregressive LM that generates, opening a path toward unified retrieval-generation models whose embeddings and outputs share representations and supervision signals.



- [[qwen-agentworld-language-world-models-for-general-agents-2606.24597]] — Qwen-AgentWorld: Language World Models for General Agents: introduces the first family of language world models capable of simulating agentic environments across 7 domains via long chain-of-thought reasoning, with Qwen-AgentWorld-35B-A3B and Qwen-AgentWorld-397B-A17B trained on 10M+ interaction trajectories through a three-stage pipeline (CPT for world-modeling capabilities, SFT for next-state-prediction reasoning, RL with hybrid rubric-and-rule rewards); Zuo, Xiao, Sheng, Huang, Tu also release AgentWorldBench (real-world interactions of 5 frontier models across 9 established benchmarks) and demonstrate two complementary paradigms — decoupled simulator for scalable agentic RL (beating real-environment training alone) and unified agent foundation model where world-model training acts as an effective warm-up across 7 agentic benchmarks; 2026-06-23 (cs.CL); discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-25 (LLM world-models / agentic-RL / foundation-models theme). Complements the Are-We-Ready-for-Agent-Native-Memory and self-compacting-agent entries on this page by providing the missing environment-simulation substrate that the agentic-RL field has lacked — instead of expensive real-environment rollouts, agents can now train against a high-fidelity language-based world model, dramatically reducing the cost of multi-step agentic RL.



- [[the-hitchhikers-guide-to-agentic-ai-from-foundations-to-systems-2606.24937]] — The Hitchhiker's Guide to Agentic AI: From Foundations to Systems: a comprehensive practitioner's reference for building autonomous AI systems, organized around the thesis that building great agentic systems requires understanding every layer of the pipeline (LLM substrate, inference optimization, tools, planning, memory, multi-agent coordination, evaluation, safety, production deployment) — not just one; Roitman, Haggai walks the full vertical stack and positions agentic AI as a systems-engineering discipline rather than a research frontier; 2026-06-22 (cs.AI); discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-25 (agentic-systems practitioner-reference theme). Complements the Are-We-Ready-for-Agent-Native-Memory and CLI-Universe entries on this page by providing the cross-layer systems-engineering framing that the substrate and data-pipeline work individually assume but never articulate together.

- [[cli-universe-towards-verifiable-task-synthesis-engine-for-terminal-agents-2606.22883]] — CLI-Universe: Towards Verifiable Task Synthesis Engine for Terminal Agents: a verifiable task synthesis engine that generates high-quality, executable terminal-agent training tasks from a constructed universe of command-line environments, addressing the data-quality ceiling that limits scaling of agentic RL in the terminal domain; Hua, Zhanbo; Yao, Yifan; Xie, Weihao; Zhao, Yongchi et al. identify and correct three failure modes of existing synthesis approaches (ambiguous instructions, shallow execution paths, brittle verification) via environment-grounded task generation with deterministic execution checks; 2026-06-22 (cs.AI); discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-25 (LLM agent training-data / terminal-agent-verifiable-task-synthesis theme). Complements the OpenThoughts-Agent open-data-pipeline and Tmax terminal-agent-recipe work on this page by specializing the verifiable-task-synthesis philosophy to the terminal-agent sub-domain and providing the deterministic-reward substrate that downstream agentic RL needs.

- [[toward-open-weight-models-without-risks-separating-public-and-private-capabilities-in-llms-2606.21638]] — Toward Open Weight Models Without Risks: Separating Public and Private Capabilities in LLMs: argues that the open-weight release dilemma (suppress dangerous capabilities pre-release vs. mediate access through closed API services) is a false binary, and proposes structured capability-separation as a middle path that lets researchers audit and disable risky behaviors post-hoc while preserving broad public utility; Feghali, Charbel El; Patel, Arkil; Meade, Nicholas; Gella, Spandana et al. frame capability-separation as a model-architecture property rather than a deployment policy; 2026-06-19 (cs.CR); discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-25 (LLM safety / open-weight-deployment / capability-separation theme). Complements the What-Intermediate-Layers-Know entropy-dynamics jailbreak-detection work on this page by addressing the open-release-side of LLM safety with an architectural primitive, while the entropy-dynamics probe addresses the runtime-side detection of adversarial prompts.

- [[dataclaw0-agentic-tailoring-multimodal-data-from-raw-streams-2606.21337]] — DataClaw0: Agentic Tailoring Multimodal Data from Raw Streams: reframes multimodal data preparation as an agentic capability rather than a heuristic pipeline, introducing the Agentic Data Tailoring paradigm where an AI agent actively refines and structures raw multimodal streams to align with downstream task intents; the authors train a 9B DataClaw_0 model via a two-stage pipeline (semantic synthesis grounded in deterministic Factual Anchors, then SFT + GRPO), and demonstrate downstream quality gains on video generation, real-world VQA, and GUI navigation at a fraction of the cost of manual curation; Wan, Cong, Guo, Zeyu, Cai, Zijian et al. also release DataClaw_0-val, the first dedicated benchmark for data-refinement capability validated against downstream post-training; 2026-06-19 (cs.LG); discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-25 (LLM training-data / agentic-data-pipeline theme). Complements the Autodata agentic-data-scientist work on this page by extending the agentic-data paradigm from synthetic-data *generation* to raw multimodal *tailoring*, and by validating against intrinsic (downstream task) rather than reconstruction-fidelity metrics.

- [[grouped-query-experts-mixture-of-experts-on-gqa-self-attention-2606.20945]] — Grouped Query Experts: Mixture-of-Experts on GQA Self-Attention: composes a mixture-of-experts (MoE) layer on top of grouped-query attention (GQA), with a router selecting k query-head experts per token while the key/value heads remain dense and unchanged; Tripathi, Vishesh and Kumar, Abhay show that at the 250M / 30B-token scale, GQE matches the all-active GQA baseline in downstream accuracy while activating only ~50% of query heads per token, preserving the KV-cache benefits of GQA while reducing active query-head compute; 2026-06-18 (cs.LG); discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-25 (LLM efficient-architecture / sparse-attention theme). Complements the standard FFN-level sparse-MoE literature by introducing a complementary query-head sparse-activation axis, opening up a 2D sparse-activation design space for transformers at long context where per-token query routing is constant-cost while pairwise attention scales quadratically.

- [[learning-from-your-own-mistakes-constructing-learnable-micro-reflective-trajectories-for-self-distillation-2606.18844]] — Learning from Your Own Mistakes: Constructing Learnable Micro-Reflective Trajectories for Self-Distillation: introduces Trajectory-Augmented Policy Optimization (TAPO), which advances self-distillation from implicit logit-level KL alignment to explicit trajectory construction — the model produces both correct and incorrect rollouts to the same query, and TAPO builds micro-reflective corrections that retain the model's own erroneous prefix and then insert a natural-language diagnosis plus corrected reasoning anchored to a correct reference from the same sampling group; Huang, Zhilin, Gao, Hang, Dong, Ziqiang et al. report consistent gains over GRPO on AIME 2024/2025 and HMMT 2025 at matched training-step budgets, with separate first-pass and error-correction improvements; 2026-06-17 (cs.LG); discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-25 (LLM reasoning / self-distillation / RL-training-method theme). Complements the Beyond Reward Engineering long-context RL data-recipe work on this page by suggesting that the next-generation reasoning-RL frontier is in *training signal construction* (contrastive trajectory anchoring at the model's capability boundary) rather than reward shaping or algorithm variants.

- [[tmax-a-simple-recipe-for-terminal-agents-2606.23321]] — Tmax: A simple recipe for terminal agents: introduces an open RL training recipe for terminal-using language-model agents that brings open-data training closer to the frontier, achieving 27% on Terminal-Bench 2.0 with a small curated dataset and simple reward shaping where prior open attempts fell well short; Ivison, Yin, Shao, Xiao, Lambert argue the gap has been a missing-data/missing-recipe problem, not an open-vs-frontier capability ceiling; 2026-06-22 (cs.CL); discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-25 (LLM agent training-data / terminal-agents / open-RL-recipe theme). Complements the AOHP OS-harness and OpenThoughts-Agent data-recipe entries on this page by showing that the RL side of the open agent stack is also closing — Tmax targets a previously hard-to-train computer-use benchmark that open models had not crossed 10% on, and the released recipe + data lets other groups reproduce and extend the result.

- [[biomatrix-towards-a-comprehensive-biological-foundation-model-spanning-the-modality-matrix-of-sequences-structures-and-language-2606.22138]] — BioMatrix: Towards a Comprehensive Biological Foundation Model Spanning the Modality Matrix of Sequences, Structures, and Language: introduces the first decoder-only multimodal foundation model that natively integrates sequences, structures, and natural language for both molecules and proteins within a single architecture, unifying the two previously disjoint axes of biological-FM design (native multimodality under a shared objective vs broad entity coverage) that prior models had to choose between; Pei, Zhou, Duan, Zhao, Li close the structural-modeling gap that prior multi-entity biological FMs either omitted or approximated implicitly; 2026-06-20 (cs.CL); discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-25 (LLM cross-domain foundation-models / biology / multimodal-pre-training theme). Complements the Qwen-AgentWorld and BioAgent-family discoveries on this page by extending the foundation-model unification story from agentic and language-only domains into the biology substrate — BioMatrix shows that a single decoder-only pre-trained model can be the substrate for both molecule- and protein-centric tasks across sequence/structure/language modalities, a property that earlier biology-specific encoders could not deliver.

- [[deeper-is-not-always-better-mitigating-the-alignment-tax-via-confident-layer-decoding-2606.21906]] — Deeper is Not Always Better: Mitigating the Alignment Tax via Confident Layer Decoding: identifies a recurring Guess-Refine-Perturb dynamic across LLM depth — early layers form coarse guesses, intermediate layers refine reasoning-relevant semantics, and final layers can perturb these refined predictions toward generic or alignment-preferred tokens — and introduces Confident Decoding, a training-free inference strategy that selects intermediate-layer predictions when the model is more confident in them than in the final layer, recovering reasoning quality lost to alignment training; Zhang, Zhoubian, Chen, Tang, Yang show the alignment tax can be mitigated at inference time without retraining or sacrificing the alignment surface; 2026-06-20 (cs.CL); discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-25 (LLM inference-time alignment / layer-dynamics / training-free-alignment-mitigation theme). Complements the What-Intermediate-Layers-Know jailbreak-detection work on this page by surfacing the dual side of intermediate-layer utility — that paper showed intermediate layers leak information useful for safety probes, this paper shows intermediate layers also leak information useful for the model itself when the final-layer alignment surface has over-written a better answer.



- [[naturebench-can-coding-agents-match-the-published-sota-of-nature-family-papers-2606.24530]] — NatureBench: Can Coding Agents Match the Published SOTA of Nature-Family Papers?: introduces a cross-discipline benchmark of 90 tasks distilled from peer-reviewed Nature-family publications paired with the NatureGym automated per-task containerized environment pipeline, and shows that under a strict web-search-disabled protocol the strongest frontier agent surpasses published SOTA on only 17.8% of tasks under the g>0.1 criterion; Wang, Cheng, Zuo, Zeng, He find that successful runs succeed primarily through methodological translation into familiar supervised prediction rather than genuine scientific invention, with failures dominated by wrong method choice and insufficient compute budget rather than task misunderstanding; 2026-06-23 (cs.CL); discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-25 (LLM agent-evaluation / scientific-discovery-replication theme). Complements the agentic-benchmarking literature on this page (EnterpriseClawBench, AGORA, Counsel) by pushing into the scientific-replication slice with a maintainer-side leaderboard and explicit environment-fragmentation mitigation.

- [[cavewoman-how-large-language-models-behave-under-linguistic-input-and-output-2606.24083]] — CAVEWOMAN: How Large Language Models Behave Under Linguistic Input and Output Compression: introduces a two-channel evaluation protocol (input = user prompt, output = model response) measuring accuracy, realized per-item cost, and reference-text agreement on the same items, and shows that output compression reliably saves cost (1.4-2.4x per model, up to 3x best case) while input compression is a strict lose-lose that raises net cost by ~1.15x on the five-benchmark mean (up to 2.7x under stronger compression) because models compensate with longer responses as accuracy collapses; Adeyemi, Rossi, Dernoncourt additionally document a "correct yet divergent" regime on non-reasoning models where roughly half of all generations are accurate but their surface text no longer entails the model's own unconstrained baseline; 2026-06-23 (cs.CL); discovered via emergent-concept search on 2026-06-25 (LLM inference-efficiency / prompt-vs-output-cost theme). Complements the Constraint Tax work on this page by quantifying the channel-asymmetric cost of compression, an effect that prior single-channel "caveman prompt" evaluations could not detect.
